title: [fable] No center-to-dial frequency conversion anywhere — Tuxlink tunes the VFO to the catalog CENTER freq, transmitting 1.5 kHz off-channel on every VARA/ARDOP dial
description: Ground truth (winlink-re decompiled corpus): RMS Relay HFInterface.cs:1471 strDialFrequency=(num-1.5); RMS Trimode Channels.cs help text: 'Center frequency is 1500 Hz higher than the upper sideband dial frequency for all modes.' The Winlink channel catalog (and Tuxlink find_stations frequencies_khz) publishes CENTER frequencies; the radio USB dial must be center-1500Hz. Tuxlink has NO such conversion (grep for 1500/center in modem_commands.rs, vara/commands.rs, catalog/: absent) — freq_hz flows verbatim to the CAT tune, so every catalog-driven dial TXes 1.5kHz high and listens equally off-channel. Confirmed live 2026-07-09: 4 on-air 20m dials at catalog freqs unanswered (kestrel-butte-granite campaign). Blocks all catalog-driven on-air connects. Fix: single conversion at the tune boundary (dial_hz = center_hz - 1500 for USB data modes), UI shows both (WLE shows center; operator manual-entry semantics need deciding + labeling), Find-a-Station 'Use' passes center consistently. Also applies to the VARA panel Frequency field semantics (operator typed 7.108 believing dial).
close_reason: Closed

title: P0: arm state does not reflect operator intent — disarm doesn't stick / re-enables without interaction
description: Live-fire (2026-07-09, PR #1061, r2-poe): operator reports the Agent-send ARM control 'wasn't actually disabled or somehow got re-enabled without me interacting with it.' Distinct from tuxlink-4u43s (disarm doesn't abort in-flight): this is the arm STATE itself not tracking operator action. Suspects to check: (1) a long arm window (observed armed_remaining_secs=14365, ~4h) where 'disarm' in the UI does not call disarm/clear the grant, only the timer expires; (2) VNC-lag double-events; (3) an auto-rearm path; (4) arm state surviving a process/UI event. Between this and 4u43s, the operator's arm/disarm is not a trustworthy transmit control — the only reliable brake right now is GUI Stop / vara_stop_session (ungated AbortPort). Needs: disarm must immediately clear the grant AND abort any in-flight egress; verify no path re-arms without an explicit operator action; surface true live arm state in server_info + UI.
notes: [fable] 2026-07-09 kestrel-butte-granite: BOTH directions now operator-confirmed on 0.87.0. Operator explicitly armed for 1h in the UI; backend server_info stayed armed:false and MCP egress was denied ('send authority is not armed'); no egress_arm trace reached the jsonl. A second arm attempt minutes later took effect normally. Arm state between UI (useEgressArm poke-then-poll, 2s egress_status backstop) and the egress guard is untrustworthy in both directions. Note the 2s status poll should have corrected a UI-only arm within seconds — it did not, suggesting the UI arm click's invoke succeeded against SOMETHING or the poll lied. Related observability gaps: tuxlink-46hof/o1e9w (swallowed invoke failures).

title: P0: operator DISARM does not abort an in-flight agent transmit session
description: Live-fire finding (2026-07-09, first real VARA dial, PR #1061 build on r2-poe): during an agent-initiated vara_b2f_exchange that was actively keying the FT-710 (ConReq cycle), the operator disarmed send authority — and the session kept keying/transmitting until VARA itself gave up (~43s). Nothing aborted. The egress guard gates NEW guarded_egress ops only; an in-flight op holds its authorization to completion. Operator expectation (correct): disarm = kill switch for agent RF, immediately. Fix shape: egress disarm (and arm-expiry?) must fire the existing abort paths for any in-flight agent egress — VaraSession abort_in_flight/vara_stop_session_inner, ARDOP modem abort writer, cms_abort — mirroring the AbortPort semantics (stopping is always allowed). The abort machinery all exists and is proven; it is just not wired to disarm. Observed on: armed 4h window, disarm clicked mid-ConReq, PTT ON/OFF continued in session log until VARA DISCONNECTED.

title: [P0][bug] Elmer Stop button no longer aborts Ollama inference — OOM risk (regression)
description: OPERATOR-REPORTED P0 (2026-07-05): Clicking Stop in the Elmer pane no longer actually stops the in-flight Ollama inference. REGRESSION ('no longer' = worked before, a recent change broke it). Impact: inference keeps running on the local/remote Ollama host after Stop -> can OOM the host (runaway/stacked inference; model stays loaded/generating). Suspect cancel path: Elmer pane Stop -> Tauri command -> ElmerSession CancellationToken -> tuxlink-agent-runner/src/runner.rs (COR-2 select! of per-turn provider call vs cancel token) -> tuxlink-agent-frontend ollama_provider.rs/provider.rs (reqwest to Ollama /api/chat). Likely: cancel no longer drops the provider future / the request is spawned or the stream buffered so it survives cancel; and/or model keep_alive keeps it hot. Investigation in progress (delta-basil-fen). Fix goes on its OWN branch off main (not the cnz5o branch).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED on-host (delta-basil-fen, 2026-07-05). Reproduced cleanly + safely via d3zwe (same CancellationToken path as the Elmer Stop button) against the Framework's Ollama, single qwen3.5:35b run:
- SIGINT d3zwe -> outcome 'cancelled', d3zwe PROCESS EXITED (OS closes the socket = maximal clean disconnect).
- Yet qwen3.5:35b stayed loaded+generating in /api/ps for 48s+ after cancel (keep_alive:0, so loaded==actively generating). It kept running to completion despite the disconnect.
CONCLUSION: **Ollama does NOT abort the in-flight generation on client disconnect** (this Framework/version). Tuxlink's cancel plumbing is CORRECT but POWERLESS (loop stops, future drops, socket closes, process exits — Ollama ignores it). So NO client-side cancel fix (drop future / pool_max_idle_per_host(0) / explicit cancel-select) can stop it. Commit 8c37c2a4 (stream false->true) is likely NOT the true root cause (Ollama ignores disconnect regardless of stream mode).
OOM MECHANISM: Stop looks successful (UI stops, loop exits) but the generation keeps running server-side; user hits Send again -> 2+ 35b generations STACK on the host -> OOM. (The prior crash also had orphaned A/B d3zwe loops from this session adding stacking; those are killed.)
FIX DIRECTION (needs operator decision): client-side = (1) bound num_predict/max output so a single gen can't run away, (2) prevent stacking: block a new Send while a generation is known in-flight (track it / poll /api/ps) since Ollama keeps running post-cancel. Ollama-side = investigate why this Ollama version/config ignores client disconnect (compare vs the host where cancel 'worked before' — likely an Ollama version/behavior difference, i.e. the real regression may be Ollama-side or the move to the Framework's Ollama, not Tuxlink). No Ollama cancel API exists; closing the connection is the only client lever and it does not work here.

title: Elmer: docs/help retrieval tool + refuse-when-ungrounded, so the model stops confabulating Tuxlink internals
description: OBSERVED 2026-07-05: model confidently invents Tuxlink's OWN architecture with NO lookup — e.g. 'passwords stored in ~/.config/tuxlink/tuxlink.cfg base64/mode 600' — which is not just ungrounded but WRONG and security-relevant: Tuxlink uses the OS keyring / in-memory only, no disk creds by default (feedback_no_disk_creds_default). Failure class: 'no tool for the question.' Tuxlink is NOT in pretraining, so 100% of what the model says about Tuxlink internals is fabricated (plausible generic-Linux-app patterns). Distinct from the station-recall class (tool exists, model skips it: tuxlink-74at8). FIX (same shape, new class): (1) DOCS/HELP RETRIEVAL TOOL (search_help/query_docs) over docs/user-guide/ (in-app Help source) + README maturity matrix + WLE-parity docs; 'where are passwords stored?' -> search_help('credential storage') -> real answer (keyring). (2) SYSTEM PROMPT: add 'You have no reliable knowledge of Tuxlink internals (not in training data). Answer product/how-it-works/config questions ONLY from the docs tool; if uncovered, say you do not know.' (3) FINE-TUNE must TRAIN the refusal reflex + tool routing (base gpt-oss confabulates past a prompt). (4) GOLD-GEN COVERAGE GAP: scenarios are station/radio tasks; add a PRODUCT/HELP question family where gold routes through search_help or refuses when uncovered. PRINCIPLE: enumerate every operator question class (station, path, grid->place, msg status, product/how-to, config), ensure each has a grounding tool + un-memorizable env + trained call/refuse behavior; any missed class = confident-hallucination surface. Relates: tuxlink-74at8 (memorization), resolve_grid feature.
notes: SCOPE CORRECTION (2026-07-05, delta-basil-fen): operator reaffirmed 'we are not training refusal at all.' This issue's clause (3) 'FINE-TUNE must TRAIN the refusal reflex' is SUPERSEDED. Grounded-honesty here is anti-CONFABULATION (say 'I don't know' / route to docs tool), which is DISTINCT from security injection-refusal (that is punted to guard+operator engineering controls — see closed tuxlink-grg1i). Deliver grounded honesty via (1) the docs/help retrieval TOOL + (2) SYSTEM PROMPT ('answer product/config Qs only from docs tool; if uncovered, say you do not know'), NOT via a trained refusal reflex. Gold-gen still adds the product/help question family, but as tool-routing / honest-decline coverage, not restraint training. cnz5o (tuxlink-as-sim honest environment) is the enabler: honest tool results instead of fake {ok:true} stubs let the student learn grounded answering.

title: Elmer gold-gen: randomize the find_stations directory per scenario (fixed table teaches recall shortcut, not grounding)
description: ROOT CAUSE of models skipping find_stations and recalling the station list (operator observed 2026-07-05, 'surprisingly successful but wrong'): simulator.py:19-29 serves a FIXED, small, real-callsign gateway table (W7GW@DM43, K7AZ@DM33, ...) IDENTICALLY across every scenario/iteration. It is memorizable, so gold-gen distills the table into the weights and the model recalls it at runtime (no gate) instead of calling the tool. The gate itself is sound (Judge.score hard-requires required_tools -> 'missing required tool: find_stations'; grounding predicates match answers against actual find_stations records, empty when the tool is skipped). But a fixed environment TEACHES the recall shortcut alongside grounding. FIX: synthesize the find_stations directory PER SCENARIO — synthetic callsigns (pattern already exists for recipients, scenariogen.py:50), randomized-but-VALID Maidenhead grids with distances COMPUTED from grid geometry (haversine on grid_to_lat_lon) so distance_band stays consistent, varied bands/freqs/schedules; seeded for determinism but different every scenario. Then recall is impossible, the only winning strategy is calling find_stations + using its output, gold yields only grounded trajectories, and the gate becomes genuinely discriminating (on a fixed table base AND teacher can recall, so grounding isn't the discriminator). Subsumes the grid-placename issue (tuxlink for resolve_grid): synthetic grids can't be recalled to places either. PRINCIPLE: any observation the model can memorize WILL be memorized -> distills the shortcut, not the skill; environments must be un-memorizable for grounded tool-use to be the only winning strategy. Relates to the discriminating-eval premise (tuxlink-6zkb6).
close_reason: Per-scenario randomized find_stations directory shipped (commits da5268fd + 66e43448 on bd-tuxlink-6zkb6/discriminating-eval). build_gateways synthesizes un-memorizable directories (synthetic callsigns, valid grids, haversine distances); seed_for_scenario (sha256, process-stable) couples generation<->judge replay; wired into all 4 sim sites. 249 tests + 2352-scenario oracle sweep (0 UNSAT). Codex adrev: coupling+invariants clean, 1 P2 (6-char mirror) folded.

title: Bundle ardopcf built from pinned pflarue/ardop develop source (CODEC + -G webgui + -p) instead of a tagged release
description: DEFINITIVE fix for ARDOP-as-shipped. tuxlink's ardopcf invocation requires THREE features no single tagged release has together: (1) CODEC host command (absent in 1.0.4.1.3, the original bundle); (2) -G/--webgui flag (present in 1.x-develop, REMOVED in 2.0.3.2.1 -> ardopcf dumps usage+exits -> bind timeout); (3) -p/--ptt. Only the develop line has all three. Pin canonical pflarue/ardop develop @ cb2c4c1eaf906ec5914514d83aef903f4b981b43 (version 1.0.4.1.3_develop, Aug 2025) and BUILD FROM SOURCE per-arch in CI (native make on the matrix runner; dep: libasound2-dev). Validated locally: built cb2c4c1, tuxlink's exact 7-arg spawn (-p /dev/ttyUSB1 -G 8514 8515 plughw plughw) binds all 3 ports + -G/-p parse + full init_tnc acks. CODEC TRUE success pends live audio (USB sound card was unplugged during the 06-23 crash). Supersedes the 1.0.4.1.3 (vbpy) and 2.0.3.2.1 (y061) release-curl bundles. GPL-3: unmodified upstream source, built not downloaded.

description: Follow-up to tuxlink-vbpy. The bundled ardopcf 1.0.4.1.3 (Latest release) does NOT implement the CODEC command, but tuxlink's init_tnc (session.rs:337) sends 'CODEC TRUE' and treats the FAULT as fatal -> 'init failed: TNC fault: CMD CODEC not recoginized'. CODEC is a post-1.0.4.1.3 feature; present in the develop build (the spike that worked on the G90) AND in the 2.0.3.2.1 release. Full init-sequence replay (INITIALIZE/CODEC/PROTOCOLMODE/ARQTIMEOUT/LISTEN/MYCALL/GRIDSQUARE) is IDENTICAL between 2.0.3.2.1 and the develop spike (both ack all; both fault only on 'ARQBW FORCED' which tuxlink only sends when bandwidth_hz is set). Fix: change AC_VER 1.0.4.1.3 -> 2.0.3.2.1 in release.yml + ect-build.yml staging. 2.0.3.2.1 has the same per-arch assets (ardopcf_amd64_Linux_64 / ardopcf_arm_Linux_64). Caveat: 2.x ARQ/connect exchange unproven with tuxlink locally (init proven); operator on-air run validates; fallback = build 1.x-develop from source.

title: ARDOP HF not self-contained in shipped build: ardopcf is neither bundled nor declared
description: Shipped tuxlink (0.74.1 / main / pending 0.75.0) cannot do ARDOP HF out of the box. The app managed-spawns ardopcf (ArdopUiConfig.binary default bare name 'ardopcf' -> Command::new -> $PATH), but the .deb does NOT bundle an ardopcf binary (dpkg -L: zero ardop files; only Tauri sidecar is go-pmtiles), does NOT Depends/Recommends it, and no CI/build/tauri.conf step fetches or bundles it. Asymmetry: VHF packet Recommends direwolf (in apt); HF ARDOP has no equivalent, and ardopcf is a community fork NOT in apt, so a Recommends can't satisfy it. Net: a real user installing 0.75.0 and trying ARDOP HF hits 'modem spawn failed' (no ardopcf). This blocks the on-air ARDOP/CMS validation gate as a faithful shipped-product test. Fix options: (A) bundle ardopcf as a Tauri externalBin sidecar (cross-compiled arm64+amd64), wire config default to the bundled path — self-contained .deb, the proper fix (note GPL bundling + the deferred 'modem monolith vs spin-off' architecture question); (B) declare + document an install path (weak: ardopcf not in apt); (C) interim: build ardopcf locally to run the gate now + file (A) as the durable fix. Surfaced by operator: 'I'm testing the shipped product, not a contrived test.'
notes:
2026-06-23 conclusion (operator): ARDOP HF is engine-complete but PRODUCT-incomplete. It only 'works' via dev-host hand-assembly (spike ardopcf + config surgery / PATH symlink) that no .deb user would have. Foundation already proven on the G90 — so running the spike rig validates nothing about the shipped product. STOP validating via the spike. The only legitimate path is to BUILD it into the product: bundle ardopcf as a Tauri externalBin sidecar (auto-resolved path, arm64+amd64), expose/auto-resolve the modem in the UI, and correct the 'Supports ARDOP' claim until shipped. Reverted all config.json surgery. This is the actual feature work, not a fix.

title: P0: APRS modem-link picker — selecting BT/UV-Pro/USB reverts instantly (incomplete-link persist + rollback)
description: ModemLinkSection.selectSegment() unconditionally emits a full packet-link persist on a bare segment switch. For USB/BT/UvproNative the bare emit sends linkKind with a null device/mac (operator hasn't chosen a radio yet) → into_packet_config rejects ('Bluetooth link needs bt_mac' / 'Serial link needs serial_device', ui_commands.rs) → usePacketConfig optimistic-rollback (tuxlink-hoi1) reverts kind → the [kind] re-seed effect snaps the segment back to the saved kind (TCP). Net: BT/UV-Pro can never be selected (catch-22: can't reach the device picker), and USB reverts once its device selection is cleared. Silent in logs (backend returns Err, frontend rolls back). Confirmed via operator repro: clicking BT flashes active then snaps to TCP very fast. Core advertised feature (README). Fix: bare segment switch must switch locally + only persist when the link is complete (device/mac present); the device dropdown's onChange persists the complete link.

title: Map renders 'drunk' / tiles blank for seconds — preserveDrawingBuffer:true throttles software-GL rendering
description: Root-caused 2026-06-19 (operator + fen-savanna-grouse) by raw-diffing the map pipeline against the last-known-good build (#819, ~24h prior). The ONLY substantive map-core change was ni5b adding preserveDrawingBuffer:true to the maplibre Map constructor (MapLibreMap.tsx) so the WX PNG export could read the canvas. On this Pi's software renderer (llvmpipe), preserving the framebuffer every frame throttles ALL map rendering -> tiles take 7-20s, areas blank for seconds ('drunk map'). Evidenced by the tuxlink-xezm tile trace: zero errors (nothing fails, just slow), a 1-feature in-memory geojson tile queued 3.7s, overview tiles 7-20s at zoom-out, basemap-wide regardless of APRS. Fix: remove preserveDrawingBuffer; capture the WX PNG during a render event (map.once('render')+triggerRepaint) so the export still works at zero steady-state cost. 228 map/aprs tests green; typecheck clean. Operator grim smoke confirms.
notes:
ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED (PR #839, moss-bog-cove 2026-06-19). NOT preserveDrawingBuffer, NOT pack data. Root cause: WxOverlay category-filter effect (AprsPositionsMap.tsx ~L618) calls setFilter(layer, null) on every styledata in the default 'all' state. MapLibre's no-op guard is deepEqual(layer.filter, filter); a cleared filter is stored as undefined and deepEqual(undefined, null)===false, so the clear never early-returns -> _updateLayer marks the source for reload + _changed -> per-frame Style.update reloads sources + re-fires styledata -> handler re-runs -> infinite self-clocking per-frame source-reload loop. Amplified by 4x usePushData styledata->setData re-pushes saturating the worker pool (1-feature in-memory tiles took 5-7s). Fix: clear with undefined (deepEqual(undefined,undefined)===true short-circuits to a no-op). Confirmed via trace logs + maplibre 5.24.0 source + independent adversarial refutation. Test gap closed: testMapLibreMock had no setFilter (effect was dead code under test); added it + a regression test that fails against the null code. 229 FE tests green, typecheck clean. OWED: operator runtime confirm (tauri dev HMR + drive map; expect no maplibre-tile-slow lines). Do NOT close until confirmed. Pack-data theory ruled out: 17.4GB was a region-manifest typical_bytes estimate, not a prior pack; download.rs changed only the progress watchdog.

description: Operator grim-smoke: the merged cn84 animation (PR #829) pegs the Pi's software-GL (llvmpipe) CPU and starves map tile rendering — map basically unusable. Root cause (confirmed): DigipeatPathLayer's rAF loop calls setData on 3 GeoJSON sources EVERY frame, forcing maplibre to re-tessellate + the CPU rasterizer to fully repaint continuously — the exact per-frame cost the map's perf design (vnk7/gq0d, fadeDuration:0, feature-state) avoids. Agent cannot verify any fix (software-GL only on operator's build), and even the line-trim-offset approach still forces continuous full-map repaint which may not relieve the peg. Operator decision: FULL REVERT now. Surgical removal of the cn84 FRONTEND animation from AprsPositionsMap (imports, PATH_* constants, pathFC/pointFC/labelFC, DigipeatPathLayer, render child) + delete digipeatPath.ts/pathTrace.ts + tests; KEEP the inert backend via-chain (decode_digi_hbits, InboundPos.via) for the eventual rework. Removing the rAF loop eliminates the CPU peg with certainty. The proper animation rework (xl4d) stays held and must be software-GL-smoke-verified before any re-merge; the continuous-animation approach itself may need to be reconsidered for llvmpipe. title: [SECURITY P0] Path traversal: unsanitized inbound Mid header → arbitrary file write (native_mailbox store)
description: HIGH-severity, remotely-reachable path traversal found in the 2026-06-16 pre-public security review (confirmed by 2 independent agents + manual verification). native_mailbox.rs Mailbox::store_ns (~line 113) writes fs::write(dir.join(format!('{mid}.b2f')), raw) where mid = msg.header('Mid') from a RECEIVED Winlink message, with NO validation. A crafted Mid (absolute path or ../) escapes the mailbox dir; raw is also attacker-controlled → arbitrary file write with attacker bytes (clobber configs, allowed_stations.json, drop autostart/.desktop → path to RCE). Data flow: wire bytes → lzhuf::decompress → Message::from_bytes → mailbox.store(Inbox,..) → sink. No Mid sanitizer exists in the crate (our own generate_mid is safe, but inbound is unchecked). Sibling sinks at native_mailbox.rs:133 (.identity), 260/274/289/297/304-317 (read/move via id.0). FIX: validate Mid at the store/from_bytes boundary (^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,64}$ → BackendError::MessageRejected) + canonicalize-within-dir assertion after join; regression test with an absolute + ../ Mid. Part of pre-public hardening.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-5lbm-fix-mid-path-traversal. Branch: bd-tuxlink-5lbm/fix-mid-path-traversal. Created 2026-06-16 17:00:21 UTC by maple-towhee-moraine.

title: B2F handshake aborts on a packet RMS positive status line (*** Connected to CMS)
description:
On-air 2026-06-16, KT7RUN-10 (real Winlink RMS): connect + acking worked; the RMS bridged us to the CMS and sent, as I-frames: ns0 'Trying ec2-...amazonaws.com', ns1 '*** N7CPZ-7 Connected to CMS', ns2 '[WL2K-5.0-B2FWIHJM$]' (the SID). Our B2F handshake parser ABORTED on the ns1 line and we DISC'd one frame before the SID. Error: Handshake(RemoteError("N7CPZ-7 Connected to CMS")).

Root cause: handshake.rs read_handshake treats ANY line starting with '***' as a fatal HandshakeError::RemoteError. But a packet RMS relays us to the CMS and emits POSITIVE status lines (*** <call> Connected to CMS) before the SID — telnet (direct to CMS) never sees these, so it is packet-specific. The code even has a comment saying '*** lines are ignored', contradicted by the early return.

FIX: in read_handshake, do NOT abort on a positive '*** ... Connected ...' status line — skip it and keep reading toward the SID. Keep erroring on genuine failure/disconnect '***' lines (existing tests: '*** Callsign not authorized - Disconnecting', '*** Rejected ...'). Add a test with the KT7RUN-10 preamble (Trying / *** Connected to CMS / SID) asserting we reach the SID, not RemoteError.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-zlo8-b2f-rms-status-line. Branch: bd-tuxlink-zlo8/b2f-rms-status-line. Created 2026-06-16 11:09:00 UTC by atoll-magpie-sandbar.
close_reason: Closed

title: AX.25 stall after connect: we never answer the gateway's RR(P=1) link-establishment poll
description: Trace (atoll-magpie-sandbar, 2026-06-16): connect succeeds (clean UA, response-framed). Gateway KT7RUN-7 then sends RR(nr=0,P=1) command polls every ~T1 (~9s) to checkpoint the link before sending its B2F SID. pump_acks (datalink.rs) handles Control::Rr {nr, ..} — discards the poll bit, acks internally, sends NOTHING. AX.25 v2.2 §6.2: a command with P=1 REQUIRES a response with F=1. Without it the gateway never advances to sending its [RMS...] SID I-frame, our Dial-role read_remote_handshake (session/mod.rs: 'remote speaks first') blocks forever, session stalls until operator abort. Also: Frame::decode discards the command/response C-bit (Path::decode drops _cr), so we can't distinguish a command poll from a response ack — but this station never originates supervisory polls, so an inbound RR/RNR with pf=1 is necessarily a command poll. FIX: pump_acks answers an inbound RR/RNR poll (pf=true) with RR(nr=V(R), F=1) (RR encodes as a response via is_command()=false). Follow-up to the merged C/R-bit fix (tuxlink-bhx6).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-uv1i-ax25-poll-response. Branch: bd-tuxlink-uv1i/ax25-poll-response. Created 2026-06-16 09:06:30 UTC by atoll-magpie-sandbar.
close_reason: Closed

title: AX.25 B2F negotiation fails on-air: Ax25Stream::read Ok(0) treated as EOF by BufReader
description: Root cause (session atoll-magpie-sandbar, 2026-06-16). After SABM/UA connect succeeds, the B2F driver wraps the AX.25 stream in std::io::BufReader (winlink_backend.rs native_packet_exchange) and reads the relay SID via handshake.rs fill_buf / wire::read_line read_until. Ax25Stream::read (datalink.rs:634) returns Ok(0) when no inbound bytes are queued after a single ~20ms poll. BufReader turns inner Ok(0) into Ok(&[]) -> handshake 'Ok([]) => ConnectionClosed' fires; wire::read_line n==0 -> UnexpectedEof. On real RF the relay SID arrives hundreds of ms-seconds later, so we declare EOF and tear down while the relay keeps RR-polling ('pings indefinitely'). The read()'s own defect-J doc-comment warned the driver must not treat Ok(0) as EOF, but the BufReader wiring (shared with blocking-TCP telnet) structurally violates it. Tests pass because FakeAx25Stream pre-loads data so inbound is never empty. Affects KISS + UV-Pro identically; likely degrades APRS too. Fix: make Ax25Stream::read block (poll pump_acks) until >=1 byte / self.closed (true EOF, Ok(0)) / bounded idle deadline (Err TimedOut, never Ok(0) on timeout) / abort (Err). Failing-test-first.
notes: Fix MERGED to origin/main 2026-06-16 (PR #748, merge 1345d7a9; fix commit 00d77b1f). CI 4/4 green; Codex confirmed C/R classification correct + no APRS regression. REMAINING: operator on-air confirmation that the gateway now negotiates (RR sent as response, gateway advances). Keep open until that smoke lands; fix-forward if it reveals more.

title: [P0] Status-bar Connect must one-click fire the last-configured session of ANY type (not just Telnet-CMS)
description:
OBSERVED: status-bar Connect button (DashboardRibbon → AppShell.onConnect, src/shell/AppShell.tsx:797) only invokes a real connect for CMS-over-Telnet (invoke('cms_connect')). For every other session type — ARDOP, VARA, packet — it falls into the early-return branch 'setSelectedConnection(activeConnection); return' which merely opens/selects that connection's radio panel and does NOT connect. When the panel is already shown, setSelectedConnection is a no-op → button appears completely dead for ARDOP.

REQUIRED (operator, P0): the status-bar Connect button MUST one-click FIRE the last-configured session of ANY type. Workflow: operator configures a session in the Radio modem pane (e.g. ARDOP target+settings), CLOSES the pane to reclaim screen real estate, then uses the status-bar Connect to periodically re-fire that same connection WITHOUT looking at specifics. Enables APRS Chat open foreground + single-click HF comms in the background.

IMPLICATIONS TO RESOLVE: (1) 'last-configured session' must be persisted/retrievable so the status bar can re-fire it without the panel open (ARDOP target appeared to be session-only useState, not persisted — verify + persist if needed). (2) RF modes transmit → RADIO-1: per radio1-governs-tx-not-UI the Connect CLICK is the per-invocation consent, so single-click fire with click-as-consent (no modal interruption — operator wants 'without looking at specifics'); ribbon must show Connecting… + working Abort (abort fix #728 just landed). (3) onConnect must dispatch the active connection's own connect flow per type (ARDOP: mint consent + modem_ardop_connect with saved target; VARA/packet analogous; Telnet-CMS keeps cms_connect).

title: Bootstrap self-heal for orphan-v2 missing activation secret (transmit un-brick)
description: P0 follow-up to tuxlink-6wz3, verified STILL PRESENT on origin/main (0.65.0): a user who migrated config v1->v2 during ~0.55-0.57 (before the migration activation-secret backfill at bootstrap.rs:160) has a CMS password in the keyring but NO activation secret (account tuxlink-identity-activation:<CALL>). resolve_auto_identity (bootstrap.rs:514) does svc.authenticate(full,pw).ok() -> None on NoSecretSet with NO heal, so auto-auth fails silently every launch AND manual unlock fails too (both need the secret) -> hard transmit brick; only re-running the wizard fixes it. GH #691 (Orv/W6BI, 0.59.0) is a likely instance. Fix: in resolve_auto_identity, on IdentityError::NoSecretSet, provision the activation secret FROM the stored CMS password (restoring the write_password lockstep invariant) and retry — heal ONLY NoSecretSet, NEVER CredentialMismatch. SECURITY-IMPLICATED (auto-provisioning a secret) — converge with Codex before implementing. Identity code byte-unchanged 0.59->0.65.0 (0/207 commits).
notes: CI GREEN on all 4 (verify+build-linux, both arches; amd64 verify was a one-time cache flake, rerun passed). PR #719 marked READY. Awaiting operator review + on-air smoke of the orphan-v2 un-brick.

title: Region-pack download broken end-to-end (0.65.0): stale planet 404 + 8 download-flow bugs (bug-hunt)
description: bug-hunt-cycle (spruce-finch-gorge 2026-06-15) on 'go-pmtiles exit Some(1)'. Reproduced + 3 hunters. B1: pinned planet build 20260608 404s (Protomaps daily builds rotate ~weekly); 20260614 is live + has the same 9 vector_layers. B2: SidecarExtractor stdout(null) discards go-pmtiles's real error (it writes to stdout). C1(all 3): free-space gate fails OPEN on statvfs err + init_packs never creates basemap-packs/ so first download skips the disk check. C2(2): register_path fail after install leaves a listed-but-404 pack (no rollback). C3: early returns (build_request fail, dup-in-flight) emit no download-done -> UI sticks. C4(2): progress denominator=estimate not real size. C5(2): cancel no-op until first progress event. C6: retry reuses downloadKey -> hook never resets. C7: disk headroom 1.2x < validation budget 3x. Minors: formatBytes rounding, throttle drops final sample, delete_pack manifest-order. Operator decision: durable planet-freshness = remote manifest refreshed weekly -> repoint bundled fallback to a live build + auto-refresh manifest from MANIFEST_URL on startup. Reports: dev/bug-hunts/2026-06-15-region-pack-download-*.md.
notes: Codex 5 follow-ups all resolved (commit 7a27a7e): HIGH backend pre-download manifest refresh (race-free) + 3 MED (free-space-in-lock, drain-join-all-paths, dup-reject no-poison) + LOW requires_restart advisory. Wire-walk PASSED both operator flows (continent NA + tiers) incl. fresh-install/first-download/post-upgrade cold-starts; operator confirmed inline progress row is intended surface. tsc clean, 29 map vitest green. PR #716 body updated; awaiting verify CI before ready.

title: Find-a-Station close-crash (REAL root cause): map-hook cleanups call getLayer/getSource on a removed map → maplibre throws this.style undefined
description: Diagnosed from the robust logs (now that tuxlink-4b96 forwards frontend errors): closing Find a Station throws 'undefined is not an object (evaluating this.style.getLayer)' from maplibre getLayer ← src/map/mapHooks.ts cleanup ← commitHookEffectListUnmount (passive-effect cleanup during unmount). The three mapHooks cleanups (useMapSource:80, useMapLayer:108, useMapOverlay:148) guard removal with if(map.getSource(id)) / if(map.getLayer(id)) — but maplibre 5.24.0 getLayer/getSource dereference this.style, which is undefined once map.remove() has run during teardown, so the GUARD itself throws. (NOTE: this is the actual crash; tuxlink-dvfh/#700's moveend NaN guard was a real but DIFFERENT bug, not this.) FIX: make the cleanups tolerate a torn-down map — wrap the getLayer/getSource/removeLayer/removeSource teardown in a safe helper that swallows (the layers/sources vanish with the map, so a throw means nothing-to-remove). TDD via the map mock simulating a removed map whose getLayer throws; assert unmount no longer throws.

title: Frontend errors invisible to robust logs: forward ErrorBoundary + window.onerror/unhandledrejection into the structured log
description: Operator (alpha): a frontend crash ('Something went wrong' ErrorBoundary) cannot be diagnosed from the robust logs. Confirmed: the tracing-based logs (~/.local/state/tuxlink/logs/tuxlink.<hour>.jsonl + logging window) capture only Rust events; the ErrorBoundary (src/ErrorBoundary.tsx) only does console.error → the React error + componentStack go ONLY to the WebKitGTK devtools / tauri-dev terminal, never to the structured log. There is no frontend→log Tauri command (logging/commands.rs is status/config/export only). FIX: add a log_frontend_error(source, message, stack) Tauri command that emits tracing::error!(target=tuxlink::frontend) so the FanoutLayer captures it to the jsonl + logging window; wire ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch to it; and install global window 'error' + 'unhandledrejection' forwarders at app bootstrap (main.tsx) so uncaught/async errors are captured too. Fire-and-forget + swallow failures (already in an error path). This makes webview crashes diagnosable from logs — the alpha expectation. UNBLOCKS: the Find-a-Station close-crash (still reproducing despite #700) — once this ships + operator rebuilds + reproduces, the real error+stack lands in the log for root-cause.

title: basemap_download_pack freezes UI: sync command blocks main thread (async+spawn_blocking fix)
description: basemap_download_pack is a sync #[tauri::command] -> runs on the MAIN THREAD -> blocks the UI for the entire pack download. A continent (North America) at z14 is many GB -> hard freeze, btop-kill only (operator 2026-06-14). Also why the #704 progress bar never appears: events emitted from the blocked main thread can't reach the webview. Pre-existing (command always sync) but surfaced + made worse by #704's emit-from-blocked-thread. Project idiom (lib.rs:235, winlink_backend.rs spawn_blocking) is async cmd + tokio::task::spawn_blocking for blocking work. FIX: make basemap_download_pack async, move install_pack into spawn_blocking (extract Arc<BasemapState>+Arc<PmtilesRegistry>+AppHandle before the boundary; cancel registry + on_progress emit work on the worker thread). Root-caused via investigate 2026-06-14 (moss-esker-osprey). Operator wire-walk + smoke REQUIRED before any shipped claim.

title: Region-pack download always fails validation: REQUIRED_LAYER_IDS has 4 phantom layers not in the Protomaps build/schema
description: Operator: 'Download North America failed: downloaded pack is invalid: PMTiles metadata missing required layers: natural, physical_line, physical_point, transit'. RCA (spruce-finch-gorge 2026-06-14): basemap/validate.rs REQUIRED_LAYER_IDS lists 13 vector-layer ids, but (a) the pinned Protomaps planet build 20260608 — verified via the bundled world-z0-6.pmtiles metadata — emits only 9 vector_layers (boundaries, buildings, earth, landcover, landuse, places, pois, roads, water), and (b) the @protomaps/basemaps@5.7.2 style tuxlink renders references EXACTLY those same 9 source-layers and NONE of natural/physical_line/physical_point/transit. So the 4 extra ids are phantom (old-schema leftovers); the style doesn't use them and the planet doesn't provide them. validate_pmtiles therefore rejects every legitimately-extracted region pack. The bundled world dodged it only because bundled packs load via register_path WITHOUT validation; the NA download was the first time validate ran. FIX: set REQUIRED_LAYER_IDS to the 9 the style+planet actually use (lossless — the 4 removed are referenced by nothing); update count/comment/tests. Authoritative ground truth = the @protomaps/basemaps@5 source-layer refs == the pinned-build vector_layers == bundled world metadata.

title: Map crash on close (ErrorBoundary): moveend pan-clamp feeds NaN center to setCenter → maplibre LngLat throws
description: Now that the map renders (tuxlink-spo2 software-GL fix), closing the Find-a-Station modal crashes the app to the ErrorBoundary ('Something went wrong, click to reload'). Root cause is in the PR #694 moveend pan-clamp handler (MapLibreMap.tsx, tuxlink-rwo6): on a degenerate/teardown transform, instance.getCenter() yields a non-finite center; clampMapCenter propagates NaN (Math.min/max), and the change-guard lng!==c.lng is always true for NaN, so the handler calls instance.setCenter([NaN,NaN]) → maplibre's LngLat constructor throws 'Invalid LngLat object: (NaN, NaN)'. The throw is synchronous in the React cleanup (a moveend fired during teardown), so the app-wide ErrorBoundary catches it. FIX: in the moveend handler, bail when getCenter() is non-finite (Number.isFinite), and guard the handler from running during/after teardown (a mapRemoved flag set before instance.remove()). Also harden clampMapCenter against NaN. Verified mechanism vs maplibre 5.24.0 LngLat source. Unit-test the non-finite-center guard deterministically via the map mock; operator verifies the real close no longer crashes.

title: Map renders magenta GPU static in shipped app: software-GL env (LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE/GALLIUM_DRIVER) never baked into lib.rs run()
description: v0.62.0 Find-a-Station map shows a magenta/green WebGL static band (all non-map UI crisp). RCA (spruce-finch-gorge 2026-06-14): WebKitGTK hardware WebGL is broken on this Pi-5 V3D/ANGLE stack (probe: bogus 'Apple GPU' renderer, clear+draw read [0,0,0,0], GL_INVALID_OPERATION). The ndi4 spike + dev/render-harness validated maplibre under SOFTWARE GL (LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 + GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe) — and the baked-dark-style decision exists because of software-GL CPU cost (spike: 45fps). But the shipped app's lib.rs:68 run() sets ONLY WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1; the software-GL env was never baked in, so the product runs on broken hardware GL. PROVEN via live A/B on the spike harness (firenze sample.pmtiles): hardware GL = magenta static (reproduces bug), +software GL = clean Florence map render (dev/scratch/harness-A-hardware-gl.png vs harness-B-software-gl.png). FIX: add LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 + GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe to the Linux-gated env block in run(), + a unit test asserting all required GL env vars are set (regression guard against re-dropping). TRADEOFF to flag: forces software GL for all Linux (desktop users with working GPU GL get software maps too) — a Pi-conditional or startup GL-probe gate is a possible follow-up.

title: Basemap blank: tile://pmtiles 206 missing Access-Control-Allow-Origin (CORS-blocks maplibre fetch) + sprite/glyphs URLs not absolute (maplibre v5)
description: With maxBounds fixed (tuxlink-rwo6), the map no longer crashes but renders BLANK in a real build. Console (real WebKitGTK, converge dev build) shows: (1) 'Fetch API cannot load tile://pmtiles/world due to access control checks' + 'Origin http://localhost:1420 is not allowed by Access-Control-Allow-Origin. Status code: 206' — the Rust tile:// pmtiles 206 response (lib.rs ~175) sets Content-Type/Accept-Ranges/Content-Length/ETag/Content-Range but NO Access-Control-Allow-Origin, so maplibre/pmtiles fetch() is CORS-blocked → no vector tiles → blank. (Old Leaflet raster used , not fetch, so never hit CORS — the swap exposed it.) (2) 'Invalid sprite URL /basemap/sprites/dark, must be absolute' — maplibre v5 rejects root-relative sprite (and likely glyphs) URLs in basemapStyle.ts. FIX: (a) add Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* + Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Content-Range,Content-Length,ETag,Accept-Ranges to the pmtiles tile response (and its 416/404/500); (b) make sprite+glyphs URLs absolute (location.origin + path). Affects packaged builds too (cross-scheme fetch). Diagnosed via grim of the devtools console; the basemap had never rendered end-to-end before now.

title: P0: identity framework un-authenticatable on fresh install AND upgrade (transmit bricked) — epic shipped no secret-provisioning path
description:
Build 0.57.1 (tactical-callsigns epic Phases 1-7 shipped). Operator on a CLEAN host: credential setup → 'CMS connection failed: no active identity — authenticate before transmitting'; callsign shows '—' in ribbon, clicking it does nothing; no UI to configure an identity.

ROOT CAUSE (evidenced on origin/main): the epic built a fail-closed transmit gate (Phase 3: NativeBackend.active_identity()? → NoActiveIdentity) + an identity system (IdentityStore + activation secrets in keyring + IdentitySwitcher unlock UI), but NO production path provisions an activation secret or (on fresh install) even creates a FULL identity:
1. FRESH INSTALL: wizard CMS step persist_cms_impl (wizard.rs) writes config.identity.active_full='CALLSIGN' + CMS password to keyring tuxlink/<CALLSIGN>, but NEVER touches IdentityService/IdentityStore/set_activation_secret (wizard.rs has zero identity-system refs). → IdentityStore empty → identity_list returns {full:[],...} → IdentitySwitcher dropdown opens EMPTY (no rows, no add affordance = 'click does nothing') → no active identity → transmit gated.
2. UPGRADE: bootstrap.rs:155 runs IdentityMigration::plan(v1).execute(..., has_cms_account, None) — passes None for activation_secret. Creates the FULL identity row but provisions NO secret → identity_authenticate → IdentityService::authenticate → keyring NoEntry → NoSecretSet → AuthFailed. User sees their callsign+🔒 but ANY credential fails.
3. identity_add_full (the only secret-provisioning command) has ZERO frontend callers.

Keyring scheme: CMS password = service 'tuxlink', account '<CALLSIGN>'. Activation secret = service 'tuxlink', account 'tuxlink-identity-activation:<CALLSIGN-UPPER>'. SEPARATE entries.

Affects BOTH fresh installs and upgrades. Shipped in v0.55.0 → still broken at 0.57.1. Not data loss; recoverable. Fix bridges wizard + migration to provision FULL+activation secret, and decides the auth-on-launch UX (auto-authenticate vs manual per-launch unlock). Discovered via /investigate, moraine-swallow-bayou 2026-06-13.

title: P0: wizard completion doesn't install native backend in-session — CMS dead until app restart
description: Fresh install / reinstall regression. The native backend installs ONLY at bootstrap (bootstrap.rs run/install_native), and ONLY when on-disk config has wizard_completed && connect_to_cms. On first launch bootstrap runs BEFORE the wizard (config NotFound -> action not_connected -> backend NOT installed). Completing wizard_persist_cms (wizard.rs:138) writes a valid config but does NOT install the backend into the live BackendState. So cms_connect (ui_commands.rs:2330) finds state.current()==None -> NotConfigured('backend offline') -> CMS telnet 'doesn't even start' to any address/port. Restart re-runs bootstrap -> action spawn -> backend installed -> works. CONFIRMED via logs (boot 019eba0e not_connected, boot 019eba4f spawn). Fix: after a successful CMS persist, call bootstrap::install_native to bring the backend online in-session (guarded against double-install). Found 2026-06-11 from operator's reinstalled 0.53.1.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-aw6g-wizard-installs-backend. Branch: bd-tuxlink-aw6g/wizard-installs-backend. Created 2026-06-12 05:36:18 UTC by arroyo-tamarack-slate.

title: P0: v1->v2 migration hides the inbox (moves to per-FULL path the read side doesn't read yet)
description: Shipped in 0.52.1 (Phase 2 / tuxlink-7iy2). The config v1->v2 migration renames native-mbox/inbox -> native-mbox/<CALLSIGN>/inbox (config.rs MigrationPlan::execute), but Mailbox::folder_dir(Inbox) still resolves the flat native-mbox/inbox (native_mailbox.rs). The per-FULL READ path is Phase 4 (tuxlink-2ns7), unbuilt — so after upgrade the inbox shows EMPTY. Sent/Outbox tagged in place (retained); config (settings/position) untouched. Data is NOT destroyed: non-clobbering rename leaves messages intact at <root>/<CALLSIGN>/inbox/. Operator (Cameron) hit this on 0.52.1; N7CPZ inbox confirmed intact on disk. ROOT CAUSE = phase-sequencing defect: the inbox MOVE was split from the per-FULL READ change (belongs together in Phase 4). Test gap: migration test read the moved inbox via a per-FULL Mailbox, never the production flat-root read path. FIX (two parts): (1) remove the inbox relocation from the v1->v2 migration — it stays flat until Phase 4 does move+read atomically; (2) startup heal for already-damaged 0.52.1 installs: if <root>/<CALLSIGN>/inbox exists and <root>/inbox does not, rename it back + rebuild the search index; idempotent, non-clobbering, runs independent of the v2 sentinel.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-ej7a-fix-inbox-migration-hide. Branch: bd-tuxlink-ej7a/fix-inbox-migration-hide. Created 2026-06-11 12:14:16 UTC.
close_reason: Fixed + merged (PR #607). v1->v2 migration no longer relocates the inbox; bootstrap::heal_misplaced_inbox restores already-damaged 0.52.1 installs on next launch. Phase 4 (tuxlink-2ns7) now owns the move, to land atomically with the per-FULL read.

title: [P0] ARDOP UI completely broken — favorites station list renders OVER the entire panel, nothing clickable
description: Operator report 2026-06-10: in the ARDOP radio mode, the favorites/station list (FavoritesTabs, mounted inside ArdopRadioPanel.tsx) renders directly ON TOP OF the entire ARDOP UI, covering all of it. Neither the favorites list nor the underlying ARDOP controls are clickable — the whole ARDOP feature is broken. Almost certainly a CSS layering/positioning regression: an absolute/fixed overlay, a z-index/stacking-context error, or a flow-layout break in the ArdopRadioPanel layout + FavoritesTabs mount. SCOPE: pure UI/CSS layout bug, NOT a transmission path — per ADR 0018 the agent fixes it freely (no operator green-light, no on-air smoke needed for a layout fix). VERIFY via WebKitGTK/grim visual smoke, never Chromium/:1420 (CSS render bugs are exactly what static gates + Chromium miss — see chromium_not_webkitgtk_proxy / grim_realapp_validation). Likely tied to recent favorites/radio-panel work (cf. tuxlink-fgc1 Favorites/Recents selector styling; the FavoritesTabs-in-radio-panels mount). START: reproduce in the ARDOP panel, compare the FavoritesTabs/ArdopRadioPanel CSS + mount structure, find what makes the favorites surface overlay instead of sit inline (inline UI is the project rule — feedback_inline_ui_no_window_clutter).
notes: Root cause: flexbox shrink-overflow, NOT a z-index/position overlay. ARDOP mounts FavoritesTabs as a bare flex child of .radio-panel-body; .favorites-tabs has min-height:0 + no overflow clip, so a long favorites list let flex shrink the surface below content height and the rows painted OVER the Radio/Start sections (body never scrolled). Packet/Telnet immune (wrapped in .radio-panel-sec, min-height:auto). Fix: flex-shrink:0 on .favorites-tabs. Reproduced+verified in real WebKitGTK/grim. PR #545.
close_reason: Closed

title: Find a Gateway (CatalogBuilderPanel) cannot be dismissed — no backdrop click, no Escape, × unreachable after results render
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): 'The Find a Gateway window has no way to close it or click off of it after it's opened and any of the functions are used.' Operator rates this BEYOND a P0 — fatal UX = a functional crash. Second time a fresh feature has shipped a no-dismiss modal.

ROOT CAUSE (verified on origin/main, src/catalog/CatalogBuilderPanel.tsx):
- The overlay root <div className='catalog-builder-overlay' role='dialog'> has NO onClick={onClose} backdrop handler. So clicking off the panel does nothing ('no way to click off of it').
- There is NO Escape-key handler anywhere in the component.
- The only dismiss path is the × button (.catalog-builder__close, onClick={onClose}). Operator reports that after fetching stations / using any function, the × is unreachable — likely the panel/overlay grows past the viewport with no sticky header / no scroll containment, pushing the × off-screen (needs CSS confirmation: overlay+panel max-height, sticky header, z-index/pointer-events).

ASYMMETRY TO COPY: the SIBLING panel src/catalog/CatalogRequestPanel.tsx already does this correctly — overlay div has onClick={onClose} with an inner stopPropagation wrapper, plus a visible close button. CatalogBuilderPanel was built without that pattern.

FIX DIRECTION: (1) add onClick={onClose} to the overlay + onClick={e=>e.stopPropagation()} on the inner .catalog-builder (backdrop dismiss); (2) add an Escape-key handler (useEffect keydown → onClose); (3) make the close affordance always reachable — sticky header within a scroll-contained panel (panel max-height + overflow:auto on the body, header stays pinned). Browser-smoke the post-results state specifically (feedback_browser_smoke_before_ship).

PREVENTION: this is the 2nd no-dismiss-modal in a fresh feature — worth a testing-pitfalls entry (every inline-overlay must have backdrop-dismiss + Escape + an always-reachable close, asserted in a test). Source: PR #465 (Catalog Builder / Find a Gateway, tuxlink-a2gd).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-29zx-catalog-dismiss. Branch: bd-tuxlink-29zx/catalog-dismiss. Created 2026-06-09 02:10:28 UTC by delta-bog-cypress.
close_reason: Closed

title: Contacts grid collapses — ContactsPanel jammed into MessageList column, radio dock slides into detail area
description:
Operator converged-build smoke walk (2026-06-08): the Contacts feature is completely broken and unusable. The render grid 'collapses to the left' and 'drags the radio modem all the way into where the contacts list should be.'

ROOT CAUSE (verified on origin/main):
- src/shell/AppShell.css: .panes uses grid-template-columns: 200px 380px 1fr (and .panes--with-dock: 200px 380px 1fr 400px). The grid tracks are sized for the 3/4-child message layout (FolderSidebar | MessageList | reading-pane | [RadioPanel dock]).
- src/shell/AppShell.tsx (~line 929): when selectedFolder === 'contacts', a single <ContactsPanel/> is rendered as grid CHILD 2. A single grid child only occupies ONE track (the 380px MessageList column) unless told to span. So ContactsPanel is crushed into 380px on the left and the 1fr (+400px dock) tracks are wrong.
- With the radio dock on (panes--with-dock), RadioPanel becomes child 3 and lands in the 1fr track (the contacts-detail area) instead of the 400px dock track — i.e. the radio modem 'slides into where the contacts list should be.' Exactly the operator symptom.
- The mount comment claims the early-return 'wraps both the MessageList column AND the reading pane', but that is not true in CSS-grid terms for a single child.

FIX DIRECTION (for the implementing session): give the contacts state its own grid template — e.g. a .panes--contacts modifier collapsing to 200px 1fr (and accounting for the dock track), OR have the ContactsPanel wrapper span grid-column: 2 / -1 so it occupies the full MessageList+reading-pane(+dock) union. Verify with a real browser smoke per feedback_browser_smoke_before_ship (grid layout is exactly the class of bug static review misses).

Source: converged-build operator smoke walk; regression introduced by PR #472 (Contacts+Favorites, tuxlink-raez).

title: [P0] Fix Logging window usability, styling, and clear-history size behavior
description:
Smoke walk of the working converged build surfaced a P0 cluster in the new Logging window. This was not caused by the current Codex logging-boundary PR; it is a first-time smoke finding against the working converged build. Symptoms: (1) once opened, the Logging window does not render controls to close it, making it functionally sticky; (2) the window styling does not match the rest of Tuxlink despite operator concern during spec/design; (3) log history/export minimum size appears to be nearly 1 MB, which is large for alpha issue upload; (4) clicking Clear History increases the minimum size by roughly 2 KB per click and cannot reset to 0 without manually deleting log files from the filesystem. Use repo bug-hunter/systematic-debugging discipline: reproduce and trace root cause before proposing fixes; use TDD before production changes. After fixing, smoke walk a simulated CMS comms failure and evaluate the filed issue/logging evidence for alpha needs.

## Required Skills
investigate,systematic-debugging,test-driven-development
acceptance_criteria: Logging window has visible/usable close controls and remains accessible after open; styling matches the established Tuxlink shell/window conventions; Clear History is idempotent and resets operator-visible history/log archive size appropriately without filesystem manual deletion; repeated Clear History clicks do not grow persisted logs; simulated CMS comms failure can file/export an issue with useful diagnostic evidence and no excessive baseline log bloat; targeted tests and smoke evidence are recorded.
notes: Implemented in commit 9da09d6 by yew-sorrel-condor. Root causes verified: logging_window.rs is borderless but LoggingView lacked custom Tuxlink chrome/resize handles; logging_clear_history preserved the active JSONL and emitted a warning after clear, so disk usage could not reset and grew on repeated clicks. Fix: LoggingView now uses shared Tuxlink chrome/titlebar/resize handles/theme tokens; Clear History flushes pending writes, truncates the active JSONL, deletes closed JSONLs, preserves unrelated files, and returns retryable error if the active log path mutex is busy. Gates: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml logging::commands::tests -- --nocapture (17 passed); pnpm vitest run src/help/LoggingView.test.tsx src/help/LoggingExportSection.test.tsx (16 passed); pnpm vitest run src/help/ReportIssueModal.test.tsx src/shell/chrome/dispatchMenuAction.test.ts (48 passed; existing React act warning only); pnpm typecheck (passed after offline frozen pnpm install in worktree); pnpm build (passed, existing Vite dynamic-import/chunk-size warnings). Safe CMS smoke assessment: docs/live-cms-testing-policy.md bars agent-run live CMS/RF; verified Report Issue/dispatch tests and code path only. Operator manual smoke should use local/invalid CMS endpoint or explicit consent for any live CMS run.
close_reason: Implemented by yew-sorrel-condor in commit 9da09d6 and merged via PR #436 (merge commit 7f63878). All GitHub required checks passed: verify amd64/arm64 and release build amd64/arm64. Agent-side safe smoke covered Report Issue/dispatch tests and code-path review; remaining converged-build GUI/operator smoke is tracked separately as tuxlink-t8c0 because live CMS/RF and GUI smoke require operator control/consent.

title: [P0 WLE parity] Prompt operator to select inbound messages to download (bandwidth/time budget)
description:
Smoke test 2026-06-05 against cms-z: tuxlink currently downloads ALL available inbound messages on connect without prompting. WLE prompts the operator with a list of pending messages and lets them select which to pull — critical for emcomm scenarios with limited bandwidth or time budgets where downloading everything would exhaust the link.

Critical WLE parity feature. P0 because emcomm users on bandwidth-constrained links (HF, ARDOP, packet) cannot trust tuxlink as a replacement until this exists.

Discovered during smoke test of latest converged build against cms-z.winlink.org (Cameron, 2026-06-05).
notes: SHIPPED to main: feature via PR #480 (merged by operator 2026-06-08T23:42Z); connect-path staleness bug found in the Task-9 cms-z smoke (no prompt — auto-download to Inbox) FIXED via PR #482 / tuxlink-u9z8 (merged 2026-06-09T02:02Z, commit 9dd1cc07). REMAINING: operator Task-9 cms-z dev-smoke on the warm main build — Hold/Delete semantics (BLOCKER), 45s socket-idle, abort, preference-OFF, multi-batch. Close after smoke passes. The fix makes the toggle take effect on the very next connect regardless of toggle timing, so the startup-window staleness that caused the no-prompt symptom will not recur.

title: [P0/perf] Infinite theme-broadcast loop pegs CPU when help window is open
description: Operator (2026-06-03): tuxlink WebKitWebProcess and Rust backend both pegged (>40% each) at idle when help window is open. Root cause: theme broadcast cycle. (1) Help window's useHelpTheme.ts:28 listens for 'color_scheme_changed'. (2) On event, it calls applyColorScheme(payload). (3) applyColorScheme in src/shell/colorScheme.ts:231-238 invokes 'theme_broadcast_scheme' — fire-and-forget. (4) Rust theme_broadcast_scheme in src-tauri/src/theme_state.rs:36 calls app.emit('color_scheme_changed', scheme) which broadcasts to ALL windows INCLUDING THE SENDER (Tauri default). (5) Help window's listener fires AGAIN with the same payload → goto 2. Infinite IPC + DOM-mutation loop. Each iteration: one Tauri command + DOM write + JS event callback. Pegs both processes. Fix: add a  option to applyColorScheme (default true to preserve menu-flip + startup broadcast behavior); useHelpTheme's two callsites pass {broadcast: false} since they're responding to a broadcast and must not echo.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-och6-theme-broadcast-loop. Branch: bd-tuxlink-och6/theme-broadcast-loop. Created 2026-06-03 23:08:04 UTC.
close_reason: PR #338 merged. Broke the cross-window theme-broadcast loop by giving applyColorScheme a {broadcast?: boolean} opt and having useHelpTheme's two callsites pass false.

title: [bug] help window crashes with 'No QueryClient set' — App.tsx help branch missing QueryClientProvider
description: Operator smoke 2026-06-03 of merged PR #312 (tuxlink-0gsy): help window opens but renders nothing; WebKit console shows 'Error: No QueryClient set, use QueryClientProvider to set one' followed by 'An error occurred in the <HelpView> component'. Root cause: App.tsx wraps QueryClientProvider only around <AppShell />; the isHelpWindow + isComposeWindow branches mount their components without it. HelpView's useHelpSearch hook calls useQuery (from @tanstack/react-query), which throws without a provider. Tests passed because HelpView.test.tsx + useHelpSearch.test.ts wrap their own QueryClient in the test render — classic dependency-injection-by-test that left the production mount path unsupported. Fix: lift QueryClientProvider up so all branches get it. Add a regression test that mounts App with window.location.pathname='/help' and asserts no error is thrown.

title: App white-screens on launch — dependabot PR #252 bumped react without react-dom
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED CRITICAL REGRESSION (2026-06-02).

Symptom: launching tuxlink (tauri dev OR production) shows a white screen with no content. React refuses to mount.

Root cause: PR #252 (dependabot) bumped 'react' from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 + '@types/react' from 19.2.15 to 19.2.16, but did NOT bump 'react-dom' (still at 19.2.6). React 19 enforces strict version parity between 'react' and 'react-dom' — peer mismatch throws an uncaught runtime exception at mount time:

  Error: Incompatible React versions: The 'react' and 'react-dom' packages must have the exact same version. Instead got:
    - react:      19.2.7
    - react-dom:  19.2.6
  Learn more: https://react.dev/warnings/version-mismatch

Captured via headless chromium + CDP against http://localhost:1420 of the converged build.

Fix:

1. Bump react-dom from 19.2.6 to 19.2.7 in package.json
2. Bump @types/react-dom to match react-dom (current spec says ^18.3.0 — this is also stale; should be ^19.2.x)
3. Run 'pnpm install' to regenerate pnpm-lock.yaml
4. Verify: 'pnpm exec tsc --noEmit' clean + 'pnpm vitest run' clean
5. Verify: headless chromium loads localhost:1420 and #root has content after mount

Follow-up hardening (defer to P2 bd):
- Configure dependabot's 'groups' so react + react-dom + @types/react + @types/react-dom always bump together. This SHOULD prevent the same class of bug from recurring. Edit .github/dependabot.yml to add a 'react-monorepo' group:
    groups:
      react:
        patterns:
          - 'react'
          - 'react-dom'
          - '@types/react'
          - '@types/react-dom'

Discovered: 2026-06-02 operator-smoked the converged build after merge of #252 / release 0.17.0.

title: Position UI/on-air locator conflation — ribbon shows config_grid not live GPS fix under LocalUiOnly
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED REGRESSION (post tuxlink-c79g merge).

Symptom: with config { position_source: Gps, gps_state: LocalUiOnly, identity.grid: 'DM33', position_precision: SixCharGrid } and a fresh GPS fix (verified: gpsd producing TPV mode=3 at ~1Hz, tuxlink connected, arbiter.has_fresh_fix()=true), the ribbon displays 'DM33' instead of the live precision-reduced fix grid 'DM33ww'.

Root cause: effective_broadcast_locator in src-tauri/src/position/mod.rs:18-49 conflates two distinct concerns:
  (a) ribbon DISPLAY = what to show the operator locally
  (b) on-air LOCATOR = what gets transmitted (privacy-gated by gps_state)

The Codex P1-B finding from the prior position-subsystem-restoration 5-round adrev (PR #215 / tuxlink-c79g) intentionally collapsed (a)+(b) into one helper, with rustdoc 'the ribbon always shows exactly what is/would be transmitted.' That collapse is the design error: under LocalUiOnly + source=Gps + fresh fix, the operator's literal intent ('see GPS locally, don't broadcast') is broken because the local display falls back to config_grid.

Fix (corrected Path A, validated by Codex consultation 2026-06-01):

Backend:
  - position/mod.rs: add effective_ui_locator(config, arbiter):
      source=Gps + fresh fix + gps_state in {LocalUiOnly, BroadcastAtPrecision} → arbiter.broadcast_grid() (live fix at precision)
      source=Gps + (no fresh fix OR gps_state=Off) → config_grid fallback
      source=Manual → config_grid
  - Keep effective_broadcast_locator unchanged (on-air gating preserved).
  - Update rustdoc on broadcast helper: it is single source of truth for on-air ONLY.
  - ui_commands.rs PositionStatusDto: add ui_grid: String. Populate via effective_ui_locator.

Frontend:
  - useStatus.ts: add ui_grid to PositionStatusDto type. liveGrid reads ui_grid (not broadcast_grid).
  - GridEdit.tsx: clean stale pjih-era comments.

Spec amendment (SAME PR, first commit per propagation contract):
  - docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-position-subsystem-restoration-design.md
  - Amend vocabulary, §2.5, §3.1, §3.2, §3.4, §6.1, §6.4
  - Replace 'ribbon equals on-air locator' with 'ribbon equals local display locator; on-air locator remains separately privacy-gated'
  - Codex P1-B finding gets a corrective note (5-round adrev correctly identified ambiguity but proposed the wrong fix shape)

Tests (TDD):
  - Backend matrix for effective_ui_locator: 5 spec states × {Off, LocalUiOnly, BroadcastAtPrecision}
  - Regression: source=Gps + LocalUiOnly + fresh fix + SixCharGrid → ui_grid='DM33ww' AND broadcast_grid='DM33' (intentionally differ)
  - DTO serialization test: both ui_grid and broadcast_grid present + serialize snake_case
  - Hook test: ribbon liveGrid reads ui_grid
  - Existing Codex P1-B tests: rename/reframe (don't blind-delete coverage)

Adjacent bug Codex flagged (decide in PR or defer):
  - gps_ready is arbiter.has_fresh_fix() regardless of gps_state. If operator switches state to Off mid-session, gpsd client task isn't killed → fixes keep arriving → gps_ready stays true. Should gate gps_ready false under Off.

Codex full consultation transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-01-position-localuionly-display-conflation-codex.md (4468 lines, gitignored, kept on disk for reference).

Base off current origin/main (NOT bd-tuxlink-c79g worktree which is now stale; PRs #216-#219 landed after #215).

title: REGRESSION fix(position): chip stuck GPS-green + accepts manual input but doesn't switch — pjih over-removed operator agency
description:
Operator-reported 2026-06-01 post-PR #189 merge: 'GPS is now fully broken. It doesn't switch to manual entry with [the prior] gps available [affordance], it just says GPS green at all times and accepts/displays whatever input. That's major regression.'

ROOT CAUSE (suspected — needs investigation)
PR #189 (tuxlink-pjih) decoupled set_manual from source-pinning and removed the explicit Use-GPS button from GridEdit, on the assumption that effective_source derived live from arbiter state would be sufficient signal. That over-removed operator agency:
  - The chip now reads positionStatus.active_source (derived: Gps when fresh fix, Manual otherwise).
  - With gpsd publishing fixes, effective_source=Gps permanently → chip is permanently GPS-green.
  - The operator can no longer FORCE Manual mode for an explicit on-air override.
  - Typing in the grid updates config.identity.grid + arbiter.manual_grid; under gps_state != BroadcastAtPrecision the broadcast falls back to config.identity.grid, so the display 'accepts whatever input' even though chip says GPS.

REQUIRED FIX (shape — operator decides)
Restore explicit operator agency over source selection. Options:
  (a) Restore the use-gps button + reintroduce stored source as the chip's primary signal (closest to pre-pjih, but pjih's stuck-in-Manual bug returns).
  (b) Add an explicit Manual ↔ GPS toggle separate from grid-edit; chip shows operator's choice; arbiter respects it.
  (c) Hybrid: chip shows effective source by default; affordance to override (force Manual / force GPS) lives in the source chip itself or in Settings.

SEVERITY P0 — operator-visible major regression caused by my PR #189.

title: RADIO-1: packet connect is unsafe on-air — unbounded airtime, abort can't halt TX, pre-connect DISC
description: INCIDENT 2026-05-22: a packet dial over the new RFCOMM socket ran ~110s of repeated SABM keying with no reachable abort; operator had to power OFF the radio to stop it. This is a RADIO-1 violation. Do NOT dial packet (any transport) until ALL of the below land. Found via Codex adrev (dev/adversarial/2026-05-22-ax25-socket-rx-runaway-codex.md, gitignored). FIX BUNDLE: (1) Bounded airtime — split the connect SABM-retry policy from data N2; one SABM + at most one retry + a HARD total-elapsed cap (~20-30s) regardless of n2_retries. (2) Abort before every transmit — AbortableByteLink::write must check the flag (currently only read() does, link.rs:80); connect() must check abort before push_kiss_params and before EACH SABM (currently TXes at top of each attempt); never key after Cancel. (3) No pre-connect DISC — connect() builds Ax25Stream{closed:false} before the link is up, so a failed dial (DM/timeout/abort) drops the stream -> Drop -> disconnect() -> sends DISC + waits T1 (datalink.rs:714). Only arm teardown after a UA is received. (4) REVERT the T1 floor (config.rs into_params MIN_RF_T1_MS, added this session) — it tripled worst-case airtime (3s->10s per retransmit) and was the wrong lever. (5) Build/test on task-amd-main-ui (has the abort + resize UI); main lacks them. Blocks tuxlink-uhc and tuxlink-nx2.
close_reason: Landed on bd-tuxlink-oxi/consolidate (PR pending): ka7+p5u via live-config refresh on config_set_*; efo via lenient packet.link deserialize + TUXLINK_CONFIG_DIR; 2y4 RADIO-1 bundle (bounded airtime + abort-before-write + no pre-connect DISC + T1-floor revert + KISS nibble), Codex-cleared (4 invariants), TOCTOU refinement tracked in tuxlink-0ja. 338 lib + 453 vitest green.

title: Acceptance Test 1 (account): run full account create/read/update/delete flow from Tuxlink — capture evidence
description: Winlink client-onboarding doc (v20260600) 'Upon Completion' item 1: full account flow (create, read, update, delete) exercised from the client application. Code surface exists on main (src-tauri/src/winlink/cms_account.rs: account_create / account_exists / account_validate_password / change_password / set_recovery_email / send_recovery / account_remove; account-create ungated via PR #860) but is non-functional live until Tuxlink holds its own access key (tuxlink-lu7t — WLE's shared key is rejected InvalidAccessKey). During the run, verify each CRUD op is user-reachable in the UI (component!=shipped). Open questions for WDT (ask in packet email): does the account API have a cms-z/test-side store, and which callsign should create/delete target (a real account op is destructive on a live callsign)? Capture evidence: screen recording or screenshots + API request/response log (key redacted).

title: [operator-action] Acceptance Test 3 (over the air): send 'VARA Send' to TEST through the temporary cms-z RMS, reconnect, verify response — capture evidence
description: Winlink client-onboarding doc (v20260600) 'Upon Completion' item 3: using a radio mode, connect through the temporary RMS to cms-z. Compose with MODE in the subject — To: TEST, Subject: 'VARA Send' (VARA is Tuxlink's proven on-air mode; use 'ARDOP Send' etc. if mode differs), short plain-text body, Priority: Normal. Reconnect and verify the response is received back correctly. Prereqs: temporary RMS live (tuxlink-dzp9n) AND its callsign whitelisted by WDT. RADIO-1: operator initiates every transmission; clear-channel check before TX. Client rig: Tuxlink+VARA+G90/Digirig (the exact stack that completed the 2026-07-10 on-air B2F handshake). Capture evidence: both-end logs, sent message, TEST response, mode/bandwidth used.

title: Acceptance Test 2 (telnet): send 'Telnet Send' to TEST via cms-z from Tuxlink, reconnect, verify response + body integrity — capture evidence
description: Winlink client-onboarding doc (v20260600) 'Upon Completion' item 2: compose from Tuxlink over direct network connection to cms-z — To: TEST, Subject: 'Telnet Send', short plain-text body (couple of sentences to verify body integrity), Priority: Normal. Reconnect and verify the TEST autoresponder reply is received back correctly. Telnet-to-cms-z is authorized standing testing (no RF). Capture evidence for the packet: session log (redacted), the sent message, the received response, timestamps. Note: Tuxlink compose has no explicit Priority field — a plain message is Normal precedence; confirm with WDT that this satisfies 'Priority: Normal' or add the field if they require it.

title: [operator-action] Stand up temporary cms-z RMS for the OTA acceptance test — Trimode/WINE on R2 primary, LinBPQ+ardopcf fallback; report RMS callsign to WDT for whitelisting
description: Winlink client-onboarding doc (v20260600) Test 3 requires an over-the-air connect through an RMS with cms-z access, and 'there are not usually existing RMS with access to cms-z' — a temporary RMS must be stood up and its callsign reported to the Winlink Development Team for whitelisting BEFORE the test. Candidate A (primary): RMS Trimode + VARA HF under WINE on R2 — the Winelink project (github.com/WheezyE/Winelink) ships Trimode-under-wine installs, R2 already has a working x86_64 wine prefix with VARA (~/.wine-wle, COM1=FT-710); TX audio via DRA-100 to the FT-710 rear DATA jack (internal codec is not a TX pathway; DATA MOD SOURCE=REAR). Candidate B (fallback, Linux-native): LinBPQ as RMS gateway — supported by the Winlink dev team as a CMS relay, drives ardopcf natively (no wine); cantab.net LinBPQ_RMSGateway doc. Client side of the test is already proven: Tuxlink+VARA+G90/Digirig completed a B2F handshake over RF 2026-07-10 (ie7dy). Callsign: must be the operator's licensed call (SSID variant fine, e.g. N7CPZ-5) — NOT arbitrary; WDT whitelists it on cms-z. Open question for WDT: how the temp RMS is pointed at cms-z (Trimode/LinBPQ CMS-host override) — ask in the packet email. Part 97/RADIO-1: operator initiates all TX; both ends are the operator's stations.

title: [fable][operator-action] Production CMS rejects tuxlink SID over RF gateway relay — request WDT client-type whitelisting
description: 2026-07-10 02:26Z, KD6OAT RF session (VARA 500, second connect of the night, PR #1063 fix proven on-air): B2F handshake completed over RF and the gateway relayed to the production CMS (136.36.234.56), which replied 'Unknown client types are not allowed on production servers -- use cms-z.winlink.org - Disconnecting'. Tuxlink presents SID [tuxlink-<version>-B2FHM$] (handshake.rs build_handshake). The telnet path avoids this by targeting cms-z directly (see cms_rejects_unknown_clients memory), but over RF the GATEWAY chooses the CMS — always production — so this now hard-blocks every real RF Winlink session regardless of code correctness. Resolution is administrative: the operator requests client-type registration/whitelisting from the Winlink Development Team for the 'tuxlink' client name (same path Pat took). Interim: P2P sessions don't touch the CMS and are unaffected; RF-gateway CONNECT+handshake validation can continue against cms rejection (the rejection itself proves the full stack). Severity: blocks the production end-to-end goal, but external dependency — P1.
notes: STATUS 2026-07-10: acceptance packet merged to main (PR #1067). Cover email SENT 2026-07-10 by the operator to Rob (Winlink CMS point of contact) — requests in flight: (1) whitelist temp-RMS callsign N7CPZ-1 on cms-z + how to point the RMS at cms-z [unblocks dzp9n→iiqoy], (2) Tuxlink application access key [unblocks lu7t→0zngx], (3) account-test target-callsign guidance, (4) Priority:Normal confirmation. gbb05 (SSID dial fix) resolved via PR #1068 same day. Awaiting Rob's reply; telnet test (o7fct) is runnable independently at any time.

title: [fable] VARA B2F dies at first RF silence: 2s data-socket read timeout + handshake maps timeout ticks to ConnectionClosed — killed the first-ever on-air gateway answer
description: 2026-07-10 01:01:57Z: KD6OAT (VARA 500, 796 km) ANSWERED Tuxlink's BW500 dial on retry 3/30 (avg S/N -11.8 dB) — first on-air VARA connect in project history. 4 s later Tuxlink sent DISCONNECT and surfaced 'B2F exchange failed: transport failed: Handshake(ConnectionClosed)'. Mechanism: vara/transport.rs:99 sets the DATA socket read_timeout to the same 2 s as the cmd socket; winlink/handshake.rs:153 maps fill_buf Err(_) — including WouldBlock/TimedOut ticks — to HandshakeError::ConnectionClosed. At 500 Hz (41 bps observed) the gateway SID needs 10-20+ s, so the first 2 s tick aborts the handshake; the graceful dial_disconnect then reads as 'DISCONNECT command received' in VARAHF.log (01:02:01, 4 s after link-up), matching the observed timeline. Scope: not handshake-only — every B2F read over the VARA data socket inherits the 2 s inter-byte budget via the shared fd, so proposals/blocks at RF speeds would false-fail the same way after any 2 s silence. Fix: per-socket budgets — keep cmd at 2 s (event cadence), give data an RF-scale timeout (~120 s, ARQTimeout regime; link death is still bounded by the cmd-socket DISCONNECTED path + existing wedge tracking in the ARQTimeout issue). ARDOP's on-air B2F likely shares this class of bug (loopback never exposed it — localhost SID arrives in ms); audit ardop transport data timeout separately. Evidence: VARAHF.log + app session log captured in the 2026-07-09 jay-marsh-yew handoff addendum.
close_reason: Closed

title: [fable] Dial path loses gateway SSID — ConReq addressed to base callsign no station owns (KB2PCN-5→KB2PCN, NS7K-10→NS7K)
description: Found by jay-marsh-yew 2026-07-09 cross-referencing the on-air campaign log against the CMS gateway/status.json API. Two of the eleven dialed gateways operate under SSID'd callsigns (KB2PCN-5 at 14108.0, NS7K-10 at 14104.9) but the campaign dialed the base callsign. VARA only answers a ConReq whose destination matches a configured MYCALL, so these dials were unanswerable regardless of RF, bandwidth, or hours. Root cause to determine: whether the text-listing ingest strips SSIDs, the catalog stores base callsigns, or the dial path normalizes them away. Fix must carry the full SSID'd callsign from catalog through finder to the CONNECT command. Related: tuxlink-hmoz8 (channel source switch to CMS API — API's Callsign field carries the SSID; BaseCallsign is separate).
notes:
CONFIRMED code-correct against origin/main 2026-07-10 (slate-fox-tanager), per operator request to re-verify against shipping code. Root cause pinned (the description left it open): src-tauri/src/catalog/stations.rs parse_header_line — 'channel.split([".", "-"]).next()' conflates the '.WINLINK' suffix separator with the '-SSID' separator, so 'KB2PCN-5.WINLINK' parses to callsign 'KB2PCN'. Proven against the real on-disk cache (~/.local/share/com.tuxlink.app/station-listings-cache.json): raw channel tokens KB2PCN-5.WINLINK and KB2PCN-8.WINLINK present, parsed callsign KB2PCN. The text listing DOES carry SSIDs — no dependency on the hmoz8 CMS-API switch to fix this. The frontend is already SSID-ready and merely starved: stationModel.ts:76 sets channel.ssid when g.callsign carries one (hasSsid is currently never true), and channelGrouping.ts channelToDial dials 'channel.ssid ?? station.baseCallsign'. Fix is backend-only: split the channel token on '.' alone (keeping '-<ssid>'), keep the empty-token guard, note a channel form of bare 'CALL-SSID' also exists (stations.rs:225 comment) and survives a '.'-only split. KB2PCN having two SSIDs (-5, -8) exercises the per-channel ssid model — good test case. Blocks tuxlink-iiqoy (OTA acceptance test vs N7CPZ-1).
