title: [fable] Channel catalog lacks per-channel HOURS and BANDWIDTH (text-listing source) — switch to CMS channels API; likely root of all-day gateway silence
description:
Synthesis of the 2026-07-09 on-air campaign (kestrel-butte-granite): 23 A/B dial attempts, published vs center-1500, across 11 gateways in three FT8-verified over-0dB regions, with an off-air-self-decode-proven signal — VARA2 decoded 13/13 ConReqs and answered them — and ZERO gateway answers at 15 and 30 retries.

The remaining systematic explanations are data gaps in Tuxlink's channel source: the public WINLINK VARA CHANNEL LISTING text report has no per-channel operating-hours and no bandwidth columns. RMS Trimode decompiled Channels.cs:4545 documents per-frequency UTC start/stop hour windows — gateways scan a frequency ONLY during its window. WLE consumes Channels.dat from the CMS channels API which carries hours, bandwidth, and center frequency.

Fix: ingest the CMS channels API as Pat does; carry hours and bandwidth through catalog to finder to dial path; filter find_stations by currently-in-window; auto-match VARA bandwidth to the channel.

Subsumes/extends the earlier bandwidth-omission issue. Also recorded here: the definitive next validation is dialing KD7ZDO from WLE on the same rig (differential test). Retries: VARA.ini on R2 now set to 30 (WLE commands RETRIES 10 explicitly over the cmd socket — Tuxlink should own this setting too rather than inherit the ini).
notes: CONFIRMED via live CMS API query (jay-marsh-yew, 2026-07-09): api.winlink.org/gateway/status.json (POST, key=Pat's public AccessKey 1880278F11684B358F36845615BD039A, Mode=anyall, ServiceCodes=PUBLIC) returns per-channel OperatingHours + SupportedModes (mode 50=VARA2300 / 53=VARA500 / 54=VARA2750) + center Frequency + gateway lat/lon. Cross-referencing the 2026-07-09 campaign log: KD6OAT 14112.5 is VARA500-ONLY (all 4 HF channels mode 53) — 2300 ConReq undecodable; KD0SFY 20m 500-ch likewise; K7DAV 14107 / WB0ECX 14102 / KD0SFY 14105.5 are VARA2750. Also found SSID-stripping defect (separate issue): actual gateways are KB2PCN-5 and NS7K-10, campaign dialed KB2PCN / NS7K — ConReq addressed to nonexistent MYCALL, unanswerable by design. KD7ZDO all-2300 00-23 = clean differential target (14108.2 center / 14106.7 dial).

title: [fable] find_stations/catalog omits per-channel VARA bandwidth — cannot distinguish 500 Hz-only channels; 2300 Hz ConReqs sent at possibly-500Hz gateways
description: Operator flag 2026-07-09: 'Make sure you aren't calling 500 Hz-only channels sending >500Hz calling reqs.' The Winlink RMS channel list carries per-channel VARA bandwidth (500/2300/2750), but Tuxlink's find_stations gateway DTO exposes only mode/channel/callsign/grid/frequencies_khz/antenna/distance/bearing — no bandwidth. Agents and the Find-a-Station UI cannot select bandwidth-compatible channels, and vara BW (config_set_vara bandwidth_hz) is not auto-matched to the target channel. Fix: carry channel bandwidth through catalog ingest -> finder DTO -> dial path; auto-set VARA BW per channel (WLE negotiates this from Channels.dat); refuse/warn on BW-incompatible dial.
notes: Subsumed by tuxlink-hmoz8 (its description says so explicitly): the CMS channels API ingest carries per-channel bandwidth; closing as duplicate when hmoz8 lands. 2026-07-10 on-air confirmation: KD6OAT (500-only) answered BW500 after ignoring eleven 2300 calls.
close_reason: Closed

title: [fable] session_log_emit writes only to the in-app pane — operator-facing errors never reach the tracing/jsonl forensic logs
description: Found 2026-07-09 (kestrel-butte-granite VARA session) while diagnosing the UI dial: session_log_emit::emit() appends to the in-memory SessionLogState + emits a Tauri UI event, and nothing else. Every emit_vara_log/emit_ardop_log line — including ERRORS like 'VARA tune failed: rigctld spawn failed' — is invisible to ~/.local/state/tuxlink/logs/*.jsonl. Consequence: post-hoc forensics on an operator bug report cannot see what the operator saw; this session repeatedly inferred 'the command was never invoked' from jsonl silence when pane-only errors were possible. Fix: session_log_emit::emit should mirror every line into tracing at the matching level (target e.g. tuxlink::session_log) so the pane and the forensic log are the same stream. Cheap, single chokepoint (session_log_emit.rs:9-21).

title: [fable] Pre-dial CAT tune sets 'M PKTUSB 0' — hamlib passband 0 engages the rig's NARROW data filter, crushing RX bandwidth below VARA's 2.3 kHz
description: Operator-observed 2026-07-09 (kestrel-butte-granite VARA session): after Tuxlink's pre-dial tune, the FT-710's DSP narrowed receive to a very narrow bandwidth. Code: tux-rig protocol.rs cmd_set_mode emits 'M <mode> 0' (comment says 'passband 0 = rig default'), but hamlib's per-backend default width for packet/data modes on Yaesu is narrow — NOT the wide data filter VARA needs. A VARA HF 2300 Hz waveform behind a narrow RX filter cannot decode the remote's answer: on-air connects will fail even with perfect propagation and the failure looks like 'nobody answers' (exactly what daytime NVIS also looks like — a nasty masker). Fix options: pass passband -1 (no change — respect the operator's filter), or explicitly set a wide data passband (~3000 Hz) for VARA/ARDOP dials; either way restore prior width+mode on disconnect (Tuxlink currently also leaves VFO on the dialed frequency after the session). Verify with CAT SH0;/NA0; before/after tune on the FT-710. Affects both ARDOP and VARA paths (shared tune_rig_for_connect).
close_reason: Closed

title: [fable] VARA panel requires two clicks (Start, then Send/Receive) to dial — every other mode dials in one action; WLE operators read Start as the dial
description: Operator defect report 2026-07-09 (kestrel-butte-granite VARA session, operator-attended). ARDOP and the other modes initiate the connection in one operator action; VARA is the outlier: Start only opens the modem TCP transport, and the actual dial is a second button (Send / Receive) that appears in Start's position after the transport opens, at the bottom of the pane below the session log. Click-probe forensics (diag build) showed an experienced Winlink operator spent a full morning pressing Start ('fire the session'), waiting, then Stop — the dial was invoked exactly once all day. In WLE, Start on a session window DIALS. Fix: single-action dial — Send/Receive (or Connect) auto-opens the transport when closed (the ribbon connectFor already composes open+exchange; the panel should too), with Start/Stop demoted to an advanced transport control or removed. Verified the underlying dial path works end-to-end from the UI once Send/Receive is actually pressed (19:3x/19:4xZ: CONNECT N0DAJ + K7HTZ, 16 PTT cycles each, 50W into dummy load). Session evidence: dev handoff 2026-07-09, tuxlink-46hof (error swallow), tuxlink-p6iq auto-open behavior.

title: [fable] Central tauri invoke() wrapper: report IPC-layer rejections to structured log — they currently vanish
description: Companion to the panel-catch issue. Any tauri invoke() whose failure happens at the IPC boundary (arg shape mismatch, serde enum rename drift, command not in invoke_handler, unmanaged State) rejects the JS promise with no backend log line at all — tracing never sees it, VARAHF/session logs never see it, stderr is silent. 2026-07-09 evidence: operator's modem_vara_b2f_exchange invokes left zero trace in any log while vara_open_session (same click flow) logged normally. Proposal: a src/ipc.ts wrapper around @tauri-apps/api/core invoke that .catch()es, calls reportFrontendError('invoke:'+cmd, msg), and rethrows — one chokepoint instead of per-call discipline; sweep imports to use it. Serde-rename drift between UI string unions and Rust enums (known project pitfall, reference_serde_rename_all_enum_fields) then becomes observable in field logs instead of silent.
notes: [fable] Filed by Claude Fable session kestrel-butte-granite, 2026-07-09 VARA dummy-load bring-up (operator-attended). Grounded in live evidence from R2 that day — see dev/handoffs for the session handoff. Not speculative.

title: [fable] Radio-panel catch blocks swallow user-action failures to console.debug — wire reportFrontendError + visible surfacing (6 sites)
description: Motivating incident 2026-07-09 (kestrel-butte-granite): operator's VARA Send/Receive click failed at the Tauri IPC boundary with ZERO operator-visible or log-visible trace — diagnosing required an instrumented rebuild on R2. Root pattern: tuxlink-n95sr decided send/receive errors 'belong in the session log, not an inline element' and the catch does console.debug ONLY (VaraRadioPanel.tsx ~566-572; ArdopRadioPanel.tsx 820/848/871; +dispatch sites; 6 total). The assumption 'backend surfaced it in the session log' is FALSE for the entire class of IPC-layer failures (arg deserialization, unregistered command, state-not-managed) which reject the promise WITHOUT any backend emit. WebKitGTK production builds route console.debug to nowhere. The fix infrastructure already exists: frontendErrorLog.ts reportFrontendError (tuxlink-4b96) lands in the jsonl + Logging window — but caught rejections bypass it. Fix: every user-initiated-action catch must (a) reportFrontendError(source, msg, stack) and (b) surface operator-visibly (actionError strip or injected session-log line) when no backend session-log line is guaranteed. On an alpha whose purpose is tester signal, invisible failures are the top product defect: the best possible field bug report becomes 'nothing happens'.
notes: [fable] Filed by Claude Fable session kestrel-butte-granite, 2026-07-09 VARA dummy-load bring-up (operator-attended). Grounded in live evidence from R2 that day — see dev/handoffs for the session handoff. Not speculative.

title: [fable] FT-710 DATA MOD SOURCE=AUTO selects modulation source by PTT method — CAT keying gets NO rear-jack audio; provisioning must set REAR or key via DATA-jack PTT
description: Found 2026-07-09 dummy-load bring-up (kestrel-butte-granite). With the FT8 preset's DATA MOD SOURCE=AUTO, the FT-710 chooses TX audio source based on how PTT is asserted. Legacy ARDOP flows keyed via the DRA-100 hardware PTT into the DATA jack -> AUTO picked REAR audio. Tuxlink's VARA keyer (PR #1061) keys via CAT TX1; -> AUTO does NOT pick REAR -> radio keys with zero modulation (PO=0, ALC=0) while VARA audio is present at the jack. Operator set DATA MOD SOURCE=REAR: immediately PO=140 units (~50W) with ALC on tone, and a full VARA dial showed operator-witnessed 50W modulated ConReqs. Product implication: radio-provisioning docs/wizard (tuxlink-0nfe2) must require DATA MOD SOURCE=REAR for CAT-keyed data modes on Yaesu, or the keyer should optionally use the soundcard-adapter hardware PTT line to stay compatible with AUTO.
notes: [fable] Filed by Claude Fable session kestrel-butte-granite, 2026-07-09 VARA dummy-load bring-up (operator-attended). Grounded in live evidence from R2 that day — see dev/handoffs for the session handoff. Not speculative.

title: [fable] VARA.ini soundcard names must be written as Wine/MME sees them (31-char truncation) — full PulseAudio descriptions cause silent MISSING SOUNDCARD
description: Root cause of all dead-air VARA dials through 2026-07-09: VARA enumerates audio devices via Windows MME under Wine, which truncates device names to 31 chars. The DRA-100 sink description 'USB PnP Sound Device Analog Stereo' (34 chars) was hand-written into VARA.ini; VARA could never match it, logged 'Soundcard Output Device missing in action!' every 10s to VARAHF.log, replied DISCONNECTED+WRONG to any CONNECT ('Connect error: Soundcard device is not ready'). Fix applied on R2: 'USB PnP Sound Device Analog Ste' (31 chars) — confirmed working. Product implication for tuxlink-0nfe2 provisioning: when Tuxlink writes VARA.ini it must truncate device names to 31 chars (MME WAVEOUTCAPS szPname limit), and should surface VARAHF.log 'missing in action' as a provisioning health check. Session: kestrel-butte-granite.
notes: [fable] Filed by Claude Fable session kestrel-butte-granite, 2026-07-09 VARA dummy-load bring-up (operator-attended). Grounded in live evidence from R2 that day — see dev/handoffs for the session handoff. Not speculative.

title: [fable] VARA dial races modem registration after fresh VARA start — CONNECT 464ms after MYCALL rejected; wait for REGISTERED
description: Observed 2026-07-09 17:13:31Z on R2 (kestrel-butte-granite session, dummy-load bring-up). Sequence: transport connect -> MYCALL N7CPZ -> CONNECT N7CPZ N0DAJ 464ms later -> VARA replies DISCONNECTED instantly, then WRONG to our reactive DISCONNECT; REGISTERED N7CPZ arrives at 17:13:36 (~5s after dial). A CONNECT issued before VARA completes registration is silently fatal to the dial. Fix: after opening the cmd socket / sending MYCALL, wait for REGISTERED <call> (or a bounded readiness window) before permitting CONNECT; surface 'modem not ready yet' instead of generic 'modem may have rejected the dial'. Same failure shape as last session's dial-4 WRONG CALLSIGN. Log: ~/.local/state/tuxlink/logs/tuxlink.2026-07-09-17.jsonl seq 170-186.
notes: [fable] Filed by Claude Fable session kestrel-butte-granite, 2026-07-09 VARA dummy-load bring-up (operator-attended). Grounded in live evidence from R2 that day — see dev/handoffs for the session handoff. Not speculative.

title: Radio setup is entirely manual: Tuxlink must own VARA/audio/CAT provisioning (live-fire gap inventory)
description: First real VARA dial (2026-07-09, PR #1061) required FIVE hand-configurations outside the product, each invisible to a user: (1) dialout group membership; (2) CAT serial port verification — config pointed at the silent UART, fixed only by hand-probing FA; on both; (3) VARA.ini soundcard device names — TX audio was aimed at the radio internal codec, RX at the DRA; Tuxlink PROVISIONS VARA (vara_install/wine-vara-setup) but never writes its audio config, though the app already enumerates audio devices; (4) PipeWire device profile — the DRA enumerated input-only, so NO app could play TX audio into it; (5) ALSA Speaker mixer level. The dial/keying/exchange code works (proven on air); the setup half of the product does not exist. Fix shape: a radio-setup flow + connect-time preflight that enumerates audio+serial devices, writes VARA.ini during provisioning, probes CAT (FA; roundtrip on the configured port/baud), validates duplex audio, and surfaces levels. Extends tuxlink-82rrf from error-surfacing to provisioning. This is what makes VARA work as-shipped instead of as-hand-wired.

title: VARA never keys the radio: InboundCommand::Ptt is parsed and discarded — wire VARA PTT events to a host-side keyer
description: VERIFIED root cause (origin/main + R2 0.87.0 logs): VARA is a soundcard modem that cannot key a rig; it emits PTT ON/OFF on the cmd socket and the HOST must key. On main, InboundCommand::Ptt is produced by the parser (vara/command.rs:205) and consumed NOWHERE — wait_for_connected explicitly absorbs it, winlink_backend.rs has zero Ptt refs, repo-wide grep finds no consumer. Radio stays in RX; VARA modulates into an unkeyed transmitter; zero RF; no CONNECTED within deadline; fails every time by construction. The ptt_method/cat_key_cmd config surface is consumed ONLY by the ARDOP spawn path (ardopcf keys itself). tux-rig HAS set_ptt (client.rs:80, used by rig_tune) but nothing connects VARA Ptt events to it. Fix: fail-closed host keyer resolved from rig config, driven for the ENTIRE session (dial + B2F exchange — cmd socket must stay pumped, Codex finding 1), unkey on every exit. Prior issue text (build missing Phase 3) was a false premise from reading a 2,985-commit-stale branch.
notes: Fix implemented + PR open: host-side PTT keyer (fail-closed resolve, CatCommand/SerialRts/Vox), PTT service in dial + wind-down, scoped PTT pump through the B2F exchange window, UnkeyGuard on every exit. Commit 3f3f0572 on bd-tuxlink-yrrjq/vara-ptt-keying. CI = compile gate; operator on-air wire-walk closes.

title: Serial/PTT preflight: surface dialout + can't-open-port as actionable error (not opaque EAGAIN)
description: ROOT CAUSE of 2026-07-09 'no agent can drive Tuxlink' investigation. On R2 the app user (administrator) is NOT in the dialout group; /dev/ttyUSB0 (PTT) + /dev/ttyUSB1 (CAT) are crw-rw---- root dialout with no udev ACL, so open() returns EACCES(13). rigctld (tux-rig ManagedRig::spawn) can't open the serial, dies, and ManagedRig's timed read to the dead daemon surfaces as EAGAIN -> 'rig I/O error: Resource temporarily unavailable (os error 11)'. This opaque error reached the Elmer agent AND the operator, making a fully-wired station look broken. Tuxlink ALREADY detects this: the serial env-probe computes in_dialout_group:false at first_paint but never surfaces it. FIX: a connect-time serial/PTT preflight that checks (a) dialout membership and (b) can the configured CAT+PTT ports actually be opened, and surfaces a specific actionable message to BOTH operator UI and the agent tool result, e.g. 'not in dialout group — run: sudo usermod -aG dialout $USER then log out/in'. This blocks CAT tune, CAT-command PTT keying, and therefore VARA transmit. End-to-end gap: diagnostics exist, not wired to actionable UX.

title: Elmer streaming card collapses to a gray line when transcript overflows (flex-shrink)
description: Regression from the streaming-surface redesign (PR #1051, shipped 0.86.0). .elmer-stream-card defaults to flex-shrink:1 and its display:flex <button> child has ~0 min-content height, so when .elmer-messages (flex column) overflows with a long agentic transcript (prompt + tool-call chips) the card is crushed to 0 height, leaving only its border (a gray line) where the in-flight indicator + token counter should be. Root-caused + fix (.elmer-stream-card{flex:0 0 auto}) verified in the WebKitGTK render harness (before=collapsed, after=full). jsdom can't catch it (no layout) and it only triggers on overflow. Also fixing two adjacent 0.87 regressions: window:allow-set-title capability missing (1695 unhandled rejections from AppShell:1041) + .elmer-stream-tokens undefined --accent-teal. Found on R2 (0.87.0). Agent: basin-juniper-fjord.
notes: Fixed in PR #1059 (flex:0 0 auto). Verified before/after in WebKitGTK harness. Pending operator verify on R2 build.
close_reason: Closed

title: Agent egress denial kills the turn + clobbers agent output; surface arm/taint as data + graceful narration
description:
Live 0.85.0 VARA HF agent-mode test (Elmer + Opus-4.8, operator N7CPZ, armed 1h supervised). At the finish line, an egress tool call (rig_tune) was denied because send authority had expired (~1195s prior). Instead of the agent perceiving this and reporting it, the runner returned RunOutcome::ToolDenied and surfaced a raw JSON-RPC -32600 to the operator, OVERWRITING the agent's in-progress output. The agent could neither see the arm/taint state up front nor narrate the denial.

ROOT CAUSE (verified in code): egress gate Denied -> router.rs:46 maps EgressPortError::Denied to ErrorData::invalid_request (-32600) -> executor classifies to ToolOutcome::Denied -> runner.rs:163-165 returns RunOutcome::ToolDenied (terminal). mcp_client.rs:22 documents this as deliberate: 'terminal, relayed verbatim, NEVER retried' = injection defense (2ouqf red-team: local 20b is fully injectable; the never-retry-egress gate is load-bearing and WORKED).

FIX (operator-approved, must preserve the injection invariant — see 2ouqf):
1. Egress LOCK stays absolute — a denied send is never retried, never succeeds while unarmed/tainted.
2. Distinguish denial REASON (expired/not-armed vs tainted). Both get graceful narration: feed the denial back to the model as a tool result so it composes a helpful message ('authority lapsed, re-arm and I'll resume from station 3') and the turn ends with the AGENT's message, not a raw -32600. Taint denials stay egress-locked but are no longer turn-killing (operator decision 2026-07-08: graceful for BOTH paths).
3. Add an agent-readable arm/taint perception surface: {armed, seconds_remaining, tainted, taint_reason} so the agent checks BEFORE burning a long station sequence, not at the final send.
4. Fix the semantic miscoding: policy denial is not -32600 InvalidRequest (request was well-formed); use an appropriate non-fatal classification.

SECURITY: touches the 2ouqf injection-defense boundary. Requires its own brainstorm + full adrev before code. Does NOT relax 'tainted+unarmed cannot transmit'. LEADS the agent-operability cluster (most acute; independent of VARA reachability/SoT). Evidence: live Elmer transcript 2026-07-08.
notes:
DESIGN + PLAN COMPLETE (hawk-kingfisher-spruce, 2026-07-08). Two 5-round adrevs (Codex+4 Claude each) converged, no surviving P0.
- Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-08-agent-operability-cluster-design.md Contract 0.
- Plan (subagent-proof, 7 tasks): docs/plans/2026-07-08-pf6re-graceful-denial-plan.md.
- Adrev raw (gitignored): dev/adversarial/2026-07-08-pf6re-design-codex.md.

VERIFIED FACT that shaped the P0 fix: session.rearm() (elmer/commands.rs:223 -> quarantine_and_rearm) DISCARDS the conversation. So the taint denial remedy string must NOT say 'resume' (impossible after taint re-arm); the expiry/not-armed remedy (plain egress_arm) CAN say resume (context preserved). Two cause-split strings in the plan (Task 3).

IMPLEMENTATION NUANCES found while starting Task 1 (fold into build):
1. TaintReason needs a 4th variant SessionLog (router.rs:371 session_log_snapshot taints too) — not just MessageRead/MailboxList/SearchResults. Map: 204=MailboxList, 223=MessageRead, 261=SearchResults, 371=SessionLog.
2. tuxlink-security has ZERO dependencies by design (minimal attack surface). DO NOT add serde to it. Define TaintReason as a plain enum (Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq + as_str()); serialize the token in mcp-core's ServerInfoDto (Task 2), not in the security crate.
3. taint() -> taint(reason): ~25 call sites (grep '\.taint('). 4 prod (router.rs 204/223/261/371) + ~21 tests across security lib.rs, mcp-core lib.rs, src/elmer/session.rs, injection_tests.rs, ui_core/security_commands.rs. Cross-crate test callers need 'use tuxlink_security::TaintReason'. injection_tests.rs:597 calls g.taint() directly (it's a test helper, gets a reason arg) — this is the ONE injection_tests edit allowed (a mechanical arg add, NOT an assertion change). taint() = first-reason-wins (monotonic).
4. Pi cannot compile Rust locally — build via CI on the PR.

title: Agent/MCP modem binding does not follow UI active-mode switch (VARA stuck as ardop)
description: Tuxlink 0.85.0 agent-mode alpha test (Elmer + Opus-4.8 API, operator N7CPZ). Operator set VARA HF active in the UI, but the agent/MCP tool layer stayed bound to a stale ardop modem context: modem_get_status.kind stuck 'ardop' after the switch; backend_status.transport empty (no backend attached to agent session); vara_* tools report engine_available:false / status closed; config_get_vara all zeros (bandwidth/drive never synced). Root cause (to confirm in code): UI active-mode change does not re-publish/rebind the active modem to the agent session, so the tool layer keeps a cached ardop handle and vara_* query an uninitialized/closed VARA engine. Fix directions from the handoff: (1) on UI active-mode change, rebind active modem to the agent tool layer so modem_get_status reflects live selection; (2) vara_* resolve the CURRENT active modem not a cached ardop handle; (3) verify VARA TNC cmd port 127.0.0.1:8300 listening + populate VARA config on bind; (4) agent-invokable 'start/attach active modem' path / backend_status.transport populates on mode activation. Blocks agent-driven VARA on-air testing. Handoff evidence: R2 Documents/tuxlink_0.85.0_VARA_HF_agent_mode-plumbing bug.pdf (Winlink msg ID ALH7GW0Q47D7).
notes: PERCEPTION BUILT + PUSHED (esker-wren-towhee, 2026-07-08). PR #1055 (bd-tuxlink-7ppfq/perception). CI verify GREEN on amd64+arm64 (head 0f5f850b). Contracts 1+2 shipped: vara_status.reachable (try_lock+cmd-port TTL cache, no-contention), read-only vara_probe tool, config active_connection + CONFIG_SCHEMA_VERSION 5->6, modem_get_status SoT (selected/running/kind via derive/gather; ARDOP liveness from snapshot_transport_present NOT active_transport_kind), frontend useActiveModemMode + persist/hydrate. Post-impl Codex review: 5 findings all resolved (stale-base -> merged origin/main restoring RELEASE_FREEZE+pf6re; 14 Config literals; private_interfaces lint; vara-fm panel; hydrate race). Wire-walk NOT run with operator flows: operator opted to validate via their own converged build (stronger than grep-trace). Seams self-checked wired (config_set_active_connection invoked AppShell.tsx:900; MCP tools in #[tool_router]). Awaiting operator merge + build + manual 0.87.0 cut. Do NOT close until operator's build validates.

title: Elmer UI: token stream box must be bounded (~10 lines, scrolling) and must NOT lock user scroll during streaming
description: Covers reported items 2 and 3. The token stream box is unbounded; it should be a bounded scrolling box, ~10 lines max. Critically, while tokens stream the box prevents the user from scrolling up, so the user cannot supervise tool calls at all. Fix: bound the box AND allow independent user scroll during streaming. From session 2026-07-08.

title: Bundle rigctl/rigctld as a sidecar; drop system hamlib dependency
description:
Tuxlink's shipped .deb hard-Depends on libhamlib-utils (and .rpm on hamlib), which pins an exact libhamlib version and CONFLICTS with any pre-existing hamlib (source builds, PPAs, WSJT-X). Verified: 0.83.0 amd64 .deb fails to install on Ubuntu 24 R2 that already has hamlib (libhamlib4t64 =exact won't resolve). The ECT low-floor build has no hamlib provisioning at all (drops it from depends, can't apt-install on a frozen EOL platform).

Fix (converged with operator): bundle rigctl/rigctld as a per-arch Tauri sidecar built in CI (mirroring the ardopcf sidecar pattern), ALWAYS use the bundled copy (deterministic, same tested version for every user), and DROP the system hamlib dependency entirely from both .deb and .rpm (no depends, no recommends). Keep the existing rigctld_binary config field as an explicit opt-out for power users who want their own hamlib. No 'install hamlib' banner needed since rigctld always ships in the package.

Main implementation risk: rigctld dynamically links libhamlib.so, so bundling is more involved than ardopcf -> static-link hamlib or ship libhamlib.so with rpath/LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Adversarial review must stress-test this first.

Applies to: src-tauri/tauri.conf.json (deb+rpm depends), .github/workflows/release.yml + ect-build.yml (sidecar staging + externalBin), src-tauri/tux-rig/src/{list.rs,managed.rs} (resolve bundled binary path), RigControlSection.tsx (default rigctld_binary to bundled). Ends in a PR; next cut is 0.84.0.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-a9ip3-hamlib-bundled-sidecar. Branch: bd-tuxlink-a9ip3/hamlib-bundled-sidecar. Created 2026-07-07 14:59:50 UTC.

title: [BUG] Serial device pickers show 'USB serial' for every port — enrich with real vendor/product/interface/serial name
description: Operator 2026-07-07: serial dev/tty/usbN listings are unusable — every USB serial device shows the fixed label 'USB serial' (classify_serial_device returns a &'static str, ui_commands.rs:4209), so you cannot tell which /dev/ttyUSBN is which. Confirmed on r2-poe: ttyUSB0 + ttyUSB1 are the two interfaces (00/01) of ONE Silicon Labs CP2105 Dual UART Bridge (a DigiRig-class dual-port adapter) — indistinguishable by path. udev/sysfs already knows vendor/product/bInterfaceNumber/serial. FIX: enrich packet_list_serial_devices/discover_serial_devices labels for ttyUSB/ttyACM with the real device name from sysfs (product/manufacturer + interface 'port N' + short serial), e.g. '/dev/ttyUSB0 — Silicon Labs CP2105 Dual UART Bridge (port 0, SN 01C71CA5)'. serialport 4.9 (UsbPortInfo has interface) is already a dep; sysfs is the robust source for bInterfaceNumber. Fallback to 'USB serial' when metadata absent. Flows to all pickers (CAT port, PTT, packet TNC) via the shared label. Part of the modem-config-fixes batch (with olxge, 6urh2).
close_reason: Closed

title: [BUG] Declare libhamlib-utils as a .deb dependency so rig control has rigctl/rigctld on a fresh install
description: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed: no rigctl on R2 r2-poe): rig_list_models hardcodes 'rigctl -l' (modem_commands.rs:170); hamlib/rigctl is NOT bundled, NOT a declared package dependency, and NOT detected-with-guidance. On failure the RigControlSection Radio picker silently degrades to a raw text box placeholder 'hamlib model # (rigctl unavailable)' (RigControlSection.tsx:257) — no explanation that hamlib is required, why, or how to install it. When rigctl IS present, the picker is the FULL uncurated 'rigctl -l' catalog (hundreds of rigs incl Hamlib Dummy/NET rigctl, raw (id) shown, no common-rigs-first, no search). Shared by ARDOP + VARA (Config.rig). FIX: (a) declare/bundle hamlib OR detect-and-guide (actionable 'install libhamlib-utils' with exact command + link); (b) curate the picker (common rigs first, searchable, radio-name-first, hide raw ids/obscure). Audience: EmComm Tools users (ECT bundles hamlib) + standalone installs (do not).
notes: Operator 2026-07-07: DECLARE the dep now (NOT bundle — rig control needs system rigctld for CAT/QSY anyway; bundling only rigctl-for-list risks model-id drift + doesn't remove the system-hamlib need + conflicts with ECT). Scope THIS issue to: add libhamlib-utils to the .deb Depends. Verify end-to-end from a FRESH .deb install (CI deb-install-test + operator). NO manual apt band-aid. Detect-and-guide + picker curation moved to the rig-control revisit issue.

title: [BUG] VARA session wedges in 'open' when the VARA instance drops — config locked, no reconnect (deferred heartbeat never wired)
description: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via R2 r2-poe + origin/main code): VaraState::SocketLost drop-detection was explicitly DEFERRED to a follow-up that never landed (src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/commands.rs:86-91: 'Heartbeat infrastructure that drives this transition is deferred to a follow-up task'). So when the operator closes/drops VARA, Tuxlink never detects it and stays VaraState::Open. Frontend VaraRadioPanel.tsx locks Host/Cmd port/Data port/Bandwidth via disabled={isOpen} (isOpen = state 'open'|'connecting', :479,520,538,554) and openSession no-ops on 'open' (:324) -> config locked, 'can't be made to' reconnect. Only recovery is a manual Stop (vara_close_session, :377) which IS a clean teardown, but it is not discoverable. Repro: connect VARA, close VARA, observe locked panel. FIX: implement the deferred cmd-port heartbeat/liveness -> SocketLost/Closed on drop (unlock fields + clear 'VARA connection lost, reopen' message); make Stop/recover discoverable. Operator hit this on R2 (x86 Ubuntu 24.04, VARA-under-wine).

title: Elmer Anthropic provider 400s on Sonnet-5/Opus-4.8: temperature is deprecated for frontier models
description: REGRESSION (operator-reported, live). AnthropicProvider::build_request_body (src-tauri/tuxlink-agent-frontend/src/anthropic_provider.rs:239) unconditionally sends top-level temperature when Some; frontend defaults temperature to 0.2. Anthropic REMOVED the temperature/top_p/top_k sampling params on its frontier models (Opus 4.7, Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, Fable/Mythos 5) — any value returns HTTP 400 invalid_request_error 'temperature is deprecated for this model'. Not conditional, no accepted value. Older models (Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6 and earlier, Opus 4.6 and earlier) still accept it. This breaks the two models people most want. FIX: gate temperature emission by model via model_accepts_temperature(model) allowlist (send only for known-accepting models; omit for frontier + unknown, which is always safe since server applies its default). Add pure-fn tests. Separate concern from ctx-meter (tuxlink-xnenf); needs its own worktree off main. Confirmed via claude-api reference skill 2026-07-07 (falcon-oriole-canyon).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-xnenf-ctx-meter/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-a8uh1-anthropic-temp-gate. Branch: bd-tuxlink-a8uh1/anthropic-temp-gate. Created 2026-07-07 09:08:29 UTC.

title: P2P peer store — track non-gateway stations (data model + peers config + settings UI + find_peers tool + telnet/RF-P2P wiring)
description: Operator decision 2026-07-01: the product has NO way to track non-gateway (peer) stations — find_stations lists only RMS gateways. P2P cannot function as a mode without a peer store. Build: Peer model (callsign + RF freq(s) + telnet host:port + keyring password + notes/last_connected); new 'peers' config section (keyring passwords, no plaintext); inline 'P2P Peers' settings UI (MOCK first per high-fidelity-mocks); find_peers agent read tool (mirrors find_stations, does NOT taint); wire telnet_p2p_connect(peer_callsign)->vetted host:port lookup (closes SSRF hole) + RF P2P (intent=p2p) pulls freq/password. RF P2P already works via intent=p2p post-#997; telnet is the divergent network-addressed mode. Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-elmer-agent-send-design.md Part 2 'P2P peer store'. Part of the unified sg5zw.2 delivery.

title: Elmer 'New conversation' button renders invisible until hover (WebKitGTK no-repaint of position:fixed child on display:none->show)
description: SYMPTOM: the Elmer header 'New conversation' button is invisible on a fresh pane open until you mouse over it (hover forces a repaint). Resisted ~7 fix attempts. ROOT CAUSE (not in the button's CSS): AppShell hides the kept-mounted Elmer pane via the HTML `hidden` attribute (display:none) on a wrapper whose child (ElmerPane root) is position:fixed. WebKitGTK does not repaint a position:fixed descendant when an ancestor flips display:none->visible, so freshly-shown content stays unpainted until a style invalidation (hover). WHY 7 FIXES FAILED: every attempt targeted the button's CSS tokens in ElmerPane.css (commit 76bc2196 fixed a real but SECONDARY undefined-token bug — that fix is present and correct, color:var(--text-dim,#94a0ad)); the real cause is the display:none toggle at AppShell.tsx wrapper, a different file/layer. 76bc2196's own commit message already named 'freshly-opened fixed pane may not recalc until a hover invalidates it' then patched only the token. FIX: replace `hidden`={!elmerOpen} with opacity:0+pointer-events:none hide + aria-hidden, keeping the fixed child painted so re-show is a composited opacity change WebKitGTK repaints reliably (AppShell.tsx ~2086). VERIFICATION: unit tests (AppShell.elmer.test.tsx) rewritten as regression locks (assert aria-hidden hide, NOT `hidden`/display:none) — pass; typecheck clean. WebKitGTK live repaint is operator-verify-only (render harness forces recalc so can't repro; CDP is Chromium not WebKitGTK; Pi can't build Tauri). Surfaced during tuxlink-xnenf re-scope toward conversation management.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remote model backend is an untrusted-instruction-source (RCE-class trust boundary): authn+encrypt the remote instruction channel + capability-gate remote-driven sessions
description:
SECURITY REFRAME surfaced by operator 2026-07-06 (poplar-oriole-butte session) while brainstorming tuxlink-xnenf (remote native Ollama). The operator correctly reframed the threat model: Elmer's 'agent' IS the model; Tuxlink ships the conversation to the model and then EXECUTES the tool-calls the model returns. When the model is a REMOTE endpoint over an unauthenticated/plain-HTTP channel, a remote source (rogue host OR a LAN/path MITM) decides which tools run on our host. Inbound-vs-outbound socket is an implementation detail — functionally this is an unauthenticated remote-control endpoint = RCE-class as the tool surface grows (the tuxlink-mcp 'hands-off execution harness', Plan 3, is explicitly growing it; the moment a tool writes files/runs a process, remote-injected tool-calls are literal RCE). Even TODAY's read-only-ish tool set (server_info, backend_status, modem/vara/rig/position status, mailbox_list/message_read, folders) means a MITM on the channel siphons full conversation context (mailbox contents, position, backend state) and can drive disclosure.

GROUNDED INVESTIGATION (this session, all read-only, verified on origin/main):
- MCP server is a LOCAL Unix-domain socket ONLY (tuxlink-mcp-core/src/transport_uds.rs): 0600, uid-owned, umask 0o077, single-session, refuses group/world-writable parent. NO TCP/SSE/HTTP MCP listener anywhere. The standalone tuxlink-mcp binary is a dumb stdio<->UDS byte-pump for local 'claude mcp', opens no port.
- Enumerated EVERY network bind in src-tauri Rust tree: all loopback (127.0.0.1) or UDS. forms/http_server.rs binds 127.0.0.1:0; managed_direwolf 127.0.0.1; gpsd is a client. No 0.0.0.0, no routable inbound listener. => There is NO separate inbound hole; the RCE surface IS the model tool-execution loop.
- The is_loopback gate at src-tauri/src/elmer/provider.rs:246 is a PROVIDER-SELECTION gate (probe native /api/chat vs compat), NOT an egress gate. build_vetted_client (tuxlink-agent-frontend/src/egress.rs) runs on BOTH branches and already PERMITS public/RFC1918/ULA/CGNAT IPs for Elmer, denying only SSRF-magnet ranges (loopback-without-opt-in, 169.254/16 metadata, link-local, multicast). So the operator's tailnet Ollama is already reachable via compat today.

WHY THE ORIGINAL xnenf FRAMING IS WRONG-SCOPED: xnenf was scoped as an SSRF egress-allowlist problem. That is ORTHOGONAL to the real risk — the SSRF gate keeps our OUTBOUND requests off the metadata IP but does NOTHING about trusting the INSTRUCTIONS that come back. Building the allowlist and calling it secure solves the wrong half.

DODGES THE OPERATOR EXPLICITLY REJECTED (do not reuse): (1) arm/taint/RADIO-1 guard TRANSMIT authority, not host integrity — irrelevant to RCE. (2) 'cloud models already drive the tool loop' is NOT a license: TLS-to-known-provider is a materially different trust posture than plain-HTTP-to-self-hosted-MITMable box; it never justifies a strictly-worse variant. NOTE: the existing CLOUD path may already under-appreciate this surface — deserves its own review (separate scope).

DESIGN DIRECTION (open, to decide next session): treat the remote model as an UNTRUSTED instruction source and require BOTH: (1) transport is authenticated+encrypted — real TLS to the remote, OR restrict 'remote native' to a Tailscale host and treat the WireGuard tunnel as the auth+crypto boundary (plain-HTTP-over-tailnet = encrypted+peer-authenticated; plain-HTTP-over-LAN refused); (2) capability-gate — remote-driven sessions get a constrained/allowlisted tool set, powerful execution-harness tools stay LOCAL-model-only. OPEN QUESTION for operator: is a remote model backend even worth this surface vs keeping the agent loop local-model-only? This issue BLOCKS/reframes tuxlink-xnenf.
design: SETTLED with operator 2026-07-06 (gully-esker-mesa). Design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-remote-model-trust-boundary-design.md. DECISION: require valid TLS for every non-loopback model endpoint; refuse plaintext-to-remote. Loopback stays first-class/plaintext-exempt (same trust domain). No cert pinning, no OS-root manipulation, no far-side/pairing component, no --skip-verify escape hatch. 'Valid TLS' = reqwest default OS-trust validation (already in force; no danger_accept_invalid_certs anywhere), so the entire security change is ONE new rule in the CI-tested endpoint.rs validate_endpoint accept/reject table (non-loopback + scheme != https => refuse, new PlaintextRemoteRefused error) + operator docs + CHANGELOG migration note. This is a TIGHTENING: endpoint.rs currently accepts non-loopback over plain http (the hole). Egress SSRF gate is orthogonal, unchanged. OUT OF SCOPE: capability-gating/scope-minimization (backend-agnostic agent-safety, own follow-up issue; OWASP LLM08); arm/taint/RADIO-1 (unrelated). xnenf re-scopes to 'native path for non-loopback hosts that present valid TLS' + rides on top. Next: Codex adversarial review -> writing-plans -> build.
notes: IMPLEMENTED 2026-07-06 (gully-esker-mesa). Spec+plan written, Codex adrev run (no TLS-gate/validation bypass; 3 fixable findings folded in), code executed inline. Change: endpoint.rs validate_endpoint now refuses non-loopback+http via new EndpointError::PlaintextRemoteRefused; loopback exempt; reqwest default TLS validation unchanged (native-tls/OpenSSL, iPAddress-SAN for bare IP); config.rs stale comment fixed; 27-settings.md operator docs added. Two hole-locking tests flipped + table/parser-edge/userinfo-precedence tests added. PR #1023 -> main (BREAKING: http://<non-loopback> now refused). Awaiting CI (both arches) then operator merge. Worktree: worktrees/bd-tuxlink-qe6ie-remote-model-rce-reframe.
close_reason: Closed

title: Provider-agnostic context meter — feed the meter from the compat path (vLLM/cloud), not just native Ollama
description: RE-SCOPED 2026-07-06 (gully-esker-mesa). SUPERSEDES the original 'remote native Ollama' framing. Two upstream decisions changed the target: (1) tuxlink-qe6ie shipped — non-loopback endpoints MUST use valid TLS; Ollama serves no TLS, so REMOTE OLLAMA IS NOT SUPPORTED (insecure). Ollama = LOCAL (loopback) only. (2) Operator's remote inference (incl. the incoming DGX Spark) is vLLM = OpenAI-COMPAT over TLS, which already connects today post-qe6ie via the custom/compat tile. So 'native Ollama' is the wrong target. THE REAL FEATURE: make the context meter (+ conversation mgmt) PROVIDER-AGNOSTIC so local Ollama, remote vLLM, and cloud all get it via one mechanism. GROUNDING (this session): the meter UI (ContextMeter.tsx) + event pipe (RunEvent::ContextUsage{prompt_tokens,eval_tokens,num_ctx} -> ElmerEvent::Context, session.rs:470) already exist. ContextUsage is emitted ONLY by the native path (ollama_provider.rs:255, parses Ollama /api/chat prompt_eval_count vs the num_ctx it set). The compat path (provider.rs OpenAiProvider, used by vLLM/cloud) never emits it ('the compat path leaves the context window at Ollama default'). Conversation mgmt is largely DONE: trim exists (tuxlink-evucv), New-conversation reset fixed this session (tuxlink-y0y9m). WHAT'S LEFT: (a) parse OpenAI-compat  (prompt_tokens/completion_tokens) in provider.rs and emit ContextUsage; (b) THE ONE OPEN DESIGN DECISION = the meter's denominator (context-window size) for compat: vLLM via /v1/models max_model_len; cloud via a per-model table; unknown endpoint -> bare token counter (no %) or hide. Decide this FIRST next session (short spec). (c) keep num_ctx (client-set window) LOCAL-OLLAMA-ONLY and move its frontend gate off string isLoopback onto real provider kind (old BUG2/BUG3, ElmerPane.tsx:616/781, GetKeyCard.tsx:200). Moderate backend feature + one design call, NOT a big build. Blocked-by qe6ie now RESOLVED (merged PR #1023).
notes:
REFRAMED 2026-07-06 (poplar-oriole-butte): the security core of this feature is NOT the SSRF egress allowlist as originally scoped — that is orthogonal to the real risk. A remote model backend is an untrusted INSTRUCTION source (RCE-class); see tuxlink-qe6ie (now a blocker) for the full grounded investigation + design direction. Do the qe6ie trust-boundary design FIRST; the isLoopback->native-selection change + persisted tile-kind flag + providerKind visibility (BUG 3) are the plumbing that rides on top of whatever trust model qe6ie settles. Backend architecture already mapped this session: ElmerConfig (config.rs:1477) add a serde-default bool; thread through ElmerProvider::new_vetted -> new_vetted_with_resolver_and_probe (provider.rs) as should_probe_native = is_loopback || opted_in; ProviderKind already computed there -> surface into ConfigReadDto (config_commands.rs:476) for the badge; frontend num_ctx gate (ElmerPane.tsx:616/781, GetKeyCard.tsx:200) moves off string isLoopback onto the authoritative providerKind.

title: [bug] Elmer tile switching carries stale fields (endpoint/model/num_ctx) — unkeyed ModelForm
description: ROOT CAUSE (delta-basil-fen 2026-07-06, confirmed): ModelTilePicker renders GetKeyCard for cloud tiles WITH key={selectedPreset.id} (remounts+resets on switch), but renders ModelForm for the no-keyPageUrl tiles (localOllama/openrouter/custom) with NO key prop (src/elmer/ElmerPane.tsx ModelForm ~=536; rendered at src/elmer/ModelTilePicker.tsx:318). React reuses the same ModelForm instance across those tile switches; its useState(initialEndpoint/initialModel/advanced/turnTimeout/keyStatus) seed ONCE at mount and never re-init on prop change -> endpoint/model/num_ctx/temperature/key carry over STALE. The tile chip + <select> DO update (derived from parent inferPreset(endpoint)), so 'tile looks selected but endpoint field lies'. Operator repro: switching ollama-local -> openrouter -> custom doesn't change the endpoint. FIX: add key={selectedPreset?.id ?? 'custom'} to the <ModelForm> at ModelTilePicker.tsx:318 (mirror GetKeyCard's key:299). TEST GAP that let it ship: no test switches between ModelForm tiles asserting elmer-endpoint-input updates (ModelTilePicker.test.tsx only tests GetKeyCard remount). Add that regression test. Verifiable via vitest on the Pi.
notes: FIXED + verified (delta-basil-fen 2026-07-06). key={selectedPreset?.id ?? 'custom'} on ModelForm (ModelTilePicker.tsx:318). Regression test walks localOllama->openrouter->custom asserting elmer-endpoint-input tracks; fails without the key, passes with it. typecheck clean, 304 elmer vitest pass. Branch bd-tuxlink-lubim/elmer-tile-switch-stale, commit 97a18386, PR #1022 (CI running). Awaiting operator wire-walk + merge.

title: [bug] Elmer context management UI regressed: context-control (advanced) + context meter missing/not working
description:
OPERATOR-REPORTED (2026-07-06), two related regressions of SHIPPED features:
1. Context control was specced + built into Elmer's ADVANCED CONTROLS; it is no longer present — users have no GUI to control context (num_ctx / context window) during use.
2. The context METER was built and is no longer present or working.

GROUNDING (delta-basil-fen): the plumbing largely still exists, so this is a UI render/wiring regression, not a data-loss:
- Backend still emits the data: RunEvent::ContextUsage {prompt_tokens, eval_tokens, num_ctx} at src-tauri/tuxlink-agent-runner/src/types.rs:224, emitted by the Ollama provider.
- Frontend still references it: <ContextMeter> + promptTokens/numCtx prep in src/elmer/useElmer.ts:227,257,408,467; num_ctx input in src/elmer/GetKeyCard.tsx (local/native tiles).
=> Investigate the RENDER TREE: is <ContextMeter> actually rendered in ElmerPane, and is the advanced-controls context input surfaced during chat (vs only in the key-setup card)? Likely a recent Elmer pane refactor detached these from the view.

PATTERN: same regression wave as tuxlink-jfpj2 (Stop/cancel), tuxlink-1zh3g (invisible 'New conversation' button), tuxlink-s587 (WX chip below mocks) — recent Elmer/pane churn is dropping shipped features. Worth a holistic Elmer-pane regression audit + a wire-walk of the advanced controls. Context control also relates to the OOM (bounding context = bounding memory), so restoring it is defense-adjacent to jfpj2.
notes: SUPERSEDED/clarified (2026-07-06): the context meter + num_ctx were built local/native-Ollama-ONLY by design (plan tuxlink-65qhn D6: 'no cloud meter in this epic; no num_ctx for cloud'). Chain intact; ContextUsage emitted only by OllamaProvider (loopback). Operator is on cloud/remote tiles so it never fires. Real fix = remote native Ollama (tuxlink-xnenf) which unblocks num_ctx+meter for the operator's Framework host, + un-burying num_ctx from the tile-picker drill-down. The switching half split to tuxlink-lubim (FIXED). This issue = the cloud-scope + reachability framing; resolve via xnenf.

title: [bug] Elmer 'New conversation' button does not render at all (only a hover/focus outline) — 5 fix rounds have failed
description:
OPERATOR REPORT (2026-07-05): The Elmer 'New conversation' / new-chat-session button in the message window STILL does not render visibly. The only thing that appears is an OUTLINE when the button is hovered or clicked/focused. Five rounds of fix attempts have not resolved it.

COMPONENT (grounded):
- Button: src/elmer/ElmerPane.tsx ~L1234-1249 — icon button, aria-label='New conversation', an SVG 'speech bubble + plus' glyph. Original feature = tuxlink-vbv2k (commit 974fc134).
- CSS: src/elmer/ElmerPane.css:75 (the icon-button rule) and a comment at :753 explicitly noting a prior 'new conversation button regression'.

FORENSIC READING OF THE SYMPTOM (leading hypothesis, verify — do not assume): the button ELEMENT is present and interactive (the hover/focus OUTLINE proves the box exists and takes focus), but its VISIBLE CONTENT — the SVG glyph — is not painting. That points at the glyph, not the button box: e.g. fill/stroke resolving to transparent or to the background color (currentColor chain), a zero-size / clipped / overflow-hidden glyph, a viewBox/path defect, or a WebKitGTK-specific SVG rendering failure. The app runs under Tauri/WebKitGTK on the Pi; icons that paint in Chromium can fail in WebKitGTK.

WHY FIVE ROUNDS FAILED: the prior fixes almost certainly targeted the wrong root cause (likely treated it as a layout/size/visibility CSS bug on the BUTTON, when the box is fine and the GLYPH is the problem — or missed a WebKitGTK-only rendering path). NEXT SESSION: run a FRESH systematic root-cause investigation (superpowers:systematic-debugging / investigate). Do NOT re-apply the same class of CSS tweak. Iron law: no fix without a confirmed root cause. Validate in the REAL WebKitGTK render harness (dev/render-harness/) or via grim on the actual Tauri app — NOT Chromium — because the bug may be WebKitGTK-specific and invisible in a Chromium preview.

Relates: tuxlink-vbv2k (original 'New conversation' feature). Cross-ref reference_webkitgtk_render_harness, white-screen-debug-via-CDP conventions.

title: Message window renders Markdown by default, with a raw-text toggle (open formats only, no proprietary rendering)
description:
OPERATOR REQUEST (2026-07-05): The main message window (the message reading/viewing pane) should render Markdown formatting BY DEFAULT, with a per-message option to switch to a RAW view.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Default: render standard Markdown (headings, bold/italic, lists, links, code/monospace blocks, tables) when displaying a received/opened message body.
- Toggle: a clearly-available 'show raw' control that reveals the exact unrendered source text.
- Raw view preserves fixed-width / monospace alignment. This matters for content like NWS tabular products (fixed-column forecast text) where Markdown/proportional rendering would destroy the column alignment and make the product unreadable. Raw = verbatim, monospace, no reflow.
- OPEN FORMATS ONLY: render Markdown (an open standard). Do NOT add proprietary rendering (no proprietary/vendor-specific message-format renderers). Markdown-in / rendered-out, plus the raw escape hatch, is the whole scope.

RATIONALE: readability for the common case (formatted text) without losing the raw fidelity emergency/weather operators depend on (tabular products, exact bytes). Markdown keeps the surface open and inspectable rather than locking rendering behind a proprietary format.

OPEN (implementer to resolve with sensible defaults, not punt): where the toggle lives in the message pane; whether the default is per-message-sticky or global; Markdown flavor + sanitization (untrusted message content must be rendered safely — no raw HTML/script injection from a message body). Cross-ref logging-redaction and untrusted-content handling conventions.

title: [architecture] Tuxlink-as-simulation-harness: scenario-driven ports over the REAL MCP router (parity-by-construction) unifying training + regression + bug-repro + observability
description: OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (kingfisher-cove-yew session, thinking-out-loud that firmed into direction). THESIS: stop maintaining a separate Python simulator that must be kept in parity with the real app; instead make Tuxlink ITSELF the training/eval environment by injecting scenario-driven synthetic state at the PORT boundary of the real Rust MCP router. Parity becomes tautological (real curate_gateway/distance/taint/egress/staging logic runs for real; zero reimplementation to drift). UNIFICATION: a bug report, a training scenario, and a regression fixture become the same schema -> ONE artifact, FOUR uses (train student, gate regressions per-build, reproduce field agentic bugs end-to-end, observe live agent). Realizes the observability architecture recorded 2026-07-05 (basin-swallow office-hours). KEY DESIGN CALL: inject at the port/tool-result boundary (evolve mcp-testserver mock ports into scenario-driven fixtures), NOT deep transport/protocol simulation - agentic behavior is a function of tool RESULTS not modem internals. OPEN SCOPE QUESTION (PoC answers it): what fraction of scenario space is reproducible at the port boundary (strong guess: large majority). Motivated empirically by this session's finding that the Python sim's {ok:true} stubs + ambiguous mock connect/send returns are the DOMINANT failure driver (fabrication/stall/false-sent are mostly environment artifacts). Additive + test-mode-gated (never touches production transmit paths). Relates: tuxlink-vqzq5 (235b->122b distill), the gold-gen honesty-filter, rig_meters (2jqjb - collapses into one real tool backed by synthetic rig state).
