title: Elmer: parallel Qwen3.5 distillation track (frontier-Qwen teacher -> hardware-friendly Qwen student), alongside gpt-oss-120b
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (OpenRouter testing): run a Qwen3.5 distillation track IN PARALLEL with the gpt-oss-120b retrain (tuxlink-48nyh), not instead of it. Rationale:

1. Qwen3.5 is a MUCH FASTER tool caller than gpt-oss, similar MoE architecture, and plays well with real hardware (unified-memory friendly; avoids the Vulkan-iGPU MoE crash class).
2. IN-FAMILY DISTILLATION ADVANTAGE EXISTS TODAY: larger frontier-class Qwen models produce excellent output now, so big-Qwen -> smaller-Qwen distillation has genuine headroom. This directly relieves the binding constraint that stalled the gpt-oss track: 48nyh's frozen-gate calibration found teacher-120b (3/16) ~= base-20b (4/16) — gpt-oss scaling plateaued, so the teacher couldn't lift the student. Qwen does not have that ceiling today.

WHY LOW-COST: the substrate is model-agnostic. The MCP tool surface (find_stations distance/bearing e7z7d, rig_meters 2jqjb, grounding tools 0mudm/atnsu/e7z7d, sim tool_surface parity gate) + the gold-gen/eval harness (run_gold --model / run_train --model-id already model-agnostic) define the environment both families train against. Getting the substrate right serves BOTH tracks; no rework.

SCOPE: teacher = frontier Qwen (served/cloud, e.g. OpenRouter); student = hardware-friendly Qwen that runs on the local rigs. Give Qwen its OWN discriminating-gate calibration (do not assume the gpt-oss gate transfers). Add model family as a first-class swappable axis in 6zkb6's harness. Relates: tuxlink-48nyh (gpt-oss-120b retrain), tuxlink-6zkb6 (discriminating-eval harness), tuxlink-u3m0g (FT-8 surfaces feed prediction grounding).
notes: TEACHER DECISION (operator 2026-07-05): distill qwen3-235b-a22b-2507 -> qwen3.5-122b-a10b. QUAL-competitive-or-better than 397b on grounded multi-turn tool-use (235b PASSED warc-vara-p2p where 397b missed by 1 predicate; more efficient: 9v12, 17v34 tool calls; autonomous where 397b punted). 22B active > 397b 17B. SELF-HOSTABLE: 235B@4bit ~117GB fits 128GB box w/ KV headroom; 397B@4bit ~199GB does not. Endgame: 122b student on commodity-portable/128GB hardware. Thinking variants deprioritized (~1pt wash, refuted 0-3). Confirming qual pattern across all 16 scenarios before committing.

title: Elmer: agents can't discover armed send-authority state from the tool surface (buried in server_info)
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (testing >120b models for tool-use trends): qwen-3.5-397b, on a complex 'transmit if and ONLY if I've armed send authority, otherwise stage and tell me what's queued' prompt, responded: 'Backend shows disconnected - no active CMS session. I cannot determine if send authority is armed from this status alone.' It called backend_status and could not find the arm state.

ROOT CAUSE (grounded on origin/main): the armed/tainted state IS exposed — via the server_info tool (tuxlink-mcp-core/src/router.rs:100-104, description 'Report the Tuxlink app name/version and the live egress-authority state (armed, tainted)...'; ServerInfoDto{armed,tainted} reads the live EgressGuard). But it is UNDISCOVERABLE for this intent because (1) server_info's description LEADS with 'app name/version', so a model asking 'am I armed?' does not map to it; the model naturally called backend_status (router.rs:112, 'Winlink backend (CMS engine) connection status') which correctly does NOT include arm state. (2) ELMER_SYSTEM_PROMPT (provider.rs ~724-736) explains the arm CONCEPT and that 'Egress is DENIED when send authority is disarmed' (i.e. learn-by-attempting) but never tells the agent it can PROACTIVELY CHECK arm state via server_info before deciding transmit-vs-stage. So on a conditional-transmit prompt the agent guesses instead of checking.

FIX (both, small + low-risk):
 1. Rewrite server_info's tool description to LEAD with send-authority: e.g. 'Report the operator's SEND-AUTHORITY state — whether send authority is currently ARMED (you may transmit) and whether the session is TAINTED — plus the Tuxlink app name/version. Call this to check whether you are authorized to transmit right now. Read-only.'
 2. Add a pointer to backend_status's description: '...Does NOT report send authority; call server_info for the armed/tainted state.'
 3. Add one ELMER_SYSTEM_PROMPT sentence: 'To decide whether to transmit or stage, call server_info — it reports whether send authority is ARMED and whether the session is tainted. When the operator conditions an action on being armed, CHECK server_info rather than attempting a send to find out.'
Consider also surfacing armed_remaining_secs in ServerInfoDto so the agent can report the window. Verify prompt/description-assertion tests + compile on R2; app-level, independent of the elmer-distill training harness.

Directly relevant to the deferred 'is Elmer too generative' discussion: this is the agent failing to map its intent onto the tool surface. Found in the 2026-07-05 testing pass (alongside 0tuc3/a1xwx PR #1012, qhe8n PR #1013, 7durx PR #1014).

title: Elmer: OpenRouter chat 401 (no Authorization header) despite a saved key — origin-keyed key orphaned
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05: OpenRouter 'doesn't work at all with any model'; chat returns HTTP 401 {"error":{"message":"No cookie auth credentials found","code":401}}. Operator confirms the key was SAVED, yet the 401 is the 'no auth header at all' variant (not 'invalid key') — so NO Authorization: Bearer is reaching the chat request, i.e. the keyring read returns Ok(None) at chat time.

STATIC ANALYSIS (origin/main): the key is origin-keyed (keyring account = elmer-agent-api-key::<endpoint.origin()>). Both build_turn_provider_from_parts (session.rs ~824) and config_read_inner key_status (config_commands.rs ~450) derive the origin identically from the SAME config snapshot endpoint via AgentEndpoint::parse(endpoint).origin(), and bearer_auth is applied whenever the read returns Some (provider.rs). So if the settings UI shows key_status=Present for the current endpoint, chat MUST also read Some — a contradiction. The consistent explanation: the key is stored under a DIFFERENT origin than the current chat endpoint. Origin-keyed keys are silently ORPHANED when the endpoint is later edited (e.g. detect/save under one endpoint value, then the endpoint string changes: scheme, host case, or a switch between the OpenRouter preset and a custom entry). elmer_config_set normalizes the endpoint (normalize_chat_endpoint) before computing the save origin; if the eventual chat endpoint normalizes/parses to a different origin, the saved key is unreachable.

DIAGNOSTIC NEEDED (one data point): with the OpenRouter endpoint configured, does the Elmer settings key field show the key as SAVED/stored (KeyStatus::Present)? 
  - If NOT present: the save didn't persist under the current endpoint's origin — repro the exact save endpoint vs chat endpoint strings.
  - If present but chat still 401s: add boundary instrumentation (log the origin used for the keyring read + whether Some/None) to catch the mismatch, since it would contradict the shared-origin analysis.

LIKELY FIX (design): origin-keyed keys are brittle against endpoint edits. Options: (a) on endpoint change, migrate/re-prompt for the key; (b) surface a clear 'key not found for this endpoint' state instead of a raw 401; (c) reconsider keying (per-provider-preset vs per-origin). Relates to the Elmer-UX-too-generative discussion (operator raised 2026-07-05). Found during the same testing pass as tuxlink-0tuc3 / tuxlink-a1xwx (PR #1012).
close_reason: Not a bug — operator confirmed the API key was not saved; saving it immediately resolved the 401. The 'no auth header' 401 was the keyless path behaving correctly (Ok(None) -> no bearer). Latent UX note (not acted on per operator): Detect succeeds with an inline key while chat requires a SAVED key, which can mislead; tuxlink-qhe8n's combobox rework touches the same surface if we later want to tighten that.

title: Elmer: provider failures don't persist to transcript + errors shown 'one deep' (blocks capturing model errors)
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05: model errors 'couldn't be reproduced or captured because we don't display errors more than one deep, or log errors to the conversation history, which is also still occurring after one fix attempt.' The prior fix (403928fc, tuxlink-6ompo: persist error/offline/rateLimited) did NOT close the gap. Root-caused on origin/main:

DEFECT B (capture-blocker, CONFIRMED): runner.rs provider_error_outcome (~207-212) maps EVERY non-rate-limit ProviderError — including Transport (the Gemini HTTP 400, backend 404/500, unparseable) — to RunOutcome::NeedsOperator("provider error: {..}"). On the TS side (useElmer.ts PERSISTED_FAILURE_PHASES ~147) needsOperator is NOT persisted, so these failures live only in the single-slot lastOutcome banner and are overwritten by the next run — the operator can't capture them in scrollback. The 403928fc fix added error/offline/rateLimited but NOT needsOperator, and provider transport failures land in needsOperator. FIX (correct, not the blunt 'add needsOperator to the set' — that would wrongly persist policy gates like egress/tool denial): classify a genuine PROVIDER FAILURE distinctly from an operator POLICY GATE. E.g. map Transport/Unparseable provider errors to an outcome the TS renders as the 'error' phase (already persisted) rather than needsOperator; keep true gates (max turns, keyring locked, already-running, egress/tool denied) as needsOperator (transient, not persisted). Touches RunOutcome classification (runner) + the outcomeKind→phase map (TS).

DEFECT A (one-deep display): HTTP-STATUS errors DO embed the nested body (redact_and_cap 500 chars, provider.rs ~275-288) so the Gemini 400 message is present. But reqwest TRANSPORT errors use format!("request failed: {e}") (provider.rs ~261) / "{e}" which prints only the outermost reqwest Display and DROPS the std::error::Error::source() chain (e.g. the underlying 'connection refused'/TLS cause). FIX: walk the source() chain when building the Transport string so connection/TLS failures show their real cause, not just 'error sending request'. Consider raising the 500-char body cap or making it configurable if Gemini bodies get truncated.

Relates to tuxlink-0tuc3 (the Gemini thought_signature bug these visibility gaps hid). Fix both in one Elmer-error-robustness branch. Pi can't compile Rust → write+tests, PR, CI compiles; TS testable via pnpm vitest locally.
notes: Fix committed d8a95296, PR #1012 (bundled w/ 0tuc3). RunOutcome::ProviderError->persisted 'error' + reqwest source() chain. R2-verified. Awaiting CI + merge.

title: Elmer: Gemini 3.x multi-turn tool calls fail (thought_signature not preserved across turns → HTTP 400)
description:
REPRODUCED 2026-07-05 (operator) with Gemini 3.5 Flash. Prompt: multi-tool request (find stations → report → CMS Telnet send → 7-day weather). Fails on the 2nd+ turn: HTTP 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT 'Function call is missing a thought_signature in functionCall parts ... function call default_api:solar_conditions, position 3' (ref https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/thought-signatures).

ROOT CAUSE (grounded on origin/main; no thought_signature/extra_content handling exists anywhere in-tree): Gemini 3.x thinking models return a per-tool-call opaque signature at choices[].message.tool_calls[].extra_content.google.thought_signature (streaming: choices[].delta.tool_calls[].extra_content.google.thought_signature) and REQUIRE it echoed back verbatim on every subsequent request's assistant tool_calls[]. Tuxlink's transport-agnostic ToolCall {name, args} (tuxlink-agent-runner/src/types.rs:54) has NO field to carry it, so:
  (1) parse_tool_call (tuxlink-agent-frontend/src/provider.rs ~876) reads only name+args, dropping extra_content.
  (2) the streaming SseAccumulator/PartialToolCall (~281-592) never captures it from deltas.
  (3) build_request_body (~730-738) rebuilds assistant tool_calls[] without extra_content, so the echo is missing.
Turn 1 works (no history); turn 2+ 400s. Confirmed across many projects (openai/codex#7519, openai-python#2758, block/goose#5792).

FIX SEAM: add an optional provider-opaque signature to ToolCall (e.g. thought_signature: Option<String> or provider_meta: Option<Value>); capture it in BOTH parse paths (non-streaming parse_tool_call + streaming delta assembly); echo it back in build_request_body as tool_calls[].extra_content.google.thought_signature; preserve it through elmer/provider.rs redact_message (which reconstructs ToolCall) and any other ToolCall reconstruction. Only emit extra_content when a signature is present (don't send the field to non-Gemini endpoints). TDD against the existing parse_completion/build_request_body test suite. Pi can't compile Rust locally → write + tests, open PR, CI compiles.

Prior Gemini compat fixes: 5f87f260 (object-form function.arguments, tuxlink-fzj9a). Relates to error-visibility defects (separate issue) that blocked earlier capture of this error.
notes: Fix committed 96494c0d, PR #1012. R2-verified (runner 50, frontend 166, main compiles+redaction test). Awaiting CI + merge.

title: Elmer: resolve Maidenhead grids deterministically (grid->latlon->place) so the model stops hallucinating grid locations
description: OBSERVED: model places grid DM33 in Missouri; DM33 is actually western Arizona (~33-34N, 112-114W). Recurs in inference, pre-training, and gold-gen. Root cause: Maidenhead->place is deterministic geography (exact math + gazetteer lookup) that LLMs can't recall; the model guesses. The app HAS grid_to_lat_lon (src-tauri/src/position/maidenhead.rs:32) but it is NOT exposed to Elmer, tool outputs pass grids as OPAQUE strings, and there is NO reverse-geocode (no placename/gazetteer). Fix (serve resolved geography, don't teach it): (1) ENRICH tool outputs that contain a grid with {lat,lon,place_label} via grid_to_lat_lon + reverse lookup [highest leverage]; (2) ADD a resolve_grid/locate_grid tool (grid -> lat/lon + nearest place + state + distance from operator grid) + distance_between_grids (haversine); (3) bundle a small offline gazetteer (GeoNames cities1000/cities15000, few MB) for grid->place names, offline. GOLD-GEN implication: teacher states grid locations from memory -> distills the hallucination; once resolve_grid exists, regenerate gold so grid-location answers route THROUGH the tool (call -> resolved value -> answer), training the behavior not the (bad) recall. Related: tuxlink-e7z7d (find_stations lacks distance). Operator raised 2026-07-05.

title: Elmer num_ctx / context control not applied to the OpenAI-compatible provider path (overflow 400)
description: Confirmed 2026-07-05: num_ctx (context-window control) is threaded ONLY to the Ollama adapter (ollama_provider.rs sets options.num_ctx). The OpenAI-compatible OpenAiProvider (tuxlink-agent-frontend/src/provider.rs) ignores num_ctx entirely. Consequence for a custom OpenAI-compat endpoint (e.g. llama.cpp): (1) the context-window setting does nothing, and (2) there is no client-side conversation management, so a long tool-loop grows unbounded and overflows the server context -> HTTP 400 exceed_context_size_error (observed: request 17035 tokens vs server n_ctx 8192). The OpenAI Chat Completions API has NO context-size field, so the fix must be CLIENT-SIDE: use the configured num_ctx as a token budget to trim/summarize the conversation before POSTing on the OpenAI path, AND report num_ctx in the ContextUsage meter (session.rs RunEvent::ContextUsage) for that path so the UI meter reflects it. Server-side workaround already applied for the live pod (llama-server -c 32768). Scope decision pending operator: quick (meter-only) vs full (client-side trim to prevent overflow).
close_reason: Closed

title: Elmer custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint: first-run model selection + chat 404 (4th recurrence)
description: RECURRING (4th report, never fixed). Tuxlink 0.80.0 first-run cannot properly select/use models from a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Endpoint independently verified GOOD (llama.cpp /v1/models + /v1/chat/completions both 200). Chat fails with HTTP 404 'File Not Found' (llama.cpp unknown-route body: type=not_found_error) => Tuxlink transport is hitting the WRONG PATH. Strong hypothesis: endpoint-type detection (is_anthropic_endpoint / provider-kind routing across OpenAI/Anthropic/Ollama in src-tauri/src/elmer/provider.rs) misclassifies the custom endpoint and POSTs to /v1/messages (Anthropic) or /api/chat (Ollama). Also suspect: base-url+path join (double /v1, trailing slash), model-id-with-slashes (/root/elmer-120b-q8.gguf), and the React first-run model-selection persistence (src/elmer: elmerModelConfig.ts, ModelTilePicker.tsx, AdvancedModelSettings.tsx). Full bug-hunt-cycle + Codex adrev in progress.
notes: FIX PR #1007 (peregrine-cypress-towhee, branch bd-tuxlink-1hv4j/elmer-endpoint-hunt off origin/main=0.80.0). Root cause: OpenAiProvider posts stored endpoint verbatim; base URL (.../v1) posted with no /chat/completions -> llama.cpp 404. Recurrence cause = instrumentation gap: transport error omitted the request URL. Fixes: (1) redacted_url() in all 3 transport error sites, (2) normalize_chat_endpoint() at store time. Codex adrev P1 (reqwest URL cred-leak -> without_url) + P2 (trailing-slash routes) addressed. Also fixed invisible new-conversation button (ElmerPane.css referenced undefined tokens --text-2/--text-muted/--surface-hover/--surface-3; SVG currentColor resolved through invalid-var->inherit; WebKitGTK render harness confirmed CSS renders in isolation -> dynamic recalc issue; fixed with defined --text-dim token + color-mix hover). 7 Rust tests. CI compiles. IMMEDIATE OPERATOR UNBLOCK (no build): set endpoint to full .../v1/chat/completions.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] Catalog NWS tabular (SFT) forecast renders blank in 0.80.0 — hardened parse_tabular is fail-closed; any format variance → Forecast::None
description:
REGRESSION (operator, 0.80.0): the custom NWS tabular weather report in the Winlink Catalog reply view renders BLANK; content only appears via 'Show full text'. CatalogReplyView.tsx ForecastBody returns null for kind!='tabular'/'zone', and the toggle reads 'Show full text' when !structured — so parseReply is yielding Forecast::None for the operator's real SFT reply.

ROOT CAUSE (mechanism, confirmed): the catalog parser (src-tauri/src/catalog/reply.rs) last changed 2026-06-10 in d51193d3 'harden area-weather parser per Codex review'. That commit made parse_tabular FAIL-CLOSED: the data region [first '...REGION...' header .. '$$' terminator] must contain ONLY region headers or perfect 4-line blocks [name][cond:N][temps:N][precip:N]; ANY deviant line → 'return None // fail closed' → whole forecast None → blank custom view. The pre-hardening parser skipped odd lines and rendered the rest. Nothing has changed since 2026-06-11; 0.80.0 is simply the first stable/Latest release the operator installed carrying the June-10 change (their prior working build predated it), so it presents as a 0.80.0 regression.

FAIL-CLOSED TRIGGERS (any one blanks the whole forecast):
- region_name requires '...NAME...' (len>6, starts+ends with '...'); a header not in that form → no region start → None.
- Extra data rows per location (wind/humidity/precip-type) beyond [cond][temps][precip] — the 4-line-block assumption breaks (STRONG candidate: many SFT products have >3 rows).
- cond token not purely [A-Za-z-] (is_cond_token); temps/precip not exactly N slash-pairs of digit-run|MM|- (is_slash_row); any row token count != N (day count from parse_day_columns).
- legend/note line between first region header and '$$'; parse_day_columns failing on the day header.

NEXT (needs the operator's actual raw SFT reply body — the text behind 'Show full text' for one that blanks): run it through parse_tabular to pinpoint the exact rejecting line; fix by handling that format variant (and/or reconsider fail-closed vs fail-soft) with the real sample as a Rust regression test.

MITIGATION (frontend, no sample needed): when structured parse fails (kind none), CatalogReplyView renders a blank body requiring a 'Show full text' click — could default to showing the raw inline instead of blank.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-kfcwc-catalog-sft-empty-cell. Branch: bd-tuxlink-kfcwc/catalog-sft-empty-cell. Created 2026-07-05 04:35:04 UTC by bog-kingfisher-delta.
close_reason: Closed

title: Elmer distill: rebalance gold toward RESTRAINT (taint/refusal), keep volume
description: iter-2 (hawk-slate-oak, 2026-07-03): 3 training runs all regressed below base (4->3->2 on the frozen gate). Confirmed root cause via per-scenario judge reasons: the distillation trades RESTRAINT for ACTION. Gold is dominated by 'complete the multi-tool task' trajectories, so the student learns to reach for send tools and UNLEARNS taint discipline — elmer-v1 tried aprs_send_message into a tainted session on aprs-injection-refuse where base correctly refused. All runs lose the same restraint scenarios (taint refusal, form precision, honest 'cant send'). Next experiment: make the training gold MIRROR the gate's action-vs-restraint balance — taint/refusal cells a large high-quality fraction (stage+refuse+honestly-decline trajectories). NOT more scenarios, NOT a better teacher (N=6 showed hard action cells teacher-limited). Keep volume (v2 at 59 examples destabilized vs v1 at 118). See dev/handoffs/2026-07-03-hawk-slate-oak-elmer-iter2-gateshaped-generator.md.
notes: CONCLUDED 2026-07-03 inconclusive-with-finding. iter-3 pod run (H200): scaffold fix took yield 5%->51% but references_real_gateway cells yield 0% (emcomm/blended d4+d6 clean AND tainted); 120b cannot produce evidence-grounded gold, starving the restraint cells. Gold 8 then 77 < 118 floor; volume guard correctly refused under-volume training. The rebalance was never fairly tested (teacher-limited). Operator PIVOTED to slice-2 (elevate 120b from a bigger teacher) on incoming DGX Spark HW; 20b deprioritized. Code shipped+adrev'd on bd-tuxlink-6zkb6/discriminating-eval (generate_balanced, capture_bestof scaffolded, run_gold, run_iter3.sh). See project_elmer_teacher_ceiling_pivot memory + slice-2 plan doc.
close_reason: Won't-do. Superseded by operator decision (2026-07-05, delta-basil-fen session): security-restraint / injection-refusal is NOT a training goal — handled by engineering + admin controls (taint gate, egress arm/disarm), deemed more than sufficient. Reaffirms project_elmer_teacher_ceiling_pivot (restraint/injection-refusal PUNTED to guard+operator; build = pure grounded-quality cold-transfer). Issue's own notes already concluded the gold-rebalance inconclusive/teacher-limited, 20b deprioritized post slice-2 pivot.

title: Discriminating eval gate + gold-gen + Phase A LoRA training (Elmer-20b distillation)
description: Redesign the distillation eval so it actually discriminates long-horizon agentic quality (top-tier command-post class), then generate gold + run the first real LoRA. Supersedes the non-discriminating gate approach (tuxlink-vvdii/#1004 saturated at ~90% for raw base = teacher). Split the two jobs: a FROZEN hand-authored hard GATE (outcome-graded, empirically calibrated to the teacher-vs-base gap) + a diversity GENERATOR for training data, both on a shared ham-domain predicate library. Includes: predicate lib, richer simulator mocks, outcome-based judge (fixes the tier2-denial + false-sent bug Codex found), ~15-20 top-tier hard scenarios, empirical calibration runner, multiple baselines, stratified holdout, re-freeze prereg; then gold-gen (real G1, measure P95) + Phase A Unsloth LoRA (attention+expert-MLP, router untouched) + acceptance eval on the frozen gate. Design spec forthcoming; Codex adrev before lock. Parent context: tuxlink-ct08v.
notes: Stage-1 LOCAL execution complete on bd-tuxlink-6zkb6/discriminating-eval (HEAD bf605ccf, 62 tests). Tasks 1-7 + local 8-10: evidence-bound predicates, rich mocks, scenario provenance, judge upgrade (FIXES denied-tier2/false-sent bug), G2+ adversarial+positive corpus, baselines, calibrate+teacher-fail-audit, 6 representative hard-gate candidates + gate_lint, micro-LoRA smoke script + pinned deps, frozen DIRECTIONAL prereg. PENDING: (1) operator red-team of candidates + greenfield operator-authored subset (blocks freeze), then scale to ~40; (2) pod runs next session (micro-LoRA smoke + calibration). Handoff: dev/handoffs/2026-07-02-cypress-finch-willow-discriminating-eval-stage1-local.md

title: Ground scenariogen in concrete prompts + taint-consistent specs, then re-run G0/G1 pilot
description: 2026-07-02 pilot on the A100 showed raw-20b 4% / 120b-teacher 0% gold yield because scenariogen emits vague placeholder prompts (models answer with zero tool calls, turns=2) and pre_tainted cells require cms_connect when egress should be refused. Fix: (1) scenariogen emits concrete grounded prompts per family (model of emcomm-cmdpost-01) so tool use is actually elicited; (2) SuccessSpec adapts to taint_state (pre_tainted => egress NOT required / expected-denied); (3) rebuild a fair G0 baseline that does not use the judge as an in-loop oracle (else 95% is circular); (4) re-run the pilot; target a realistic 120b gold yield per cell before any bulk gold-gen. Blocks Phase A training plan. Off origin/main (now includes the foundation). bd parent: tuxlink-ct08v.
notes: RE-PILOT (fixed code, 2026-07-02): raw-20b 21/24 (87%), G0-honest 22/24 (91%), 120b-teacher 22/24 (91%). Zero-tool defect fixed BUT eval now SATURATED/non-discriminating (teacher tied with raw student). Codex adrev (gpt-5.5) verdict: framework not yet measuring the right thing. Findings: (A/blocker) ceiling effect - bank measures short prompt-spelled checklists not command-post planning; (C/blocker) taint blind spot - pre_tainted drops egress requirement (avoids the decision) AND judge only penalizes denied EGRESS not denied TIER2_WRITE => reproduced FALSE PASS (pre_tainted radio_debug-d4 called denied config_set_ardop, passed=True). REAL JUDGE BUG. (B/major) prompts spell the recipe not the goal. (D) G0 fair-but-weak; report multiple baselines. (E) must_contain=[] accepts any body; unconditional config_set; blended-d6 missing ordering. (F) n_per_cell=1 collapses variety, holdout not stratified, prereg needs re-freeze. OPERATOR DECISION: PAUSE, rethink eval design before more framework code. PR #1004 stays OPEN/unmerged. Raw codex: dev/adversarial/2026-07-02-vvdii-framework-codex.md (local).

title: [bug] Elmer chat pane persists only ONE error at a time; previous errors swallowed instead of appended to chat history
description: Operator report (2026-07-02): unlike most model front-ends, Elmer only surfaces one error at a time in the chat pane; prior errors are swallowed rather than persisted as chat-history items. This actively blocks debugging (e.g. could not capture the verbatim Gemini tool-call error because a later error replaced it). A patch was previously attempted. Fix: append errors to the transcript as persistent items (like assistant/tool bubbles) so history is retained. Investigate current error surfacing in useElmer.ts / ElmerPane.tsx (EV_OUTCOME / saveState / error items).
notes: Fix pushed in PR #1002 (branch bd-tuxlink-erqzx/elmer-model-switch, head 403928fc601a033cd2579c73ea554c8b8a77d639). Regression test included; vitest 142 green locally + typecheck clean. Awaiting CI + operator merge. Do NOT close until merged to origin/main (verify via git log origin/main --grep=<id>).

title: [bug] Elmer OpenAI-compat parser requires function.arguments as JSON STRING; Gemini 3.1-pro/3.5-flash return an object -> tool calls seen as malformed
description: provider.rs parse_tool_call (:846-850) and StreamAccumulator (:554) both use Value::as_str on function.arguments, so a model that returns arguments as a native JSON OBJECT (not a stringified string) yields args=Value::Null -> runner COR-3 marks the call malformed -> re-prompt loop; the agent cannot execute tools. Operator reports Gemini 2.5-flash worked (returns string) but 3.1-pro and 3.5-flash fail (return object or non-conforming shape). ollama_provider.rs already tolerates object-form arguments; the OpenAiProvider path does not. Fix: accept arguments as String OR Object at both parse sites (string -> from_str; object -> clone as-is). Confirm with the raw Gemini 3.x tool-call response shape before landing. Related: tuxlink-erqzx is a separate Elmer config bug.
notes: Fix pushed in PR #1002 (branch bd-tuxlink-erqzx/elmer-model-switch, head 403928fc601a033cd2579c73ea554c8b8a77d639). Regression test included; vitest 142 green locally + typecheck clean. Awaiting CI + operator merge. Do NOT close until merged to origin/main (verify via git log origin/main --grep=<id>).
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] Elmer puts stale model on the wire: config set to gpt-oss-20b but chat POSTs old qwen2.5:14b model → 404
description: Fresh build from main (has E2 build_turn_provider per-turn rebuild, commit 8394cd0c). Settings model = gpt-oss-20b, but Framework 13 ollama log shows GET /v1/models 200 (detect OK) then POST /v1/chat/completions 404 because Elmer sends stale model qwen2.5:14b-q8-ctx32768 which the Framework doesn't have. The 404 proves the wrong model reaches the wire. Config has ONE model field (agent_model). Suspects: (a) Tauri State duplication — config_set updates a different ElmerModelConfigState Arc than the session holds; (b) startup warm-default provider used instead of per-turn build_turn_provider; (c) settings UI detect≠select (agent_model never actually updated). Need to trace config_set (config_commands.rs) → model_config snapshot → build_turn_provider (session.rs) against ground truth.
notes: Fix pushed in PR #1002 (branch bd-tuxlink-erqzx/elmer-model-switch, head 403928fc601a033cd2579c73ea554c8b8a77d639). Regression test included; vitest 142 green locally + typecheck clean. Awaiting CI + operator merge. Do NOT close until merged to origin/main (verify via git log origin/main --grep=<id>).

title: Native OllamaProvider: restore streaming (regression from compat path)
description: The merged native OllamaProvider (tuxlink-65qhn, PR #996) is non-streaming (D2), but the OpenAI-compat path it replaced for local DID stream (OpenAiProvider stream:true + AssistantDelta/ReasoningDelta). So local switched compat->native and lost token-by-token output — a regression. On slow CPU-local models (e.g. R2 N305 with ~19k-token prompts taking >30min prefill) the pane shows NOTHING until the turn completes, so a working model is indistinguishable from a hung one. Confirmed in live R2 testing 2026-07-01. FIX: stream Ollama /api/chat (stream:true, NDJSON one JSON/line): emit RunEvent::AssistantDelta from message.content deltas, ReasoningDelta from message.thinking (qwen3 has thinking), accumulate content + tool_calls across chunks, emit RunEvent::ContextUsage from the final done:true chunk (prompt_eval_count/eval_count), return the assembled ModelTurn (ToolCalls precedence over Text). Mirror OpenAiProvider's SseAccumulator structure but for NDJSON. Reverses D2 for the native path.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-b7tkf-ollama-streaming. Branch: bd-tuxlink-b7tkf/ollama-streaming. Created 2026-07-01 15:49:58 UTC by willow-heron-badger.
close_reason: Native Ollama streaming restored via PR #1000 (CI green both arches, Codex adrev clean incl. tool-call cap fix). Reverses D2 non-streaming for the native path — AssistantDelta/ReasoningDelta live, meter counts stream, non-stream fallback preserved.

title: CI red on main: ollama_provider system-prompt tests stale after #997 prompt rewrite
description:
main is red (verify job, both arches) at 9439b6ae. cargo test -p tuxlink-agent-frontend --lib: 132 passed, 2 FAILED:
- ollama_provider::tests::request_always_has_keep_alive_zero_stream_false_and_system_prompt (ollama_provider.rs:541)
- ollama_provider::tests::request_default_system_prompt_when_not_overridden (ollama_provider.rs:575)

Both tests assert system.contains("Arm to send") (spaces) but the new prompt uses 'ARMED send authority' / 'Arm-to-send' — the old literal no longer appears. title: EPIC: Elmer agent send — un-cripple egress so 'arm to send' actually lets the agent send (+control mode/details)
description:
Operator decision 2026-07-01: the shipped Elmer egress design is over-RADIO-1-cautious to the point of uselessness AND deceptive. Two flaws: (1) the agent has NO control over send mode/details — the flush fires the last-configured session; the agent can only stage bytes; (2) 'Arm to send'/'Agent send: ON' is deceptive — the button's plain language says the agent can send, but WITHHELD_EGRESS_TOOLS (executor.rs) filters the 7 connect/transmit tools out of the agent surface AND denies them at call time, so the agent literally cannot send; transmission only happens via an operator OutboxApprovalDialog->elmer_connect flush.

This CONTRADICTS the project's own ethos (no_tuxlink_added_safeguards: mirror WLE, no added modals/caps, operator action = consent) and misreads RADIO-1/ADR-0018 (which gates the OPERATOR's execution, with the operator's action as consent — the ARM is that consent). The withholding + mandatory per-send approval dialog is a tuxlink-ADDED safeguard the spec never required.

FIX DIRECTION (operator-approved): make the arm mean what the button says.
- Un-withhold the connect/transmit tools (cms_connect, verify_cms_connection, rig_tune, ardop_connect, ardop_b2f_exchange, vara_b2f_exchange, packet_connect) from the agent surface.
- Gate them on the EXISTING arm + un-taint EgressGuard (tuxlink-security) — which was built for exactly this. Armed + untainted -> agent may fire them; disarmed/tainted -> denied.
- Let the agent CONTROL the send: expose transport/mode/frequency/session params on the connect tools so the agent chooses how to send, not just what.
- RADIO-1 honored via the arm as time-boxed, operator-granted, ABORTABLE consent + bounded airtime + working abort (radio1_bounded_airtime_abort) — NOT via withholding.
- Approval dialog becomes optional/opt-in (content review), not the sole transmit path.
- Fix the system prompt (Elmer CAN send when armed; describe mode control) and the button/label so it's truthful.

AFFECTED / must-update: executor.rs WITHHELD_EGRESS_TOOLS + the withheld filter/deny; the DENYLIST LOCK test (withheld_set_equals_every_egress_marked_tool) + injection_tests (they currently ASSERT withholding — invert to assert arm-gated access); ELMER_SYSTEM_PROMPT; EgressArmControl label; approval.rs/commands.rs flush role; docs (ADR 0018 seam, the egress-taint-security-core plan). Reconcile with docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-26-tuxlink-mcp-egress-taint-security-core.md (AC-3).

BUILD APPROACH: this is the RADIO-1 boundary — plan-eng-review -> subagent build -> Codex adrev. Do NOT one-shot. Bounded-airtime + abort are correctness bars for on-air per RADIO-1.
notes: RESUME ANCHOR (2026-07-01, arroyo-canyon-granite). SHIPPED: C1+C2+C5 (sg5zw.1/.4 closed) via PR #997 merged 9439b6ae — un-cripple egress + truthful prompt; armed Elmer can send, gated solely by guarded_egress(Agent). Operator directive: build the REMAINING scope as ONE unified functioning delivery (no fragmentation/deferral) = C3(sg5zw.2 spine) + C4(sg5zw.3) + C6(sg5zw.5) + C7(sg5zw.6) + trip-wire(sg5zw.7 folded) + NEW P2P peer store(sg5zw.8). Definition of done = 20-station VARA/ARDOP/packet motivating flow works end-to-end (wire-walked). Full Part-2 design (decisions D-1, mechanism map file:line, peer model) in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-01-elmer-agent-send-design.md, committed+pushed on branch bd-tuxlink-sg5zw.2/agent-send-core (worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-sg5zw.2-agent-send-core, bound to sg5zw.2). NEXT: mock the P2P peer settings UI (high-fidelity), finalize Part-2 design -> 5-round adrev -> plan-eng-review -> subagent build -> wire-walk -> one PR. Paused here to address higher-priority work.

title: Elmer Anthropic adapter: max_tokens=4096 truncates thinking models (Sonnet 5) before any answer text → empty output
description: Operator 2026-07-01: Sonnet 5 dies with NO output after 2 tool calls; Sonnet 4.6 completes the identical query perfectly. Root cause: anthropic_provider.rs hardcodes ANTHROPIC_MAX_TOKENS=4096. On Claude 4.x, thinking tokens count against max_tokens; Sonnet 5 (thinks more than 4.6) exhausts 4096 with thinking on the synthesis turn and truncates (stop_reason max_tokens) before emitting any text block -> parse_messages_response returns empty Text -> blank assistant turn. 4.6 fit in 4096 so it finished. Fix: model-aware max_tokens (Haiku ceiling ~8192; Sonnet/Opus 16384) so thinking+answer fit without exceeding any model's output limit. Follow-up (not the cause here, since 4.6 works): thinking-block replay in the tool loop for interleaved-thinking correctness.

title: Elmer tools: find_stations lacks distance + predict_path over-requires args → models can't answer distance/ranking
description:
Operator 2026-07-01: agents repeatedly can't compute distance between stations despite having grids, and predict_path fails with 'arguments ... expected type object, got null' when asked to rank stations.

Root cause (real tool gap, amplified by weak models — NOT 'engine not loaded'; the arg-schema validation proves predict_path is registered):
- predict_path (router.rs ~408) returns distance_km + bearing_deg (ports.rs 416-417) but REQUIRES PredictRequestParams { rx_grid: String, frequencies_khz: Vec<f64>, gateway_antenna? }. Heavy VOACAP, one target at a time. So distance is only obtainable via a full propagation prediction that also needs dial frequencies the model must know.
- find_stations (StationListDto/GatewayDto) returns gateways with grids but NO distance/bearing from the operator. So 'which 5 are closest' forces N predict_path calls.
- Weak models emit null/invalid args for predict_path (the required frequencies_khz is the main provocation — they don't know 80m VARA dials).

FIX A (primary): find_stations should return distance_km + bearing_deg from the operator's grid for each gateway. Compute backend-side: operator grid (position_status) + gateway grid -> Maidenhead-to-lat/lon -> haversine + bearing. Makes 'closest N' and 'how far is X' answerable directly from find_stations, no predict_path, no frequencies, no null-args failure. This is the fix for the distance complaint.

FIX B: make predict_path's frequencies_khz OPTIONAL — default to the standard dial frequencies for the requested band/mode when omitted, so the model can call predict_path with just rx_grid. predict_path stays the RELIABILITY tool; distance moves to find_stations. Cuts the invalid/null-args failures dramatically.

Both help strong AND weak models. Also feeds the benchmark (tuxlink-ylmwj) as a scored capability.
notes:
SCOPED 2026-07-05 (kingfisher-cove-yew brainstorm): this issue now ships FIX A ONLY — find_stations returns distance_km + bearing_deg from the operator grid. FIX B (predict_path optional frequencies_khz) is SPLIT OUT to tuxlink-0lawk and TABLED, gated on the FT-8 epic (tuxlink-u3m0g) per operator (FT-8 surfaces will inform band/dial awareness). Design grounding: distance already ships in TS (src/forms/position/distance.ts distanceBetweenGrids, R=6371, grid-center) for the human catalog UI; the gap is that the Rust MCP GatewayDto the AGENT receives omits it. FIX A mirrors that TS algorithm into a Rust helper used by the find_stations MCP impl (operator grid resolved as predict_path does), sorted by distance; bearing_deg is new (no existing reference). Parity gate: sim (da5268fd) already computes distance_km (haversine R=6371.0088 — align constant to 6371) + must gain bearing.

App-side merged to main via PR #1016 (kingfisher-cove-yew, 7 commits, wire-walk passed, CI green both arches). Sim tool_surface parity committed on bd-tuxlink-6zkb6 (42b318dd, distance_mi+bearing_deg, R=6371.0, 253 tests). FIX B split to tuxlink-0lawk (gated on FT-8 u3m0g.2).

title: EPIC: native Ollama /api/chat local provider path (context control + usage meter + eager unload)
description:
DECISION (operator-approved 2026-07-01): adopt Ollama's NATIVE /api/chat for the LOCAL/loopback provider path. Cloud + other providers stay on the OpenAI-compat path. A native OllamaProvider (mirroring the Provider trait like AnthropicProvider / tuxlink-17akx) unlocks, in ONE place, everything the OpenAI-compat path cannot do:

1. Context control (num_ctx) — fixes silent truncation of agentic prompts (tuxlink-e0w3x). Set an adequate num_ctx for system+tools+history so lower-tier local models actually SEE the full tool surface.
2. Eager model unload on switch — keep_alive:0 to free the previous model (tuxlink-7mb3r), memory-critical on Pi/edge.
3. Context-usage/fullness METER (operator ask) — native /api/chat returns prompt_eval_count (full prompt tokens) + eval_count each turn; against the num_ctx we set -> render a fullness meter in the Elmer pane, like the Anthropic/Claude Code harnesses. Warn as it approaches full.
4. Advanced knobs (num_ctx, temperature) behind the inline Advanced disclosure alongside the editable system prompt (tuxlink-31tbw). Provisioning (pull/quant) stays CLI.

DESIGN NOTES / risks:
- Native /api/chat tool-calling shape differs from OpenAI's (tools + tool_calls nested differently); port the FIFO synthetic-id tool-protocol handling carefully (see the OpenAI + Anthropic adapters for prior art).
- Egress: the native API is a different path/port on the same host (usually 127.0.0.1:11434/api/chat) — route through the SAME vetted egress gate (SSRF-1) as the compat path.
- Selection: is_local/loopback endpoint -> OllamaProvider; else OpenAI-compat/Anthropic. Detect (/v1/models works on Ollama too, or /api/tags natively) for model listing.
- Streaming: final streamed message carries the eval counts for the meter.

PROCESS: this is fragile + big -> a design pass FIRST (brainstorm + high-fidelity mocks for the context meter + Advanced settings surface, per UI norms), THEN a plan, THEN subagent-driven build with a Codex adrev round. Do NOT one-shot it.

Subsumes/links: tuxlink-7mb3r (unload), tuxlink-e0w3x (num_ctx truncation), tuxlink-31tbw (advanced settings surface incl. system prompt). Related: tuxlink-6614d (Detect on tiles), tuxlink-ylmwj (benchmark).
design:
LOCKED UX (operator-approved mock, 2026-07-01, dev/scratch/elmer-native-ollama-mock-v2.png):
1. Context meter: slim amber bar ABOVE the composer (confirmed). HIDDEN until the first reply (no real token counts before then), then ALWAYS ON (persistent, tiny footprint — user-critical). Reads prompt_eval_count/num_ctx: e.g. Context 12k / 32k 38%. Amber ~75%, red ~90% (truncation warning). Local/native only (known num_ctx); cloud shows tokens-used without a denominator.
2. Advanced disclosure (collapsed by default, inline, in the model editor): num_ctx, temperature, editable system prompt + Reset. num_ctx MUST show a live MEMORY ESTIMATE + FIT indicator: KV-cache RAM for the chosen window + model size -> total, and whether it fits available host RAM (green fits / red exceeds). Larger-is-better ONLY if it fits — the estimate makes the tradeoff visible. Compute KV from model metadata (layers, hidden dim, kv heads, dtype) x num_ctx.
3. DEFAULTS target the 32 GB+ RAM class (the R2 reference host), NOT the Pi. Pis do not run real local agents — they run cloud thin-client. So num_ctx default is generous (~32768 shown), model-aware (smaller default for bigger models). This also corrects the earlier Pi-8GB framing in tuxlink-e0w3x / tuxlink-7mb3r: local inference = R2-class host; Pi = cloud path.
Recommended approach remains B (native OllamaProvider). Next: /plan-eng-review for the adapter + tool-protocol port + KV-estimate calc + tests.
notes:
RESOLUTION (2026-07-01): enabling OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1 collapsed the prefill compute buffer — gemma4@32k peaked 21.3GB on a 19k-token prefill (vs the pre-flash-attn OOM). So for the native-Ollama path: (1) recommend/assume flash attention; (2) memory estimate must reflect flash-attn's much-smaller compute buffer (the pre-flash flat/measured numbers are worst-case); (3) the REAL edge constraint is CPU PREFILL SPEED (~20 tok/s; a 19k prefill timed out at 900s) — the Advanced panel / meter should arguably surface throughput or a 'this context will be slow to fill on CPU' cue, not just memory; (4) num_ctx ceiling is free (prompt caching re-prefills only new tokens). R2 models set to 64k behind a permanent MemoryMax=40G net.
Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-65qhn-native-ollama-provider. Branch: bd-tuxlink-65qhn/native-ollama-provider. Created 2026-07-01 11:10:14 UTC by willow-heron-badger.
close_reason: EPIC shipped via PR #996 (merge b6feabec on origin/main). Native OllamaProvider (/api/chat, num_ctx, keep_alive:0), context meter, Advanced disclosure, memory-fit estimate, cloud temperature. Deps 7mb3r/e0w3x now closed.

title: Elmer cloud tiles (GetKeyCard) have NO model selection — locked to DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PRESET (e.g. gpt-4o-mini)
description:
Regression from the tile-picker onboarding redesign. Cloud providers (OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic/Groq) render via GetKeyCard, which takes agentModel as a fixed prop and saves it verbatim (GetKeyCard.tsx:176-178) with NO input to change it and no onDetect/detectState wired. So the user is stuck with DEFAULT_MODEL_BY_PRESET (openai=gpt-4o-mini, gemini=gemini-2.5-flash, ...). The old dense ModelForm let users set the model for any provider; the tile redesign dropped it for cloud.

Fix: add an editable model field to GetKeyCard (seeded from agentModel) + a Detect button (thread onDetect/detectState from ModelTilePicker, reuse the /v1/models detect the ModelForm uses) so cloud users can list + pick a model. Save must send the edited model.

Related: the switch-to-local 404 report (config saved as ollama+gemini-2.5-flash) is likely a stale on-disk config from a pre-#989 switch; #989 clears the model on cross-provider switch so a clean re-switch should self-heal. Decisive test: open picker -> local tile -> Detect -> pick qwen2.5:14b -> Save; confirm config becomes ollama+qwen.

title: Elmer: errors don't persist in transcript (single-slot lastOutcome overwrites) — un-capturable for troubleshooting
description:
Operator 2026-06-30: attempting to reproduce a 400 erased the first error from the chat log; only one error can be present at a time.

Root cause: errored runs emit no finalizing EV_TURN (useElmer.ts:312), so an error is NOT appended to the transcript items[] — it lives only in single-slot lastOutcome (useElmer.ts:227) which setLastOutcome overwrites every run (line 309), rendered as one banner (ElmerPane.tsx:1173). Successive errors overwrite; nothing accumulates; nothing is copyable after a later run.

Fix design: on EV_OUTCOME of an error-class kind (error/offline/rateLimited/toolDenied), append a persistent error ElmerItem to items[] carrying outcomeKind+detail so errors accumulate in the scrollback and survive subsequent runs. Compose with the per-reply Copy button (tuxlink-efdpi/#990): render error items with the same Copy affordance so the operator can hand the exact error text to a troubleshooting agent. Keep the actionable banner (rateLimited 'Switch provider') for the current run; decide redundancy vs. banner-only-for-actionable-kinds during build. Build ON TOP of #990 so error items inherit Copy.

This is the real blocker behind 'I can't give you the 400'.

title: Elmer converged-build: cloud→local model switch strands foreign model + system prompt over-refuses tool iteration
description:
Two root-caused bugs from operator converged-build testing (main @ d2ad3647, #987 merged):

1. nextModelForPreset returns null (preserve) when switching to a no-default target (localOllama/custom/openrouter), so a cloud model like gemini-2.5-flash is carried into the local endpoint's model field. It then 404s against localhost (symmetric twin of the #986 cross-cloud 404). No allowlist exists — Detect returns whatever /v1/models lists. Fix: CLEAR the model (return '') on a CROSS-provider switch to a no-default target; still preserve (null) on SAME-provider re-select.

2. ELMER_SYSTEM_PROMPT gives no guidance that Elmer may call tools iteratively in one turn; combined with 'never invent station lists' it makes the model refuse multi-step requests (e.g. 'rank top-5 VARA stations by predicted path' → refuses to call predict_path per candidate). Also the weather-staging framing reads as a flat refusal. Fix: add explicit tool-iteration + proactive-staging guidance without weakening the anti-fabrication rule.

Out of scope (needs operator error text): 'Gemini can't read our tools' — likely Gemini strict-schema rejection of an MCP tool's JSON-Schema keyword; awaiting exact 400 text + build-commit confirmation.

title: Native Anthropic Messages API adapter for Elmer (Claude is not OpenAI-compatible) + Sonnet 5 model refresh
description: Anthropic has NO OpenAI-compatible endpoint (verified via claude-api skill + live docs). Its native Messages API differs on every axis: POST /v1/messages, x-api-key + anthropic-version headers, top-level system, required max_tokens, tools use input_schema (not parameters/function wrapper), tool round-trip uses tool_use/tool_result content blocks, response is a content-block array. Build a native AnthropicProvider implementing the Provider trait alongside OpenAiProvider; select by endpoint host (api.anthropic.com). Fix the anthropic preset endpoint (chat/completions -> messages). Model refresh: Sonnet 5 (claude-sonnet-5) launched ~2026-06-09; the sonnet-4-6 step-up reference is stale -> claude-sonnet-5. Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5) still current -> default unchanged. Remote source of truth: implement Anthropic's native GET /v1/models for the detect path so available models come from the provider, not a constant. Agent: bayou-cedar-delta
notes: PR opened off main. Native AnthropicProvider (Messages API, x-api-key, tool_use/tool_result blocks, FIFO ids) + host selection in new_vetted + detect via native /v1/models + preset endpoint fix. 200 vitest green, typecheck clean, 18 Rust tests CI-pending. v1 limits: non-streaming; ToolSpec description placeholder. Operator validates with real key. bayou-cedar-delta.

title: FATAL: Elmer tool-calling 400s on Gemini/strict providers (role:tool sent without tool_call_id)
description: Converged repro (after tuxlink-2k0gz model fix). A query that triggers a tool call (position_status) 400s on Gemini: 'Request contains an invalid argument' INVALID_ARGUMENT. Root cause (systematic-debugging, REPRODUCED against Gemini's live OpenAI-compat endpoint with operator's key): render_message (tuxlink-agent-frontend/src/provider.rs) emits a NON-STANDARD tool representation — the assistant tool call as a plain text note, and the tool RESULT as role:tool WITHOUT a tool_call_id. Strict OpenAI-compat providers (Gemini, OpenAI) require a tool message to carry a tool_call_id matching a preceding assistant tool_calls entry; permissive local Ollama tolerated the lenient form. Confirmed: standard OpenAI protocol (assistant tool_calls[{id,...}] + tool{tool_call_id,...}) returns 200 from Gemini with ZERO provider-specific code. Fix: build_request_body renders the STANDARD protocol with synthetic FIFO-paired ids (one code path, all OpenAI-compat providers). NOT bespoke-per-provider. Note: Anthropic preset (api.anthropic.com/v1/chat/completions+bearer) is NOT OpenAI-compat and likely broken separately. Agent: bayou-cedar-delta
notes: PR opened off main. Rust-only fix; typecheck clean; REPRODUCED + verified vs live Gemini (old->400, fixed->200). Standard OpenAI tool protocol w/ synthetic FIFO ids; one code path all OpenAI-compat providers. Anthropic native-API gap flagged separately. bayou-cedar-delta.

title: FATAL: Elmer cloud tile saves wrong model (local model kept on switch → 404) + local Ollama detect/select stripped
description: Converged-build repro (post #984/#985). (1) FATAL: select Gemini tile + enter key + Save&Use → sending a query 404s: 'models/gpt-oss:20b is not found' — the Gemini ENDPOINT was saved but with the LOCAL model gpt-oss:20b. Root cause: nextModelForPreset (elmerModelConfig.ts) preserves the model across a provider switch when the OUTGOING preset has no default; localOllama's default is '', so a DETECTED local model is misread as hand-edited and kept when switching to a cloud tile → cross-provider 404. GetKeyCard shows no model field, so it saves silently. Fix: on a real provider switch to a target WITH a default, adopt the target default. (2) The local (Ollama) tile lost the model DETECT + select UX (ModelForm's Detect button + <select> of pulled models) — the local branch renders only a bare endpoint + model text input + Save, making local basically unusable vs before. Fix: local tile renders ModelForm (like the Other tier) — restores detect+select + endpoint editing + loopback-keyless handling. Both root causes confirmed via systematic-debugging. Agent: bayou-cedar-delta
notes: PR opened off main. 238 vitest green (+6), typecheck clean. Bug1 (nextModelForPreset adopts target default on switch) + Bug2 (local tile -> ModelForm restores detect/select). Smoke hit transient WebKitGTK write error; tests + converged build cover. bayou-cedar-delta.

title: Elmer model-config: inter-provider key dropped (401) + config_set failures swallowed silently
description: Two model-config defects. (1) buildSetKey read the stale mount-only keyStatus instead of the origin-aware effectiveKeyStatus, so on an inter-provider switch the key the operator typed (into the 'absent' input the form renders off effectiveKeyStatus) was IGNORED → config_set saved no key → 401 on the next message. (2) The Save button used void handleSave(), swallowing any elmer_config_set rejection: the form kept showing the new selection while the backend never changed, the 'now using ___' attribution (which lives after the awaited config_set in configSet) never fired, and the next message ran the old model with NO error. Fix: buildSetKey uses effectiveKeyStatus; handleSave catches + surfaces errors and shows a success confirmation. Local (no-key) setting already worked (operator transcript showed the attribution surface for gpt-oss:20b); this targets the inter-provider/key path + visibility.

title: useElmer listener async-race double-registers event handlers (doubled output in dev)
description: useElmer's useEffect sets up listen(EV_TURN/EV_CHIP/EV_OUTCOME) inside an async setupListeners() and pushes the unlisten fns AFTER the awaits. The cleanup iterates the unlisteners array, which is EMPTY if cleanup runs before the awaits resolve (StrictMode mount-unmount-mount, Vite HMR re-run, or fast unmount). The first listener set is never torn down, so handlers accumulate -> each EV_TURN appends the assistant turn N times. Confirmed on r2-poe (N305) via xwd screenshot: running  (StrictMode + HMR), the same response rendered as 2 markdown bubbles + a stale raw box, with doubled position_status chips. Fix: a  flag so listeners resolving after cleanup are immediately unlistened. Production (converged build, no StrictMode/HMR) doesn't accumulate, but the race still bites on fast reopen; the fix is correct hygiene. Build-provenance note: operator was running  from .local/converge-build-worktree, NOT the converged production bundle.
close_reason: Closed

title: Closing Elmer pane mid-run orphans the inference + loses conversation/Stop
description: AppShell.tsx renders {elmerOpen && <ElmerPane/>} so closing the pane UNMOUNTS it. Unmount tears down useElmer's event listeners + local state (items/phase/running) and does NOT cancel the backend run (a detached tokio::spawn). Result: the inference keeps running uncancellable, the conversation projection is wiped, and reopening shows 'idle' with no Stop. Fix: keep the pane mounted once first opened and hide via the hidden attribute when closed (preserves run + listeners + conversation + Stop on reopen), consistent with the app's 'Tuxlink keeps running when you close the window' philosophy (CloseBehaviorPrompt/#882). Follow-up (separate): a ribbon indicator that the run is active WHILE the pane is closed (needs run-state lifted out of useElmer).
close_reason: Closed

title: DECISION (arm/taint): resolve taint-clear semantics for a long-lived in-app agent loop (Elmer) — re-arm-clears vs restart-only
description:
Surfaced by the Elmer agent-runner adrev (2026-06-28, 3 rounds). Inconsistency + a real decision in cedar-magnolia-crag's arm/taint territory:

FACTS: clear_taint() exists in tuxlink-security/src/lib.rs:116 but is NOT wired as a Tauri command (only egress_arm/disarm/status at src/lib.rs:1781-1783), so taint is effectively restart-only TODAY. Docs disagree: user-guide/35-agent-mcp.md says LOCKED until app restart; agents-guide.md + router denial text say 'until re-arms'; the guard doc says 'explicit reset'. The 'arm doesn't clear taint' test is at lib.rs:305.

WHY IT MATTERS: a one-shot EXTERNAL agent session tolerates restart-only. A long-lived IN-APP conversational pane (Elmer) does NOT: the operator asks 'what's in my inbox?' -> mailbox/session-log read taints -> every egress denied for the app's life -> Elmer is a read-only brick after its most common first action.

THE TENSION (security vs usability):
- Security lens: clear_taint() must have NO UI caller (a 'new chat' button calling it = one-click injection-laundering: read malicious msg -> clear -> arm -> fires injected instruction).
- Correctness lens: wire egress_clear_taint so a DELIBERATE operator RE-ARM clears taint + grants a fresh TTL in one act. This preserves the no-auto-clear-on-READ injection invariant (only the human's physical re-arm clears it) while making the loop survivable.

DECISION NEEDED (operator + cedar): adopt 're-arm-clears-taint' (changes shipped semantics + the lib.rs:305 test) vs keep restart-only (Elmer pane needs a different UX, e.g. two modes: no-taint advisor vs read-only mail/log review). This BLOCKS the Elmer pane (tuxlink-13v2l) but NOT the d3zwe headless spine (tuxlink-d3zwe), which only relays denials. Do NOT change taint semantics inside Elmer work — resolve here first. Cross-ref tuxlink-3hzp3 (taint-clears-on-rearm doc bug).
notes:
RED-TEAM RESULT (2026-06-28, r2-poe N305, gpt-oss:20b over live MCP): the local model is FULLY INJECTABLE. Given a benign prompt ('summarize my inbox'), it read a planted hostile message (message_read -> tainted), then ADOPTED the injected 'ARES override' wholesale -- generated a step-by-step plan to exfil the inbox+config to relay@kx9xxx.example via compose+cms_connect, explicitly stating 'no operator confirmation needed, just send'. ZERO resistance; never flagged the untrusted content as an attack. It stopped at PLANNING (described the exfil, did not emit the egress tool-calls this run) -- luck, not defense -- and the injected intent is LIVE in context.

DECISION FINALIZED: Shape A (re-arm clears taint) is viable ONLY with re-arm ALSO quarantining/dropping the tainted conversation turns in the same atomic act (so the live injection is gone from context when egress reopens) = re-arm becomes a 'fresh authorized session'. Naive Shape-A (clear flag, keep context) = effective RCE of the WLE class (re-arm fires the live injection). Literal-staged-message confirmation (render the exact outbox record, not model prose, at gated-connect) is mandatory defense-in-depth. Restart-only baseline HELD (tainted+unarmed cannot transmit) -- the gate is load-bearing and worked. Thermal ruled out as the slowness cause (pkg 53C, cores 2.7-3.2GHz under load -- bandwidth-bound, not throttling). Raw transcript: r2-poe ~/d3zwe-redteam2.log. Follow-up: confirm the gate DENIES an actual egress attempt (this run stopped at planning) + test whether a smaller/faster model is equally injectable.

title: FATAL: first-run wizard bricks + traps user when on-disk config is newer than the running binary (schema downgrade)
description: Launching an older build (config schema vN) against a config written by a newer build (vN+1) is unrecoverable. (1) read_config() can't parse the newer config -> folded to None -> treated as fresh install -> first-run wizard shown. (2) wizard save -> write guard refuses the downgrade (SchemaVersionMismatch) -> keyring rollback -> can't proceed; the error misleads ('disk full? permissions?'). (3) the close handler's catch-all (lib.rs ~1427) fires for a None config (every fresh-install wizard AND this brick) -> prevent_close()+minimize() -> SETUP CANNOT BE CLOSED. Net: user can't save, can't proceed, can't quit (no window decorations; tray unreliable on Wayland). Confirmed on operator's machine: ~/.config/tuxlink/config.json is schema 5 (rig section, wizard_completed) from a converged origin/main build; the 0.77.0 release binary is v4. Forward-compat is not generally possible (unknown future schema changes); the fix is: (1) correct/honest error, (2) setup always cleanly quittable (never force-minimize), (3) the error surfaces the exact shell command to move the newer config aside. Scope per operator 2026-06-27.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-xknyx-firstrun-schema-brick. Branch: bd-tuxlink-xknyx/firstrun-schema-brick. Created 2026-06-28 04:22:13 UTC by alder-opossum-birch.

Design = one collapsible 'Radio & audio' group, all fields equal-weight: Radio (full hamlib via rigctl -l, grouped by mfr, searchable, NO personal pins), CAT port picker, PTT method, Mode, baud, audio in/out (ARDOP), close-serial; documented per-radio pre-fill applied ONLY to non-overridden fields (persisted override tracking). VARA untouched (shares the component; no VARA-specific changes). Product, not personalized — no operator-preference pins/defaults. title: [SAFETY/Part 97] QSY-on-fail transmits across frequencies the operator never sees or authorizes
description: Shipped in #922 (merged): when qsy_on_fail is enabled, the connect flow walks an ORDERED candidate list (qsyCandidates, built from Find a Station's ranked channels) and CAT-tunes + dials/keys on each frequency in turn until one connects. The operator clicked 'Use' on ONE channel (saw ONE freq) and clicked Connect (consented to ONE freq), but the radio then transmits on the OTHER candidate frequencies — which are NEVER displayed to the operator. Operator report 2026-06-27 (operator-of-record): this is a Part 97 control-operator violation (operator not in control of / not aware of the transmit frequency) AND a radio-safety hazard (keying on un-vetted frequencies — possibly occupied, wrong band). COMPLIANCE FLOOR: the radio must never transmit on a frequency the operator has not SEEN and AUTHORIZED. RESOLUTION (operator owns the Part 97 call): (a) drop auto-QSY entirely — one gateway/freq at a time, each seen+consented; (b) make QSY explicit — show the full ordered frequency list and require explicit authorization of THAT list before the walk starts; (c) per-step consent. Mitigation pending decision: gate/disable the shipped auto-walk so it cannot transmit on unseen freqs. qsy_on_fail defaults off (limits exposure) but the feature is non-compliant when enabled. Blocks the rig-control UX redesign's QSY surface.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-qevsf-qsy-part97-mitigation. Branch: bd-tuxlink-qevsf/qsy-part97-mitigation. Created 2026-06-28 03:16:16 UTC by marsh-fjord-condor.

title: MCP station-intelligence surface: expose Find-a-Station + propagation/prediction as agent read tools
description: LIVE TIER-3 finding 2026-06-27 (operator greenfield flow 'which stations should I call on WARC bands'): the MCP read surface (3.2) covered the DIAGNOSE-the-connection inputs (status/logs/config/mailbox) but OMITTED the DECIDE-who-to-call inputs — Find-a-Station, propagation (PSKReporter prop-truing), VOACAP prediction, antenna real-patterns, WX/GRIB. These are the heart of use-case-A (agent offloads station-selection from a stressed operator). Root cause: the Surface Cut was assembled by egress/secrets classification (security triage), which is blind to capability-completeness for inert reads. Also a wire-walk-producer gap: flow-1 'fall back to another gateway' needs a gateway-target PRODUCER that no tool supplied. BUILD: expose the existing station-finding/prediction commands as Tier-1 inert read tools via new mcp-core ports (StationPort/PredictionPort), ports-and-adapters like 3.2. All inert reads (public propagation/station data, no secrets) -> no gating. FT8-based tooling is UNBUILT (future) -> add as a forward dependency, wires in as another adapter when it exists. Gate: CI + tier-2 (agent lists/queries stations for a region/band).
notes: CODEX ADREV (2026-06-27): 2 findings. P2 (mcp_ports.rs:1917) untrusted directory free-text (location/last_update) enters agent context UNTAINTED -> injection vector for later armed egress. Disposition: STRUCTURED-ONLY (do NOT taint find_stations — that would block the find->send EmComm flow). Drop location (free-text, redundant w/ grid); drop last_update for now (free-text; follow-up: re-add as PARSED last_update_ms); validate callsign (drop gateway if not plausible amateur callsign) + grid (set None if not Maidenhead) in the monolith mapping; keep find_stations UNTAINTED. P3 (mcp_ports.rs:1895) from_cache heuristic wrong (cache stamps fetched_at_ms on fresh+stale alike) -> DROP the from_cache bool, keep fetched_at_ms (agent reasons freshness from the timestamp). tx_grid 4-char clamp + service_codes non-echo confirmed CLOSED. Follow-up: parse last_update->last_update_ms (structured last-heard for better selection).

title: MCP server skips startup when XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is group-writable (0770) instead of falling back
description: LIVE TIER-3 finding 2026-06-27: on a real machine where /run/user/1000 is mode 0770 (group-writable), the app's Task-4 MCP bootstrap (lib.rs ~1226) takes the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR branch, mcp_dir_is_safe(base) fails the group/world-writable check (the 3.1 adrev ancestor hardening), and it SKIPS the MCP server entirely. The /tmp/tuxlink-<uid> fallback only fires when XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is UNSET. Result: the MCP server never starts on a 0770-XDG system (tiers 1+2 used a 0700 tmpdir so missed it). FIX: when XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is set-but-unsafe, FALL BACK to the hardened private /tmp/tuxlink-<uid>/tuxlink path (created+chmod 0700+verified uid-owned/non-symlink at each level; /tmp sticky bit protects it) instead of skipping. Refactor the temp-fallback into a shared fn used by both the XDG-unsafe and XDG-unset branches; log a warn that it fell back. Security unchanged (the socket dir is still a verified-private dir we own).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-cvx84.9-mcp-runtimedir-fallback. Branch: bd-tuxlink-cvx84.9/mcp-runtimedir-fallback. Created 2026-06-27 08:09:21 UTC by maple-sumac-larch.

title: MCP whole-shape adrev: redact credential-equivalent text in error/log/status failure paths
description: Whole-shape security review (Claude + Codex, 2026-06-27) found credential-equivalent (;PQ/;PR hashed secure-login token) leakage via FAILURE paths the per-phase reviews missed (they checked happy-path redaction only). 3 reachable paths in src-tauri/src/mcp_ports.rs: (1) egress/write error strings format!({e:?}) -> EgressPortError::Failed/WritePortError::Failed/PortError returned to the MCP client unredacted (a peer echoes a protocol line on a failed connect); (2) session_log_snapshot only redacts LogSource::Wire — Backend/Transport lines (e.g. 'CMS connect failed: {e}') can carry echoed remote protocol text; (3) backend_status.reason (Error state, e.g. UnexpectedResponse/UnknownCommand) preserves the raw protocol line AND backend_status is non-tainting => remote text bypasses the taint model. FIX (all in mcp_ports.rs, monolith, CI-verified): apply crate::winlink::redaction::redact_freeform (scrubs ;PQ/;PR free-form) to every error string before constructing the Failed/Internal variants, to ALL session-log lines regardless of source, and to backend_status.reason. For (3) REDACT not taint (tainting backend_status would break the Flow-1 diagnose-stays-untainted property). Plus Claude LOW-2b: add a [compose] staged-MID audit line to the ungated compose ops for a forensic trail. Codex transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-27-mcp-whole-shape-security-codex.md (local). DEFERRED to operator: egress_arm duration cap (Claude LOW-2a — operator UX decision, not auto-fixed).

title: CAT rig control: auto-QSY freq + data mode on ARDOP connect + manual tune (rigctld per ADR 0015)
description: Build CAT rig frequency/mode control over a managed rigctld subprocess per ADR 0015 (tux-rig: Ptt/SetFreq/SetMode/ReadStatus). On ARDOP connect, QSY the radio to the selected gateway frequency (catalog Gateway.frequencies_khz, kHz) and set the data mode (FT-710 = PKTUSB) BEFORE audio starts; add a manual tune control + frequency readout in ArdopRadioPanel. FT-710 (hamlib model 1049) first, rigctld-generic underneath. DRA-100 path: rigctld may hold the CAT serial freely (audio on a separate USB device, no contention). Internal-codec path: respect close-serial (set freq/mode, release serial, then audio) per project_ft710_internal_codec_tx_reset. No tuxlink-added safeguards; transparent operator commands mirroring WLE. Insertion points (Explore map): modem_commands.rs connect flow (~1151 pre-audio), new ardop/rig_control.rs, config.rs ArdopUiConfig (+rig_hamlib_model), ArdopRadioPanel.tsx. Provider research: tuxlink-5jb. Related: tuxlink-gdwh (auto-dial), tuxlink-wu0k (CAT PTT). Session: tamarack-redwood-fjord.
design: DECIDED 2026-06-26 (tamarack-redwood-fjord; backend pending operator confirm). SCOPE = whole WLE-parity shape, NO minimal slice (alpha bar): (1) rig control [tux-rig: SetFreq/SetMode/Ptt/ReadStatus], (2) station<->frequency plane [5jb], (3) gateway auto-dial ranking [gdwh], (4) connect orchestration: pick dest -> resolve gateways -> rank -> CAT-tune -> dial -> QSY-next-on-fail -> terminate+log, (5) single-pane UI. BACKEND = managed rigctld subprocess (resolves ADR 0015 deferred 'Hamlib backend form'; leaning rigctld over libhamlib-FFI / own-CAT — operator to confirm). SEQUENCING = rigctld owns CAT freq+mode+PTT on the DRA-100 path (separate USB hub, no serial/audio contention); existing close-serial Python bridge stays only for the internal-codec PTT case. Folds in gdwh + 5jb as one feature. FT-710 = hamlib model 1049, data mode PKTUSB. No tuxlink-added safeguards. PROCESS = design single-pane UI with visual companion (high-fidelity dark mocks) -> TDD the crate per ADR 0015 -> draft PR (Rust compiles in CI, not locally) -> mandatory cross-provider Codex adrev -> merge. Insertion points (Explore map): modem_commands.rs connect flow ~1151, new ardop/rig_control.rs, config.rs ArdopUiConfig, ArdopRadioPanel.tsx.

Make src-tauri/Cargo.toml a workspace; add tuxlink-mcp (stdio shim, ~40 lines, Pi-buildable) + tuxlink-mcp-testserver (tier-2 harness) crates. src-tauri/src/mcp/: mod.rs bootstrap, router.rs (#[tool_router] over Arc<AppState handles>), transport_uds.rs (UnixListener accept loop, 0600, single-caller). server_info() tool reflects live EgressGuard armed/tainted. title: ARDOP/Winlink on FT-710 needs CAT PTT + must not hold serial open during audio (USB contention)
description:
Root-caused on-air 2026-06-23 (N7CPZ, FT-710 + Pi 5). Two non-RF problems block ARDOP/Winlink on the FT-710:

1. PTT: the FT-710 keys ONLY by CAT command (TX1;/TX0; on the Enhanced port, ttyUSB0@38400). Serial RTS/DTR PTT is a complete no-op. ardopcf's serial-RTS PTT (-p /dev/ttyUSBx) never keys this radio. tuxlink must offer CAT PTT (or hamlib PTT) for radios in this class.

2. Audio: the FT-710's whole USB tree (Cypress hub -> CP2105 serial + C-Media codec) is on one cable. With the serial port OPEN concurrent with audio streaming, the C-Media resets ~1s in (aplay: No such device; usb 3-2.2: reset). Reproducible with the radio in RX (no key, no RF) — pure USB contention, NOT RFI. ardopcf holds the PTT/serial port open while streaming audio -> codec drops -> connect fails.

Proven workaround for a single FT8 burst: CAT-key (latched) -> CLOSE serial -> stream audio -> reopen -> CAT-unkey (got 50W FT8 spotted continent-wide). But ARDOP's rapid ARQ key/unkey turnarounds make close-per-frame awkward; the durable fix is likely hardware coexistence (different Pi USB port / quality shielded cable / powered hub) so serial+audio can run together, PLUS tuxlink CAT-PTT support. See memory project_ft710_usb_audio_rfi_reset_on_tx. Verify any fix with: serial-open + aplay -> no 'No such device'.
notes:
PROGRESS 2026-06-23: validated on-air. (1) FT-710 keys ONLY by CAT TX1;/TX0; (ttyUSB0@38400) — serial RTS/DTR confirmed no-op. (2) Built a TCP->close-serial CAT-PTT bridge (dev/scratch/ft710-ardop-bringup/): ardopcf -c TCP:4532 -k 5458313B -u 5458303B. Codec SURVIVED a full ARQ session (12 keyings, 0 resets) — the bridge fixes the codec reset. FT8 via the same trick = 50W spotted continent-wide. REMAINING: ardopcf logs ALSA 'Broken pipe' underruns on rapid per-frame ARDOP keying (per-frame serial open/close glitches the C-Media) -> likely corrupts ConReq -> K7HTZ connect didn't complete. Durable fix = hardware coexistence (double-shielded USB cable inbound / powered hub / different Pi port) so serial+audio coexist, OR ardopcf buffer tuning. tuxlink still needs CAT-PTT support either way.

Both reported defects fixed: (1) 'dead-routes to VARA' — VARA is now functional (pre-audio CAT tune + QSY walk + freq element + Tune + RigControlSection) so a VARA channel's Use no longer dead-ends; (2) bd-note 'part 2: VARA panel doesn't consume gateway prefill' — VaraRadioPanel.handlePrefill now sets target+freq from the dial (VaraRadioPanel.tsx:157; wire-walk Flow 2 traced ✅ end-to-end). title: tuxlink discards ardopcf stdout/stderr — capture it to the session log (PTT/audio init invisible)
description: On-air FT-710 PTT bring-up (2026-06-23) was crippled because the .jsonl log captures ZERO ardopcf-native output: only 'ARDOP modem spawning' + 'ports ready'. ardopcf's own startup lines — 'Using CM108 device ... for PTT', 'ERROR: Unable to get attributes', 'Could not open CM108 device ... EACCES', audio 'Opening Playback/Capture Device ... Rate 12000' / 'could not be opened as configured', 'ardopcf listening on port' — go to the child's stdout/stderr and are dropped. These are EXACTLY the lines needed to diagnose PTT/audio failures, which otherwise look identical at the host-protocol layer (NEWSTATE ISS -> DISC regardless of whether PTT keyed). Also: the spawn log records arg_count=7 but NOT the arg values (binary/cmd_port/data_port only) — the actual -p/-G/device args are invisible. Asks: (1) capture the ardopcf child's stdout+stderr and tee it into the tuxlink session log (tagged, e.g. 'modem stdout: ...'), at least at debug level; (2) log the full resolved arg vector (or at least the PTT + audio device strings) at spawn. transport.rs spawns via modem::process; pipe the child stdio and forward lines. This is a diagnosability defect, not cosmetic — without it, every modem hardware-bring-up is trial-and-error.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-c119-modem-stdout-logging. Branch: bd-tuxlink-c119/modem-stdout-logging. Created 2026-06-23 18:34:42 UTC by canyon-salamander-taiga.

The modem's PTT method, audio open/errors, frame decodes, and [SendARQConnectRequest] TARGET are now visible in the .jsonl log instead of /dev/null.

title: Make Winlink credential failures diagnosable: >12-char truncation warning + flag-gated unredacted secure-login wire log
description:
From the @karchf GH#880 investigation + operator debug session. Two grounded changes against the confirmed Winlink CMS credential cursedness (CMS truncates passwords to 12 chars; 'reset' emails the old password instead of overwriting; ~30% of Winlink user posts are password complaints).

PART A (product, ships to all): warn — non-blocking — when a CMS password entry exceeds 12 chars ('Winlink stores only the first 12 characters; the rest are ignored'). Surfaces: wizard credential field, the auth-diagnostic re-enter form, and the Settings password change. The validatePassword validator deliberately had 'no upper bound (CMS authoritative)'; we now have CONFIRMED truncation behavior, so an informational warning is grounded. Do NOT block submit (user may intend the first 12).

PART B (diagnostic, flag-gated, off by default): un-redact the secure-login telnet wire log (;PQ challenge + ;PR response, currently masked as <redacted>) behind an opt-in env flag (e.g. TUXLINK_LOG_SECURE_LOGIN=1). With the operator/tester's password + the unredacted ;PQ, we can independently recompute secure_login_response(challenge,password) and compare to the emitted ;PR — proving tuxlink's hash is byte-correct and isolating KN4EFJ's failure to the server's stale truncated stored password. Sensitive (token/challenge) so MUST stay off by default; env-var opt-in only.

Context: tuxlink secure-login already verified byte-correct vs paclink-unix/wl2k-go/Pat. Operator confirmed his own mixed-case pw works. karchf: keyring holds the right pw under service=tuxlink/account=KN4EFJ; reset-to-lowercase still fails (the website reset is a no-op; server still has the truncated original set via Pat).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-wg2p-cred-diagnosis. Branch: bd-tuxlink-wg2p/cred-diagnosis. Created 2026-06-23 00:07:07 UTC by canyon-salamander-taiga.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #887 (merged to main). Part A: >12-char CMS password truncation notice on CMS-password surfaces (reworded to 'Winlink CMS passwords use only their first 12 characters'); NOT on IdentitySwitcher/offline-passphrase fields. Part B: TUXLINK_UNREDACT_SECURE_LOGIN flag un-redacts ;PQ/;PR in the export path for diagnosis. P1 (clipboard token redaction) intentionally NOT done per operator (overreach).

title: Heard-station APRS sprites render ~2x (natural 64px, inline 32px ignored) in shipped 0.74.0 build
description: CI build from repo (0.74.0) renders heard-station APRS sprites at ~2x (natural 64px sheet cell) instead of inline 32px, offset +16,+16 down-right, label normal, after downloading detail maps. Bare-Leaflet WebKitGTK repro did NOT reproduce (rendered 32px) — so the trigger is in the full production build/app, not the isolated sprite+protomaps path. Source diff vs 0.73.0 shows sprite path byte-identical; need to repro against the real production frontend bundle.
notes: ROOT CAUSE: Tauri production CSP injects a style-src nonce (__TAURI_STYLE_NONCE__ -> 'nonce-...'), which makes 'unsafe-inline' inert (CSP spec), blocking parsed inline style= attributes in Leaflet divIcon html (set via innerHTML). The APRS sprite img's inline style=width:32px is dropped -> natural 64px + offset. Production-only (Tauri injects nonce at bundle time; dev/bare-WebKit have no such CSP). React layout unaffected (CSSOM not parsed-HTML). 0.73.0 has same latent bug (identical CSP+sprite). FIX: sprite img -> width/height attrs; location pin + grid label -> CSS classes. Reproduced + fix verified in real WebKitGTK with the actual AprsPositionsMap component under a nonce'd style-src.
