title: LocationMap/GridPicker Leaflet grid layer freezes app: O(N) DOM label markers explode on zoom-out
description: Regression from the Leaflet map migration (tuxlink-4hol, #858). LeafletMaidenheadGridLayer.draw() renders one DOM L.marker (divIcon) per Maidenhead cell label; label count = lonCells x latCells. At Square level (zoom 3-8) zooming out widens the span while the 2deg step stays fine, and padBounds() doubles the span without clamping longitude (low-zoom Leaflet getBounds returns >360deg phantom world width). Confirmed counts from real origin/main gridLines(): z6=1860, z5=10465, z4=27900, z3=57600, z3-fullwidth=136800 DOM markers. Rendering 10k-130k DOM nodes synchronously on the Pi software-GL WebKitGTK = hard freeze + dense-label artifacts. MapLibre original used a single GPU symbol layer (count free); the 1:1 port inverted the cost model to O(N) DOM. Fix (operator-approved): clamp grid generation to the real world (lon +/-180, lat +/-90), cap label count, guard gridLines against non-finite bounds (latent infinite-loop). Frontend-only; gate = typecheck+vitest+build.

title: Config: deny_unknown_fields + additive field w/o schema bump → older binary rejects config & can CLOBBER it (data loss)
description: Observed: 0.73.0 logs 'config deserialize failed: unknown field trash_auto_purge, expected one of ...'. ROOT CAUSE (verified in code): Config uses #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] (config.rs:181, the AMD-11 drift defense). trash_auto_purge was added to the struct (config.rs:267) WITHOUT bumping CONFIG_SCHEMA_VERSION (still 2 on BOTH v0.73.0 and main). So: a newer build (main, has the field) writes trash_auto_purge into config.json at schema_version=2; an OLDER binary (0.73.0, lacks the field) reads it → deserialize_schema_version passes (2==2) → deny_unknown_fields REJECTS the unknown field → read_config() returns Err. Consumers fold the Err via .ok()→None and fall through to a FRESH WIZARD / first-run (config.rs:642-644). DATA-LOSS PATH: on the next write, write_config_atomic's guard probes ONLY schema_version via SchemaVersionProbe (config.rs:708,739) — it does NOT do a full parse — sees 2 = Current → does NOT refuse → atomically OVERWRITES the real config with defaults. Identity/connect/modems/APRS/favorites lost. The schema-version downgrade guard is DEFEATED because the field was added without a version bump. This is a STANDING LANDMINE: every additive field added under deny_unknown_fields without a schema bump reproduces it; will bite stable users the moment 0.74.0 ships trash and anyone downgrades to 0.73.0. FIX OPTIONS: (PRIMARY, data-safety, low-risk) make write_config_atomic refuse to overwrite an existing config this binary cannot FULLY deserialize (try a full Config parse of the existing file; on failure preserve it + surface a clear error) — closes the clobber hole for unknown-field/newer/corrupt alike. (SECONDARY, forward-compat UX, architecture decision) either (A) drop deny_unknown_fields so older binaries gracefully IGNORE unknown fields (replace the AMD-11 drift defense with a CI/golden-schema test) — eliminates the re-wizard entirely; or (B) enforce 'every additive field bumps CONFIG_SCHEMA_VERSION + a migration entry' so the existing write-guard machinery works as designed. Coordinate with the active trash work (bd-tuxlink-wl7n) which owns trash_auto_purge, and the AMD-11 config owner. Operator's data currently appears intact (visible configs have identity + no orphan trash field).
notes: PR #859 (moss-bog-cove): schema v3 bump + MigrateAdditive forward-migration (existing v2 configs self-heal, no re-wizard) + write_config_atomic full-parse refusal (ExistingConfigUnreadable) + discipline test locking the always-serialized field set to the schema version. Keeps deny_unknown_fields per operator steer. Awaiting CI (Rust compiled/tested on CI, not locally).

title: Region-pack download 404s: resolve current Protomaps build at download time
description: Pack downloads carve the region out of build.protomaps.com/<build>.pmtiles. The build id comes from region-manifest.json (planet_build), pinned to a single dated build. Protomaps keeps only a ~6-day rolling window and offers no 'latest' alias, so the pin 404s within a week (observed 2026-06-20: pinned 20260614 -> 404; 20260615..20260620 -> 206). The pre-download refresh fetches the manifest from the repo's own main, so it refreshes to the same stale pin. Operator decision (2026-06-20): resolve the current build dynamically at download time -- probe build.protomaps.com/<YYYYMMDD>.pmtiles from today back N days, use the first available, staying within the existing validate_planet_url allowlist (host build.protomaps.com, https, no creds/port; dates are self-generated, not attacker input). Validation already enforces the 9 required vector_layers, so any current daily build is schema-safe. Blocks the tuxlink-c973 placenames smoke (can't install a pack to see placenames until download works).
close_reason: Closed

title: Message Delete + Trash folder (the missing 'D' in CRUD)
description: Users reported there is no way to delete a message from any folder. Add Delete across all folders with a recoverable Trash. APPROVED DESIGN (2026-06-20 brainstorm): Delete = move to a new Deleted system folder (recoverable, no confirm, like Archive). Restore = move back to origin (tracked via a {mid}.trash sidecar holding {origin_folder, deleted_at}; missing -> Inbox). Empty Trash + per-item permanent delete = existing permanent-delete plumbing (file unlink + index_delete), confirm required. Auto-purge Trash items older than N=30 days, ON by default, configurable in Settings, runs on init + periodic sweep. Archive stays the 'keep long-term' bucket (distinct from Trash). Per-folder: delete from every folder -> Trash; Outbox delete -> Trash, Restore re-queues to Outbox, but block delete while actively transmitting; inside Trash the actions are Restore + Delete-permanently. Backend: add Deleted to Rust MailboxFolder enum (winlink_backend.rs:34) + as_str 'deleted'; new commands message_delete(_bulk), message_restore(_bulk), trash_empty, trash_purge_one (mirror existing move/bulk shapes in ui_commands.rs). Frontend: Delete in MessageContextMenu/MessageView(Del key)/MessageBulkBar; enable Deleted folder in FolderSidebar + Empty Trash; reuse DeleteFolderDialog confirm for permanent actions only. Docs: update docs/user-guide/07-mailbox-model.md. Spec: docs/design/2026-06-20-message-delete-trash-design.md.

title: Evaluate map-engine alternatives to MapLibre for the edge-compute APRS situational display
description: STRATEGIC (operator 2026-06-20). MapLibre repeatedly fights this target: it is a GPU vector engine running on a CPU rasterizer (llvmpipe). Symptoms across the project: the maxBounds crash (rwo6), continuous perf gymnastics (vnk7/gq0d, fadeDuration:0, feature-state-not-FC-rebuild), the cn84 animation CPU peg, the preserveDrawingBuffer tile-pipeline strangle, and styled-chip overlays being awkward (the ni5b WX badge shipped as plain GL text, not the approved chip). The APRS map is NOT a general slippy map — it is a LOCAL-AREA situational display (basemap + pins + a few honest overlays + simple fade lines). Evaluate alternatives as a deliberate architecture decision (office-hours -> plan-eng-review -> decision doc): (1) Leaflet (DOM/canvas, no WebGL, gentler on llvmpipe; the project USED react-leaflet before ndi4 — establish WHY it was dropped and whether that reason still outweighs the Pi pain); (2) a purpose-built 2D <canvas> situational renderer scoped to just this view (basemap image + pins + fade-able path lines + styled chips — trivial in 2D canvas, awkward in GL). Cost spans ALL the maps (StationFinderMap, AprsPositionsMap), offline tiles, migration. This decision GATES the WX-display fidelity rebuild — do not rebuild map UI in MapLibre if we're switching. NOT a from-scratch general map engine (sinkhole). Agent: mink-yew-osprey.

title: Security: audit tuxlink receiving-end forms/attachment handling (Winlink XSS/RCE class)
description: From the 2026-06-19 RMS Relay/Trimode + Pat/WLE security review. tuxlink is a client/endpoint so it cannot be a propagation amplifier, but it CAN be a victim of received-message payloads: malicious Winlink HTML Forms (the Pat F2 stored-XSS class) and mishandled attachments (the WLE F1 ShellExecute-by-association RCE class). Post-RCE a client becomes a spreader, so this is the real residual exposure. AUDIT: (1) HTML-forms rendering path in the WebKit/Tauri context (CSP, dangerouslySetInnerHTML ban already present — verify completeness vs the Pat sinks: header fields, attachment filenames, list views); (2) attachment open/save handling — never OS-default-launch untrusted attachments by association. Reference: library-of-hamexandria/winlink-re/findings/2026-06-19-rms-relay-trimode-security-review.md and the Pat patches in winlink-re/findings/disclosure/.
notes: CI GREEN on PR #927 (verify+build-linux+deb-install, both arches). Marked READY. Adversarial round = the two-pass audit (recon + refute). Pending operator merge (no-squash, ADR 0010).

Operator-approved design: restore resolveDigipeatPath (honest hybrid path — solid through located hops, dashed pos? across unknown), now fed real multi-hop via-chains. RENDER WITH FADE, not the per-frame rAF draw-in: each path geometry uploaded ONCE, appear via maplibre line-opacity-transition (~0.4s in / dwell / ~0.6s out — a bounded paint transition that stops, no JS loop, no re-tessellation, no preserveDrawingBuffer). The map is the one subsystem flying blind. Fix: wire map.on('error') (+ relevant data events) in MapLibreMap.tsx (and any standalone maplibregl.Map instance, e.g. AprsPositionsMap) to reportFrontendError('maplibre', ...), deduped/throttled so a 404 storm doesn't flood the log, capturing message + sourceId + tile/url context. title: [bug] AX.25 decoder drops frames with 3+ digipeaters (caps path at 2) — silently discards all long-path stations
description: ROOT CAUSE of tuxlink-vnm5 (weather stations missing) + broader. Path::decode in src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/frame.rs caps the address field at dest+src+2 digis: 'if addrs.len() >= 4 { return Err(BadAddressLength) }'. Any frame with a 3rd digipeater is rejected as undecodable and the ENTIRE packet (position/weather/telemetry/messages) is dropped before APRS parsing. AX.25 v2.2 allows up to 8 digipeaters (10 address fields total). PROVEN from operator raw capture (dev/scratch/aprs-raw.txt via TUXLINK_APRS_RAW_CAPTURE): every '[undecodable N bytes]' frame has exactly 3 digis and includes full weather reports from mountaintop NPS stations heard via a fill-in digi (MINGUS/UNION/GREENS/W7MOT-10) -> W7MOT-3 -> WIDE2: KG7KID-7 (_223/001g018t097...), WX7FGZ-6 SnowBowl (_188/012g023t063...), KG7KID-5 NPS_Tuzigoot, KB7KFC third-party telemetry, KJ7IKK-9 Mic-E. The 2-digi versions decode fine; the weather PARSER is correct. FIX: raise the decode cap from 4 to 10 address fields (dest+src+8 digis); raise Path::encode's >2 guard to >8; update 'digis: Vec<Address> // 0..=2' to 0..=8; ensure decode_digi_hbits + the engine via-chain/hbits handle up to 8. Regression test: decode the real 3-digi KG7KID-7 weather frame + an 8-digi frame. Affects ALL packet types, not just weather. RX-only, no TX — no RADIO-1 concern.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-j5cj-ax25-digi-path. Branch: bd-tuxlink-j5cj/ax25-digi-path. Created 2026-06-19 21:28:44 UTC by plover-granite-vale.
close_reason: Closed

title: cn84 follow-up: animated digipeat path janky on software-GL + dwell override with concurrent traces
description: Operator grim-smoke of the merged cn84 animated digipeat path (PR #829) found two defects, both rooted in the animation architecture. (1) JANK: the rAF loop rebuilds + re-tessellates 3 GeoJSON sources via setData every frame; on the llvmpipe software-GL target this per-frame cost (the exact thing tuxlink-vnk7/gq0d engineered away via feature-state) drops frames so the draw-in renders in chunks. (2) DWELL OVERRIDE: DigipeatPathLayer keeps a SINGLE liveRef + cancelAnimationFrame on each new trace, so a station heard in quick succession clobbers the prior trace's timeline; dwell times must be independent. Operator-approved fix (option A, preserves the aprs.fi feel): support N concurrent traces (each its own startMs/timeline) rendered via a per-trace LINE-LAYER POOL — draw-in via line-trim-offset (a paint property; lineMetrics:true source; no re-tessellation), independent fade via line-opacity paint, per-trace packet dot via a cheap 1-point source. line-trim-offset + lineMetrics confirmed present in maplibre 5.24.0. Verify via operator grim smoke (software-GL, agent cannot run).
notes: Superseded by tuxlink-k0zz (fade-rendering rework on the solid multi-hop foundation). The line-trim/jank/dwell framing here is obsolete — the real CPU cause was preserveDrawingBuffer (operator's parallel agent fixed). Closing in favor of k0zz.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] APRS weather: stations heard but not in Station Data (parse/emit gap) + raw RX frames not observable
description: Operator 2026-06-19: ~7 weather stations heard but only 3 in Station Data/Telemetry history; KA7WSB-2 (an NPS weather asset, heard regularly) is among the missing. CONFIRMED it's RX PARSING, not broadcasting: useEnvStations listens to BOTH aprs-weather:new + aprs-telemetry:new keyed by callsign, so weather stations DO belong in the panel; the backend just isn't emitting aprs-weather:new for them. engine.rs emits weather on only two paths: (1) positionless DTI '_' via parse_positionless_weather; (2) a position whose symbol code is '_' (is_weather_symbol) AND whose comment parses via parse_weather_data, which REQUIRES the comment to begin exactly at the wind group ddd/sss. Likely misses: compressed weather reports, layouts with course/speed/altitude/data-extension between symbol and wind group, or stations not using the '_' symbol. SECOND GAP: the UI shows only 'weather report' with no raw-packet access, so neither operator nor agent can see the bytes -> can't diagnose. PLAN: (step 1, diagnostic) instrument the APRS RX path to log each inbound frame's sender + raw info bytes + decode classification + weather parse outcome (target tuxlink::aprs::rx), so KA7WSB-2's exact format shows in the jsonl and WX-decode gaps become log-diagnosable (mirrors xyi7). (step 2) from the logged bytes, extend parse coverage to whatever KA7WSB-2 (and the other missing stations) actually send. Frontend later: surface raw packet in the UI (show-raw for weather rows).
notes:
DESIGN (operator-approved 2026-06-20): A) range-validate WX fields in weather.rs (wind dir 0-360, temp ~-60..140F, pressure ~870-1085hPa, humidity 0-100, wind/gust <=199); out-of-range -> not shown as a real reading BUT the raw WX run is PRESERVED (operator: 'data must still be transparently available, not thrown out'). B) engine emits weather for EVERY heard weather-symbol station so they roster in Station Data with an explicit honest state: readings / sensors-offline (had a WX run but no valid fields, e.g. WX7FGZ-2 _767/255g255t200..b00000) / position-only (name beacon, e.g. KA7WSB-2 _NPS_003_Chiminea). Frontend EnvStationCard renders the honest state + a raw view (subsumes vnm5 raw-visibility). Verified in code: parse_weather_data has NO range validation today (parse_u16_field just parses digits) so impossible values render as real (RF-honesty violation); #832 confirmed live in operator build (commit 2044434e descends from merge 06e8d811).

title: APRS map: orange arterial 'spaghetti' persists — extend road-density generalization to major arterials (roads_major), keep highways
description: Follow-up to tuxlink-hzwc #8 (PR #816). That fix gated minor/residential/service/link roads to minzoom 13, but those classes are yellow/white. The operator still sees ORANGE spaghetti. Root cause (fen-savanna-grouse 2026-06-19): roads_major arterials are colored #f2933a (orange) by tuxlinkFlavor and were DELIBERATELY left ungated (minzoom=-) by the #8 fix ('arterials give context'). In dense metros the orange arterial grid IS the spaghetti. Operator rejected desaturation ('same spaghetti, less color') — the lever must be DENSITY. Fix: extend generalizeRoadDensity in src/map/basemapStyle.ts to also gate roads_major (+ its casings, tunnels, bridges) to a tunable MAJOR_ROAD_MINZOOM floor (default ~11) so mid/low zoom shows only sparse highways; arterials fill in when zooming into a city. Highways (roads_highway) stay ungated for context. Unit-testable (pure id→minzoom mapping). Floor is tunable — operator smoke on converged build to confirm level.

title: [bug] APRS connect: in-flight 'connecting' state not shared between status-bar control and dock connect-strip
description: Operator 2026-06-19 (testing a1j3 on converged build): clicking the status-bar APRS control starts a connection, but opening the dock (Ctrl+Shift+M -> APRS Chat tab) during the in-flight window shows 'Disconnected/Connect' in the AprsConnectStrip. Once the backend reports listening, both surfaces sync correctly -- so it's ONLY the in-flight connect that desyncs. ROOT CAUSE: listening is shared backend truth (aprs-listening:change event) but the in-flight 'connecting' state is per-surface. AprsConnectStrip derives its displayed state from a LOCAL connecting flag (src/aprs/AprsConnectStrip.tsx: state = connecting ? 'connecting' : listening ? 'listening' : 'off') set only by its own handleConnect; the status-bar path (AppShell onToggleAprsListening) sets a separate aprsToggling flag. A connect initiated from the status bar never flips the strip's connecting. Exposed (not caused) by a1j3 making the status-bar control the primary connect path without auto-opening the dock. FIX: lift connect-in-flight to AppShell (a shared aprsConnecting flag set around onAprsConnect for BOTH surfaces); pass it to the strip as a 'pending' prop (state shows Connecting when connecting||pending) and to the DashboardRibbon as toggleBusy; route the strip's onConnect through the shared wrapper so a strip-initiated connect also shows in the ribbon. Likely part of the tuxlink-9grg family. Frontend only; TDD.

title: [bug] P1: APRS connect/listen path is uninstrumented — 'can't connect' + listening desync invisible to logs
description: Operator 2026-06-19: APRS sometimes reports 'can't connect' when it is connected, and the tab-switch listening desync (tuxlink-9grg) can't be pinned because the APRS connect/listen subsystem writes NOTHING to the structured log. Confirmed by reading fresh repro logs (tuxlink.2026-06-19-16.jsonl): connect attempts produced zero lines. aprs_listen_start (ui_commands.rs:3965) returns UiError on failure (no active identity / misconfigured link / transport error / engine already-listening) straight to the frontend (shown inline in AprsConnectStrip) but never tracing-logs the reason; aprs_listen_stop and the aprs-listening:change emit (engine.rs:907) are also silent. FIX: add tracing on aprs_listen_start entry (resolved transport+linkKind, base call), success, and each error path (warn/error with reason); aprs_listen_stop; and log the on/off value at the aprs-listening:change emit. This is the operator's P0 observability ask for this subsystem and the diagnostic enabler for tuxlink-9grg. Backend-only; CI-gated; must reach the converged build (origin/main) for operator repro to be diagnostic.

title: Offline-map download progress/liveness broken: go-pmtiles pre-sizes output so file-size polling never reflects progress; 8g28 stall-watchdog kills long extracts
description: Root cause (confirmed empirically + in code) of 'detailed map download stalls and never completes', independent of maxzoom (8g28 maxzoom wiring was orthogonal/symptomatic). go-pmtiles extract pre-allocates the output file to its FINAL size within ~2s (computes the full tile directory before downloading tile data), then fills it in place over the real multi-minute download. The app polls output-file size (commands.rs:221 metadata(dest).len()) as the SOLE progress + liveness signal, so: (1) progress bar reads ~full/finishing from t+2s, speed/ETA never populate, operator perceives a stall; (2) the 8g28 stall-watchdog (commands.rs:246, kill after 600s of no file growth) sees size constant from t+2s and KILLS any extract >10min (z13 ~9GB, z14 ~17GB, slow links) — a regression. Every zoom actually completes (dry-run exit 0; z14=17GB ~12min @24MB/s matches the installed 17.4GB pack). Fix: parse go-pmtiles stderr progress line ('fetching chunks NN% | (X/Y, RATE) [elapsed:eta]', survives piping — 738 lines captured in non-tty redirect) for real bytes; rebase liveness on progress-line recency not file growth; remove file-size polling + the file-growth watchdog. Backend-only (SidecarExtractor); frontend (panel + status-bar useActiveDownload) already consumes bytes/total.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-k9pg-download-progress-parse. Branch: bd-tuxlink-k9pg/download-progress-parse. Created 2026-06-19 01:29:41 UTC by osprey-taiga-hawk.

title: CI: cache apt system-deps to stop verify/build-linux mirror-slowness timeouts
description: verify (and build-linux) re-download ~100 apt packages incl. 25MB libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev every run, uncached, from Azure's Ubuntu mirror. On a slow-mirror day the download alone eats 25-40min and blows verify's 45min timeout before clippy/test start (observed PR #816, verify amd64 cancelled at 45m16s mid-apt; arm64 hit a faster mirror, 13m). Fix (operator-chosen 2026-06-18): replace raw apt-get with awalsh128/cache-apt-pkgs-action (tag-pinned, repo convention) in verify + build-linux; add Acquire::Retries=3 for miss-path resilience; modest timeout headroom bump. Applies to ci.yml; release.yml/ect-build.yml install the same stack (fast-follow candidate).

Wire it: add push:tags['v*'] trigger + a publish-etc job (needs build-etc + etc-install-test; contents: write) that softprops-uploads the *_etc.deb to the same release (upsert, race-free with release.yml). title: Window controls + Connect/ribbon controls clipped at narrow (non-maximized) desktop widths
description: Alpha-tester report: when the Tauri window is not maximized (below the ~1280-1365px the layout naturally needs), the titlebar window controls (min/max/close) and the ribbon Connect/Stop buttons are clipped past the right window edge with no way to reach them. ROOT CAUSE: the responsive 'compact' layout is gated on '(max-width:1365px) and (any-pointer:coarse)' (useViewport.ts COMPACT_MEDIA_QUERY + compactShell.css @media). any-pointer:coarse only matches touch devices (built for the FZ-M1 touchscreen), so on a mouse desktop compact never activates; the full fixed-width layout overflows a narrow window and overflow:hidden clips the right side (documented at compactShell.css:290 re the Connect button). FIX (operator-approved 2026-06-18, mock dev/scratch/narrow-degrade-mock.png): keep the DESKTOP control nature (NOT switch to the touch compact mode). Pin titlebar controls + ribbon Connect to the window right (never clip; middle ribbon stats give way). Panes degrade by column-shrink (minmax) by default, falling back to dock-overlays-reader when shrink alone can't seat a dock without crushing the reader. Verify both invariants on a real render before done. Iterate if further alpha feedback objects.
notes: REAL FIX in PR #822 (branch bd-tuxlink-be2q/narrow-window-vw-fix, off main). Root cause (found by grimming the live half-tiled window): .layout-b used width:100vw/height:100vh -> under WebKitGTK these = SCREEN size not WINDOW size, so a half-tiled/non-maximized window rendered the shell screen-wide and the right side (Connect/Stop + titlebar window controls) fell off the window edge. Fix: 100vw/100vh -> 100% (tracks #root = the window) + min-width:0/max-width:100%. SUPERSEDES #812 (merged but mis-diagnosed as in-shell content overflow; its minmax columns + search-zone shrink remain as genuine graceful-narrowing, compose cleanly). STATUS: #822 CI running, AppShell guards green locally; AWAITING operator narrow/half-tiled smoke before merge. Keep be2q OPEN until the smoke confirms.

title: APRS channel UX bugs: map symbols, packet decoding, compose target dup, station-data expand
description:
Four related APRS Tac Chat UX defects reported by operator 2026-06-18:
1. APRS symbols wired but only show in a tiny click tooltip, not rendered on the map (intent: per-pin map symbols). Related: tuxlink-90xb.
2. Captured APRS packet text renders raw/gibberish in the message pane (compressed/MIC-E/uncompressed position, status, telemetry, WX). Mature clients (APRSIS-32) decode to human-readable; we must too.
3. Compose pane: clicking a station in the scrolling feed BOTH switches the target from 'all' to that callsign AND writes the callsign into the message body (duplicative). Should be one or the other.
4. Station-data expand button does nothing — expanded pane always says 'no station data / stations not heard yet' despite data in the scrolling feed.
notes: 10/11 fixed on branch bd-tuxlink-hzwc/aprs-channel-ux (off origin/main): #2 packet decode (+backend MsgKind discriminator), #3 compose target chip, #4 station-data pop-out snapshot seed, #5 tab order, #6 Ctrl+Shift+M, #7 map scale, #8 minor-road minzoom generalization, #9 pop-out chrome, #10 dock body flex, #11 read-aware unread. DEFERRED: #1 authentic APRS symbol sprites on map pins -> tuxlink-90xb (needs vendored sprite asset + addImage pipeline + public-repo licensing nod + converged-build smoke). Map fixes (#7/#8) + sprite render are smoke-gated (no WebKitGTK map render in headless env).

title: [bug] Offline maps: detail tier (Local/Regional/Wide) NOT wired to continent download — always extracts full continent @ maxzoom=14; stuck 'already in progress'
description: Observed on latest (2026-06-18): 'downloading detailed maps still doesn't work'. Root cause (confirmed): DownloadArgs::Continent (src/map/offlineMaps.ts ~74) carries only continent_id — the Local~17MB/Regional~200MB/Wide~1GB detail tier the operator selects is NOT passed to the backend continent path. build_request (src-tauri/src/basemap/commands.rs ~653) uses the full continent bbox + constant PACK_MAXZOOM=14, so clicking Download on a continent ALWAYS extracts the full continent at full detail (observed: pmtiles extract --maxzoom=14 --bbox=-170,5,-50,84 → .part growing past 17GB). Secondary: the in-flight guard (download_cancels map, commands.rs ~462) never clears while that orphaned/hung pmtiles process runs, so Retry bounces with 'a download for continent-na is already in progress'. 3 prior bug-hunt docs (dev/bug-hunts/2026-06-15-region-pack-download-*) fixed peripheral issues but missed this core wiring gap. PRODUCT DECISION NEEDED: wire detail tiers to the continent download (bbox/maxzoom per tier), or simplify to location-anchored tiers + honest continent sizing. Plus: clear the stale in-flight guard on orphaned process / make resumable, and surface the running download honestly.
notes: Fix implemented in PR #814 (draft, branch bd-tuxlink-8g28/continent-detail-tier). Per-tier maxzoom wired to continent downloads + size-estimate scaling + stall-watchdog guard fix. Codex adrev dispositioned (1 P1 compile break + 2 P2 fixed). Frontend gates green; Rust on CI. REMAINING before close: operator-greenfield wire-walk + browser smoke, then mark #814 ready + merge.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #814 (merged to main). Per-tier maxzoom wired to continent downloads + size-estimate scaling + stall-watchdog guard fix + app-level status-bar download indicator. Codex adrev cleared (P1 compile break + 2 P2). Operator wire-walk passed both flows (download NA/Wide w/ status-bar progress; interrupt+return recoverability+visibility). CI 12/12 green both arches.

title: [bug] Callsign identity dropdown renders UNDER other shell elements (z-index/stacking trap)
description: Observed on latest (2026-06-18). Clicking the callsign in the status/ribbon opens the IdentitySwitcher dropdown but it renders beneath other on-screen elements, effectively invisible. Root cause: .identity-switcher-list is position:absolute; z-index:40 anchored INSIDE the dashboard ribbon (IdentitySwitcher.css:51-55), deliberately NOT a portal per its design note. z-index only competes within a stacking context; shell regions sit at z-index 80 (AppShell.css:131) and 200 (AppShell.css:872, message-list sort), so they paint over the trapped z-40 dropdown. Fix: escape the ribbon stacking context — portal the dropdown to body with position:fixed + z-index above 200 (anchored to the trigger via getBoundingClientRect), OR raise the open dropdown's stacking context above the competing regions. The no-portal design choice (tuxlink-noa0) is what broke; revisit it.
notes: Fixed in PR #810: dropdown portaled to document.body with position:fixed + z-index 1100 (escapes the ribbon's z-80 stacking context); click-outside checks the portaled panel; portal regression test added. Close on merge.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #810 (merged): Report Issue opens system browser + intro confirm; callsign dropdown portaled to <body>. Verified CI-green both arches.

title: [bug] Report Issue: opens GitHub inside the app (window.open) + fires Save As immediately with no context
description: Observed on latest converged build (2026-06-18). Two defects in src/help/ReportIssueModal.tsx: (1) openBrowserFallback (line ~84) uses window.open(url,'_blank') — in WebKitGTK this navigates/embeds the GitHub page INSIDE the app webview ('opens github below the main app, forcing the rest up') instead of launching the system browser. Fix: use the Tauri opener (tauri-plugin-opener openUrl, or a backend ShellExt::open command) like the backend auto-open does (logging/commands.rs report_issue_flow uses ShellExt::shell().open → external). (2) useReportIssueController.start() (line ~355) goes idle→choosing-path and fires saveDialog() immediately, so clicking Help→Report Issue pops the OS Save As dialog with no explanation/confirmation. Consider an idle modal state with a one-line explanation + a Begin button before the Save As.
notes: Fixed in PR #810: window.open→@tauri-apps/plugin-shell open() (real browser, not in-app embed) + intro modal state before the OS Save As. 29 modal tests + 643 shell/help suite green. Close on merge.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #810 (merged): Report Issue opens system browser + intro confirm; callsign dropdown portaled to <body>. Verified CI-green both arches.

title: CI: intermittent rust-lld Bus error (SIGBUS) linking Rust test binaries on amd64 verify
description: verify (ubuntu-latest, amd64) > 'Rust tests' step intermittently fails while LINKING the test binaries (wizard_integration_test, lib test): rust-lld (bundled LLVM linker, -fuse-ld=lld) crashes — 'collect2: fatal error: ld terminated with signal 7 [Bus error], core dumped' + an llvm::parallelFor stack + 'PLEASE submit a bug report to llvm-project'. It never reaches running tests. NOT a test failure, NOT a dependency issue. Observed on PR #800 (dompurify 3.4.10->3.4.11 patch) and #801 (vite 5->6 major) 2026-06-18 — both JS-only bumps that don't touch Rust; both pass verify+build on arm64, fail only amd64-verify. Intermittent (verify-amd64 passed for non-dependabot PRs the same day). Likely resource exhaustion during the huge debuginfo=2 parallel link on the amd64 runner. REMEDIATION: re-run usually clears it. If recurring: lower test-profile debuginfo ([profile.test] debug=line-tables-only or split-debuginfo), or switch off bundled rust-lld to system ld/mold, or bigger runner. Affects ALL PRs, not just dependabot.
notes:
ESCALATED 2026-06-18: recurred on a CLEAN re-run of PR #800 (3rd amd64-verify hit overall; 2x on-merit, 1x was a concurrency cancellation). Same rust-lld link crash in the 'Rust tests' step ('linking with cc failed' + orphaned collect2/rust-lld terminated; clippy+vitest+build all pass first). #801 cleared on one re-run, #800 did not — it's probabilistic resource exhaustion during the huge debuginfo=2 test-binary link; #800's branch rebased onto latest main (more code → bigger link) likely tips it over more often. NOT the dompurify dep. STOP re-running (whack-a-mole). FIX: lower test-profile link memory in src-tauri/Cargo.toml — [profile.test] debug="line-tables-only" (keeps line numbers in backtraces, slashes debug data lld must link) and/or split-debuginfo="unpacked". Fallback if it persists: move x86_64 off bundled rust-lld to system ld/mold via .cargo/config.toml. Lands on main as its own PR → dependabot PRs rebase → green. Can't reproduce locally (contended Pi); CI confirms.

title: Release .deb requires glibc >=2.39 (built on Ubuntu 24.04) -> won't run on Debian 12 Bookworm, which docs claim is supported
description: CI deb-install-test (tuxlink-w636, PR #794) found: the .deb installs on Debian 12 Bookworm (apt resolves all package Depends) but the binary fails to load with 'GLIBC_2.38/2.39 not found' — Bookworm ships glibc 2.36. The release builds on Ubuntu 24.04 runners (glibc 2.39), setting a >=2.39 floor. docs/install.md claims 'WebKitGTK 4.1 ships on Debian 12 (Bookworm) and newer' implying Bookworm support, which is false. The .deb also declares NO libc6 version floor, so apt installs a binary that can't run instead of refusing cleanly. Trixie (glibc 2.41) passes. Two resolutions: (A) Trixie+/Ubuntu24.04+ only — fix docs + add libc6(>=2.39) to .deb Depends so apt errors cleanly + drop/flip Bookworm CI; (B) support Bookworm — build the release in a debian:bookworm container (has glibc 2.36 AND webkit2gtk-4.1) to lower the floor. Operator decision pending (audience distro mix).
notes:
Operator decision (2026-06-18): ship honest Trixie+ floor now; real Bookworm support deferred. The DEFECT portion is fixed in PR #794 (cbbd4b78): docs no longer claim Bookworm support, and the .deb's libc6 (>= 2.39) Depends makes apt refuse cleanly on Bookworm instead of installing a binary that dies at ld.so. REMAINING (this issue, now a feature-scale effort, kept open): true Bookworm support needs static-linking libheif 1.17 + libde265 (Bookworm ships libheif 1.15) AND a lower glibc floor (build in a Bookworm container) — decode-only so no GPL x265/aom. Revisit if the Bookworm/EmComm-Pi audience warrants the lift.

title: [flaky-CI] AprsSettings.test.tsx 'persists a link change' races config-load on arm64
description: AprsSettings.test.tsx #4 ('persists a link change from Settings via packet_config_set') flakes on arm64 CI (passes amd64 + locally) — blocks every PR's verify job during launch (seen on PR #770, a Rust-only change). Root cause: usePacketConfig.setLink is a NO-OP until config loads (if (!config) return). The test awaits findByTestId('modem-link-section') — which renders before the async packet_config_get resolves + setConfig runs — then clicks modem-seg-tcp. On slow arm64 the click lands while config is still null → setLink no-ops → packet_config_set never called → waitFor times out. Fix: wait for the persisted config to re-seed the picker (UvproNative → UV-Pro segment aria-pressed) before clicking, so setLink is functional. Test-only change.

title: Daylight theme: actually high-contrast for outdoor/sunlight use (release gate)
description: Operator (recurring request, 2026-06-16): the Daylight light theme is not actually high-contrast — load-bearing for AZ-summer outdoor use, needed for real release + outdoor testing. Black-on-white text is good, but the status colors (weak reds/greens: success #0a6d3b, error #a3171e, info #0b4f9c) collapse under sun glare — mid-dark colors compress to one dark smudge + hue discrimination fails in glare → red/green indistinguishable. Wants: good-looking AND color-coordinated AND actually high contrast; 'punch up' the whole palette. NOT the existing industrial high-contrast-light. Approach: brainstorm with high-fidelity mocks (operator must SEE + approve before code), then implement in App.css daylight tokens + redundant cues (filled chips/labels not thin glyphs) + luminance-separated states, grim-verify, operator tests outdoors.
notes: IMPLEMENTED (PR #776, opossum-marsh-oriole 2026-06-16): operator-approved direction after long visual-companion iteration. COLORS ONLY (no shape/font/border changes; borders left alone per 'no added lines'). Key fix = bold stateful fills: selected message (.row.selected) + active folder (.nav-item.active) -> solid --accent #a83800 + white text, DAYLIGHT-SCOPED so other themes untouched. Palette punch: text near-black, secondary darkened (no vanishing), accent #a83800, green #0a6b2e. App.css only. DEFERRED: --reach-* map-tier daylight overrides (operator said main-UI not map). GATE: operator real-app field test (grim+sun) — mocks proved untrustworthy. Approved-design memory: feedback_high_contrast_means_dramatic (overshoot-then-dial-back; theme=palette-only).

title: APRS tac map performance is poor vs the Find-a-Station map — investigate (P1)
description: Operator 2026-06-16 (P1): the APRS Tac positions map (src/aprs/AprsPositionsMap.tsx) performs noticeably WORSE than the Find-a-Station map (src/catalog/StationFinderMap.tsx), even though both use the same MapLibreMap stack. Investigate the delta — likely the per-tick now-recompute (NOW_TICK_MS setInterval rebuilding the FC), uncertainty-polygon rebuilds, or pushData churn on every position update vs StationFinderMap's feature-state approach. Related but distinct from tuxlink-vnk7 (general vector-map RCA). Label 'roadmap-2026-06-16'.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (investigated 2026-06-16, operator GPU hunch): NOT a per-map CPU/GPU split — GL mode is process-global (apply_linux_webview_gl_env in src-tauri/src/lib.rs); on Pi-5 BOTH maps render software-GL/llvmpipe because hardware WebGL is broken (magenta static, handoff #698). The real delta is per-surface: AprsPositionsMap MISSED the tuxlink-vnk7 (B9) render-perf optimizations that StationFinderMap got. (1) AprsPositionsMap.usePushData (AprsPositionsMap.tsx:255-272) is the OLD single-effect form [map,sourceId,data] that re-subscribes 'styledata' + re-pushes the whole source on EVERY data change; StationFinderMap.usePushData subscribes styledata ONCE [map,sourceId] via a dataRef + pushes separately. (2) AprsPositionsMap rebuilds the ENTIRE FeatureCollection (buildPositionFC+buildUncertaintyFC) every NOW_TICK_MS=30s (AprsPositionsMap.tsx:284) just to recompute staleness, then re-pushes through the churny usePushData. (3) selection/popup not feature-state-driven. FIX: port StationFinderMap's two-effect usePushData; drive staleness via feature-state or a cheaper update instead of a full FC rebuild; avoid the 30s full rebuild. Mirrors a proven pattern → low risk.

title: APRS dock tabs (+ other transparent buttons) render as native GTK default buttons on WebKitGTK — global button{} missing appearance:none
description: Operator reported 4x: the APRS/Map/Modem dock tabs look like generic 'Claude default-style buttons' instead of the intended minimal underline rail. NOT a design/taste issue (operator confirmed he WANTS the rail) and NOT missing CSS (.aprs-dock-tab IS styled + imported). ROOT CAUSE: src/App.css global rules style every <button> with background-color:var(--surface-2)+border:1px solid var(--border-strong)+border-radius:6px but DO NOT set appearance:none. On WebKitGTK (the real client; Chromium does NOT show this), a transparent/borderless button (.aprs-dock-tab/.aprs-dock-maptoggle/.aprs-dock-close set background:transparent;border:none) falls back to NATIVE GTK button chrome — gray bordered boxes. Same class PR #752 fixed for <select> (GTK-gray without appearance:none). VERIFIED on real WebKit2GTK 4.1 (dev/scratch/webkit-tab-repro.png): without appearance:none = native button boxes; with appearance:none = clean transparent rail. FIX: add appearance:none (+ -webkit-/-moz-) to the global button{} rule in App.css. Low risk: styled buttons already paint their CSS box so it's visually inert for them; only suppresses native chrome on under-styled (transparent) buttons. Add a pitfall entry (WebKitGTK native-control-appearance class). Resolves the #4b half of tuxlink-rypw.
notes: Superseded by tuxlink-sdjd / PR #779. appearance:none (PR #760) was the WRONG root cause (no-op); kept as harmless defensive hygiene. Real fix = border-radius:0.

title: AX.25 T1 retransmit timer does not scale for digipeated paths (collides with peer response)
description:
On-air trace 2026-06-16 (KT7RUN via W7MOT-6 digi): our SABM retransmit and data-phase I-frame/RR retransmits fire on a FIXED params.t1 (3s) regardless of digipeater count. The round-trip response through one digi is ~6s (SABM 10:50:45 -> UA 10:50:51), so our 3s retransmit lands near/on the peer's response and collides ("we cut off the response packet with our SABM every time"; gateway also re-sends ns=1 because our ack races its retransmit).

Classic AX.25 (TNC FRACK behavior) scales the retransmit timer by digipeater count: effective_T1 = T1 * (2*n_digi + 1). Direct path (n=0): 3s. One digi (n=1): 9s. We are MISSING this scaling, so digipeated connects are flaky and acks race.

FIX: scale params.t1 by the connection's digipeater count everywhere the retransmit/await timer is used in datalink.rs — connect() SABM retry interval (next_sabm_at), await_window + await_ack data retransmit sleeps, and disconnect() UA-await deadline. Add a t1_scaled() helper = params.t1 * (2*digis.len()+1). This is standard AX.25 timing, NOT a tuxlink-added safeguard; longer timers also REDUCE keying (RADIO-1-safe). CONNECT_MAX_SABMS + connect_timeout + n2_retries still bound total airtime.

Context: validated this session that the data link, poll-response (tuxlink-uv1i), C/R-bit framing, and in-order acking work on-air; the remaining friction is this retransmit timing through digis.
notes: PARKED 2026-06-16: operator correction — T1 retransmit-interval is NOT the on-air symptom. The real collision is a sub-second half-duplex TURNAROUND (relay answering into the tail of our TX, frame never cleanly received/logged), not the 3s retransmit timer. FRACK digi-scaling may still be a minor correctness improvement but is not the operator's issue; do not ship as 'the timing fix'. Re-diagnose turnaround via KISS TNC params (TXTAIL not currently set/tunable; TXDELAY) and/or ack frequency (we RR-ack every I-frame vs only on polls / T2).
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate: Bluetooth + Benshi KISS interface is unreliable (cross-platform; recovery needs a full BT/radio reset)
description:
The Bluetooth link to the Benshi (UV-Pro / BTECH) KISS TNC interface is unreliable. After a connection failure it frequently cannot re-establish without a low-level reset of the Bluetooth stack AND/OR a radio power-cycle.

NOT Tuxlink-specific and NOT specific to this Pi/radio. The same instability is reported on other hosts, including Windows, where after a connection failure the host sometimes has to be REBOOTED to recover Bluetooth. So this is fundamentally a Benshi-BT / KISS-stack reliability problem in the broader ecosystem, not (solely) a Tuxlink defect.

CURRENT RECOVERY on pandora (Pi 5, onboard Cypress UART BT) — observed 2026-06-16:
- Lowest-level BT cycle: unload+reload kernel modules hci_uart + btbcm (forces a controller firmware reload), rfkill block/unblock, hciconfig down/up, systemctl restart bluetooth. (RX byte counters reset to ~0 confirm a true re-init, not a soft toggle.)
- PLUS a full radio power-cycle to clear lingering links the radio firmware still thinks exist.
- This is the Linux equivalent of the Windows "reboot to recover" workaround.

EARLIER RELATED (partly separate): the `Host is down` (os 112) / `Connection refused` (os 111) failures on opening the KISS link were traced partly to OTHER nearby devices contending for the pairing; disabling BT on those devices abated that. That is a distinct contention issue from the half-open-link-after-failure problem this issue tracks.

INVESTIGATION PLAN (deeper than we have gone):
- Capture HCI-level traces with `btmon` (BlueZ HCI monitor) across a full connect -> fail -> attempted-reconnect cycle. Correlate with `dmesg` kernel BT errors, bluetoothd logs (`journalctl -u bluetooth`), `hcidump`, and the RFCOMM socket errno tuxlink surfaces.
- Compare a CLEAN connect trace vs a FAILED-then-stuck trace: identify exactly where the RFCOMM/SPP link half-opens and which side (host controller, bluetoothd, or the radio firmware) fails to release it.
- Determine whether a TARGETED recovery (force-release the RFCOMM channel / reset just the controller / re-pair) can substitute for the full module-reload + radio power-cycle.

OUTCOMES (it may not be fully fixable at the Tuxlink layer):
1. Characterize the failure mode precisely with btmon evidence.
2. Find the minimal recovery short of a host reboot / radio power-cycle.
3. Assess whether Tuxlink's graceful-DISC (PR #754) + clean socket teardown reduce how often Tuxlink leaves a half-open link behind (a contributing factor we CAN control), even if the underlying Benshi BT instability cannot be eliminated.

CONTEXT: this BT instability is why the operator needed a manual release-cut path — to test Tuxlink's AX.25 fixes (C/R-bit framing, RR(P=1) poll response, on main) despite the flaky transport. Whether a freshly-built client + the lowest-level BT cycle + a radio power-cycle are sufficient for a clean on-air test is TBD.
notes:
btmon capture is NON-exclusive with live BT (verified on pandora 2026-06-16, btmon 5.82): btmon uses the HCI MONITOR channel (HCI_CHANNEL_MONITOR), a passive kernel tap of all HCI command/event/ACL traffic — it does NOT claim or down the controller. (The exclusive one is the HCI USER channel, HCI_CHANNEL_USER — different thing.) Proof: a 3s `sudo btmon` ran while hci0 stayed UP RUNNING and even captured bluetoothd's MGMT activity concurrently.

Capture recipe: `sudo btmon -w dev/scratch/benshi-bt-<ts>.btsnoop` running in the background, reproduce connect->fail->stuck via the normal Tuxlink UI, Ctrl-C, then open the .btsnoop in Wireshark or `btmon -r <file>`. Pair with `journalctl -u bluetooth -f` + `dmesg -w`. Operator runs these on-demand (foreground), not the agent on its own recognizance.

title: [BUG] Ribbon Connect (vu97) bypasses Recent recording + panels don't restore saved target on mount
description:
Two regressions left by tuxlink-vu97 (status-bar Connect fires last-configured session of any mode). Verified against converge-build-worktree @ d18e6102 (~origin/main).

3b — RECENT NEVER POPULATES FROM RIBBON CONNECT: connectFor() in src/connections/connectDispatch.ts:86 (the ribbon/status-bar Connect path) invokes the transport connect+b2f_exchange but NEVER calls recordAttempt/favorite_record_attempt. The four radio panels DO record (reached+failed) on their in-panel Connect, but the ribbon path — now the primary connect surface per vu97 — does not. So connecting from the status bar leaves the per-mode FavoritesTabs 'Recent' tab empty. Operator: 'called and failed stations don't appear in Recent.'

3a — PANELS DON'T RESTORE SAVED TARGET ON MOUNT: writeLastTarget(mode.kind, gateway) persists the last-called station per mode to localStorage['tuxlink.lastTarget.<mode>'] (vu97). readLastTarget() is consumed ONLY by connectDispatch (so the ribbon can dial it) — NO radio panel calls it on mount to restore the visible target field. target is useState(''), mount effects hydrate host/port but not target (src/radio/modes/VaraRadioPanel.tsx:130). Switch modes -> panel remounts blank even though localStorage still has it. Operator: 'modem settings reset when switching mode to mode, clearing previously called stations.'

FIX: (3b) connectFor records reached/failed via the same favorite_record_attempt path the panels use, building the dial from the connection's mode+target. (3a) each panel reads readLastTarget(mode.kind) on mount and seeds target (+ pendingDialRef so the connection-record matches). Regression tests: connectFor records on success+failure; panel restores persisted target on mount.
notes: PR #755 (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/755) open — 3a+3b fixed, Codex P2 addressed, 146 tests green. Branch bd-tuxlink-ypz3/ribbon-connect-recents-target. Leave in_progress until merged.

title: Packet Stop must send a graceful DISC to the remote (not a rude socket kill)
description: Operator 2026-06-16: Stop must broadcast a session-end DISC to the remote, not just kill our side. The rude kill orphans the gateway into the stale half-open state that RR-polls/no-UAs our next connect. Mechanism: cms_abort sets aborting; AbortableByteLink::write refuses to key once aborting is set, so when the stream drops and disconnect() sends its DISC, the write is blocked and the DISC never reaches the remote. A NORMAL session end already DISCs gracefully via Drop when aborting is unset; the gap is the mid-session Stop. FIX: two-flag split — a disconnecting signal that unwinds the read but does NOT block the write, so Drop disconnect() sends its DISC; hard aborting stays as the force-kill fallback. Stop button calls cms_disconnect (graceful); second press calls cms_abort (force). Restores Winlink-Express parity.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-avu9-packet-graceful-disc. Branch: bd-tuxlink-avu9/packet-graceful-disc. Created 2026-06-16 09:49:05 UTC by atoll-magpie-sandbar.

But radio-only+ardop-hf IS built: isBuilt() returns true (radio-only intent built, ardop-hf built) and computePanelMode maps it to {kind:'ardop-hf', intent:'radio-only'} so the right-pane ArdopRadioPanel renders. title: Recover hardware WebGL on Pi — guarded MESA_GLES_VERSION_OVERRIDE + safe-mode fallback (supersedes forced llvmpipe)
description: Empirically proven (raven-poplar-clover 2026-06-15): Pi-5 hardware WebGL was NOT broken — WebKitGTK/ANGLE's WebGL2 init wants GLES 3.2, V3D advertises 3.1, so it failed with GL_INVALID_OPERATION → magenta canvas, and the team wrongly forced software GL (llvmpipe). MESA_GLES_VERSION_OVERRIDE=3.2 restores GPU rendering (loader: vc4/v3d, not swrast). Codex adrev: ship guarded (V3D-targeted) + software-GL fallback, never naked (the override is a version-lie). Replace the unconditional LINUX_WEBVIEW_GL_ENV software-GL with: default hardware (override, keep dmabuf-disable, drop llvmpipe), TUXLINK_GL escape hatch, and a persisted safe-mode fallback (auto software after a hardware launch that never confirmed a map render). Go/no-go = real packaged app on Pi: visual parity, better p50/p95, no GL errors/crashes/magenta/dmesg-v3d-faults. Raw adrev: dev/adversarial/2026-06-15-hardware-gl-adrev-codex.md (gitignored). Corrects memory project_pi_webkitgtk_webgl_software_gl.
notes: PR #744 OPEN. lib.rs: guarded GlMode decision (Pi+auto→hardware MESA_GLES_VERSION_OVERRIDE=3.2; off-Pi→native; software fallback) + TUXLINK_GL escape hatch + self-healing safe-mode marker cleared by new gl_render_confirmed cmd (map calls on load). Frontend tsc clean + map tests green; Rust unit tests (linux_gl_env_tests) cover the matrix; CI compiles. OPERATOR go/no-go: converge-build + open map on Pi → visual parity + better p50/p95 (dev/perf-harness) + no GL errors/crashes/magenta + clean dmesg v3d. Self-heals to software or TUXLINK_GL=software if hardware misbehaves. Corrects memory project_pi_webkitgtk_webgl_software_gl.

title: [P1] Post to Outbox requires an authenticated active identity — RF-only/offline operators can't queue (GH #691)
description:
GH issue #691 (v0.59.0, confirmed still present on origin/main). 'Post to Outbox' → message_send (ui_commands.rs:1675) → NativeBackend::send_message (winlink_backend.rs:1591) whose FIRST line is 'let session_id = self.active_identity()?;' — it derives the message From from the AUTHENTICATED active identity. active_identity() (winlink_backend.rs:1389) returns NoActiveIdentity unless the slot was set, and bootstrap (bootstrap.rs:455-484) only auto-sets it via AutoAuth::Authenticated/Healed — BOTH require a STORED CMS PASSWORD in the keyring. So an operator with a configured callsign (shown as From in the compose window) but NO stored CMS password / not authenticated gets 'Send failed: no active identity — authenticate before transmitting' when merely QUEUEING a draft to the Outbox.

ROOT DEFECT: queueing to the Outbox is NOT transmitting; it should require only a configured From callsign (already known — OutboundDraftDto has no 'from' field, so the backend derives it). Requiring CMS authentication to queue breaks the core EmComm field workflow (compose offline / RF-only / tactical, queue now, send when a gateway is reached) — the exact ECT/Tech-Prepper audience.

FIX: decouple queue-From from transmit-auth. NativeBackend::send_message must derive the From from the active identity IF set, ELSE fall back to the configured identity (config.identity active_full / callsign) — so queueing works without auth. The authenticated-active-identity gate stays at connect/transmit time (where 'authenticate before transmitting' actually belongs). Ensure the stored From matches what the per-identity outbox drain (drain_returns_only_active_identity_outbox) expects once that identity becomes active.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-spbw-outbox-queue-without-auth. Branch: bd-tuxlink-spbw/outbox-queue-without-auth. Created 2026-06-16 01:53:06 UTC by atoll-towhee-juniper.

title: [bug] Link config does not persist (APRS tac chat + all modes): physical device retained, rest dropped on reload
description: Operator 2026-06-15: link configuration does not persist across reloads for APRS tac chat AND other radio modes. The physical DEVICE settings (device path / BT MAC / host) are retained, but 'nothing else about them' — e.g. the link KIND / transport selection / other link fields reset. Symptom surfaced via the APRS connect strip showing 'no link / not set up' even after a working UV-Pro setup. Shared persistence path (not APRS-specific). Investigate: frontend usePacketConfig.setLink -> Tauri packet_config_set; Rust config struct (serde) round-trip on config_read; find which field(s) are dropped (serde skip/default, partial write, load-side reset). Root-cause before fix.

Codex RF round DONE (agent butte-isthmus-alder): one real P1/HIGH — to_voa() didn't validate gain/efficiency VALUES, so a real NEC deep null below -100 dBi ('-100.000' = 8 chars) would overflow the F7.3 field, widen the column, and silently shift voacapl's gain table (NaN/inf fit width 7 -> need is_finite guard). title: [bug] APRS RX on UV-Pro native is LOSSY: receives some packets, far fewer than channel traffic heard
description: Operator report 2026-06-15 (sumac-vetch-dahlia): on the UV-Pro native GAIA path, APRS chat TX works (visible on-air send), but RX is PARTIAL — some inbound packets appear in the feed, far fewer than the channel traffic audible on the radio. NOT total RX failure. Frontend RX path (useAprsChat) is sound: it subscribes to 'aprs-message:new' and appends. So the defect is in the Rust backend RX pipeline. Hypotheses to investigate (bug-hunt-cycle): (1) backend only emits aprs-message:new for APRS *message* (text ':') frames, while the channel is dominated by position/status/telemetry beacons -> need to confirm intended scope (positions go to map via tuxlink-l2pu/useAprsPositions; is the operator expecting those as feed rows or not?); (2) GAIA RX_DATA BLE notifications lost / not reassembled across MTU chunks under load; (3) over-aggressive dedup; (4) strict parse rejecting decodable frames. Validate on-air (operator). Relates: tuxlink-7my9 (native GAIA TX+RX), tuxlink-l2pu (position parser RX). Separate PR from UI punch-list (tuxlink-l0z5).
notes: PR #737 open (fix + diagnostic): removed the parse_info==None feed-drop so ALL heard frames surface as raw feed rows (positions still also to map). Branch bd-tuxlink-8tz1/aprs-feed-all-traffic, worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-8tz1-aprs-feed-all-traffic. NEXT: operator builds from branch + runs on-air. If feed now full -> drop was the cause; if still sparse -> native GAIA reassembly loss (next hunt). UI frame-type filter = follow-up.

title: ARDOP connect freezes UI + no abort during TX (blocking Tauri command on main thread)
description: modem_ardop_connect is a synchronous #[tauri::command] that runs connect_arq (3x attempts, up to CONNECT_DEADLINE=120s) inline on the WebKitGTK main thread, freezing the entire UI during TX. The Stop/abort button is gated on the modem:status event, which can't be delivered until the thread unblocks, so it only appears AFTER the handshake fails and ardopcf closes — i.e. NO working abort during transmission (RADIO-1 working-abort gap). Confirmed live by operator 2026-06-15. Fix: move connect + disconnect off the main thread (async fn + spawn_blocking), mount Stop on the synchronous local 'connecting' state. Root cause: agent atoll-towhee-juniper investigation, this session.
design: ROOT CAUSE (confirmed in live build 3d80516d): modem_ardop_connect (sync, line 894), modem_ardop_disconnect (sync, 108), modem_ardop_b2f_exchange (sync, 1002) are synchronous #[tauri::command] fns -> run blocking I/O (connect_arq up to CONNECT_DEADLINE; disconnect 5s; b2f exchange) on the WebKitGTK main thread -> UI frozen, Stop button (status-event-gated) only mounts after the blocking call returns. Same defect freezes the listener 'Stop' (routes through disconnect). FIX: convert all three to 'pub async fn' + tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || <blocking body>).await, KEEPING the fast RADIO-1 consent gate synchronous BEFORE spawn_blocking (gate-before-I/O invariant). Pattern already in-repo: packet_listen (ui_commands.rs:1994) + spawn_blocking at winlink_backend.rs:965/1105. No internal Rust callers of the 3 command wrappers (tests use _with_factory/_inner seams), so async conversion is safe. Frontend defense-in-depth: gate ArdopRadioPanel Stop button on local 'connecting'/'disconnecting' state (set synchronously in doConnect/onStopClick) in addition to !isStopped, so abort is mounted instantly. BASE NOTE: must be implemented against the CURRENT modem line (3017-line modem_commands.rs in 3d80516d / fas-controlband / identity-selfheal), NOT the stale recover-handoffs branch (1640-line version, different signatures). Validate via CI compile + operator live smoke (UI responsive during TX, Stop abortable mid-connect).

title: Find-a-Station antenna Phase 1 (C): precomputed NEC pattern library (default) — recycled catalog × height grid
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-13d8 / PR #707 (MERGED, not stranded). #707 fixed the gross isotropic collapse (pre-fix 5/10 presets -> ccir.000) by introducing 3 IONCAP archetypes (dipole-23 / vertical-22 / yagi-24) + operator height/ground. But antenna.rs ioncap() still maps all 10 UI presets onto just 3 byte-identical models: the 5 horizontals {type 23,-0.50,0dB} are identical; the 3 verticals {22,-0.25,0dB} identical; beam {24,6dB}. So WITHIN a bucket different selections produce identical .voa -> identical prediction (operator-observed 2026-06-15). Wiring is COMPLETE (AntennaControl->propagation_prefs_write; predict reloads prefs each call, commands.rs:184) — this is modeling granularity, not a stranded wire.

OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-15: add real archetype granularity incl. the planned type-27 sloping vee + per-preset geometry.

GROUNDING (must, per feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability — no fabricated gain-vs-angle):
- Type codes verified vs voacapl iongain.for/decred.for: 22 vert monopole, 23 horiz dipole (param7=height), 24 horiz yagi (param8=gain), 27 sloping vee.
- sample.27 (~/itshfbc/antennas/samples/sample.27): type 27 = 9 params incl [6] 1/2 apex angle, [7] leg length, [8] ht abv ground, [9] terminated height — distinct geometry, NOT a drop-in.
- Design note docs/design/2026-06-14-find-a-station-prediction-recalibration.md has the per-preset mapping table + confidence flags: EfhwSloper->27 is LOW-MED conf; RandomWireUnun + MagneticLoop have NO native type (honest dipole proxies, LOW conf). Height is the real differentiator for horizontals (NVIS lobe at low height); verticals ignore feedpoint height.
- Engine change required (note Fix C): make_scratch_itshfbc currently symlinks antennas/ read-only; must create a WRITABLE antennas/default/ (real dir + per-file symlinks to stock) so generated tx/rx .voa can be written. tx_antenna_voa_content already threaded.

SCOPE/SHAPE TO RESOLVE (brainstorm + Codex): which presets get genuinely distinct grounded models (dipole vs vertical vs yagi vs sloping-vee + per-preset default height) vs which stay honest proxies (random wire / mag loop) — do NOT invent physics to make all 10 differ if not defensible. Honest alternative considered: collapse the UI to the models that actually differ.

DONE = different *defensible* selections measurably change predicted reliability; deck tests extended (assert distinct .voa per archetype + height-sensitivity, e.g. type-23 at 0.15λ vs 0.6λ short-path REL); CI-green; Codex RF round; wire-walk. Supersedes the open antenna-model portion of tuxlink-13d8.
notes: Phase 1 DONE pending wire-walk (agent basin-fern-spruce, 2026-06-15/16, branch bd-tuxlink-bl01/phase1-picker-library, DRAFT PR #745). SHIPPED+CI-GREEN (all 4 checks: build-linux + verify, both arches): C enum curation+#[serde(other)] migration; D1 patterns.rs lookup; D2 operator_voa_content -> precomputed library (deleted dead ioncap/voa_title/IoncapAntenna/GroundType::constants); D3 antenna_pattern_preview command; E frontend picker+PolarPattern+snapping slider+conditional vertical state+live preview (137 catalog vitest pass, tsc clean); F1 distinctness/height-sensitivity tests. F2 Codex RF round DONE: found+fixed 3 real NEC defects (single-azimuth wire patterns -> 24-phi power-average; floating radial counterpoise -> connected at z=0.1; non-resonant linked dipole -> per-band resonant), library regenerated (600 nec2c runs), physics re-verified, gen 25 tests pass. REMAINING: F3 wire-walk (operator supplies flows greenfield; my trace shows preview+forecast both wired and share pattern_voa) then mark PR #745 ready + operator smoke. Freq table block i=i MHz is CORRECT (do not revert). Ground selector inert-but-kept, labeled in UI.

title: Find-a-Station → VARA HF flow dead-ends: make connect + Send/Receive reachable end-to-end
description: Real alpha-user report (2026-06-15): selected a station in Find a Station, picked a frequency, clicked 'Use →' (target prefilled), but Send/Receive and Arm listener are both disabled with no obvious next step. Root cause: handleStationUse (AppShell) mounts the VARA panel + prefills the target but does NOT open the transport; Send/Receive is gated on status.state==='open' (VaraRadioPanel.tsx ~527) and Arm listener on !isOpen (~575). The required step (Start = vara_open_session) is at the bottom of the panel and only explained in hover tooltips → dead-end. Per operator: alpha = make-it-work end-to-end, no atomic patch (a dead-end flow is lying to real users). Build the Find-a-Station → VARA path so using a station lands the operator connectable and Send/Receive is genuinely reachable; handle failure states (VARA not running/reachable; aarch64 needs remote x86 host) with actionable UI, not silent disabled buttons. Verify the full chain prefill→open→exchange (modem_vara_b2f_exchange) + listener arm. Branch off main.
notes: PR #717 (base main) — DONE pending CI+smoke. handlePrefill auto-opens VARA transport (socket, not TX) so Find-a-Station 'Use →' lands connectable; visible closed-state hint for manual path; open-failure surfaces error+Start. Codex round: 2 race findings (P1 wait-for-initial-status + openInitiatedRef clobber-guard; P2 synchronous openInFlightRef double-open guard) BOTH FIXED + tested. 40 VARA tests + tsc green. NOT applied to ARDOP (its Start transmits). Transcript dev/adversarial/2026-06-15-vara-p6iq-codex.md.

title: Backend panics invisible to robust logs: install a std::panic::set_hook that emits tracing::error
description: Observability-holes audit (spruce-finch-gorge, alpha, alongside tuxlink-4b96 frontend-error forwarding): there is NO std::panic::set_hook anywhere in src-tauri. A Rust panic in a Tauri command, a spawned thread, or an async task therefore crashes/aborts with NOTHING in the structured logs (~/.local/state/tuxlink/logs/tuxlink.<hour>.jsonl + Logging window) — the most catastrophic and most invisible failure class. FIX: after crate::logging::init() installs the tracing subscriber (lib.rs setup(), the Full branch ~line 500), install a panic hook that emits tracing::error!(target=tuxlink::panic, location, payload) then chains the previous hook (preserve the default stderr backtrace). Process-global → covers command/thread/async panics. Batch with #710 into the next manual cut build so backend panics + frontend errors are both diagnosable from logs. Lower-priority follow-up holes (not blocking): no centralized Tauri-command-error logging (233 cmds, ~18 files tracing::error); 45 frontend .catch(()=>{}) swallows (mostly intentional).

title: Find-a-Station prediction recalibration: per-mode REQ.SNR (dB-Hz) + noise environment + antenna type/height (.voa generation)
description: Item 5+4 from operator 2026-06-14. Predictions over-optimistic (low NVIS+1W = excellent for all <500mi) and antenna picker is largely cosmetic. ROOT CAUSE (investigated by sandbar-crag-cardinal, evidence via direct voacapl runs): (1) REQ.SNR hardwired 22.0 dB-Hz = CW-grade -> over-optimism; VOACAP REQ.SNR is dB-Hz = SNR_dB + 10log10(BW). VARA-HF reliable-connect ~35-37, SSB 38-44, unknown-mode default ~38. (2) Antenna presets collapse: 5/10 -> ccir.000 isotropic; NO antenna-height input though height is THE NVIS variable. Fix: emit parametric .voa (IONCAP type 23 horiz dipole / 22 vertical / 24 yagi / 27 sloping vee) with height as NEGATIVE param (wavelengths, freq-independent); voacapl computes height-dependent lobe internally. (3) noise -145dBW is residential (fine default) but expose City -140..Remote -164 picker. (4) SSN static 100 (minor). Validated: req_snr 36 + low NVIS dipole reproduces correct 80m day/night swing (midday 0.83->night 0.98) and 40m night-close. Research transcripts + per-mode/noise/antenna tables in design note. Supersedes s0r1 #3.
notes: PR #707 status: recalibration triad A+C+B CI-VERIFY GREEN (both arches) + item-1 band-bar tier colouring [ec7a15d7] pushed (frontend-only, locally green, CI re-running). DONE: item 5 over-optimism (REQ.SNR), item 4 (antenna height/ground via IONCAP .voa), noise environment, item 1 (band bars coloured by %). REMAINING needs operator: items 2 (dot/symbol disambiguation) + 3 (field compaction) → UI gate requires brainstorm + grim/WebKitGTK verify; per-mode REQ.SNR derivation → has a UX question (manual Req-SNR control vs auto-mode-derive). Spec: docs/design/2026-06-14-find-a-station-prediction-recalibration.md.

(P2) basemapStyle.ts selfOrigin() concatenation emits invalid sprite/glyph URLs when location is undefined (root-relative, rejected) OR when tauri://localhost is an opaque origin (location.origin='null' -> 'null/basemap/...'); switch to new URL('/basemap/...', location.href).href which resolves correctly for tauri:// and http:// alike (Codex-verified). (P3) CORS Allow-Origin only added to the pmtiles branch; legacy tile://localhost raster success/error paths still lack it (latent future blank if maplibre ever fetch()es them). (P3) URL tests are tautological (reassert location.origin). Adrev transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-14-map-cors-maxbounds-sprite-codex.md (gitignored). title: Tac Chat is unusable: no transport/radio/connect controls exist on the chat surface
description:
APRS Tactical Chat (tuxlink-2f2n, shipped #642 + native GAIA ve3j/7my9 + multi-transport a20f) is NOT reachable by a user. The spec's Phase-1 success criterion is 'an operator picks a transport, opens the chat surface, sends/receives' (docs/design/2026-06-12-aprs-tactical-chat-design.md). The chat surface (AprsChatPanel) exposes ONLY Start/Stop listening + Send + Path. Operator (Cameron) confirms, three times, that NONE of the salient connect controls exist/are reachable for this feature.

MISSING CONTROLS (definition-of-done — every one must be present + wired on the chat surface, traced file:line, wire-walked against a fresh-install starting state):
1. Pick a transport (UV-Pro native GAIA / KISS BT / managed Dire Wolf / TCP / serial TNC)
2. Pick a radio (the device for the chosen transport)
3. Start the radio connection (connect, with disconnect + live status)

Today the connect strip is AppShell.tsx:1401 controlStrip={ linkKind==='UvproNative' ? <UvproControlStrip/> : undefined } — an empty/unsatisfiable seam: undefined unless linkKind was already set on the SEPARATE Packet-radio settings screen, which is not the chat surface. aprs_listen_start (ui_commands.rs:3821) silently depends on cfg.packet.link being pre-set; with it unset, Start fails to 'radio disconnected' with no control to fix it.

ROOT-CAUSE NOTE: this passed a prior wire-walk that graded command registration, not human-usable controls — the precise anti-pattern the wire-walk SKILL forbids. Re-run wire-walk with operator-supplied flows from fresh-install state before any 'done' claim.

This is a UI feature → brainstorm the connect surface (where the 3 controls live on/near the chat panel; reuse vs. rebuild ModemLinkSection's UV-Pro segment + UvproControlStrip) BEFORE building.
notes:
--- 2026-06-14 (fjord-vale-kite): code-complete, PR #695 ---
Connect surface (AprsConnectStrip in dock header: pick transport+radio, per-transport connect/disconnect, live status) + inline addressing (parseCompose colon-required + tap-to-reply) shipped on bd-tuxlink-ckmb/tac-chat-connect. Wire-walked fresh-install flows (all hops traced). Codex adrev: 4 findings (config-persist race, UV-Pro orphan, parseCompose false-positives, dead props) all fixed. 49 FE tests green, tsc clean. title: App-wide error boundary: an unguarded map (maplibre) init throw blanks the entire UI
description: 0.60.0 first-run: the wizard location step renders the new MapLibre basemap (yy1m location map on the ndi4 MapLibre port, both in-flight when 0.60.0 was cut). new maplibregl.Map() at src/map/MapLibreMap.tsx:94 is unguarded; the app has ZERO React error boundaries, so a synchronous construction throw (likely WebGL-init-fail in WebKitGTK, or invalid style/duplicate addProtocol) unmounts the entire React root -> dark-blue empty window. Operator hit it at the location step (first map mount). Root cause is architectural: any unguarded throw blanks the whole app. Fix: generic ErrorBoundary; wrap app root (catch-all recovery screen, never-fully-blank guarantee) + wrap map mount points (degrade to 'map unavailable, enter grid manually' so surrounding controls survive). Found during operator validation of 0.60.0; investigate skill root-caused 2026-06-13 (moss-esker-osprey).

title: GPS/Location wizard: map position-confirmation + unconditional Linux diagnostics + one-click fix
description: Extends the approved GPS UX design (docs/design/2026-06-05-gps-setup-ux-design.md) per the 2026-06-13 amendment (docs/design/2026-06-13-gps-location-confirmation-design.md). Three gaps in shipped slice 1 (tuxlink-9xy1): (1) no map / no position display / no confirmation of correctness after selecting a GPS source; (2) Linux GPS diagnostics gated on device-presence (classifyGpsSources hasSerial) so the triage is invisible exactly when Linux is broken and the device won't enumerate — make diagnostics unconditional, gated on 'no working fix'; (3) manual location-setting lacks a map pin (click + drag). Folds tuxlink-m9ej (pkexec one-click Fix-it) into the same delivery per operator call. Backend: plumb raw lat/lon through Fix/parse_tpv/arbiter/PositionStatusDto (local-display-only, never broadcast) for a precise pin. Shared component so Settings -> Location gets it too. title: Find a Station ignores GPS location (reads manual config.identity.grid only) → no bearing, no HF prediction
description: REGRESSION. StationFinderPanel.tsx resolves the operator grid solely from invoke('config_read').grid (= config.identity.grid, the MANUALLY-entered grid). For GPS operators (position_source=Gps, identity.grid=None — the default), that field is empty, so Find a Station shows 'set your location (status bar) for distance + bearing' even though the status bar shows the live GPS position. Result: no aiming/bearing header AND HF prediction never fires (both gated on the empty grid). Confirmed via the live config: identity.grid=None, position_source=Gps. Root cause: GPS-as-default-position (tuxlink-686 position arbiter) landed after Find a Station was wired to the static manual grid; every other location surface (CheckInForm, PositionFormV2) resolves the effective grid via the position_current_fix command, but Find a Station was never reconnected. FIX: StationFinderPanel resolves the effective grid via position_current_fix (GPS-aware PositionArbiter.active_grid), falling back to config_read().grid for the manual case — mirroring CheckInForm. Frontend-only; the backend predictPath already takes the grid the panel supplies. Add a regression test.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-q1tm-findstation-gps-grid. Branch: bd-tuxlink-q1tm/findstation-gps-grid. Created 2026-06-13 05:48:56 UTC by thistle-willow-chasm.

title: Find-a-Station/offline tiles: maxZoom never rises after async tile-source bind (react-leaflet mount-once)
description: Operator (0.57.1 .deb): binding a LAN tile server in Settings shows 'source validated' and persists, but Find a Station can't zoom past the default (z3) and the bind appears not to take. Root cause: BaseMap passes maxZoom to react-leaflet <MapContainer>, which reads it ONCE at mount (init-only, like center/zoom). useTileSource() delivers the tile descriptor asynchronously AFTER mount, so the live Leaflet map keeps maxZoom=3 forever; StationFinderMap also opens at OPERATOR_ZOOM=3 (the cap). Backend tile pipeline is correct+tested. Secondary: 'Use this source' shows the same 'source validated' string as 'Test', giving no distinct bind confirmation. Fix FE-only: apply maxZoom imperatively via useMap().setMaxZoom when tileSource changes (mirror RecenterOnOperator), and give the bind a distinct confirmation.
notes: Complete remediation in PR #659 (branch bd-tuxlink-k61j/lan-tiles-remediation, off main). PR #654 merged only the partial maxZoom+bindMessage fix; the CRITICAL config_read ConfigViewDto map_tile_source drop + TMS double-flip + zoom-band + URL-validation fixes are in #659. Parallel 3-hunter bug hunt (reports in dev/bug-hunts/). Deferred offline-cache/probe-philosophy → tuxlink-w5xn. NOTE: operator's running tauri dev is on the OLD worktree (bd-tuxlink-k61j-tile-maxzoom-reactive) whose WORKING TREE has all fixes applied — so the live build already has the complete fix for testing against the raster test server (pandora.local:8099). Old worktree branch is merged-dead; dispose after operator session ends.

title: Compose drops un-Entered recipient → empty To: in Outbox (GitHub #648, first user bug)
description:
FIRST EXTERNAL BUG (GitHub issue #648, user Orv). SILENT RECIPIENT DATA-LOSS.

Repro: New Message, type a recipient in To: (e.g. w6bi@winlink.org), click "Post to Outbox" WITHOUT pressing Enter. The Outbox message's To: is empty (em-dash). Self-send is incidental; ANY un-Entered recipient is lost (To AND Cc).

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by full code trace, kite-taiga-hawk 2026-06-12):
src/contacts/RecipientInput.tsx holds in-progress typed text in internal "text" state (useState, line 61). It commits to the real "value" (a chip) ONLY on Enter / dropdown-pick (onKeyDown lines 119-127). There is NO commit-on-blur (Escape even CLEARS pending text). Compose.tsx reads the committed "value" via the "to" state in buildRecipients (line 521) and never flushes the pending buffer on send. So a recipient typed-but-not-Entered stays in the buffer, "to" is empty, message_send gets to:[], compose_message writes no To: header (winlink/compose.rs:62-64 loops an empty slice), Outbox shows em-dash.

The entire Rust write/store/parse/list/view path is CORRECT and tested (traced end-to-end; v0.55.0..main byte-identical) - the loss is purely the FE buffer-not-flushed. Screenshot evidence: To: rendered as plain text, not a committed chip.

FIX (small, FE-only): RecipientInput commits pending "text" on blur (onBlur -> addRawToken(text)); AND Compose flushes pending recipient text before buildRecipients as belt-and-suspenders (RecipientInput exposes an imperative flush()/pendingValue() via useImperativeHandle; Compose To/Cc refs flush in every send path - message_send / send_form / send_webview_form all call buildRecipients). Applies to BOTH To and Cc.

REGRESSION TEST (must fail before, pass after): render Compose, type a recipient WITHOUT Enter, click "Post to Outbox", assert invoke('message_send', { draft: { to: ['...'] } }) carries the typed recipient. Plus a RecipientInput unit test: type + blur emits the committed value via onChange.

VERIFY: vitest (no Rust change). CI for the full gate per no-local-cargo.

Public GitHub issue #648 is mislabeled "enhancement" (it is a bug). Relabel + user response are OPERATOR-GATED (do not auto-comment/close).
notes: FIX IMPLEMENTED + PR #652 (condor-bayou-sage, 2026-06-12). FE-only: RecipientInput forwardRef+flush()+commit-on-blur; Compose flushes To/Cc refs in buildRecipients (covers all 3 send paths). TDD RED→GREEN. Full vitest 2604/2604 green, tsc clean, no Rust change. Awaiting CI + merge. GH #648 relabel/response still operator-gated — untouched.
close_reason: Closed

The position subsystem (tuxlink-va1i) already sources the ribbon grid from live position_status.ui_grid (?? config grid) via useStatusData(); the location hero (#587) never adopted it.
