title: Tray restore broken on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: window hidden-to-tray can't be brought back
description:
Found 2026-05-20 (pika-glade-bluff) during operator review of the cbz tauri-dev run. Close-to-tray hides the main window (lib.rs CloseRequested -> window.hide() + prevent_close), but on this Pi (Wayland + wf-panel-pi) the operator cannot bring the window back from the tray — it 'persists but is not accessible'. Pre-existing Task-8 (tuxlink-rit) behavior; tray.rs is untouched by tuxlink-cbz. The handoff only verified tray->Quit, never tray->restore on this compositor.

LIKELY CAUSES (tray.rs):
1. Left-click restore relies on on_tray_icon_event TrayIconEvent::Click{Left,Up} -> show_main_window. wlroots/SNI panels (wf-panel-pi) commonly do NOT forward left-click 'Activate' to the SNI host, so left-click never fires -> nothing happens.
2. The right-click menu 'Show/Hide Window' (tray:show_hide) calls toggle_main_window, which branches on window.is_visible(). After a Wayland window.hide(), is_visible() is unreliable; if it reports stale 'visible', the toggle hides again and the window never re-shows.
3. window.hide()->show() reliability on Wayland/WebKitGTK is itself a known Tauri pain point (window may not re-map).

FIX IDEAS:
- Replace the visibility-TOGGLE menu item with an explicit, unconditional 'Show Window' that calls show() + unminimize() + set_focus() (and a separate 'Hide'), so restore never depends on is_visible(). show_main_window already does the unconditional show — route the menu through it.
- Add unminimize() to the show path.
- Don't depend on left-click activation as the only obvious restore (keep it, but the menu must be reliable).
- Verify on the actual Wayland/wf-panel-pi setup (operator-run; agent can't see the native window) AND on X11 for regression.

IMPACT: a window closed to tray can become unrecoverable except by killing/restarting the process — bad for an emcomm tool whose whole point is surviving close-to-tray mid-session. P1.
acceptance_criteria: From a hidden-to-tray state on Wayland/wf-panel-pi: right-click tray -> Show Window reliably restores + focuses the main window every time (no double-click, no inverted toggle). Restore also works after multiple hide/show cycles. Verified by the operator on the Pi's actual compositor; X11 path not regressed. Pat child process still survives close-to-tray (the feature that motivated hide-on-close).
notes: WORSE THAN RESTORE-TOGGLE (pika-glade-bluff 2026-05-20): on the operator's current Wayland/wf-panel-pi session the tray icon does NOT appear AT ALL (no SNI host -> TrayIconBuilder.build() silently no-ops). So close-to-tray (lib.rs CloseRequested -> hide()) strands the window: process stays alive (confirmed target/debug/tuxlink running hidden) with NO GUI path back. Only recovery is pkill + relaunch. COMPOUNDING: tauri-plugin-window-state (lib.rs:40, default flags) persists VISIBILITY for ALL windows incl. main -> a clean exit while hidden would save visible=false and the NEXT launch could start hidden too (not yet triggered: no .window-state.json saved yet). REQUIRED FIX must guarantee recoverability, e.g.: (a) minimize instead of hide on Linux (stays in compositor window list), OR (b) verify the tray actually registered before enabling hide-on-close (else fall back to minimize/real-close), OR (c) a global show-window shortcut; AND exclude StateFlags::VISIBLE from window-state for the main window so it can never restore hidden. Title broadened: not just 'restore broken' but 'tray may be absent -> close strands window'. Still P1 (arguably higher — strands an emcomm app).
close_reason: grounding-sweep grouse-thistle-wren 2026-06-10 (codebase-verified): shipped — tray.rs unconditional Show Window restore + VISIBLE-flag exclusion (commit 39da45e); operator-Pi check is smoke only.

title: v0.0.1 visual-fidelity gap: global CSS layer never implemented (starter App.css, no reset, undefined --tux-* tokens, light form controls)
description: Found at M2 smoke 2026-05-20. The main-UI build gated logic + functional structure but had NO visual-fidelity gate, so the GLOBAL design layer was never implemented and the shipped UI diverges sharply from docs/design/v0.0.1-ux-mockups.md + docs/design/mockups/images/. Specific VERIFIED findings (worktree bd-tuxlink-8zg-integration-wiring @ feat 3a94790): (1) NO global reset — default body{margin:8px} offsets the 100vw/100vh .app-shell from top/left + overflows viewport ('floating' bug); add *,*::before,*::after{box-sizing:border-box} + html,body,#root{margin:0;height:100%}. (2) src/App.css is the unmodified create-tauri-app starter — light :root (#0f0f0f on #f6f6f6), .logo/.container/.row dead rules, light form-control theming -> inputs/buttons render LIGHT on the dark shell. Replace with the real design system. (3) The --tux-* design tokens (--tux-bg/fg/border/hover/accent-bg/badge) are used as fallbacks in component CSS but NEVER defined centrally -> no theme source of truth; define them on :root from the mock palette. (4) No box-sizing:border-box -> paddings inflate widths past grid tracks -> cramming/overlap. (5) index.html <title> still 'Tauri + React + Typescript'. NOTE: per-component CSS (AppShell grid, DashboardRibbon, StatusBar, MessageView, Compose) is largely sound + matches the mock LAYOUT; the gap is the global foundation + mock-fidelity polish (typography/spacing/aesthetic), not a rebuild. Suggest two tiers: (A) global-CSS foundation fix (reset/box-sizing/tokens/base+form theming/retitle) — fixes the reported floating+cramming+theme-clash; (B) a frontend-design fidelity pass against the mocks. PROCESS follow-up: add a visual-smoke / design-fidelity gate to the build pipeline so this can't recur.

title: Keyring integration tests write to the operator's REAL system keyring (XDG_DATA_HOME/HOME not isolated)
description: ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-05-20 keyring breakage. tuxlink's keyring integration tests call entry.set_password() against the real Secret Service: wizard_integration_test.rs:92/209 ('should write to real keyring') and wizard_persist_cms_test.rs:171. The XdgGuard (wizard_integration_test.rs:47-53) isolates ONLY XDG_CONFIG_HOME. gnome-keyring stores under XDG_DATA_HOME / HOME (~/.local/share/keyrings/), which is NOT isolated; dbus-run-session isolates only the D-Bus session bus, not the on-disk keyring files. So 'isolated' keyring tests AND any setup recipe (e.g. SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/user/1000/keyring/ssh with a typed password) reach straight into the operator's REAL, cross-project-shared (geographica also uses secret-tool against this same login keyring) system keyring. On 2026-05-20 a manual setup one-liner (bash history line 1809) re-keyed the operator's login keyring with an unrecoverable password, breaking secret access for BOTH tuxlink and geographica until a reset. FIX: (1) keyring tests MUST set a throwaway HOME (or XDG_DATA_HOME) so gnome-keyring uses a temp keyring dir, IN ADDITION to dbus-run-session; assert the test never resolves to ~/.local/share/keyrings. (2) Provide a documented safe headless-keyring-test recipe (ephemeral HOME + dbus-run-session + gnome-keyring-daemon --unlock with EMPTY password inside the sandbox). (3) Purge any setup guidance that runs --unlock against the real keyring. Cross-project hazard; bites the shared-CI/dev-environment transfer goal.

title: Define WinlinkBackend trait — v0.5 prep Step 1 (Pat greenfield refactor)
description:
Operator decision 2026-05-18 (Codex + Cameron convergence): tuxlink moves off Pat to native Rust at v0.5 via staged replacement (not flag-day). Step 1 is the architectural boundary — define a WinlinkBackend trait that decouples tuxlink's UI/config layer from the Pat HTTP sidecar so future native + Pat implementations can co-exist behind one interface.

Trait surface (from current Pat usage in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs + pat-client integration):
- list_messages(folder: Folder) -> Vec<MessageMeta>
- read_message(id: MessageId) -> Message
- send_message(msg: OutboundMessage) -> Result<MessageId>
- connect(transport: TransportConfig) -> Result<SessionHandle>
- disconnect(session: SessionHandle) -> Result<()>
- status() -> BackendStatus
- stream_log() -> impl Stream<Item = LogLine>

Two conceptual impls:
1. PatBackend — wraps current HTTP-to-Pat-sidecar. Lands in this issue's PR.
2. NativeBackend — placeholder/stub in this issue; fleshed out in Steps 3-10.

Discipline: this IS a hard-to-undo architectural decision (the trait shape constrains every future backend implementation), so build-robust-features applies — BUT TIGHTLY SCOPED per the operator's 2026-05-18 framing:
- Brainstorm: 1 hour max (concept only — error model, async vs sync, session-handle ownership, log-stream cancellation)
- Spec: trait definition + behavior contract + 5-10 test cases (NOT 24+)
- >=1 cross-provider adrev round (NOT 5 — Codex review on the trait surface only)
- Plan: trivial (trait file + stub NativeBackend + PatBackend wrapper around current pat-client code)
- Ship as one PR

Follow-up steps (each gets its own bd issue when this one merges):
2. Freeze Pat backend as reference (PatBackend implements the trait)
3. Build native mailbox + B2F parser/writer with golden fixtures from Pat / wl2k-go
4. Build B2F session state machine over generic async IO
5. Build CMS telnet client + fake CMS harness
6. Pat-vs-native parity tests over same fixtures
7. Operator-only live CMS smoke gate
8. AX.25/KISS layer (KISS = protocol compliance per memory feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability)
9. Native VARA as separate milestone (clean-room: reverse-engineering writeups read as inverse spec, NOT adoption guide)
10. Default native after parity+soak; remove Pat sidecar after one fallback release

Memories to read before starting:
- feedback_discipline_triage_rule (this IS architectural; pipeline applies, but tightly scoped)
- feedback_clean_sheet_concepts_only
- feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability
- project_v05_modem_design_posture
- project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand
- feedback_no_atomic_decisions_to_operator

Replaces nothing in v0.0.1 — this is purely v0.5 prep. v0.0.1 continues to ship the Pat sidecar via the cred-handling fork (PR-A merged + PR-B merged); the trait wraps that existing surface without disturbing the shipped wizard or PatProcess flow.

title: Task 2 config implementation (AMD-1 nested schema + AMD-11 + validate_identity + write_config_atomic) — HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster plan
description:
Surfaced by 2026-05-18 wizard-cluster plan-review R1 friction lens (P0-1, P0-3) and verified against shipped src-tauri/src/config.rs.

Current state of src-tauri/src/config.rs (verified 2026-05-18):
- HAS flat schema with: callsign, grid_square, pat_mbo_address, winlink_password_present, wizard_completed (all top-level)
- MISSING: nested connect/identity/privacy structs per AMD-1
- STILL HAS: winlink_password_present per AMD-11 should-be-dropped
- MISSING: validate_identity() function (per AMD-1 loose-validator semantics)
- MISSING: write_config_atomic() function (per Task 2 spec; used by wizard_persist_cms in wizard-cluster spec §3.2)

Gap pattern: AMD-1 (2026-05-17) was a plan-text amendment; the corresponding CODE update to config.rs never shipped. Same gap exists for AMD-11 (2026-05-18 field drop). The plan amendments document intent; code amendments implement it — they don't auto-cascade.

Scope:
1. Update Config struct to nested shape per AMD-1: connect{connect_to_cms, transport}, identity{callsign, identifier, grid}, privacy{gps_state, position_precision}, pat_mbo_address.
2. Drop winlink_password_present per AMD-11.
3. Implement validate_identity(s: &str) -> bool per AMD-1's loose-validator semantics (non-empty + no internal whitespace + ≤32 chars + ASCII-printable).
4. Implement write_config_atomic(config: &Config) -> Result<(), ConfigError> using tempfile + std::fs::rename to /tuxlink/config.json.
5. Update all existing call sites + tests in config_test.rs.
6. Pitfalls entry: document the 'plan amendment != code amendment' gap pattern (in implementation-pitfalls.md as a new entry; cross-cutting discipline).

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev — no upstream skip.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 wizard impl plan (which assumes this work shipped). Without this landing first, wizard impl plan Phase 0 will catch the gap AND Phase 3.2 wizard_persist_cms will fail to compile (calls undefined validate_identity + write_config_atomic).

Also flagged by R3 P0-2 (§5.13 schema version downgrade refusal — implemented in write_config_atomic's pre-write read-check).

title: Task 3 (PatProcess) amendment — render Pat's non-secret config at spawn time
description:
HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster impl (tuxlink-ln3 / Tasks 9/10/11/11.5). Surfaced by Codex R5 P1 on wizard-cluster spec adrev §7.3 item 4.

Pre-cred-handling: Pat's config.json was pre-existing or operator-managed; PatProcess just spawned the binary.
Post-cred-handling (PR-A merged): Pat's config.json no longer carries the WL2K password (deleted from cfg.SecureLoginPassword); Pat reads password from OS keyring. BUT Pat still expects its own config.json with non-secret fields (callsign, MBO address, transport selection).

Wizard now writes: (1) tuxlink config.json + (2) keyring entry. Wizard does NOT write Pat's config. Without something filling that gap, Pat spawns but has no callsign/MBO config = nonfunctional.

Scope: amend Task 3 (PatProcess in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs) to render Pat's config.json from tuxlink's config + the keyring callsign convention, at Pat-spawn time. Likely involves: read tuxlink config; build Pat config struct/json; write to Pat's expected path (~/.config/pat/config.json); spawn Pat.

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev (no upstream skip). Pipeline: brainstorm (small; design is settled by cred-handling spec + AMD-13 + this gap) → spec → 5-round adrev → spec revision → plan-write → 4-round plan-review → revision → TDD impl.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 impl plan (wizard cluster) — without this amendment landing, the wizard impl would ship but Pat couldn't operate. Wizard impl plan should document this as a HARD prerequisite if not yet done.

title: Cred-handling refactor: Pat reads WL2K from OS keyring (first tuxlink-pat patch)
description:
Per ADR 0011 §2 'First refactor target.' First agentic patch against tuxlink-pat after fork-setup completes.

Scope:
- Pat reads WL2K password from OS keyring (secret-service on Linux Gnome/KDE, Keychain on macOS, CredentialManager on Windows) instead of from config.json
- config.json retains callsign + non-secret config; secrets move out entirely
- Tuxlink wizard (Task 9) writes the password into the keyring directly via Rust keyring crate; wizard never touches a config.json path containing the password
- Live-CMS smoke binary (Task 6) reads from keyring at run time; no env vars, no temp files, no disk persistence beyond keyring

Upstream PR contribution: pursue after fork-side patch ships. Secret-service / Keychain / CredentialManager are platform-native and Pat upstream may welcome the security improvement.

title: ADR 0011: Fork Pat for tuxlink (cred-handling + ongoing refactor)
description:
Architectural decision triggered 2026-05-18 during Task 6 brainstorm. Cameron rejected continuing to depend on upstream Pat as-is because Pat persists WL2K passwords to its config.json in plaintext, which is a non-negotiable defect for a production system. Bandaging the symptom at each tuxlink call site (env-var workarounds, temp-file mitigations) is bandaid spiraling.

Decision (Cameron, inline): FORK Pat as a tuxlink-owned variant. Refactor cred-handling to use OS keyring as the first patch. Continue refactoring other Pat limitations as discovered, using the full build-robust-features pipeline per refactor. Open to upstream contributions where the fork's changes are accepted upstream.

This ADR captures: context (the bandaid pattern), decision (fork), alternatives considered (continue-as-is / Rust rewrite / hybrid), consequences (Go codebase to maintain, fork-sync discipline, v0.0.1 scope expansion to include fork-setup + cred refactor + plan amendments for affected tasks 5/6/9), and the transition plan.

Light pipeline: decision is made; ADR is a write-up + Codex round + PR. Full build-robust-features pipeline NOT required for the ADR itself (it's documentation of a made decision). Subsequent tasks (fork-setup, cred-refactor, plan amendments) DO use the full pipeline.

title: Bug: get_tuxlink_sessions.py disagrees with block-main-checkout-race.sh on live-session detection
description:
Per towhee-wren-aspen's 2026-05-18 reviewer response (dev/incidents/2026-05-18-main-checkout-race-hook-loop-reviewer-response.md, reviewer Action 3) — the script and the hook read the same .git/session-leases/*.json files with the same 30-min default cutoff, but in the 2026-05-18 incident the script reported 'No live tuxlink sessions in this repo' while the hook was treating a 4-minutes-ago lease as live. They should agree by construction; they don't. This is a port-quality bug independent of the CLAUDE.md framing fix in PR #39 — even after the framing fix, a script that reports 'no live sessions' while the hook denies will continue to confuse future agents who consult it for legitimate informational purposes (e.g., 'is anyone else working in this repo right now?').

Reviewer's likely causes, in priority order:

1. **Timestamp parse failure (most likely).** The hook writes lastSeenUtc using bash's date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ (6-digit microseconds + trailing Z). Python's datetime.fromisoformat is stricter than people expect about fractional-second precision and trailing-Z handling. The script's parse_iso_utc returns None on parse failure, and on None the lease is silently skipped — causing under-reporting without any error message.

   Reproduce:
       ts=$(jq -r .lastSeenUtc .git/session-leases/<any-session-id>.json)
       python3 -c "
       import sys
       sys.path.insert(0, '.claude/scripts')
       from get_tuxlink_sessions import parse_iso_utc
       print(repr(parse_iso_utc('$ts')))
       "

   If None: that's the bug. Fix: align format on both sides (widen parse_iso_utc OR constrain the hook).

2. **Lease dir path mismatch.** The hook resolves the lease dir via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (= .git/session-leases/). The script resolves via Path(__file__).resolve().parent / '..' / '..' / '.claude' / 'session-leases'. If these resolve to different paths, the script reads an empty/stale dir and reports 'no sessions.'

   Reproduce: print both dirs from a test harness; compare.

3. **File-extension/glob mismatch.** Less likely. The script's sorted(lease_dir.glob('*.json')) should pick up every *.json file. If the hook is writing to a dir without .json extension, the script silently skips.

Priority: P1 (blocks future port-quality regressions of same shape; cheap fix; safety-stack relevance). Should land as its own atomic PR after diagnosis confirms which cause applies. No dependencies on PRs #37/#38/#39/#40.

title: GPLv3 §6 corresponding-source offer for the bundled ardopcf sidecar

GPLv3 §6 conveyance duties attach to tuxlink as the distributor of that object code. Likely-sufficient fix: a standing line in README + release notes: 'ardopcf built unmodified from <pinned upstream URL> at commit cb2c4c1e...' satisfying §6(d). Independent of the Station Intelligence epic (jt9 is deliberately NOT bundled, so no equivalent duty arises there).

title: [fable] VARA Compression enum uses invalid wire tokens BINARY/AUTO — spec vocabulary is OFF/TEXT/FILES
description: src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/command.rs Compression enum renders COMPRESSION TEXT/BINARY/AUTO/OFF. Per the official VARA Protocol Native TNC Commands doc (EA5HVK, 2022-02-13) the valid modes are OFF / TEXT (default, recommended for Winlink) / FILES. BINARY and AUTO would be rejected with WRONG. Currently latent (no send site calls Compression), but the enum is the API surface future code will reach for. Also: WLE empirically sends COMPRESSION ON/OFF (VaraSession.cs:2511/2515) which VARA evidently also accepts — spec vocabulary preferred. While in there: consider sending WINLINK SESSION explicitly before CONNECT (it is the modem default, but explicit send is free insurance against a modem left in P2P/CHAT mode by another host app) and owning RETRIES via the cmd socket rather than inheriting VARA.ini (WLE sends RETRIES 10). Found by jay-marsh-yew during the command-stream audit vs WLE decompile + VARA spec.
notes: Design done + adversarial review complete (5 rounds, folded into spec). BRF Step 3 (writing-plans) + Step 4 (3 plan-review rounds: R1 Codex 10 findings, R2 Claude 13 findings incl. the frontend-connect-seam stub, R3 Claude re-attack caught 2 P1s the fold introduced) COMPLETE. Plan: docs/plans/2026-07-10-p2p-peer-model-plan.md (28 tasks + Task 23a, pushed 051179b6). EXECUTION BLOCKED only on operator's greenfield wire-walk flows (plan Definition-of-done section is PENDING; Iron Law forbids agent-drafting). title: MCP surface has no vara_open_session — agents can dial (vara_b2f_exchange) but cannot open the session it requires
description: mcp_ports.rs exposes set_vara / vara / vara_status / vara_probe / vara_b2f_exchange (egress-gated, calls modem_vara_b2f_exchange) / vara_stop_session / vara_install_*. But modem_vara_b2f_exchange requires an ALREADY-OPEN session (take_transport -> 'VARA session not open — press Open Session'), and no MCP tool calls vara_open_session. Net: an Elmer agent can configure VARA and invoke the dial, but every dial fails 'session not open' unless the operator first clicks Open Session in the GUI. Add a (non-egress, pre-air TCP-only, mirrors GUI posture) vara_open_session + vara_close_session pair to the agent port so agents can drive the full VARA flow. Found answering 'do agents have the MCP tools to test VARA' during tuxlink-yrrjq.

title: rig_status should read LIVE CAT (VFO/mode) through the gated session, not config-only
description: Discovered during the 2026-07-09 dialout investigation. modem_commands.rs comment (near ardop_tune_rig): a read-only CAT VFO/mode/PTT probe was REMOVED (Codex P1) because reading live VFO requires spawning rigctld, which opens an unauthenticated command-capable CAT server that would bypass the egress gate from an un-gated read-only tool. Consequence: rig_status reports config-derived state ONLY and never reads the live radio. So the Elmer agent has NO true 'read CAT' capability — a core gap for the agent-drives-the-station vision. FIX (the tracked follow-up the comment names): provide a safe live readout by either reusing a running session's already-open rig, or gating the read behind the same authority path. Make rig_status return live VFO/mode when a rig session is available.

title: Elmer run limits: remove 10-tool-turn cap; add configurable per-response timeout

Operator: this nag is not desired; remove it. Keep the existing per-turn timeout (guards a single stuck model call). ADD an operator-configurable per-response (whole-run) wall-clock timeout in the same config area as the per-turn timeout, so a run is bounded by time, not by an arbitrary tool count (preserves the ADR-0018 no-runaway bar). Backend: Limits + runner overall deadline; config_commands new field; session wiring. Frontend: config input mirroring the turn-timeout row; elmerModelConfig DTO. From session 2026-07-08 (basin-juniper-fjord).

title: Agent clear-channel (listen) check + duty-cycle cooldown pacing for sequenced dialing
description: The operator's live agent-mode workflow (2026-07-08) assumed capabilities the agent needs to run a supervised sequenced-dial test: (1) LISTEN to each frequency before dialing to avoid trampling traffic (clear-channel check — ear + BUSYDET; see clear_channel_check_before_tx memory), and (2) enforce >=60s dead-air cooldown between calls for antenna/PA duty-cycle + Phoenix-July thermal, regardless of channel-clear. Capability needed: an agent-usable clear-channel/listen surface + a pacing/cooldown mechanism the agent can honor across a dial sequence. Part of the agent perceive->operate loop. RADIO-1: listen is RX-only (safe); dialing stays operator-armed. Related: agent-operability cluster.

title: Elmer loops predict_path ~40+ times in one task — loop-control / batching gap
description: Observed in the live 0.85.0 VARA HF agent-mode transcript (2026-07-08): given 'compile 20 gateways + predict propagation', Elmer called predict_path dozens of times (~40+) across multiple operator 'Continue' prompts, burning turns/tokens without converging. Signals either (a) no batch/multi-station predict surface so the agent one-shots per station in a loop, or (b) missing loop-control guidance so the agent doesn't know when it has enough. Investigate: does predict_path support batch input? Should there be a find+predict combined surface, or agent guidance to bound the sweep? Efficiency/operability, not correctness. Related: agent-operability cluster (tuxlink-7ppfq et al).

title: Agent-invokable start/attach active modem (vara_start) — local-vs-remote launch design
description:
Split out of tuxlink-7ppfq per operator (2026-07-08). The agent should be able to START VARA when the reachability probe says it's down (it can already INSTALL VARA, so gating start is incoherent). But adrev (round R5 + Codex) found this is NOT 'expose existing launch code' and needs its own brainstorm:

- Launch primitive exists ONLY in the vendored shell engine (wine-vara-setup 'launch'/'stop', pipeline.sh:87-116, checkpoints.sh:48-72). NO Rust/MCP caller — net-new adapter + guards.
- TOPOLOGY: agent runs on the Pi (aarch64); operator's VARA runs on REMOTE R2 (x86_64). Probe can target a remote host:cmd_port, but a launcher spawns a LOCAL process — cannot start remote VARA locally. On the Pi itself the engine hard-refuses launch (box64/ARM, env.sh:10-19) and VARA TX is box64-blocked.
- Prefix mismatch: engine expects ~/.local/share/wine-vara/prefix/drive_c/'VARA HF'/; operator's real install is ~/.wine-vara/drive_c/VARA/. detect_vara would refuse a running VARA.
- Needs: idempotency (probe-first, launch-if-down), detached lifecycle (survives app restart), bounded start-then-poll sized for box64 cold-start, structured AlreadyRunning|Started|Failed result. RADIO-1: launch transmits nothing (confirmed safe).

DEPENDS ON tuxlink-7ppfq (needs the reachability probe as the idempotency signal). Brainstorm the local-vs-remote host question FIRST.

title: Agent audio-device inspection + VARA audio-setup guidance capability
description: Surfaced by the 0.85.0 agent-mode test (deliberate: a common 'how do I set this up?' request). Test step 6 asked the agent to check available audio devices and surface what VARA Input/Output should be. The agent's audio surface was too coarse: it collapsed devices to generic names ('all enumerate as USB PnP Sound Device') and fell back to proposing a manual unplug test, unable to distinguish the radio interface. But the disambiguating detail exists and is cheap to get (verified over SSH): USB VID:PID (e.g. 0d8c:013a vs 0d8c:0013), USB port path, ALSA card name, and capture/playback in-use state via /proc/asound + fuser. The two USB cards on R2 ARE distinguishable by all of these. Capability needed: an agent-usable audio-device inspection surface rich enough (name + USB VID:PID + bus/port path + capture/playback availability/in-use) that the agent can identify the likely radio interface (DRA-100 class) and advise what VARA's Input AND Output should be set to (same full-duplex card). Do NOT hardcode device identity; give the agent the data + a method (incl. the disambiguation approach) so it can guide the user. Operator will ID devices; agent must be able to help a NEW user do so. Related: tuxlink-7ppfq (agent live-state perception), tuxlink-z2nwx (agent report-to-file).
notes: BUILT + PUSHED (esker-wren-towhee, 2026-07-08) as part of PR #1056 (bd-tuxlink-z2nwx/agent-capabilities), combined with z2nwx. Contract 4: widened ardop_list_audio_devices with per-USB-card cards (name/alsa/index/vid_pid/bus_path/in_use) projected from winlink::ax25::devices snapshot (pure project_audio_cards, fixture-tested) + best-effort in-use via probe_device_busy. Disambiguation METHOD ships as tuxlink://playbook/audio-setup guidance resource (never a code ranking; guides user to find THEIR card). CI verify GREEN. Awaiting operator merge+build. Do NOT close until validated.

title: Add MCP tool for agent print / print-to-file (report generation)
description: During the 0.85.0 agent-mode test, Elmer had no filesystem-write/print/PDF tool - its only disk-write is message_attachment_save (extracts an existing attachment from a received message). So it could not produce a report file (step 9 wanted a report compiled + staged); it fell back to staging an unsent Winlink message. Operator: 'add an MCP tool surface to allow the agent to print, or print to file. That's missing and we should have that.' Scope: an agent-invokable tool to write a report/document to disk (e.g. a text/markdown/PDF file under a safe location) so agent runs can produce artifacts the operator can open, independent of the Winlink outbox. Consider: safe path sandboxing, format (md/txt vs PDF), naming, and whether it surfaces the path back to the agent+operator.
notes: RESOLVED (esker-wren-towhee, 2026-07-08). PR #1056 was CONFLICTING after 7ppfq (#1055) merged to main and collided on shared MCP files. Merged origin/main (fa2bbef5), resolved 2 conflicts keeping BOTH change-sets (scenario_ports imports; mcp_ports test submodules z2nwx/audio_77seh/modem_sot). PR now MERGEABLE/CLEAN; verify + build-linux + ECT build all GREEN both arches. Delivers Contract 3 (print/export) + Contract 4 (77seh audio). Awaiting operator merge+build. Do NOT close until validated.

title: Bump pbf to 5.x — BLOCKED on re-vendoring protomaps-leaflet (declined #875)
description: Dependabot #875 bumps pbf 4→5 (major). pbf decodes protobuf map tiles (basemap/PMTiles/vector tiles). Major bump → likely API/ESM changes. Fails CI verify+build on both arches. Check basemap/PMTiles/vector-tile usage sites, update call sites, CI green, merge. Agent: arroyo-knoll-sorrel
notes: INVESTIGATED + DECLINED #875 (session arroyo-knoll-sorrel, 2026-07-07). pbf 5 is a breaking change, not a bump: default export removed, Pbf class split into named PbfReader/PbfWriter, pure ESM. Our only direct consumer is the pre-built minified vendored bundle src/vendor/protomaps-leaflet/index.js, which does `import yt from "pbf"` (default) expecting pbf 4's combined Pbf. Reproduced locally: `pnpm build` fails — 'default is not exported by pbf@5.1.0'. main already runs pbf@4 (direct, for the vendor) + pbf@5 (transitive via @mapbox/vector-tile@3.0.0) side-by-side and that works; bumping the DIRECT dep to 5 is what breaks it. Absorbing pbf 5 requires re-vendoring protomaps-leaflet against pbf 5 (or dropping the direct pbf dep) — a separate task, no security/functional driver. Do this when protomaps-leaflet is next re-vendored. Agent: arroyo-knoll-sorrel

title: Tuxlink setup-wizard step: provision VARA HF via wine-vara-setup
description:
Fast-follow to the new sibling repo github.com/cameronzucker/wine-vara-setup (built 2026-07-07 by hemlock-falcon-thistle). Add a step INSIDE Tuxlink's existing first-run/setup flow (NOT a new wizard/panel — that was explicitly rejected as over-scope) that provisions VARA HF at prep time.

Flow (see the repo's docs/tuxlink-integration.md for the frozen contract):
- Setup-time, online: open the rosmodem / Winlink software pages in the USER'S OWN BROWSER (never a server-side fetch; winlink.org IP-blocks automated probes). Offer a file-picker for the downloaded VARA .exe (auto-detect ~/Downloads/VARA*setup*.exe as a convenience). Spawn: wine-vara-setup install --installer <path> --yes --json --autostart. Render the JSONL stream (event=hello contract:1, then checkpoint running/done/skipped/failed by id+index/total, then summary) as native progress. Do NOT parse detail for control flow.
- Run-time, field/offline: VARA autostarts via systemd --user; Tuxlink only connects to 127.0.0.1:8300/8301 as it does for any external VARA. Posture unchanged (Tuxlink does not manage VARA at runtime).
- Bundle bin/ + lib/ from wine-vara-setup into the Tuxlink .deb (MIT, our code) so provisioning logic is present offline; only payloads (wine, vb6run, user's VARA download) need internet at prep.
- Setup surface reflects VARA configured/not via: wine-vara-setup status --json (offline, exit 0 iff ready). No separate readiness dashboard.

Constraints: x86_64 only (ARM/box64 VARA is TX-blocked); never bundle/download VARA; never contact winlink.org server-side.
notes: BUILT + PR #1035 READY (branch bd-tuxlink-w7212/vara-setup-wizard). All 3 wire-walk flows wired: fresh-install wizard, VARA-panel entry (upgrade path), Elmer MCP tools + tuxlink://playbook/vara-wine-setup. CI verify green both arches @cdb57091. Flow 2b (schema-v6 auto-prompt) dropped by operator. Pending: operator merge + real on-air VARA validation (separate).

Every datum annotated with capture recency + band dwell so a swept sample is never over-trusted. Works whether or not any window is open (queries the service, not the UI).

Fold in (additive, gateway finder untouched): live-openness dots on band chips, FT-8 heat map-layer toggle, Live-decodes rail tab, collapsible waterfall+decode strip (Canvas2D waterfall + backend FFT stream) along panel bottom. Contextual modem-pane entry labels (Find a Gateway [CMS] / Find a Station [P2P/radio-only]); Tools menu -> 'Station Intelligence...'; ambient FT-8 listening badge in DashboardRibbon. Must render states: no-device/device-busy/clock-unsynced/waiting-first-slot/band-dead/decoding. Exit gate: waterfall perf budget under Pi software-GL (column cadence/FFT size/event batch) proving paint never starves decode. Mock: dev/scratch/ft8-integrated-render.png.

title: L2: live audio capture + slot-timing decode service

CAT band-QSY for sweep (default: hold one band; sweep opt-in round-robin). Define behavior for device-busy (yield to active VARA/ARDOP, surface device-busy), device-absent (refuse to start clearly), cat-absent (fixed single band, no sweep), clock-unsynced (warn, decode unreliable).

title: L1: FT-8 decode service on managed jt9 — slot WAV in, structured decodes out (fallback decision 2026-07-07)
description: Supersedes the clean-room pure-Rust engine per the operator's recorded L0 NO-GO decision (B+C, 2026-07-07): fall back to the jt9 dependency; tuxlink-ft8 stays a reference artifact. Deliverable: a decode-engine service that takes a 15s slot capture, writes/streams it as WAV, invokes /usr/bin/jt9 (WSJT-X Debian package — ECT-compatible managed-binary pattern like Dire Wolf; GPL boundary = subprocess invocation ONLY, never link, vendor, or read wsjtr/WSJT-X source) with FT8 mode + fixed decode depth, AP off, and parses stdout into structured decodes {slot UTC, SNR dB, DT s, audio freq Hz, message, parsed calls/grid}. Subprocess discipline: timeout + kill on overrun, jt9-absent detected at startup (clean 'wsjtx not installed' state, not a crash), version probe, PATH + config override for the binary. REUSE: the M3 crate's src/oracle.rs jt9-log parser (tested, hash-class-aware) as the parse layer's starting point; the committed SDR WAV fixtures (40m ordinary 5 msgs, 20m quiet 2 msgs) become e2e fixtures with jt9's own output as ground truth. NOTE: jt9 IS installed on the dev Pi and runs on a WAV in seconds — this layer supports genuine local red-green TDD, unlike src-tauri builds.
notes: READY FOR EXECUTION 2026-07-10 (slate-fox-tanager). Design delta v2 (5 adversarial rounds: 4 Claude lenses + Codex, all empirical) at docs/design/2026-07-10-station-intel-jt9-engine-delta.md; implementation plan (3 review rounds incl. scratch-crate compilation + real-jt9 runs) at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-station-intel-l1-jt9-decode-service.md. Both committed on branch bd-tuxlink-b026z.2/station-intel-jt9 (worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-b026z.2-station-intel-jt9, claimed by this issue). Execute via subagent-driven-development, tasks strictly in order, from the worktree. Architecture: new std-only leaf crate src-tauri/tuxlink-jt9 — locally red-green-able on the Pi (tuxlink-ft8 precedent). L2 (b026z.3) owns slot scheduler/tmpfs/backpressure and consumes the N=5/k=20 thresholds pinned in types.rs.

title: L0: clean-room FT-8 decoder SPIKE (throwaway decision gate)
description: Pure-Rust FT-8 decode of a known SDR/WAV capture (rustfft/realfft), diffed vs WSJT-X AND wsjtr on the same audio. NO UI, no live audio. Clean-room discipline: wsjtr is a PRE-BUILT BINARY ORACLE ONLY (never clone its GPL source); read only ft8_lib (MIT) + RustFT8 (MIT) + QEX/WB2FKO papers. EXIT GATE: recover >=85% of WSJT-X's decoded MESSAGES (callsign-pair+payload match, not raw count), ZERO false decodes (no message absent from both oracles), on EACH of an ordinary AND a crowded-band capture. If unreachable, revisit depend-on-wsjtr fallback before sinking L-effort.
notes: L0 SPIKE OUTCOME 2026-07-07 (bison-delta-birch): NO-GO. M3 real-capture parity = 1/5 (40m ordinary) + 0/2 (20m quiet) vs jt9 5/5 + 2/2 on the same WAVs, zero false. Root cause = weak-signal coarse TIME localization (t0 mislocated beyond fine-refine ±40ms on -14..-19dB signals), NOT the sync floor (floor-free targeted decode still fails). Operator decision B+C: fall back to jt9/wsjtr dependency; save the built M3 work (hash-table population + within-slot dedup + oracle comparator harness, all green, PR on branch station-intel-ft8-m3); revisit only if dependency problematic or project matures. Missing lever = robust sub-sample time-sync (ft8_lib/WB2FKO sync8d-class).

title: Station Intelligence: fold native passive FT-8 into Find-a-Station
description: Rename Find-a-Station -> 'Station Intelligence' and fold in a native, clean-room, PASSIVE (RX-only) FT-8 band-listening layer as a second live lens beside VOACAP gateway ranking (two lenses, never blended; gateway finder untouched). Answers 'who can I communicate with, on what band, right now' for both the operator (waterfall+decodes+traffic on one surface) and agents (machine-readable band intelligence over MCP). Contextual modem-pane entry stays task-framed (Find a Gateway / Find a Station). Clean-room pure-Rust decoder (no C FFI, no GPL wsjtr dependency); WSJT-X + wsjtr are pre-built black-box test oracles only. Design doc: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-ant8s-ardop-connect-fixes-design-20260705-034957-passive-ft8-listener.md
notes: Direction update 2026-07-10 (slate-fox-tanager): L0 spike closed NO-GO (see .1 notes + dev/handoffs/2026-07-07-bison-delta-birch-ft8-l0-nogo-jt9-fallback.md). Engine = managed jt9 per operator decision; .2 rewritten accordingly. Design doc's Approach-C rejection is overridden by the operator's post-evidence call; the agent-legibility wedge (MCP structured band intel) survives — it never depended on WHO decodes, only on the structured in-process state. .7 provenance guard rescopes to: no GPL source (wsjtr/WSJT-X) vendored or linked; jt9 subprocess only. M3 worktree disposed 2026-07-10 (transcripts archived to .claude/worktree-archives).

title: Add agent workflows for Elmer (named/reusable multi-step task sequences) — needs brainstorm to scope
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (kingfisher-cove-yew session): 'We should add agent workflows. An absurd escalation for a Linux-native Winlink client but...we're here. No reason to half-ass it.'

INTENT (as understood, to be confirmed in brainstorm): reusable/named multi-step Elmer agent workflows — encode a common operator task as a defined sequence the in-app agent can execute end-to-end, rather than re-eliciting it each time. The e7z7d wire-walk flow is the archetype: 'find the closest 80m VARA gateways to me -> compose a short report -> stage to <recipient> -> send via armed CMS.' A workflow would package that (with the arm/taint egress gate honored at the Send step) as a one-invocation, observable, resumable unit.

RELATES: recent Plan-3 'hands-off execution harness' design (commit 1e274958 docs(mcp)) is the likely substrate/predecessor — agent workflows may be its user-facing layer. Also relates to the observability/grounded-generative thesis (office-hours 2026-07-05): a workflow is a deterministic scaffold around generative steps.

NOT YET SCOPED — this is a captured direction, not a spec. Next step: office-hours/brainstorm to define what a 'workflow' is (declarative recipe? agent-authored + saved? templated?), how it surfaces in the UI, how egress/RADIO gates compose with a multi-step run, and whether it feeds the distillation (workflows as training scenarios). Do NOT build before brainstorm.

title: Global metric/imperial units preference (distance, temperature, etc.) — switchable, imperial default
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (kingfisher-cove-yew session, raised during e7z7d): units are hardcoded and inconsistent across the app. The station catalog is miles-hardcoded (StationRail.tsx:147 renders '${distMi} mi'; StationFinderControls search radius in miles; kmToMi ×0.621371 used across src/catalog/). APRS weather is Fahrenheit-hardcoded and unmodifiable. There is NO global unit-preference setting (no config field; grep of src-tauri/src/config.rs finds none). Audience is largely American → imperial (miles, °F) strongly preferred as DEFAULT, but units should be SWITCHABLE metric<->imperial globally.

SCOPE: a single global units preference (config-backed) driving all unit-bearing displays — distances (catalog/station finder/reachability), temperature (APRS weather; operator is fine with °F as the default but wants switchability), and any other unit-bearing surfaces (altitude, speed if present). Default = imperial. The Elmer agent surface should respect it too (see e7z7d: find_stations already serves BOTH distance_km + distance_mi, so the agent can pick the operator's preferred unit once this setting exists; the system prompt can key off it).

NOTE: e7z7d ships find_stations distance in BOTH km+mi unconditionally (no dependency on this issue). This issue is the product-wide toggle. Precedent/context: catalog miles-hardcoded, APRS weather °F-hardcoded. Relates: tuxlink-e7z7d (agent distance already dual-unit).

title: [bug] Elmer pane: 'New conversation/session' header button still doesn't render on converged build (after first-round token fix)
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (kingfisher-cove-yew session): the 'New conversation/session' icon button in the Elmer pane header STILL does not render on the converged (WebKitGTK) build, even after the first-round fix attempt. So the affordance to reset Elmer context is not reachable in the shipping UI.

HISTORY:
- Feature: 974fc134 feat(elmer): add 'New conversation' reset to reclaim context (header icon) — tuxlink-vbv2k, PR #1005.
- First-round fix (did NOT resolve it): 76bc2196 fix(elmer): header buttons reference undefined tokens — invisible new-conversation icon. Intended to make the icon render by fixing undefined CSS tokens; operator reports it is still not rendering.
- Existing regression marker: src/elmer/ElmerPane.css:753 comment already flags a 'new conversation button regression'.

COMPONENT: src/elmer/ElmerPane.tsx:1245 (aria-label='New conversation', self-emphasis at >=85% context), styles src/elmer/ElmerPane.css:75 (icon button, speech-bubble+plus) and :753.

WHY PRIOR FIX MISSED IT: jsdom unit tests for this button PASS (src/elmer/ElmerPane.test.tsx:184 '<ElmerPane> -- New conversation reset') — jsdom does not evaluate WebKitGTK layout/CSS, so an invisible-on-device button is green in CI. Same failure class as the tuxlink-7durx/#1014 re-arm popover 'needs a converged-build visual eyeball' gap. ROOT-CAUSE must be done on a real converged build (WebKitGTK render harness / grim / Chromium CDP per project debugging playbooks), NOT jsdom — reproduce the non-render first, then fix, then verify on-device.

Relates: tuxlink-vbv2k (original feature), tuxlink-7durx (sibling converged-build CSS-render gap).

title: Elmer: predict_path frequencies_khz optional (default dials) — DEFERRED, gated on FT-8 reachability surfaces (was e7z7d FIX B)
description:
Split out of tuxlink-e7z7d (2026-07-05, kingfisher-cove-yew brainstorm). e7z7d now ships FIX A only (find_stations distance/bearing). FIX B — making predict_path callable with just rx_grid — is TABLED at operator direction because the FT-8 epic (tuxlink-u3m0g) is being built in parallel and the band/propagation surfaces it exposes will inform the correct default-dials / band-awareness behavior; inventing a dial table now risks rework.

GROUNDING (origin/main, verified 2026-07-05): predict_path (router.rs PredictRequestParams ~1159) takes rx_grid (required) + frequencies_khz (required Vec<f64>) + gateway_antenna (optional). There is NO band/mode param and NO standard-dial-frequency table anywhere in the repo — dials come from the live Winlink directory (GatewayDto.frequencies_khz). So 'default to standard dials for the requested band/mode' has nothing to key off today. Weak models emit null/invalid args for the required frequencies_khz (the original bug symptom).

WHEN UNBLOCKED: make frequencies_khz optional; when omitted, default to an FT-8/u3m0g-informed canonical HF dial set (operator to bless the actual dials — he runs VARA HF on the G90 firsthand). predict_path stays the reliability/propagation tool; distance already moved to find_stations via e7z7d FIX A. Also feeds benchmark tuxlink-ylmwj. Relates: tuxlink-e7z7d (parent), tuxlink-u3m0g (FT-8 epic, gating), tuxlink-3ij0v (VARA dial gaps).

title: Audit: mirror shell-capabilities used during the ARDOP saga into the Elmer MCP tool surface
description: OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (office-hours): the main-loop shell-capable agent has an enormous implicit tool surface (cat configs, rigctl, poke ardopcf, grep backend, read logs, PTT tests) that Elmer's embedded models (qwen/gemini/local-20b/120b) do NOT have — they only have tuxlink-mcp-core tools. Every low-level thing the shell agent did during the ARDOP debugging that ISN'T an MCP tool is an invisible tool-surface gap (invisible because the agent we watched never hit it). CAT rig-meters is confirmed gap #1 (filed separately). ACTION: walk the ARDOP saga (handoffs 2026-06-25/26/30, dev/bug-hunts/2026-06-26-ardop-session-restart-*) and enumerate every shell-level capability exercised for diagnostics; each one absent from the MCP surface is a candidate tool. Prioritize the station-diagnostic ones (rig meters, modem/ardopcf state, config reads, session/log inspection). Feeds the grounded-generative architecture (substrate half) + the observability thesis (most agent failures this session were 'agent couldn't see state the shell agent could'). PARITY GATE: each new tool lands in BOTH mcp-core router AND the elmer-distill sim so the retrain teaches the shipping surface.

title: Elmer: CAT rig-meters MCP tool (SWR/ALC/RFPOWER/PO) — station-health grounding the agent lacks
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05 (office-hours, observability thesis): the in-app Elmer agent has NO way to read the radio's live meters, even though the main-loop (shell-capable) agent read them trivially via rigctl during the ARDOP saga. That shell capability masked the gap: watching the shell agent debug, we concluded CAT 'didn't need a tool.' It does. VERIFIED on this Pi (hamlib 4.6.2): the Xiegu G90 backend's get_level set includes RFPOWER, SWR, ALC, COMP, RAWSTR (rigctl -m 3088 --dump-caps). So Tuxlink can read power/SWR/ALC over the CAT/rigctl path it already has — no external meter needed for the basics.

BUILD: add a read-only station-health MCP tool (e.g. rig_meters, or extend rig_status) exposing live SWR / ALC / RFPOWER (RFPOWER_METER_WATTS where supported) / PO via hamlib get_level, so the agent can answer 'is this station transmitting correctly?' and diagnose (e.g. 'SWR 3:1 on 40m, check antenna/feedline') instead of flailing. This is the substrate half of grounded-generative option C at the RF layer.

Caveats (operator on-air validation, RADIO-1): token presence != live update during TX for every rig; and serial contention (modem holds the port during a session) means the read must degrade to null gracefully, same as rig_status. External in-line meters (LP-100A reference-grade + networkable via VCP + documented protocol; PowerMaster II fastest + hardware alarms) are the independent ground-truth layer for a later phase, not required for v1. PARITY GATE: mirror this tool into the elmer-distill simulator/tool_surface so the retrain (tuxlink-48nyh) teaches the agent to use the surface that actually ships. Relates: rig_status (existing), tuxlink-8f1yv (arm-state discoverability, same class).

title: Elmer: tainted send-authority re-arm popover renders unstyled — buttons mashed, no flex layout
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05: with armed send-authority + taint triggered, clicking the 'LOCKED' send-authority chip opens a popover whose re-arm box ('Your chat will be cleared. Anything Elmer staged in your Outbox is kept.') has buttons mashed together, not matching the normal (disarmed) arm-preset dropdown formatting.

ROOT CAUSE (src/shell/EgressArmControl.tsx tainted branch ~168-197 + src/shell/AppShell.css ~509-554): the normal disarmed branch lays presets out via '.egress-presets { display:flex; gap:7px }' with '.egress-arm-button { flex:1 }'. The tainted re-arm branch instead uses '.egress-rearm-presets' (NO CSS rule -> not a flex container, so the flex:1 buttons collapse/mash) and a primary button classed 'egress-rearm-button egress-arm-button--primary' that does NOT include the base 'egress-arm-button' class and has NO CSS of its own (no padding/border). '.dash-egress-locked' only sets error-red text + no layout, so the start-button / presets-row / consequence stack with no spacing.

FIX (CSS-only, mirror the proven pattern): make '.dash-egress-locked' a flex column with gap; give '.egress-rearm-presets' the same 'display:flex; gap:7px' as '.egress-presets'; style '.egress-rearm-button' as a full-width primary action (padding/border/radius, accent); style '.egress-rearm-consequence' as faint help text like '.egress-pop-help'. Keep data-testids. Verify visually (WebKitGTK render harness / operator) since CSS layout isn't jsdom-testable.

Found during the 2026-07-05 Elmer testing pass (alongside 0tuc3/a1xwx PR #1012, qhe8n PR #1013).

title: Elmer: detected-models list overflows viewport (native <select>) — build type-to-filter scrollable combobox
description:
OPERATOR 2026-07-05: 'the model list, when there are many, goes off the bottom of the screen, making some models which render too far down unselectable.' Reproduces with OpenRouter (~300 models). Root cause: the detected-models control in GetKeyCard.tsx (~421) is a NATIVE <select> whose option popup, under WebKitGTK with hundreds of options, extends past the viewport bottom without usable scrolling. ModelForm likely has the same native-select pattern for its detect results.

FIX (operator chose type-to-filter + scrollable-capped, 2026-07-05): replace the native <select> with a custom combobox — a text input that filters the model list as you type, backed by a height-capped (max-height + overflow-y:auto) scrollable listbox that never exceeds the viewport. Keyboard accessible (arrow/enter/escape), data-testid preserved for the existing GetKeyCard tests. Applies to BOTH GetKeyCard and ModelForm detect-results selects. vitest-coverable locally.

Relates to the Elmer-UX-too-generative discussion (operator 2026-07-05) and the OpenRouter provider experience (tuxlink-fqsfd). Found during the 2026-07-05 testing pass alongside tuxlink-0tuc3 / tuxlink-a1xwx (PR #1012).

title: Extract the gpt-oss MoE re-fuser to a standalone public repo (once empirically proven)
description: Operator wants the novel MoE re-fuser (per-expert-LoRA'd gpt-oss -> canonical fused servable checkpoint) published to its own public durable repo, for community benefit + resume line items. Build it decoupled from tuxlink ham-domain code (standalone package: key-gate + stacking re-fuser + content-oracle verifier). GATE extraction on empirical proof: key-gate PASS + content-oracle diff match on a real served checkpoint (do NOT publish an unproven fuser). Consider permissive license (Apache-2.0/MIT) for adoption vs tuxlink AGPLv3 — operator decides. References the ecosystem gap: unsloth #3701 (unfixed), #3817, llama.cpp #15146, vLLM #42242. Parent: tuxlink-pt2xo.

title: Re-fuser: per-expert-LoRA'd gpt-oss -> canonical fused servable checkpoint (unblocks ALL fast serving)
description: THE central blocker for serving fine-tuned gpt-oss MoE (vLLM/SGLang/llama.cpp all need the fused canonical layout). unsloth trains via per-expert LoRA (experts.gate_up_projs.<i>/down_projs.<i>); unsloth's own re-fusing save (save_pretrained_merged) is BROKEN for dense per-expert LoRA (unsloth #3701, unfixed). Build a re-fuser that takes (base + per-expert LoRA/merged checkpoint) and emits a clean FUSED canonical gpt-oss checkpoint (stack per-expert tensors -> (num_experts,...) with correct transpose/interleave; rename mlp.router.linear.weight -> mlp.router.weight; handle bnb-4bit/MXFP4 quant). Verifiable via (a) mechanical key-gate (index.json must show experts.gate_up_proj/down_proj stacked + mlp.router.weight, NOT gate_up_projs.0.. or router.linear.weight) and (b) correctness oracle (diff completions vs the slow in-process transformers server). Scoping fork to settle empirically: FIX unsloth #3701 upstream (minimal, community-contributable) vs STANDALONE re-fuser (more reusable). Once it exists, every future fine-tune serves. This is the 'iterative/recursive LLM infra' project: AI-agent-built deploy tooling for fine-tuned open MoE models. Full context: dev/handoffs/2026-07-04-moss-oak-lichen-120b-trained-serving-wall.md (ADREV VERDICT). Prereq for the elmer-120b Tuxlink hookup (tuxlink-48nyh).
notes: SERVED + VALIDATED 2026-07-05 (peregrine-cypress-towhee): full chain works end-to-end. merged-fused v2 (with sinks) -> convert_hf_to_gguf q8_0 (124GB) -> llama-server /v1 (25/37 layers GPU, 80GB VRAM, healthy). BUG FOUND+FIXED mid-run: run_refuse_live dropped gpt-oss attention sinks (self_attn.sinks are bare Parameters, not .weight) -> llama.cpp 'missing tensor blk.0.attn_sinks.weight'; fixed with a named_parameters straggler pass (passthrough 435->471). Content-oracle: auto char-ratio flagged DIVERGENCE (0.05-0.47) but that is a representation artifact (as-trained capture has harmony analysis CoT; served /v1 is final-only; q8 vs bf16 greedy + markdown formatting). EYEBALL of full pairs: served answers are correct + semantically equivalent to as-trained on all 6 (tuner impedance-match, K=Kilo, QRM=interference, Winlink=radio-email, TX-checklist). Coherent+correct = fuse structurally sound (a bad transpose/dropped-expert would be garbage). CAVEAT: probe prompts are general ham knowledge, don't discriminate tuned-vs-base; run the frozen agentic acceptance gate vs the endpoint to prove deltas moved behavior (follow-up). pt2xo re-fuser is PROVEN: adapter->fused(gate PASS)->GGUF->served coherent.

title: run_train.py --precision bf16 blocked for gpt-oss expert-LoRA (MXFP4 unpack is 4bit-only)
description: Loading unsloth/gpt-oss-20b with load_in_4bit=False,dtype=bf16 throws 'no per-expert modules' — MXFP4 experts only unpack to per-expert Linear4bit in the 4-bit load path; in bf16 they stay fused so the expert-LoRA regex finds nothing. iter-2 ran 4bit r32 (fallback caught it). bf16 'use the hardware' lever is unavailable until the bf16 fused-expert layout is discovered + targeted in _attach_lora, or accept 4bit base. Do NOT default --precision to bf16. Memory: project-gptoss-bf16-base-expert-lora-incompatible. tuxlink-6zkb6 iter-2.

title: run_serve.py pollutes the training torch env (converter pip-install -> torch +cpu)
description: run_serve.py ensure_converter pip-installs llama.cpp convert_hf_to_gguf requirements, which downgraded torch to 2.11.0+cpu (CPU-only) + torchvision mismatch on the pod. Breaks the NEXT unsloth training run (v1 trained fine before serve; v2's train failed with ImportError). Worked around by reinstalling torch==2.10.0+cu128 + torchvision==0.25.0. Fix: isolate the converter pip-install in a venv, OR run all trains before any serve, OR pin+restore torch around the converter install. tuxlink-6zkb6 iter-2.

title: [feature] Elmer: pre-warm the model KV cache on pane open so the first message is snappy (esp. local 20b)
description:
MOTIVATION: A local 20b on modest hardware (e.g. Framework 13 Radeon 780M) takes ~1 min to PREFILL the static prefix (ELMER_SYSTEM_PROMPT + ~50 tool schemas, ~5-8k tokens) before the first token. Decode after that is interactive. The prefix is byte-identical every conversation, so that ~1 min is recomputing something that never changes.

APPROACH (Level 1, app-side pre-warm): when the Elmer pane opens / a model is configured (and after a New conversation reset, tuxlink-vbv2k), fire a throwaway completion containing ONLY the static prefix with max_tokens~1, discard the output. llama.cpp/ollama caches the prefix KV; the operator's first REAL message then only prefills their few tokens -> snappy first response.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Non-blocking: pre-warm runs in the background; never blocks the UI or the input.
- Cancel/skip if the operator sends a real message before pre-warm completes (don't queue behind it; a real send supersedes).
- Re-warm on model switch and after New conversation (both reset the cache).
- Byte-stable prefix: the warm-up request MUST use the exact same system prompt + tool serialization as a real turn, or the cache won't hit. Tool-surface drift busts it (same tax as the fine-tune).
- keep_alive: document that OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE must be long enough (e.g. -1 or 30m) that the model+cache aren't evicted between warm-up and first send; possibly surface a hint.
- Idempotent / rate-limited: don't spam warm-ups on rapid re-renders.
- Only meaningful for local/loopback-ish endpoints with prompt caching; harmless (but skip to save tokens/cost) for cloud endpoints — gate on loopback/local or make it opt-in for remote.

OUT OF SCOPE (Level 2, infra): llama.cpp slot save/restore (--slot-save-path + /slots action=save|restore) for a PERSISTENT prefix cache across reboots (near-zero cold prefill ever). Requires running llama-server directly, bypassing ollama. Note as the stronger DEFCON option; not a Tuxlink feature.

CAVEAT: pre-warm cuts prefill LATENCY, not KV-cache SIZE -- it does not help the long-context crash (that is the New-conversation reset's job).

title: [feature] Elmer: 'New conversation' reset (speech-bubble+plus icon in header) to reclaim context, esp. local/Custom
description:
PROBLEM: Elmer has no way to start a fresh conversation. The transcript (items) is in-memory only (useElmer.ts:272) and the backend ElmerSession.conversation grows every turn, filling a local model's tiny context until replies degrade. Every other model GUI has a 'new chat' affordance; local agents need it MORE because context is vanishingly small.

SCOPE (operator-chosen): reset-only. No saved-session history (no disk persistence).

UX: industry-standard speech-bubble-with-a-plus ICON (26px) in the elmer-header, next to the existing 24px close button (ElmerPane.tsx:1228). Borrowing the universal new-chat glyph (ChatGPT/Claude/etc.). aria-label 'New conversation'.

BEHAVIOR:
- Frontend: clear items -> [], reset streaming buffers + ContextMeter, keep model/endpoint config.
- Backend: NEW Tauri command elmer_new_conversation -> reset SessionInner.conversation = Conversation::new("") and bump generation (invalidate any in-flight turn). model_config + keyring untouched. Clearing only the UI would NOT reclaim the model's context -- the backend reset is the load-bearing part.
- No confirm modal (modals banned; you reset constantly on local models). Inline 'Started a new conversation - Undo' toast ~5s (recoverable since nothing is persisted). [Optional: operator may prefer one-click-no-undo.]
- Context-pressure emphasis: when ContextMeter is high, the icon self-emphasizes (amber) + a 'context nearly full - replies may degrade' chip. Highest-value for local/Custom.
- During a running turn: cancel-then-reset (one action), not disabled.

MOCK: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/elmer-newconv-render2.png (3 states: normal / context-filling / just-reset).

BUILD SIZE (small): one header icon button + reducer clear (useElmer), one Tauri command (session.rs reset + config_commands or commands.rs wiring), ContextMeter reset, vitest for the clear/undo + a Rust test for the conversation reset. Related: tuxlink-2ouqf (taint-clear semantics) -- new-conversation does NOT touch egress arm/taint state (separate concern) unless decided otherwise.

title: Elmer-20b tool-use distillation epic (gate ladder: G0 null → G1/G3 pilots → A LoRA)
description: Distill gpt-oss-120b's long-horizon agentic tool-use into a LoRA-tuned gpt-oss-20b so Elmer runs interactively on operator hardware (Framework 13) while orchestrating multi-tool sessions (radio debug/config, emcomm traffic, docs help-desk) without stalling mid-plan. Design spec (Codex-reviewed): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-elmer-20b-tooluse-distillation-design.md. Cheapest-first gate ladder: G0 prompt-only baseline (may need no training) → G1 teacher-ceiling pilot → G2 stateful judge → G3 cost pilot → A LoRA prove-run. A100 SXM pod staged+validated (spec §12). Next: writing-plans for pre-registered margins, coverage cells, G0 baseline, judge negative-test corpus.
notes: PR #1003 MERGED to main 2026-07-02. G0/G1/G3 pilot RAN on A100 (24-scenario bank, seed=1): raw-20b 1/24 (4%), G0-scaffold-20b 23/24 (95%), 120b-teacher 0/24 (0%); ~9-12s/task (G3 timing). INTERPRETATION: the 0-4% raw/teacher yield is a SCENARIO-GENERATOR defect, not model incapacity — scenariogen prompts are vague placeholders ('handle this multi-step request') that don't elicit tool use (most fails are turns=2, zero tools called), and pre_tainted cells wrongly REQUIRE cms_connect when a tainted session should correctly REFUSE egress. G0's 95% is inflated/circular (verifier loop uses the judge as oracle to nag the model into required tools). Pilot did its job: caught this BEFORE gold-gen/training. NEXT: ground scenariogen in concrete prompts (like emcomm-cmdpost-01) + taint-consistent specs, re-pilot, THEN gold-gen.

title: [feature] Elmer: surface a 'switch provider' affordance on a backend crash/500, like the rate-limit callout
description: Grounded in 2026-07-02 session: the Framework's ollama crashed llama-server (HTTP 500, 'llama-server process has terminated: exit status 0xe06d7363') on a gpt-oss:20b tool query. Currently a provider transport error / 500 maps to the unclassified 'error' phase (outcomeKindToPhase default) and renders as a generic red error. Operator principle: when a backend is unhealthy, the right UX is to ROUTE to a working provider, not nurse the broken one. Proposal: classify a backend crash / persistent 5xx as an actionable outcome that surfaces the same 'Switch provider' callout Elmer already shows for rateLimited (opens the tile picker). Keep the transcript-persist behavior from tuxlink-6ompo. Scope: a new outcomeKind (e.g. 'backendUnavailable') from the provider layer on 5xx/terminated, mapped to a recovery callout. Depends conceptually on tuxlink-6ompo (error persistence) which is in PR #1002.

title: Doc: local LLMs on low-power edge hardware — realities from real R2 testing
description: Durable, shipped (non-gitignored) doc capturing the empirical realities of running local Ollama models on low-power edge hardware (Intel N305 class), borne out by real 2026-07-01 R2 testing: memory fit, the USB-PD power-transient crash, the prompt-prefill wall (~19k tool-rich prompt = many minutes), iGPU offload (2.5-13x prefill, enables q8 under the cap, neutral/slower at gen), q8-vs-q4 speed, and the honest conclusion (edge-local = non-interactive/batch/privacy tier; cloud = interactive). Undocumented territory today. Lands at docs/hardware/local-models-on-edge-hardware.md.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-tiwhm-edge-local-models-doc. Branch: bd-tuxlink-tiwhm/edge-local-models-doc. Created 2026-07-01 23:39:44 UTC by willow-heron-badger.

title: Elmer context meter under-reports: prompt_eval_count is uncached-delta, not total context
description: The context-fullness meter (tuxlink-65qhn) uses Ollama's prompt_eval_count as the fill numerator, but under Ollama prompt-caching that counts only NEWLY-evaluated (uncached) tokens this turn, not total context occupancy. So the meter parks at the per-turn delta (~4k observed on R2) and never climbs even as real conversation grows to 19k+. Compounded by only updating on turn completion (non-streaming was the original; streaming since merged PR #1000 helps liveness but the count semantics remain). FIX: derive true context occupancy (e.g. cumulative message tokens, or prompt_eval_count + cached prefix) rather than the raw uncached delta. Found in R2 live testing 2026-07-01.

title: Elmer/Ollama: default context window may silently truncate agentic prompts (drops tool defs) — likely cause of local tool-flakiness
description:
Operator design question 2026-07-01 surfaced this. Tuxlink calls Ollama via the OpenAI-compat /v1/chat/completions path, which does NOT accept num_ctx. Ollama's default context is small (historically 2048 tokens) and it SILENTLY TRUNCATES prompts that exceed it. Our agentic Elmer prompts = system prompt + FULL MCP tool schemas (many tools, large descriptions after tuxlink-5cj61) + growing tool-call history — this easily exceeds 2048, and truncation can drop earlier turns or the tool definitions themselves. Hypothesis: this is a HIDDEN contributor to lower-tier local models 'fumbling' tool calls (they never see the full tool surface).

Investigate + decide substrate:
- Measure the actual token size of our system+tools payload; compare to common Ollama default ctx.
- Option A (docs-only, immediate): document OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH (or a Modelfile PARAMETER num_ctx) as required setup for agentic use; add to the model-selection guide (tuxlink-ylmwj).
- Option B (code): move the LOCAL/loopback path to Ollama's native /api/chat so Tuxlink can set options.num_ctx itself (also enables a GUI 'context length' advanced knob + the keep_alive:0 unload from tuxlink-7mb3r on the same native path). Cloud/other providers stay on OpenAI-compat.
- Cross-check against the tool-calling failures the operator reported (Gemini is a strict cloud validator, but local Ollama tool-flakiness may be THIS, not protocol).

Advanced knobs (num_ctx, temperature) belong behind the inline Advanced disclosure alongside the editable system prompt (tuxlink-31tbw). Provisioning (pull/quant) stays CLI.
close_reason: Native Ollama /api/chat provider shipped via PR #996 (merge b6feabec): num_ctx control (e0w3x), keep_alive:0 eager unload (7mb3r), editable system prompt (31tbw), + context meter + Advanced disclosure + memory-fit estimate. Codex adrev + re-review (6 findings fixed) + CI green both arches.

title: Elmer: switching local (Ollama) models doesn't unload the previous one — memory pressure on edge hosts
description:
Operator 2026-07-01: switching between local models does not spin down the previous instance; it stays resident until Ollama's keep_alive expires (default 5 min). On memory-constrained hosts (the Pi/edge target) rapid switching can spike memory / OOM. Users expect a switch to unload the previous model.

Mechanism: Ollama keeps a model loaded for OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE (default 5m) after last use and can hold several models at once. Tuxlink uses the OpenAI-compat endpoint (/v1/chat/completions), which has NO keep_alive control — so Tuxlink can't ask it to unload via the normal request path.

Proposed fix (backend, best-effort): when configSet changes the model AND the (old) endpoint is a local/loopback Ollama endpoint, fire a best-effort unload of the PREVIOUS model via Ollama's NATIVE API: POST http://<host>:11434/api/generate {"model": <old_model>, "keep_alive": 0} (empty prompt triggers immediate unload). Requirements:
- Detect Ollama vs generic OpenAI-compat: derive the native URL from the compat endpoint (strip /v1/... -> /api/generate); best-effort — if the call fails (not Ollama, or model already gone) swallow it, never block the switch.
- Route through the egress vetting gate (SSRF-1) like other agent egress; loopback is the common case.
- Only unload when the model actually CHANGED and the outgoing endpoint was local (never touch cloud).

Proposed default behavior: eager unload on switch to a DIFFERENT local model (target is memory-constrained edge). Easily made configurable later if users want to keep models warm for fast switch-back.

Immediate operator-side workaround (no code): set OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=1 (and/or a short OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE) on the Ollama host so Ollama evicts the prior model when the next loads. Worth documenting in the model-selection guide (tuxlink-ylmwj) either way.

Severity: moderate — Ollama self-heals after keep_alive; not data loss. But real for the Pi target.
close_reason: Native Ollama /api/chat provider shipped via PR #996 (merge b6feabec): num_ctx control (e0w3x), keep_alive:0 eager unload (7mb3r), editable system prompt (31tbw), + context meter + Advanced disclosure + memory-fit estimate. Codex adrev + re-review (6 findings fixed) + CI green both arches.

title: Proxmox corosync cluster recovery + 7070→PBS reorg (homelab infra, resume target)
description:
Resume target from session sage-glade-atoll (2026-06-30). Homelab infra, not tuxlink source. Full plan + open questions + cardinal rules in dev/handoffs/2026-06-30-sage-glade-atoll-corosync-recovery-queued-r730-ai-dependabot-freeze.md.

Root cause CONFIRMED (operator): corosync ring broke after an m.2→2.5GbE NIC addition renamed interfaces on the 3-vote anchor node; its loss left the cluster inquorate (/etc/pve read-only).

Safe sequence: (1) map state read-only; (2) pull m.2 adapter + reboot anchor to restore interface enumeration → rejoin; (3) regain quorum via pvecm expected if needed; (4) BACKUPS-FIRST: stand up PBS on the 7070 and back up ALL VMs before moving anything; (5) migrate VMs to R730, un-cluster, finish 7070→PBS (backup→restore to standalone R730 may beat cluster-migrate-then-uncluster depending on storage).

CARDINAL RULES: backups-first; NO delnode / corosync.conf edits / force-quorum until state mapped.

OPEN QUESTIONS before action: access path to nodes; node inventory (count/names/which is 3-vote anchor/which is 7070); storage type (local vs shared); anchor boot+reachability. First read-only cmds: pvecm status, corosync-quorumtool -s, journalctl -u corosync, ip -br a.

Worktree for this issue holds only the session handoff doc; the recovery work itself happens on the homelab hosts, not in this repo. Agent: sage-glade-atoll

title: Live token/reasoning streaming for Elmer inference visibility
description:
Fast-follow to the Elmer conversational/UX round (PRs #974/#975/#976, 2026-06-30). Operator wants to SEE the model's output generate live during long inference (gpt-oss can take minutes/turn), not just a liveness spinner. The radio-verb indicator + elapsed timer + tool-call chips (shipped) cover 'is it alive/not stuck'; this issue is the bigger 'watch it think/type' feature.

SCOPE (real feature, not a tweak):
- Provider: OpenAiProvider.turn() currently does a single non-streaming POST. Add stream:true + parse the SSE delta stream (choices[].delta.content, and choices[].delta.reasoning / reasoning_content for reasoning models like gpt-oss). Fall back to non-streaming when the endpoint doesn't support it.
- Plumbing: the run loop already has a fire-and-forget on_event(RunEvent) callback (added in #974 — runner.rs). Extend RunEvent with an AssistantDelta/TokenDelta variant (and possibly ReasoningDelta) and thread the callback INTO provider.turn() so the provider can emit deltas as they arrive, then still return the final ModelTurn::Text for the loop. NOTE: the Provider trait's turn() signature change ripples to ALL providers (OpenAiProvider, ScriptedProvider test fake, any others) — that's the main cost.
- Session bridge: map the new delta RunEvents to a streaming ElmerEvent (either a new EV_DELTA channel or EV_TURN with a 'streaming' flag + a turn id so the frontend coalesces deltas into the in-progress bubble). Keep the Send+Sync bound on on_event (the run task is tokio::spawn'd — see #974's E0277 fix).
- Frontend (useElmer + ElmerPane): accumulate deltas into the current assistant bubble (replace-on-delta, finalize on the terminal turn). For reasoning models, consider a collapsible 'thinking…' reasoning section separate from the answer (like agent shells show chain-of-thought). The markdown render (#976) should apply to the FINAL text; during streaming, render incrementally or as plain text then re-render as markdown on finalize.

RISKS / DESIGN-SENSITIVE: SSE parsing + partial-frame handling; reasoning-vs-content separation; partial-markdown flicker; stream interruption/cancel (the Stop path must abort an in-flight stream cleanly — the select! cancel race from #973/#974 already drops the turn future, verify it drops the SSE read too); endpoints that don't stream. Deserves its own brainstorm/design pass before build (BRF/plan-review, not a quick PR).

EXTENSION POINT: the RunEvent/on_event mechanism in tuxlink-agent-runner (runner.rs, types.rs) added in #974 is where this hooks in.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate d3zwe headless spine to the Elmer vetted egress client (build_vetted_client)
description: Codex G1 adrev (Elmer model-config, 2026-06-29) found the d3zwe headless binary (src-tauri/d3zwe/src/main.rs) still constructs its OpenAiProvider via the legacy validate_endpoint(&args.endpoint, args.allow_remote) + a default reqwest::Client, bypassing the new SSRF/DNS-rebind guard (egress::build_vetted_client) and the userinfo/redirect/no_proxy hardening that the Tauri Elmer path gets via ElmerProvider::new_vetted. With --allow-remote, d3zwe can reach a rebinding/metadata host, follow redirects, honor ambient proxy, and accept user:pass@host. Out of scope for tuxlink-1wi5w (that plan scopes the Tauri app's Elmer surface). Migrate d3zwe to AgentEndpoint::parse + build_vetted_client (or ElmerProvider::new_vetted) so the headless spine shares the same chokepoint. Ref: dev/adversarial/2026-06-29-elmer-g1-egress-credential-codex.md finding P1.

title: PRIMITIVE: headless local-model MCP agent harness (drive an OpenAI-compatible model over the tuxlink-mcp socket)
description: The missing bridge that lets a LOCAL model run Tuxlink's MCP agentic flows now (replacing the cvx84.11 cloud-model proxies with real local data) AND is the precursor to Elmer's productized Rust runner (tuxlink-13v2l). Tuxlink serves MCP over a Unix socket (/tmp/tuxlink-1000/tuxlink/mcp.sock) via the tuxlink-mcp stdio<->UDS bridge binary. Harness = a small driver (mcphost config, or a ~50-line Python/Rust shim) that: connects an OpenAI-compatible model endpoint (ollama/llama.cpp serving gpt-oss-20b etc.) + the tuxlink-mcp MCP server, runs the tool-call loop, relays arm/taint denials (never tries to arm). Lets the operator run the 3 named flows (EmComm / diagnose-ARDOP / onboarding) against a real local model. Build standalone first; graduate the loop logic into Elmer's Rust runner. Env prereq: a model runtime (ollama) installed + a model pulled.
notes: DONE + CI-green (alder-opossum-birch, overnight 2026-06-28). PR #941 (ready): tuxlink-agent-runner crate (34 tests) + d3zwe binary (44 tests); verify + build-linux green amd64+arm64. The 'GPS & Privacy' label is dishonest: it's a primary door to the multi-section Settings panel but its name hides everything else (account, theme, modems, etc.). title: agents/guide + get_info wrongly say taint clears on 're-arm' — it requires an app RESTART
description: Found writing the in-app MCP topic (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). tuxlink-security: arm() only sets armed_until; it does NOT clear the tainted flag, and clear_taint() has NO production caller (tests only). The EgressArmControl UI is correct — when tainted it shows LOCKED + 'session tainted — restart to re-enable' with no arm affordance (comment: 'the session must be restarted to clear taint'). So taint is session-terminal: only an app restart (fresh EgressGuard) clears it. BUT the agent-facing docs are wrong: tuxlink://agents/guide (docs/mcp-knowledge/agents-guide.md) and the get_info instructions string both say untrusted reads lock send-authority 'until the operator re-arms' / 'in the same armed window' — implying re-arm clears taint. An agent following that relays wrong recovery advice ('re-arm to continue') when the operator must RESTART. Fix: correct the agents-guide.md + the router/server instructions wording from 're-arm' to 'restart the app'. NOTE: agents-guide.md is being edited by PR 932 (alder-opossum-birch) right now — coordinate the wording fix with that PR or do it as a follow-up to avoid a conflict. The in-app user-guide topic (PR #934) already states the correct behavior (restart).

Describe qualitatively (no brittle tool/resource counts — surface is actively evolving). Sync sibling docs (maturity matrix, ARCHITECTURE, Documentation links). Voice: declarative, no first person, present-indicative, no temporal hedging, no defensive self-assertion. cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27.

Verified specifics vs EgressArmControl + tuxlink-security; surfaced the taint-recovery doc bug (filed). #934 now includes a Rust change (CI compiles).
