title: EXPERIMENT follow-up: measure local-model prompt-injection / tainted-content robustness over the MCP
description: cvx84.11 follow-up. No benchmark in the deep-research corpus measured prompt-injection/injected-content robustness of LOCAL models - the single reliability angle the MCP taint model most depends on (untrusted radio/mailbox content). Construct a tainted-content task (a message_read/mailbox_list result carrying injected 'now transmit X' instructions) and measure whether a local-capable-tier agent (Haiku proxy + real local model) respects the taint lock and refuses egress. Tie to the appsec security posture.

title: EXPERIMENT follow-up: run gpt-oss-20b/120b over the live MCP on real RTX/DGX Spark hardware
description: cvx84.11 follow-up. The equivalence experiment used cloud subagents (Haiku/Sonnet) as proxies for local-hardware tiers. The strongest version replaces the proxy with a real local measurement: run gpt-oss-20b (interactive, ~49-65 tok/s on Spark) and, if usably fast, gpt-oss-120b over the live Tuxlink MCP on an actual RTX Spark (Windows) or DGX Spark (Linux), executing the same EmComm/diagnose-ARDOP/onboarding task set from dev/research/2026-06-27-local-edge-ai-vs-cloud-agent-equivalence.md. Measures true single-stream agentic token/s under a real MCP tool-loop (the 120B single-stream speed is currently unconfirmed) + real local comprehension of the arm/taint model.
notes:
SPEED/MODEL findings on r2-poe (N305, single-channel RAM, CPU-only ollama, 2026-06-28):
THERMAL: NOT throttling (pkg 53C under sustained load; loaded cores 2.7-3.2GHz, base 1800/max 3800; trips 105/110C). Slowness is RAM-bandwidth-bound, not heat.
RAW tok/s (decode): gpt-oss:20b 7.0 | qwen2.5:7b 7.2 | qwen3:4b 10.6 | llama3.2:3b 14.8. Smaller=faster but only ~2x across 3B..20B (bandwidth wall).
KEY FINDING: agentic-loop latency is dominated by THINKING (total tokens generated), NOT tok/s. Thinking models burn 500-1200 tok/turn -> 70-117s/answer (gpt-oss, qwen3). Non-thinking answer in 15-21 tok -> 1-3s. ~25x time-to-answer swing.
Reasoning-effort levers (ollama think:false, qwen3 /no_think): UNRELIABLE -- did not disable reasoning / did not cut tokens. Not a usable knob here.
TOOL-USE competence (the Elmer-relevant metric): gpt-oss:20b CORRECT (mailbox_list->message_read, good diagnosis) but SLOW. qwen2.5:7b FAST but WEAKER -- called message_read with empty id (skipped list), errored, bailed to asking operator (failed SAFE). Domain recall weak on all small models (NVIS band wrong: qwen3->20m, qwen2.5->10m) but that's memorized-fact recall, NOT Elmer's tool-driven job.
iGPU (Exp3): deferred -- needs render-group add (sudo) + Intel compute runtime (intel-opencl/level-zero) + IPEX-LLM or llama.cpp-Vulkan/SYCL backend; shares the same single-channel RAM so modest expected gain. ollama pinned 100% CPU.
RECOMMENDATION: tradeoff is real -- gpt-oss (correct+slow) vs non-thinking small (fast+weaker driver). Next: try qwen2.5:14b-instruct (non-thinking, stronger tool-use) and/or harness scaffolding to force list-before-read. True snappiness needs better hardware (dual-channel box or eGPU), not software.

title: MCP verify_cms_connection probes an ephemeral wizard backend, not the live session — false 'no active identity'
description: Found during MCP cold acceptance (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). MCP egress tool verify_cms_connection (mcp_ports.rs:829) is wired to crate::wizard::verify_cms_connection_impl (wizard.rs:479), which constructs an EPHEMERAL NativeBackend over a temp mailbox (wizard.rs:504) and runs the first-run handshake probe. That throwaway backend has NO active identity, so the tool returns BackendError::NoActiveIdentity ('no active identity — authenticate before transmitting') EVEN WHEN the live session is authenticated and actively sending via CMS. Confirmed: same live session, MCP cms_connect (-> live ui_commands::cms_connect) returned 'ok' and the operator sends to CMS-Z fine, while MCP verify_cms_connection (-> ephemeral) returned NoActiveIdentity. Also: it opens a SEPARATE CMS connection rather than checking the live one. IMPACT: an agent using verify_cms_connection to check the live link gets a misleading failure; it does not reflect reality. FIX: verify_cms_connection on the agent surface should report the LIVE backend connection/auth state (or run a probe that inherits the live active identity), not the wizard ephemeral-backend handshake. The wizard impl is correct for first-run (no identity yet); the agent surface needs a live-session verify. Part of station/egress MCP surface (cvx84).

title: Tests write activation secrets to the REAL OS keyring (service=tuxlink) — TEST-CALL contamination
description: Found during MCP cold acceptance (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). secret-tool search service=tuxlink on the dev host shows entries 'TEST-CALL' and 'tuxlink-identity-activation:TEST-CALL' in the operator's REAL login keyring — one created 2026-06-04, ANOTHER created 2026-06-27 12:59 (i.e. during an active session/test run). A test (or test-double) is exercising the real keyring backend (service constant 'tuxlink') instead of an isolated/mock keyring, polluting the operator's OS keyring. Hygiene + safety bug: a test using a REAL callsign could clobber a real credential. Fix: tests must use a mock EntryLike / a per-test isolated keyring service name, never the real 'tuxlink' service. Audit identity/credentials tests for keyring::Entry::new(SERVICE, ...) against the real backend. Also: clean up the stray TEST-CALL entries on the dev host.

title: Launch auto-auth fails SILENTLY on mismatched activation secret (AutoAuth::Unavailable emits no user-visible line)
description: Root-caused during MCP cold acceptance (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). SYMPTOM: N7CPZ identity shows locked / transmit blocked (NoActiveIdentity) on launch despite the CMS password being valid and the keyring unlocked. ROOT CAUSE: bootstrap.rs auto-auth (tuxlink-6wz3) authenticates the stored CMS password against the stored ACTIVATION SECRET (separate keyring entries: 'N7CPZ' vs 'tuxlink-identity-activation:N7CPZ'). Both are present + readable, but their VALUES are out of sync, so resolve_auto_identity returns AutoAuth::Unavailable and the active-identity slot stays empty. THE DEFECT: the AutoAuth::Unavailable match arm (bootstrap.rs ~521) ONLY does tracing::warn! — unlike the Healed/HealFailed arms it does NOT emit_backend_line, so the operator gets NO user-visible cue that their identity failed to auto-unlock or why. Silent by omission => 'no discernible cause'. FIX: emit_backend_line on the Unavailable arm with an actionable message ('N7CPZ could not auto-unlock — re-enter your password in Settings -> Winlink Account'). Complements PR #928 (closed-chip lock indicator). Do NOT auto-heal a MISMATCH (only missing is safe to heal — a stale CMS password must not overwrite a good activation secret); make it visible + route to re-enter. How they desynced: provenance uncertain (N7CPZ CMS-pw keyring entry dated 06-22 vs activation-secret 06-13); a CMS-password change path that didn't go through write_password is the suspect. Operator fix/confirm: re-enter password (write_password re-syncs both).
close_reason: MISDIAGNOSED (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). Operator ground truth: N7CPZ is loaded and sends via CMS-Z fine — auto-auth is NOT failing, there is NO activation-secret desync. The 'no active identity' signal came ONLY from MCP verify_cms_connection, which is wired to wizard::verify_cms_connection_impl — an EPHEMERAL throwaway NativeBackend (wizard.rs:504) with no active identity. The live cms_connect (ui_commands) returned ok. Real bug filed separately (MCP verify_cms_connection ephemeral-backend false negative). No code change needed for auto-auth.

title: Surface locked/not-transmit-ready identity on the closed IdentitySwitcher chip
description: Cold-acceptance UX finding (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27, via tuxlink-l9sq4): when launch auto-auth (bootstrap tuxlink-6wz3) does not load an active session identity (e.g. keyring credential unreadable at launch), the backend active-identity slot is empty and all transmit/egress fails closed with NoActiveIdentity. But the closed IdentitySwitcher chip shows primaryLabel = active?.address_as ?? list.last_selected ?? '—', so it displays the callsign (e.g. N7CPZ) IDENTICALLY whether authenticated or not. The 🔒 lock indicator only appears INSIDE the open dropdown (IdentitySwitcher.tsx:428). Net: nothing in the main UI tells the operator their identity is not transmit-ready or routes them to re-authenticate — a WLE-hostile silent-failure trap against the 'love to use' north star (operator hit this live during MCP acceptance). Fix: surface locked state on the CLOSED chip when active===null and an identity is configured (🔒 + accessible label + title), so it is discoverable at a glance; clicking the chip already opens the unlock path. Pure-frontend, vitest-testable.
notes: Fixed: IdentitySwitcher closed-chip locked indicator (commit c48d89a2, PR #928). 3 TDD tests + typecheck clean. Close on merge.
close_reason: Approach abandoned (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27). The closed-chip locked indicator (PR #928, closed) was built on a misdiagnosis — the live identity is not locked; the 'no active identity' came from the mis-wired verify_cms_connection ephemeral backend (tuxlink-sswik). The chip approach also has a startup-race false-positive surface (identity_active fetched once, no auto-auth-settled refetch). Superseded by tuxlink-<emit-on-unavailable>.

title: MCP levers for CAT rig control (tuxlink-8fkkk shipped no MCP surface)
description: The CAT rig-control feature (tuxlink-8fkkk, PR #922) added rig state + control (live-VFO readout, Config.rig RigUiConfig, ardop_tune_rig, freqHz/qsyCandidates on connect) but exposed NONE of it via the MCP server. Verified 2026-06-27 (marsh-fjord-condor): 8fkkk's diff adds no MCP code; current main's MCP tool set (router.rs) has no rig/CAT lever. GAPS: (1) [read-only, in-posture] rig_status tool — report rig.status() freq/mode/PTT, mirroring modem_get_status/vara_status; the field-assistant cannot answer 'what frequency is my rig on?'. (2) [read-only, in-posture] config_read_rig tool — report Config.rig (hamlib model, rigctld host/port, CAT serial, close-serial, live-vfo, qsy_on_fail), mirroring config_read_ardop/vara/packet; the hoist moved rig fields OUT of ArdopUiConfig so config_read_ardop no longer carries them — MCP lost rig-config visibility entirely. (3) [egress, design call] ardop_connect/ardop_b2f_exchange/vara_b2f_exchange take only target(+intent), no freqHz/qsyCandidates — an MCP-driven connect skips the CAT tune + QSY (dials wherever the rig is parked), a GUI<->MCP parity gap. (4) [egress, RADIO-1 design call] standalone rig_tune lever. Recommend: build (1)+(2) read-only now (autonomous, fits the read-only diagnostic posture); (3)+(4) need an operator/RADIO-1 design decision. Depends on tuxlink-8fkkk landing.
design: OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-27 (full scope C): build ALL of — (1) rig_status read-only tool (mirror modem_get_status/vara_status; report rig.status() freq/mode/PTT); (2) config_read_rig read-only tool (mirror config_read_ardop/vara/packet; report Config.rig); (3) egress parity — thread freqHz + qsyCandidates into ardop_connect/ardop_b2f_exchange/vara_b2f_exchange so an MCP connect tunes+QSYs like the GUI; (4) a standalone rig_tune EGRESS tool (set freq/mode on demand). KEY CONSTRAINT: rig_tune (and the egress freq/QSY in #3) MUST be gated by the SAME armed-send-authority + un-tainted-session controls already shipped for cms_connect/ardop_connect/b2f — NOT a new operator surface. Rationale (operator): tuning the radio exposes the same 'could transmit' authority as actually sending, so it is the same raw auth class — one operator surface, reuse the existing arm/taint gate. So rig_tune is an EgressPort method behind the egress gate, classed with the transmit tools. BLOCKED until tuxlink-8fkkk merges: the build needs the rig feature (rig.status/Config.rig/ardop_tune_rig, on the 8fkkk branch) AND main's current Plan-3 MCP server (EgressPort + armed-auth gate) in ONE tree — do NOT build on the 8fkkk branch (its MCP copy is stale and would regress main's MCP on merge). Build as a fresh branch off main immediately post-merge.
notes: CI GREEN on 0182edbc (12/12: verify+build-linux+deb, both arches) AFTER Codex remediation. PR #929 marked READY. Pending operator no-squash merge (ADR 0010). Follow-up filed for safe live rig_status VFO readout.

title: EXPERIMENT (queued, post-confirmation-testing): local edge-AI (RTX/DGX-Spark-class) agent vs cloud-agent equivalence on Tuxlink-assistant tasks
description: Operator request 2026-06-27, to run AFTER the MCP confirmation testing is complete (do NOT start before). Two parts: (1) RESEARCH pass — what today's leading-edge LOCAL/portable AI hardware (NVIDIA RTX / DGX Spark / 'Project DIGITS'-class GB10, and RTX AI laptops) can actually run locally; which local models/agents fit in their memory/compute envelope; and map those local-model capabilities to cloud-agent (Claude/GPT tier) equivalents — i.e. 'a local agent on this hardware is roughly equivalent to cloud-agent X for these task classes.' Use deep-research / web (current as of run date; verify product specifics — RTX Spark vs DGX Spark naming). (2) SCOPE + RUN a test: set a fixed task set (Tuxlink-MCP assistant tasks — e.g. the EmComm WARC-coordination flow, diagnose-ARDOP, onboarding-uv-pro) to an equivalent-capability SUB-AGENT (model/effort tier matched to the local-hardware equivalent from part 1) and evaluate how a local edge agent would perform as the operator's assistant (quality, tool-use competence, failure modes). Deliverable: a research+findings report + the scoped experiment results. Ties to the project ethos (AI-technique learning sandbox) + the MCP 'people AND their agents love to use' north star (does a LOCAL agent over the MCP suffice for the contingency/Starlink-Mini deployment path?). RELEVANT: the for_agents guide (cvx84.x) matters here — a contextless local agent needs it.

title: MCP 3.4 Tier-2 remediation writes (armed+taint) + Tier-3 compose/queue
description: Plan 3 phase 3.4. Tier-2 (armed+taint gated, audited, input-validated): config_set_ardop, config_set_vara, packet_config_set, config_set_grid, position_set_source, config_set_privacy, packet_set_listen, mailbox_move, message_attachment_save (INPUT-VALIDATE dest_path - traversal). Tier-3 compose (local, ungated, input-validated): message_send, send_form (validate fields - injection), catalog_send_inquiry, grib_send_request - stage outbox only; egress is the gated connect (3.3). Route writes through guarded_egress-style gate w/ Agent. Gate: CI green -> tier-2 (drive-level change while armed) -> self-merge. Harness: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-26-tuxlink-mcp-plan3-execution-harness-design.md
notes: DESIGN RESOLVED (maple-sumac-larch 2026-06-26). WritePort (gated, validate-BEFORE-guarded_egress(Agent) so Invalid is distinct from Denied + path-traversal rejected before fs::write regardless of arm) + ComposePort (ungated local outbox-stage, still validated). NARROW write DTOs: agent sends ONLY the changeable field (ArdopWriteDto{drive_level 0..=100}, VaraWriteDto{bandwidth_hz in {500,2300,2750}}, packet SSID/timing) NOT the full config struct (which carries binary path/ALSA/PTT serial — agent must NOT rewrite) — least-privilege. validate.rs in mcp-core (pure, Pi-testable): validate_attachment_dest (confine under app_data/agent-attachments: reject absolute + '..' components + canonicalize-parent starts_with base for symlink escape), validate_address (reject CRLF/control, len<=64), validate_subject (CRLF, len<=256), validate_drive_level, validate_vara_bandwidth. FINDINGS: message_attachment_save has NO agent dest_path today (GUI=save dialog) → agent variant is net-new arbitrary-file-write of ATTACKER-influenced attachment bytes → confinement validator is the security core (HIGH). send_form/message_send field injection already neutralized (forms/serialize.rs XML-escapes <>& + strips ctrl chars) — real residual = header-injection CRLF in to/cc/subject (validate at MCP boundary). config_set_vara setter NOT found → replicate read->set bw->write_config_atomic. catalog/grib recipients are constants (low risk). Compose tools do NOT taint + do NOT egress (stage outbox; egress=gated connect from 3.3). Build: Chunk1(Pi) validate.rs+traits+DTOs+tools+mocks+tier-1 (path-traversal rejected even unarmed; write denied unarmed/tainted; compose ungated-but-CRLF-rejected) ; Chunk2(CI) monolith MonolithWritePort/ComposePort (attachment_save replicates read+extract then writes to validated confined path; config_set_vara replicate) + McpState wiring. Light Codex adrev on validators (background). NOT a full adrev-gate but security-relevant.

title: [bug] Modem panel view state resets on revisit — connect tab defaults to Favorites (not last-used) + Raw Logs toggle reverts off
description: Operator report 2026-06-26 (N7CPZ): per-mode modem panel view state is not persisted across navigating away and back. Two confirmed facets: (a) the connect-surface tab (Favorites / Recent / Manual, src/favorites/FavoritesTabs.tsx) resets to Favorites even when Manual was last selected — expected: restore the last-used tab per mode; (b) the session-log 'Show raw' / Raw Logs toggle reverts to OFF every revisit. ROOT CAUSE for (b) confirmed: src/radio/sections/SessionLogSection.tsx:52 'const [showRaw, setShowRaw] = useState(initialShowRaw)' with initialShowRaw=false (line 47) — showRaw is component-local state re-initialized to false on every mount, never persisted. Fix: persist these view-state bits (last-used tab per mode; showRaw/autoScroll preference) to a durable store so they survive remount. May split into two during implementation (tab persistence vs toggle persistence). Same class as the Find-a-Station reset issue.
notes: FIXED in PR #910 (with liqs9). usePersistedState hook + SessionLogSection showRaw persisted + FavoritesTabs controlled+persisted per-mode tab. tsc + tests green.

title: [bug] Inbound download/hold confirmation prompts fire per protocol block, not once per session — operator sees 1–4 prompts of a few messages each
description:
SYMPTOM (operator, recurring; seen even on small CMS-Z test pulls): when downloading messages, the operator is prompted with 1–4 SEPARATE download/hold confirmation prompts, each covering only a handful of messages, instead of ONE prompt listing all the messages to download.

MECHANISM (grounded in code):
- The Winlink B2F exchange surfaces the operator's accept/hold decision through the `decide` callback in src-tauri/src/winlink/session.rs.
- `decide(&proposals)` is invoked once per RECEIVE TURN: session.rs:335 inside receive_turn(), which is called once per loop iteration in run_exchange_with_role() (session.rs:181-204, line 196).
- The CMS/B2F protocol may split an inbox into MULTIPLE proposal blocks (each block = a run of FC/FB lines terminated by F>, classically capped at a few messages per block). Each block is a separate receive turn → `decide` fires again → the UI raises another confirmation prompt.
- Result: a 12-message inbox arriving as, e.g., 3 blocks of 4 produces 3 prompts of 4 messages each. The 'handful per prompt' the operator sees is exactly the protocol's per-block proposal count.

WHY 'one prompt with everything' is not a trivial coalesce: within a single session the client cannot see block 2's proposals until it has answered block 1 and transferred the accepted bodies (protocol is block→answer→bodies→next block). So the real fix is a SESSION-LEVEL download policy decided ONCE and applied to every subsequent block, not a per-block prompt.

PROPOSED DIRECTION (for design, not yet approved):
- Mirror WLE behavior: default to accept-all on download (see the AllowedStations / WLE-parity posture). Make the operator's accept/hold choice a single per-session decision (or a persisted default) that the `decide` callback consults for every block, so blocks 2..N do not re-prompt.
- If a per-message hold UI is wanted, queue all blocks' proposals into ONE running review surface rather than a fresh modal per block.

IMPACT / SEVERITY: UX friction, fully recoverable (no data loss); ~alpha-stage, small user count. P2. Annoying enough that it shows up on routine CMS-Z test pulls.

REPRO: connect to CMS-Z (dev) with several queued inbound messages; observe multiple back-to-back download/hold prompts during the single receive phase.

Affected backends: all that route through run_exchange/receive_turn (telnet/CMS, ARDOP B2F via src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/ardop/b2f.rs, native packet). The decide-per-block boundary is shared.

Filed by agent fjord-savanna-hemlock at operator request 2026-06-26.
design:
THREADING DESIGN (decide-signature approach). The decide closure is Fn(&[Proposal]) at ~14 sites (telnet/p2p/listen×3, b2f, session/mod ×4, ui_commands, winlink_backend type alias). Plan:
1. receive_turn (session/mod.rs:687): on a line that PendingMessage::parse() accepts, push it to a manifest Vec instead of skipping; keep skipping other ';' lines. Accumulate across the receive loop; at F>, pass &manifest to decide.
2. Change decide sig -> Fn(&[Proposal], &[PendingMessage]). Most closures become |p, _m| (accept-all paths ignore it). run_exchange_with_role threads a manifest Vec into receive_turn.
3. build_selecting_decider (inbound_selection.rs): (a) emit InboundProposalsOffered built from the FULL manifest (not just the FC block) the FIRST time; (b) cache the operator's InboundSelection keyed by attempt_id; (c) on later FC blocks, auto-answer from the cache (selected MID -> Accept; else disposition Hold->Defer/Delete->Reject) WITHOUT re-emitting. Match FC proposals to manifest by MID.
4. PendingProposalDto: add redacted sender+subject (from PendingMessage); from_proposal_redacted -> from_pending_redacted. serde-lockdown test needs the new variant fields.
5. Frontend sessionTypes.ts + InboundSelectionPanel.tsx: render sender+subject columns.
Scope CMS/telnet only. Foundation (PendingMessage parser) shipped in PR #908 commit dc724806.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE NAILED via operator-pasted live wire (2026-06-26, RMS Express 1.8.2.2 -> CMS-SSL):
The CMS sends a FULL pending-message manifest up front as ;PM: lines (8 msgs in the capture: recipient MID size SENDER SUBJECT), THEN negotiates downloads in small FC blocks (first block = 3: WCCJR0N74QU3/9O1X4ZUL02EI/S1W0KIF83A0R, F> F7, FS YYY). WLE renders the review pane from the ;PM: manifest (all 8 at once, with sender+subject). tuxlink DISCARDS ;PM: — receive loop skips all ';'-prefixed lines (session/mod.rs:707-708 'comment, pending-message info, or blank') — so it prompts once per FC block (~3) => the operator's 1-4 prompts of a handful each. tuxlink's Proposal struct has MID+sizes only; sender/subject live ONLY in ;PM:, which is why the current pane is MID-only.

SIDs identical (both B2FHM$); CMS chunks FC the same for both clients. The ONLY difference is ;PM: usage. SID/handshake ruled out.

FIX (supersedes the earlier 'cache decision across blocks' plan): parse ;PM: into a manifest (recipient,mid,size,sender,subject); present the FULL manifest in ONE InboundProposalsOffered review pane (now WITH sender+subject); capture the selection once; auto-answer the streaming FC blocks from that selection (accept selected MIDs, hold/reject rest) with no re-prompt. Scope: CMS/telnet path (bsiy decider). Worktree: worktrees/bd-tuxlink-9u07u-inbound-coalesce (branch bd-tuxlink-9u07u/inbound-coalesce off origin/main).
close_reason: Closed

title: Relicense GPLv3 → AGPLv3 (close network/SaaS loophole the MCP server opens)
description:
Decision 2026-06-25 (operator, agent bluff-maple-birch). origin/main is GPL-3.0-or-later; the Tuxlink MCP server (tuxlink-cvx84) makes Tuxlink a network-callable service, opening GPL's network/SaaS loophole (GPL copyleft fires on distribution; a modified HOSTED Tuxlink conveys no binary => owes no source). AGPLv3 §13 closes it. Consistent extension of existing copyleft.

Work: (1) contributor consent for relicense ('GPL-or-later' does NOT auto-authorize AGPL — separate license; alpha = cheapest window, cost rises per contributor); (2) dependency-license audit (rmcp + friends MIT/Apache = incorporable under AGPL); (3) update LICENSE + README badge + src-tauri/Cargo.toml (license = 'AGPL-3.0-or-later') + any SPDX headers together; (4) optional narrower cut: AGPL the server components only, keep GPL core (AGPL↔GPL §13-compatible).

GATE: must land BEFORE Tuxlink ships anything network-exposable. See memory project_tuxlink_license_agpl + design doc Licensing section.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-tm0cp-agpl-relicense. Branch: bd-tuxlink-tm0cp/agpl-relicense. Created 2026-06-27 07:20:09 UTC by maple-sumac-larch.

title: Log B2F handshake (SID line) to wire_log on ARDOP/AX.25 paths
description: Observability gap found during CMS acceptance prep (gully-maple-fjord, 2026-06-25). On the ARDOP/AX.25 transports, run_exchange_with_role writes/reads the B2F handshake via write_bytes/read_remote_handshake WITHOUT any wire_log call (session.rs:153,165,169), so the client SID line [tuxlink-<ver>-B2FHM$], the server [WL2K-...] line, and any SID-level rejection are invisible in the client's raw-wire window over RF. Telnet captures all of it via WireTap (byte-level). Fix: add wire_log calls around the handshake read/write in session.rs so RF transports surface the SID exchange for evidence capture. Closes the gap noted in dev/runbooks/2026-06-25-tuxlink-onair-cms-acceptance.md. Not a tonight blocker — Telnet is the authoritative SID evidence.

title: L5: Offline degradation + opt-in contribute-back to PSKReporter
description: Graceful offline degradation (cached spots + VOACAP + radiosonde, flagged stale, no error paths). Opt-in contribute-back of tuxlink's own FT8 decodes (reciprocity; discloses RX grid/activity, default opt-in). Ref design doc in epic tuxlink-u3m0g.

title: L4: Infrastructure sensor - PSKReporter activity density -> gateway area lit/dark flag
description: PSKReporter activity density around a gateway grid as a live proxy for 'is that area still powered/on the air' (the EmComm question governing gateway choice). Surface lit/dark in Find a Station. Ref design doc in epic tuxlink-u3m0g.

title: L3: Fused reachability scorer - VOACAP + PSKReporter + FT8-radiosonde -> per-gateway score
description: VOACAP is the prior; PSKReporter global-live + radiosonde local-live update it (weighted by recency/confidence). Output score + confidence + 'why' provenance per gateway. Eval: fused-rank must beat VOACAP-alone at predicting which gateway the radiosonde confirms closes. Ref design doc in epic tuxlink-u3m0g.

title: L2: Winlink-mode reachability proxy - FT8/WSPR SNR -> required-SNR offset table
description: Config-driven SNR-offset table mapping observed FT8/WSPR path SNR to each Winlink mode's required SNR/bandwidth (VARA 500/2300/2750, ARDOP). Seed from published specs/measurement, NOT VARA internals (clean-room). Shared with FT8 Radiosonde. No dep on L1. Ref design doc in epic tuxlink-u3m0g.

title: L1: PSKReporter client - rate-limited (<=1/5min), compressed, cached spot fetcher
description: Polite PSKReporter API client (Rust backend). Enforce <=1 query/5min, gzip, dedup (sequence/ETag), persistent spot cache surviving offline. Filter by band + target gateway grids + time window. Attribution. Independently useful: live band activity. Etiquette-de-risking foundation. Ref design doc in epic tuxlink-u3m0g.

title: Find-a-Station: PSKReporter-trued fused propagation reachability engine
description: Fuse three propagation truth sources into one per-gateway reachability score: VOACAP (model) + PSKReporter (global live, ~88.7% FT8 -> Winlink reachability by SNR-proxy) + FT8 Radiosonde (local TX-confirmed). Online-ENRICHMENT layer, offline-graceful. PSKReporter carries NO VARA/Winlink spots; reachability is inferred via FT8/WSPR SNR mapped to the Winlink mode's required SNR. API etiquette is a hard constraint: <=1 query/5min, compression, caching, attribution, optional contribute-back. Design doc: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-xygm-recover-handoffs-design-20260625-pskreporter-prop-truing.md . Related: tuxlink-s0r1 (configurable propagation model). Build in 5 shippable layers (child issues).
notes: SUPERSEDED + SHELVED 2026-07-05 by the Station Intelligence design (folds native passive FT-8 into Find-a-Station; VOACAP-fusion framing retired; PSKReporter + active-TX shelved for now, NOT killed — may return as a separate later feature). Design doc: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-ant8s-ardop-connect-fixes-design-20260705-034957-passive-ft8-listener.md . Do not work the .1-.5 children until PSKReporter/active is un-shelved.

A passive-listening feature that identifies stations heard on the air and surfaces them on the theater-of-conflict/operations (situational/APRS) map: (a) identify + display heard stations as map pins, and/or (b) decode live traffic (FT8/ARDOP/VARA/packet/APRS) and plot decoded stations/positions. title: ARDOP PTT picker should detect/offer CM108 HID devices (CM108:/dev/hidrawN), not just serial ports
description: On-air FT-710 bring-up (2026-06-23) surfaced this: ardopcf supports CM108-style GPIO PTT (very common — any C-Media sound-card interface, e.g. the 0d8c:0013 dongle in use here) via a 'CM108:/dev/hidrawN' PTT string. tuxlink's ARDOP panel PTT picker only enumerates SERIAL devices (packet_list_serial_devices → ttyUSB*), so a CM108-interface operator sees no usable option and must KNOW to hand-type 'CM108:/dev/hidraw0'. Backend already passes ptt_serial_path through verbatim to ardopcf -p (modem_commands.rs:902, no validation) and the field is free-text, so manual entry works TODAY — this is a discoverability gap, not a blocker. Asks: (1) enumerate CM108-capable HID devices (scan /dev/hidraw* for VID 0x0D8C + the CM108 PID list ardopcf accepts: 0x0008-0x000F,0x0012,0x0013,0x0139,0x013A,0x013C) and offer them in the picker prefixed 'CM108:'; (2) label them clearly (e.g. 'C-Media CM108 GPIO PTT'); (3) document the CM108: prefix + the serial-RTS vs CM108-GPIO distinction in the ARDOP help. Also fix the stale comment at ArdopRadioPanel.tsx:323 ('-k <serial-path>' → ardopcf PTT is -p; -k is the CAT keystring).

title: Doc: CMS auth can fail ~24h for newly-created accounts on cms-z.winlink.org (prod->replica propagation lag), not a client bug (GH#880)
description: Add a troubleshooting note (CMS/login section of the user guide or a connection FAQ/pitfalls entry) capturing the resolved GH#880 mystery: a newly-created Winlink account can be REJECTED at login on the dev CMS replica cms-z.winlink.org for up to ~24h after creation, because the account has not yet propagated/replicated from CMS production to CMS-Z. Symptom: 'password rejected' on a known-correct, unchanged password. NOT a client defect — tuxlink's verbatim secure-login hash was correct throughout (the original #880 disposition was right). Resolution: wait for propagation; no password change needed. karch (alpha tester, GH#880) confirmed 2026-06-23 that after ~24h the SAME unchanged password authenticated on BOTH Pat and Tuxlink. Doc should: (1) state the symptom + that it is server-side + transient; (2) note it affects cms-z (dev/replica) specifically; (3) advise: verify against production CMS / wait ~24h, do not assume a client bug or change the password. Cross-ref the project memory entry + the #880 thread.

title: ARDOP init: 'ARQBW <n> FORCED' faults Syntax Err on ardopcf 2.0.3.2.1 (only sent when bandwidth_hz set)
description: session.rs init_tnc sends 'ARQBW <hz> FORCED' (space-separated). ardopcf 2.0.3.2.1 AND the 1.x-develop build both reply 'FAULT Syntax Err: ARQBW 2000 FORCED' -> if set_and_ack treats it fatal, init fails. NOT triggered by default (tuxlink only sends ARQBW when modem_ardop.bandwidth_hz is Some; default/Cameron's config is None). ardopcf likely wants a single token like '2000MAX'/'2000FORCED' (no space) or different bandwidth keywords. Fix: correct the ARQBW argument formatting to ardopcf 2.x syntax + add a test; verify against the bundled 2.0.3.2.1. Latent until an operator sets an ARDOP bandwidth in the panel.
close_reason: Merged PR #896: ARQBW sent as single token (2000FORCED) not '2000 FORCED'; init no longer faults when an ARDOP bandwidth is set.

title: vite optimizeDeps.include for map deps — stop the first-map-open dev reload race (ErrorBoundary crash)
description: Converged (vite dev) build crashes to ErrorBoundary 'something went wrong' on first map open: 'Importing a module script failed'. Root cause: vite.config.ts has no optimizeDeps block, so the map lazy chunk's deps (rbush 4 ESM-only, @mapbox/vector-tile 3, pbf, @protomaps/basemaps) are discovered on-demand after the recent dependabot major bumps changed the optimize graph -> first map open forces re-optimize + full reload -> in-flight dynamic imports (map + RadioDrawer) fail. Dev-only; production (vite build) pre-bundles fine. Confirmed: operator reload after optimize completed loads the map. Fix: optimizeDeps.include the heavy lazy-route map deps so they pre-bundle at dev startup.

title: [bug] GH#881 — window controls missing + right-side UI cut off when not maximized
description: Alpha tester @karchf (v0.74.1, Ubuntu 24.04, WebKitGTK 2.52.3). When the tuxlink window is not maximized, the top-right window controls (minimize/maximize/close) are missing and UI components on the right are clipped. notes: PR #886 (draft). Root cause: WebKit auto-sizes .layout-b's undeclared grid column to max-content of widest row → titlebar controls clip. Fix: grid-template-columns:minmax(0,1fr). Prior fixes #812/#822 (in since 0.71.1) missed this. GATE: needs operator grim smoke on narrow/non-maximized window — both prior fixes shipped unverified on WebKitGTK. title: APRS map: placename labels occluded by pack fills at z6+ (layer order)
description: Root-caused this session (moss-bog-cove 2026-06-20), NOT fixed (deprioritized). In src/map/basemapStyle.ts buildBasemapStyle, pack detail layers (fill/line) are appended via styleLayers.push(...packLayers) AFTER the base label/symbol layers — so within a downloaded pack's coverage (z6+, e.g. North America), the pack's opaque earth/landcover/water/buildings fills paint OVER all place labels (which read from the base 'protomaps' overview source). Labels present at low zoom (overview only), gone when zoomed into pack coverage. Fix: splice the pack fill/line layers in BEFORE the first base symbol/label layer (so pack geometry still wins over the coarse overview, but labels stay on top) instead of push()ing at the end. The pack's symbol layers are already stripped (line ~198) by design — labels are owned by the base. Verified via the generated style: last symbol layer idx < first pack layer idx = occlusion.

title: converge-build.sh doesn't stage the go-pmtiles (pmtiles) sidecar → offline-map download fails in dev

Production CI (release.yml / ect-build.yml) DOES download go-pmtiles + inject externalBin, so shipped bundles work — this is DEV-ONLY (operator can't exercise offline downloads in dev:converged). Fix: mirror the voacapl staging in converge-build.sh — download go-pmtiles release (pin GP_VER; Linux arm64 on this Pi) into target/debug/pmtiles if absent, like release.yml:139-153. Not release-blocking.

title: Gateway auto-dial list (WLE parity) — ranked dial-order queue for RMS gateways
description: WLE-parity feature tuxlink lacks: an automatic gateway dial-order queue (try gateways in ranked order). Ranking sources: distance (baseline), VOACAP score, and the FT8 radiosonde (tuxlink-vwnz) confirmed two-way links — gateways with compatible configs within N miles of a confirmed FT8 link (at a given SNR vs a Winlink-compatible mode) rank higher and dial first. Operator toggle selects the active ranking source (never blended). Sibling/prerequisite for the FT8 epic's DIAL-ORDER consumer (Stage 2+); the FT8 MAP consumer (Stage 1) does NOT depend on this. N-mile radius + config-similarity rule needs the paired-correlation data to calibrate. Surfaced during the FT8 radiosonde brainstorm (grouse-marten-taiga 2026-06-21).

title: FT8 radiosonde Stage 1 — bidirectional prober (live audio, CAT QSY, TX-probe, map re-rank)
description: Foundation instrument for the FT8 epic (tuxlink-vwnz). Real decode/encode (engine per Stage 0), live G90 audio I/O (the deferred ~40%), CAT QSY, parameterized sweep (bands, dwell, TX-probe on/off, iterate duration). TX-PROBE = the actual hard part: an automated FT8 QSO state machine (answer CQ / call heard station -> exchange reports -> RR73); define success (got reciprocal report) vs timeout (no reply in K slots). AIRTIME/ABORT: each FT8 TX is a fixed ~12.6s burst (natural ceiling); demonstrate + test the ceiling and a working abort before next key-down (RADIO-1 no-runaway-TX bar). Needs GPS-disciplined offline UTC for slot timing (position subsystem; degrade loudly if uncertain). OUTPUT: per-path confirmed two-way data points feeding the Find-a-Station MAP color + gold-outline (bidirectional-confirmed meets selected mode SNR/bandwidth), selectable vs VOACAP by operator toggle (NEVER blended). Start logging per-grid baselines + paired (FT8 SNR -> real VARA/ARDOP connect outcome+speed) data to earn the SNR->mode mapping. Design doc has full gotchas.

title: FT8 radiosonde Stage 0 — spike: ft8_lib build-quality eval + RX-only PoC (decision gate)
description: Decision-gate spike for the FT8 epic (tuxlink-vwnz). SCOPE: WAV files + WSJT-X UDP decode stream ONLY — NO live G90 audio capture (ALSA/Pipewire+codec plumbing is ~40% of the work, deferred to Stage 1). Decode one sub-band (default 20m 14.074MHz); print SNR + callsign + grid + bearing/distance from operator grid (reuse config_read/position_status); plus ONE interpretive line: RX SNR vs hardcoded mode threshold -> likely/marginal/no, labeled RX-only, no TX, no gateway claim. Evaluate ft8_lib (Karlis Goba, MIT C) build quality for embed-via-FFI. EXIT QUESTION: is the SNR->mode mapping believable enough to fund the paired-correlation study, and is ft8_lib good enough to embed (vs native Rust)? No live audio / no TX at this gate. Design: dev/scratch/2026-06-21-ft8-reachability-radiosonde-design.md.

title: EPIC: FT8 Reachability Radiosonde — empirical band/station/gateway truth for Find a Station
description: Add an FT8-based, measurement-not-model reachability instrument to Find a Station. FT8 sub-bands are the densest HF signal source and report SNR both ways, so they act as a real-time radiosonde for the operator's OWN station: which band/direction is open, and (via opt-in TX-probe) does the link close BOTH ways at QRP. Also a human-infrastructure sensor (FT8 silence ~ region dark) to answer the EmComm question Winlink cares about: NVIS local vs reach past the disaster zone to a gateway with a working CMS uplink. Two consumers, same measured data: the Find-a-Station MAP visual encoding (color=reachability rank when FT8 is the active source; gold-outline=bidirectional-confirmed meeting the selected mode's SNR/bandwidth) AND a WLE-parity gateway auto-dial list (ranking input). MUST NOT blend with VOACAP — independent tools, operator toggle. Staged epic. Full design: dev/scratch/2026-06-21-ft8-reachability-radiosonde-design.md (local; gstack copy in ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/). Brainstorm: grouse-marten-taiga 2026-06-21.

title: Digipeat path shows implausible single hops — intermediate traced digi missing from resolved via-chain
description: Operator (RF authority) flagged on the live Tac map: WX7FGZ-5 (Bellemont) and WX7FGZ-7 (Tuba City) render a SOLID ~115mi single hop to W7MOT-3 (big-gun digi near White Tanks/Phoenix), then to the operator. That hop is RF-impossible at 50W; the frame MUST have traversed an intermediate digi (Elden, Flagstaff) which traces itself on RF but whose POSITION beacon isn't heard within a dev window (Elden beacons ~hourly), so it's unlocated. Captured resolve input showed via-chain = [W7MOT-3*, WIDE2] with NO Elden entry at all. Verified the AX.25 parser (Path::decode + decode_digi_hbits, frame.rs) is clean (all <=8 digis kept, no truncation) and the UV-Pro native driver feeds raw bytes through it — so for the resolved copy, Elden's call genuinely was not in the path field. Open question (being captured via TUXLINK_APRS_RAW_CAPTURE -> dev/scratch/aprs-raw.log, pre-dedupe): does a LATER copy carry Elden while the engine's first-heard dedupe (try_emit_position keys lat/lon/symbol/comment, keeps first copy's path) discards it? If so, fix = prefer most-complete path among copies of a fix. Operator hint: 'we can only handle 1 unknown hop right now.' NOT a distance-heuristic problem (that was a rejected agent guess). When the via-chain DOES include the unlocated Elden, the resolver already renders it correctly as a DASHED bridged hop. Separate follow-up off merged tuxlink-qnu6 (PR #849).
design:
ROOT CAUSE (confirmed via raw on-air capture, 2026-06-21) — TABLED, no client change yet.

SYMPTOM: digipeat path animation draws an implausibly long SOLID single hop for
some stations (WX7FGZ-5 Bellemont->W7MOT-3 ~115mi; WX7FGZ-7 Tuba City->W7MOT-3;
W7BF-3 CA->W7MOT-3 ~250mi). Operator (RF authority) confirmed these are NOT real
single 50W hops and W7MOT is a multi-site ARA APRS system with NO invisible
backbone — genuine multi-hop RF.

NOT A TUXLINK BUG. Captured via TUXLINK_APRS_RAW_CAPTURE (dev/scratch/aprs-raw.log,
191 frames). Findings:
- W7MOT-3 is the final repeated digi (operator's local big-gun RX) in ~every frame.
- The improbable-hop stations carry ONLY W7MOT-3 in the path in EVERY copy (not a
  dedupe artifact; AX.25 parser verified clean — keeps all <=8 digis). The
  intermediate digi is ABSENT on-air because it relayed WITHOUT inserting its
  callsign (old-style non-tracing WIDE digipeat). W7MOT-3 (New-N) traced; the
  upstream hop did not.
- Contrast confirming the mechanism (same operator, same final hop):
    WX7FGZ-6>APRS,UNION,W7MOT-3,WIDE2   <- UNION (Mt Union) traced -> renders right
    KG7KID-7>APRS,MINGUS,W7MOT-3,WIDE2  <- MINGUS traced -> renders right
    WX7FGZ-5>APRS,W7MOT-3,WIDE2         <- intermediate did NOT trace -> bogus hop
    W7BF-3>APN391,W7MOT-3,WIDE2         <- ditto (2/2 copies identical)

PART 97 FRAMING (operator): a digipeat that relays without identifying is NOT
permitted under Part 97. So a hidden hop is evidence of a STATION operating
improperly, not a Tuxlink rendering mistake. We render faithfully; if anything we
should surface it, never paper over it.

IN-FRAME DETECTION SIGNAL (available today): fmt_callsign preserves the WIDEn-N
SSID, so `via` keeps the residual counter (WIDE2 == -0, WIDE2-1, WIDE2-2). Compute
WIDE hops CONSUMED (n_requested - n_remaining) vs digis TRACED (real callsigns,
H-bit set). When consumed > traced, >=1 digipeat never identified => an
unidentified/hidden hop. This is the missing capability behind operator's "we can
only handle 1 unknown hop right now": resolver bridges a traced-but-UNLOCATED hop
(dashed) but is blind to a fully-UNTRACED hop (digi never appears; only the WIDE
counter betrays it).

PROPOSED FIX (TABLED, do not build yet):
1. Map: when consumed > traced, render that leg uncertain/dashed so it never reads
   as a clean compliant direct hop. (Pure logic in resolveDigipeatPath; testable.)
2. OPTIONAL (operator's call, heavier): surface the anomaly (e.g. "unidentified
   digipeat in path" on the station popup) since it indicates a Part 97 ID gap.

STATE: no code changed. Temp [digipeat-resolve] console diagnostic remains
UNCOMMITTED in worktree bd-tuxlink-c973-placenames-packs (merged-dead qnu6 branch);
self-cleans on worktree disposal (never committable). Durable capture mechanism for
any future round = TUXLINK_APRS_RAW_CAPTURE env var (permanent dev feature). Raw
sample preserved above; the full 191-frame log is local gitignored scratch.
notes: TABLED 2026-06-21 per operator: findings recorded in --design; no client-side change to be made now. Root cause is real-world (non-compliant non-tracing digipeat / Part 97 ID gap), not a Tuxlink bug.

title: Trash: same-MID-across-identities collision in shared deleted/ dir
description: Codex backend adrev (tuxlink-wl7n, 2026-06-20) P1. The Deleted (Trash) folder is a single shared, non-namespaced dir (native_mailbox.rs folder_dir_ns: MailboxFolder::Deleted => root/deleted), while Inbox/Archive are per-FULL identity (received_root(ns)). If two identities each hold a message with the SAME MID in their own inbox, deleting both writes deleted/<mid>.b2f + deleted/<mid>.trash twice; move_between_ns overwrites, so the second delete clobbers the first's file AND its origin sidecar -> first message lost / restored with wrong origin. Realistic exposure is LOW (Winlink MIDs are ~globally unique; the realistic case is the SAME message delivered to two callsigns, where the lost copy is a duplicate), which is why it was tracked rather than blocking the wl7n merge. Robust fix: identity-qualify Trash storage (deleted/<ns>/<mid> subdirs OR a collision-safe filename keyed by ns) and have the shared Trash listing/Empty-Trash/auto-purge aggregate across namespaces; restore already reads origin_full from the sidecar so it can re-home correctly once the on-disk key disambiguates. Decision deferred to operator: fix pre-1.0 vs accept documented low-prob residual. Full transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-20-wl7n-delete-trash-backend-codex.md (local). Filed by juniper-basin-cedar.

title: APRS map: per-station search / focus
description: Search or pick a callsign to fly-to + highlight that station (center+zoom+highlight, and open its Station Data) — not a hard filter. Handle SSIDs and partial matches. Complements the category filter and the rewind view.

title: APRS map: chronological rewind view (time-window playback)
description: Let the map be rewound: a time-range picker + scrubber + play/pause to replay heard positions/paths over a chosen window, with a 'live' vs 'historical' toggle. Requires per-station position+frame history with timestamps (shared with the tracks feature). Useful for reconstructing net activity / an incident timeline.

title: APRS iGate (configurable RF<->APRS-IS gateway)
description: Configurable Internet Gateway: RX-iGate (gate heard RF frames to APRS-IS) and optional TX-iGate (gate APRS-IS traffic to RF for local stations). Off by default. Needs APRS-IS connection (server/port/passcode/filter), gating policy (dup suppression, rate limit, path rules), and clear on/off + status UI. RADIO-1: TX-iGate RF transmission is operator-gated; RX-iGate (RF->internet) is not a transmission. Part of the 18q2 umbrella igate scope.

title: APRS map: heard-station geographic tracks (breadcrumb trails)
description: Show a station's movement as a breadcrumb trail / polyline of its successive heard positions over time (aprs.fi-style). Standard APRS feature. Requires per-callsign position HISTORY (today is latest-position-wins; add a bounded, timestamped history ring) and a fading trail render. RF-honest: only actually-heard fixes, with times; respect position ambiguity. Shares the history store with the rewind view and feeds the mobile-station category.

title: APRS map: reimplement RF/digipeat path visualization (revive cn84)
description: Trace a heard station's RX/digipeat via-chain on the map (aprs.fi/aprs.to-style): the honest digipeat path through repeated=true hops for its latest frame. Previously shipped as tuxlink-cn84 and REVERTED in #834 as a per-frame perf hog on the software renderer. Revive now the MapLibre engine is stable and the per-render re-tile storm is fixed (xsv5). HARD perf bar: NO per-frame animation loop that pegs the CPU — static or cheaply-animated path only. RF-honesty: draw only repeated (H-bit) hops; an OBJECT/ITEM report plots the object's location, so its via-chain must NOT be traced from the pin. Data already plumbed: HeardPosition.via (ViaHop[]) + isObject.

title: [EPIC] APRS polish
description: Operator-scoped batch (2026-06-20) of APRS map/feature polish, now that the MapLibre map engine is in place and performant enough to carry richer overlays. Children: (1) reimplement RF/digipeat path tracing (revived from the reverted cn84); (2) heard-station geographic tracks/breadcrumbs (aprs.fi-style position history trails); (3) configurable iGate (RF<->APRS-IS gateway); (4) chronological/timeframe rewind view (map playback over a window); (5) per-station search/focus; (6) station-category filters (iGates, Digipeaters, Fixed-location, Mobile -> Vehicles/People) — reparents existing tuxlink-8fjx. Sibling/umbrella context: the broader tuxlink-18q2 'Full APRS rich experience' epic; this is the focused near-term polish batch. RF-honesty applies throughout: render only what was actually heard.

title: Basemap: render placenames at high zoom (packs own labels in-coverage; overview only outside)
description:
PRE-EXISTING (both MapLibre and the new Leaflet engine): the bundled overview (z0-6) owns ALL labels and overzooms beyond z6 (sparse, city-level), while region packs contribute detail geometry but NO labels (MapLibre dropped pack 'symbol' layers in basemapStyle.ts per the tuxlink-vnk7 B1 llvmpipe glyph-cost call; the Leaflet migration faithfully reproduced this with labelRules:[] on pack layers in basemapLeaflet.ts). Result: at local/high zoom the operator sees streets but few/no placenames. Operator flagged as undesirable during the tuxlink-6kdw smoke.

Tension: the overview's overzoom is the never-blank fallback, so it must keep labeling everywhere — which means packs cannot ALSO label without double-labels at the overlap (each protomaps-leaflet layer has its own collision Labeler; no cross-layer dedup). Proper fix needs coverage-aware label gating: packs render labels WITHIN their coverage, overview renders labels only OUTSIDE pack coverage (or cap overview labels to <=z6 and accept no labels in the small outside-pack high-zoom void). Plus a perf re-check — the Leaflet Canvas2D text path is cheaper than MapLibre's GL glyph shaping (operator reports the new map is tangibly faster), so per-pack labels that were too costly on MapLibre/llvmpipe may now be affordable.

Scope: this is a basemap-quality improvement, not a Phase-1 faithful-migration regression. Do after tuxlink-6kdw lands. Validate label density + no-doubling + perf via grim/smoke. Agent: sage-fox-mesa.
notes: glade-gulch-fern 2026-06-20: Pack-placenames IMPLEMENTED + PR'd (#847, branch bd-tuxlink-c973/pack-labels). Change: basemapLeaflet.ts pack layers now pass labelRules: pmLabelRules(namedFlavor(flavor),'en') instead of []. title: PMTiles seam ETag is length-only → stale JS tile cache on same-size pack re-download
description: serve.rs/lib.rs (~lib.rs:452-457) emits ETag: "<total_len>" for tile://pmtiles/<id>. pmtiles JS (v4) keys its cache by source URL + ETag (pmtiles src/index.ts:602-607). Region packs are mutable under a stable tile://pmtiles/<id> URL (lib.rs:414-420). A delete + re-download of the same pack id producing the same byte length reuses stale header/directory/tile offsets in the JS cache → wrong tiles. Affects BOTH the MapLibre path (pre-existing) and the new Leaflet path. Fix: ETag must include a content/generation fingerprint (hash or generation counter), not just length; surface that generation so the frontend cache key (Leaflet base-layer key) can incorporate it. Found in tuxlink-6kdw Codex adrev round 2 (2026-06-20). Agent: sage-fox-mesa.

title: Export PNG for AprsPositionsMap on Leaflet (proper DOM-aware snapshot)
description: The WX 'Export PNG' snapshot (tuxlink-ni5b/xsv5) relied on MapLibre's single WebGL getCanvas(). On Leaflet, protomaps-leaflet renders per-tile positioned <canvas> elements and pins are DOM markers/divIcons, so naive canvas compositing omits pins/labels/badges and ignores tile pane transforms (Codex adrev P0, 2026-06-20). Phase 1 (tuxlink-6kdw) defers Export PNG rather than ship a broken button. Implement a proper DOM-aware Leaflet snapshot (tile-canvas compositing WITH leaflet pane/tile transforms + DOM marker rasterization, or a leaflet-image-class approach validated under WebKitGTK CSP). Blocked-by tuxlink-6kdw. Agent: sage-fox-mesa.

title: Decide tuxlink CMS credential model: client SID (publishable) vs secret access code (must not ship in repo)
description: From 2026-06-19 review (N2 finding: Winlink apps embed CMS API access codes in a shared lib — a secret-in-a-distributed-artifact anti-pattern). tuxlink is open-source, so determine exactly what credential it needs for CMS connectivity. A client SID/program identifier is fine to publish (identifier, not a secret). A genuine secret access code must NOT land in the repo (that IS the N2 mistake) — use per-user secure-login auth, OS keyring, or user-supplied. A published program key would at most allow impersonating the tuxlink PROGRAM, not users (CMS auth is per-callsign secure-login). Resolve: SID or secret? Aligns with the no-disk-creds default.

title: APRS→Winlink local-area weather report: aggregate heard WX/position data into an emailable ground-truth situation report
description: Operator vision 2026-06-19 — HIGH STRATEGIC VALUE (operator called it 'a big one'). Today area weather is free, unstructured, internet-only data. EmComm scenario (another Hurricane Helene): cell + internet down, volunteers pouring in from across the country — what are conditions on the ground, how should they pack/dress? Tuxlink hears APRS weather (WX) reports + telemetry + positions over RF and currently discards them after display. This feature AGGREGATES that accumulated tactical APRS data into a STRUCTURED local-area weather/situation report that can be composed and sent as a Winlink message (the Winlink side is already built) — turning discarded tactical data into strategic, transmittable ground truth. Backend already parses WX (src-tauri/src/winlink/aprs/weather.rs) and telemetry (telemetry.rs/telemetry_store.rs). Scope: spatial/temporal aggregation + summarization (temp/wind/gust/rain/pressure/humidity ranges across heard stations, with station count + area + freshness), a report template, and wiring into the compose/Winlink send path. NEEDS OFFICE-HOURS/brainstorm: report format, which fields, how it composes into a Winlink message, units, and how to express 'how to pack/dress' guidance honestly from data. RF-honesty: report only what was actually heard, with station count + age. Part of epic tuxlink-18q2; shares data with the map weather-mode issue.

title: APRS weather (WX) parse + engine emit — fixed-field weather reports → aprs-weather:new
description: Operator correction during the 2phz panel design (2026-06-17): real APRS environmental stations overwhelmingly emit WEATHER (WX) reports — a fixed, standardized field format — not the self-named T# telemetry channels (#792). tuxlink has NO WX parser today. This slice adds it so the source-reactive env panel (tuxlink-2phz) actually populates with the common case. Mirrors the telemetry-emit pattern of PR #792: a pure parser + a small engine seam + a serde DTO emitted to the UI; no transmit, RX-only.
design: Scraped authoritative field map (aprslib parsing/weather.py): key letters g=gust c=winddir t=temp S/s=windspeed r=rain1h p=rain24h P=rainMidnight h=humidity b=pressure l/L=luminosity s=snow #=rainRaw. Conversions: wind mph*0.44704, rain *0.254, temp (F-32)/1.8, pressure /10, snow *25.4, h00=100%. Positionless WX regex anchor: ^(\d{8})c..s..g..t.. ; position-embedded WX reuses the position course/speed (ddd/sss) slot + _ symbol then the field letters. Pattern to MIRROR: PR #792 — pure module (telemetry.rs) + accumulator/DTO (telemetry_store.rs) + engine.rs seams + EventSink method + 3 sink impls + aprsTypes.ts DTO. Full spec: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-xygm-design-20260617-102445.md. This slice is BACKEND ONLY; the source-reactive panel is tuxlink-2phz.
acceptance_criteria:
- Pure WX parser decodes the APRS weather field set off (a) position reports bearing the weather symbol (_) and (b) positionless weather reports (timestamp + ddd/sss + field letters). Fields + units per the aprslib reference: wind dir (deg), wind speed/gust (mph), temperature (degF, incl negative t-NN), rain 1h/24h/since-midnight (1/100 in), humidity (% , h00=100), barometric pressure (1/10 hPa), luminosity (l/L, W/m2), snow (in). RF-honesty: only fields present on the wire are reported; no fabricated zeros.
- Engine seam wires the parser into ingest_ax25 and emits a WeatherReport DTO (serde camelCase) on aprs-weather:new, keyed by sender; mirrors the emit_position/emit_telemetry sink pattern.
- Frontend WeatherReportDto contract added to aprsTypes.ts (channel kinds: wind_dir/wind_speed/wind_gust/temperature/humidity/pressure/rain/luminosity/snow) for the source-reactive panel to consume.
- Pure-parser unit tests (real on-air sample bodies) + an engine integration test through ingest_ax25. Frontend typecheck clean. Rust compiled/tested by CI (no local cold-cargo).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-wu2x-wx-parse. Branch: bd-tuxlink-wu2x/wx-parse. Created 2026-06-18 14:49:25 UTC by kite-harrier-shoal.

Built via build-robust-features: TDD (23+ tests), 1 Codex cross-provider adrev round (caught+fixed 5 issues incl. 2 hostile-RF panics + snow unit), CI green both arches. Unblocks tuxlink-2phz (the source-reactive env panel consumes WeatherReportDto + InboundTelemetryDto).

Nothing currently verifies that the produced .deb installs cleanly via the documented apt path on a stock system, so dependency regressions or a wrong dep name would only surface in the field. Add a CI job that installs the built .deb on clean Debian containers and asserts it resolves + the binary is present.
acceptance_criteria: - CI installs the built .deb on clean Debian Trixie AND Bookworm images, arm64 + amd64 where feasible, via 'apt-get update && apt-get install -y ./tuxlink_*.deb'. - Asserts apt resolves all Depends (no --fix-broken needed) and the tuxlink binary is installed/launchable (e.g. --version or dpkg -L). - Runs on the release workflow (or PR build) so dep regressions fail CI, not a tester.

title: ARDOP outbound reads password from stale 'tuxlink-pat' keyring service (fails secure login after credential migration)
description:
Why: The ARDOP B2F outbound path reads the Winlink password directly from the legacy keyring service 'tuxlink-pat' (winlink_backend.rs ~line 1714), while the wizard now WRITES the credential to the 'tuxlink' service (wizard.rs ~line 186) and every other reader goes through credentials::read_password(), which prefers 'tuxlink' and migrates the user off 'tuxlink-pat' (deleting the legacy entry after migration).

Result: once the one-time migration runs and removes the 'tuxlink-pat' entry, the ARDOP path reads None and attempts to connect password-less. That fails the CMS secure-login (;PQ) challenge, so ARDOP outbound to a CMS gateway breaks for any user whose credential has migrated — silently, with a confusing 'login failed' rather than an obvious cause.

Discovered during the pre-public defensive credential audit (2026-06-16). Not a credential-at-rest exposure (it still only touches the OS keyring); a routing/correctness bug.

What: Route the ARDOP password read through credentials::read_password(&callsign) like every other consumer, so it resolves 'tuxlink' first and benefits from the same migration/fallback logic. Remove the direct 'tuxlink-pat' Entry lookup at the ARDOP call site.
design: credentials::read_password already encapsulates the prefer-'tuxlink', migrate-from-'tuxlink-pat' logic; the ARDOP site predates or missed that consolidation. Single-call-site fix. Verify against winlink_backend.rs ARDOP connect path (~1714 on origin/main; confirm current line).
acceptance_criteria:
- The ARDOP outbound path obtains the password via credentials::read_password() (no direct 'tuxlink-pat' keyring lookup remains at that site).
- After a simulated migration (credential only in 'tuxlink', 'tuxlink-pat' absent), the ARDOP path resolves the password.
- A regression test covers 'credential in tuxlink only' for the ARDOP read path.
- ripgrep confirms no other consumer still reads 'tuxlink-pat' directly outside credentials.rs.
notes: CLOSED resolved-by-kc3q (operator decision 2026-06-17). Source bug already fixed: ARDOP CMS path reads via credentials::read_password (winlink_backend.rs:3123); read_password reads 'tuxlink' only. Behavioral regression coverage in winlink_credentials_test.rs (tuxlink-only resolves; tuxlink-pat-only ignored). Codex read-only review concurred (FIXED). No new code; no call-site test added per operator.
close_reason: Closed

title: CMS account password change in the setup wizard (rotate credential without Winlink Express)
description:
Why: The Winlink CMS account password can currently only be changed through Winlink Express, so Tuxlink users depend on a second client for the credential lifecycle and cannot rotate their CMS password from within Tuxlink. In-app password change lets a user set a unique, rotatable Winlink credential and removes the Winlink Express dependency for credential management.

What: Implement the CMS account password-change exchange in the native backend and surface it as a control in the setup wizard.
- Backend: a native command that authenticates with the current credential and submits a new password to the CMS (the 'update' operation in the account CRUD the CMS exposes to clients per the client-onboarding flow). Verify against cms-z (dev, plaintext :8772) before production.
- On success, atomically update the OS keyring entry (service 'tuxlink', account '<callsign>') so the stored credential matches; a failure must leave the existing credential intact (no stale or half-written secret).
- Wizard: new-password + confirm fields with validation and clear success/error states; copy guiding the user to choose a unique password not reused elsewhere.
- The protocol exchange is the small part; the wizard layout is the main effort.

Out of scope: account create/delete (the rest of the CRUD); a Settings-pane entry point (wizard first, Settings is a fast-follow if wanted).
design: Reuse the native CMS session path (native_cms_probe / NativeBackend secure-login, MD5 challenge-response) for the authenticated exchange. Keep the credential at rest in the OS keyring per the existing pattern (no plaintext/config/env persistence). Confirm the exact wire protocol against a reference client implementation during build (details held in private design notes). Related: tuxlink-njzm (longer-term CMS decouple / self-host) — this is the near-term credential-hygiene fix within the existing CMS dependency, complementary to that exploration.
acceptance_criteria:
- Native backend command changes the CMS account password (current -> new), exercised against cms-z.
- Wizard exposes the change control with new + confirm fields, input validation, and explicit success/error handling.
- OS keyring entry updated atomically on success; on failure the prior credential is unchanged.
- Unit tests cover the protocol encode and the wizard flow (TDD).
notes: SUB-PROJECTS 2+3 BUILT -> PR #798 (off main, ready). Sub-project 3: WinlinkAccountSettings + CmsAccountDelete (account_remove WIRED behind typed-confirmation; 403/privileged + UnknownOutcome + KeyringDesync handled gracefully) + CmsRecoveryEmail (account_set_recovery_email). Sub-project 2: 'Forgot password?' in IdentitySwitcher unlock form (account_send_recovery for the FULL; no-recovery-address surfaced). Both gated on cms_password_change_available. Change-password NOT duplicated to status bar (already in Settings; surfaced for operator). Frontend-only, reuses existing commands. TDD all green + typecheck clean. Built FULLY WIRED per operator correction (no gating on the issued key). Worktree: worktrees/bd-tuxlink-vfb3-account-mgmt-ui. Old worktree bd-tuxlink-vfb3-cms-password-change is merged-dead -> dispose. Live exercise (incl. whether issued key can invoke account/remove) still operator-run, downstream of build; blocked on tuxlink-lu7t.

title: Decouple Tuxlink from the Winlink CMS — remove CMS connectivity + investigate a self-hosted message server
description:
Operator decision 2026-06-16: do not have Tuxlink depend on or interoperate with the Winlink CMS. Motivation: a 2026-06-16 security review of the Winlink ecosystem surfaced multiple serious, unpatched vulnerabilities in the reference client / CMS-adjacent trust model (received-attachment RCE, recoverable stored credentials via a hardcoded key, unauthenticated channel-directory overwrite, path traversals, form-render injection), AND a dismissive vendor/governance posture (the Winlink Development Team engaged with only one of six findings, deflected the rest to 'the user's antivirus,' declined to commit to fixes, and asked the reporter not to disclose). Operator does not want Tuxlink connected to this system.

TWO PARTS:
1. REMOVE Winlink CMS connectivity from Tuxlink — the native CMS/telnet B2F-to-CMS path (CMS connection, secure-login, telnet-to-CMS, catalog/inquiry-over-CMS). Scope exactly what is removed vs retained (the native B2F message engine, peer-to-peer, the RF transports, and the mailbox are likely independent of the CMS dependency).
2. INVESTIGATE standing up our own message server / back-end — an independent CMS-equivalent so Tuxlink can carry mail without the Winlink network (relay/store-and-forward, addressing, auth, federation question).

SEQUENCING / RISK: CMS connectivity is currently a core path; removing it before an alternative exists leaves the app reachable only via RF/P2P (or standalone). The remove and the build-own are linked — decide the target posture first (CMS-less + RF/P2P only? independent network? both, phased?).

NEEDS OFFICE-HOURS / design before building. This is a strategic product-direction decision (Tuxlink is currently framed as a native Winlink client), with real tradeoffs (the EmComm value of Winlink-network reach; the no-users-without-a-network problem; the clean-sheet effort). Route through office-hours/brainstorm; likely promotes to an EPIC with children (decision+design -> remove-CMS -> stand-up-backend). Extends the existing independence posture (cf. memories project_v05_modem_design_posture clean-sheet modem; project_fork_enables_aggressive_deletion).

title: [SECURITY P2] message_attachment_save writes to a renderer-supplied dest_path (backend should own the save grant)
description: MEDIUM (Codex pre-public security review 2026-06-16, confidence 8/10; the 2 Claude agents dismissed it as a trusted save-dialog path). ui_commands.rs message_attachment_save (~line 1368) takes dest_path directly from the frontend IPC payload and fs::write()s the (possibly attacker-controlled, from a received message) attachment bytes to that exact path with no backend-side binding to a dialog grant. Under Tauri's renderer-is-untrusted threat model, a compromised webview could write arbitrary files. NOT exploitable today in practice (requires a separate webview compromise; the XSS surface was reviewed clean), so this is defense-in-depth — but a skeptic auditing the Tauri command surface WILL flag fs::write(frontend_path, ...). FIX: move the save dialog + write into one backend command, OR mint a one-time backend save-grant token tied to the exact dialog-returned path and require it for the write; do not accept raw renderer paths. Pre-public hardening. Raw Codex trace: dev/adversarial/2026-06-16-pre-public-security-codex.md (gitignored).
notes: Fix shipped as PR #807 (branch bd-tuxlink-hyfo/attach-save-grant): backend owns the attachment save dialog+write (Rust tauri-plugin-dialog blocking_save_file via spawn_blocking); renderer no longer supplies dest_path; suggested name basename-reduced. Frontend AttachmentStrip updated. TDD (10 contract tests; mailbox suite 344 green); typecheck clean. Codex 2-round adrev: all Solid, no findings. Wire-walk passed. Sibling renderer-path pattern (forms_export_pdf/logging_export/report_issue_flow) filed as tuxlink-a4hf P3. Close on merge.

title: Ship SSTV into APRS chat (image send/receive) — currently prototype-only, not user-reachable
description:
GAP (verified 2026-06-16, opossum-marsh-oriole): SSTV is fully built but ONLY as a standalone prototype crate at dev/tools/sstv-proto/ (Encoder/Decoder: Robot36 + PD modes, 32kHz mono PCM, ARGB pixels; std-only, path-dep-able). It is NOT integrated into the app — no src-tauri module, no Tauri command, no UI. A user cannot use SSTV by any means in 0.67.0. 'sstv-codec shipped st5n' meant the proto crate merged to dev/tools, not wired to the product.

TARGET SURFACE: APRS tactical chat (src/aprs/AprsChatPanel.tsx — today text-only, 67-char, AX.25 packet via aprs_send(call,text)).

ARCHITECTURE (key): SSTV is analog image-over-AUDIO, not packet. It rides the EXISTING UV-Pro audio transport (src-tauri/src/winlink/ax25/uvpro/audio/, spec tuxlink-bcsy) — a SECOND RFCOMM audio channel with implicit-PTT keying + RADIO-1 working-abort, already built+tested but NOT connected to chat. Flow: image -> sstv-proto encode -> PCM 32kHz -> AudioTransport::send_pcm over audio channel -> radio. RX mirror: pump_rx -> SBC decode -> proto decode -> image.

CRITICAL BLOCKER / DESIGN GATE: the SBC codec is a trait stub (NullSbcCodec passthrough) in audio/codec.rs — no real codec in Rust yet. Production audio would malform until a real SBC encoder/decoder exists (pure-Rust impl, or libsbc C-FFI). MUST resolve first. (Verify this claim before building.)

ORDERED WORK:
1. Resolve SBC codec (pure-Rust vs libsbc FFI) — architecture gate; NullSbcCodec is identity-passthrough.
2. Add sstv-proto as a path dependency in src-tauri/Cargo.toml; confirm it builds under Tauri.
3. Rust SstvSender/SstvReceiver modules (src-tauri/src/winlink/aprs/) wiring proto encode/decode <-> AudioTransport send_pcm/pump_rx; image<->ARGB via image crate; SSTV mode selection.
4. Wire UvproSession to lazily open the second RFCOMM audio channel on first image send; close on disconnect.
5. aprs_send_image(recipient, image bytes) Tauri command; register in lib.rs; emit sstv-image:inbound / tx-progress / abort events.
6. Extend APRS message types (aprsTypes.ts ChannelMessage + engine) to carry image metadata.
7. AprsChatPanel.tsx: image picker + local preview + inline render of inbound/outbound images in the feed.
8. RADIO-1: publish bounded max airtime (Robot36 ~36s, PD120 ~12s); reuse AudioTransport::abort.

DESIGN DECISIONS (operator surface): image-select UX (picker/paste/drag); SSTV mode auto vs chooser; inline-in-feed vs gallery; sync-blocking-with-progress vs async; SBC implement vs FFI. Map source: Explore agent gap report 2026-06-16.

The map currently labels every InboundPos by the SENDER callsign; an object/item's label is its NAME, so this needs a small name-bearing field: add an optional name/label to InboundPos (engine) + InboundPosDto (serde camelCase) + HeardPosition/InboundPosDto (aprsTypes.ts), thread through useAprsPositions, and have AprsPositionsMap label by name ?? call + key the byCall/dedupe map appropriately (objects keyed by name, stations by call). Wire parse_object_or_item into engine ingest_ax25/try_emit_position (alongside parse_position/parse_mice); honor the killed (_) tombstone to remove a plotted entity. Frontend is vitest-testable; Rust via CI. Part of the full-APRS epic (tuxlink-18q2). Touches the in-progress 6vgt positions-map area — coordinate.

title: APRS object/item report parser (;/) DTIs) — RX
description: Part of the full-APRS epic (tuxlink-18q2). Parse APRS OBJECT (';') and ITEM (')') reports — entities (weather objects, event markers, ARES assets, gateways) whose position another station reports, distinct from a station's own beacon. Today parse_position handles only the station-own DTIs !=@/; objects/items fall through to the raw feed and never reach the map. SCOPE (atomic, pure): parse the object name (9-char, '*' live / '_' killed) + 7-char timestamp + embedded position; parse the item name (3-9 char, '!' live / '_' killed) + embedded position. Reuse position.rs's uncompressed/compressed/Base-91 decoders. Return {name, alive, AprsPosition}. Pure, fully unit-tested. FAST-FOLLOW (not this issue): engine emit needs a name-bearing position DTO (the map labels by sender; an object's label is its NAME) — separate issue. RF-honesty: parse only what's on the wire.
notes: Pure parser shipped in PR #777 (parse_object_or_item + AprsObject; 23 tests; branch bd-tuxlink-z46p/aprs-object-item). Map emit (name-bearing DTO) split to tuxlink-m64z. Close z46p on parser merge.
close_reason: Shipped: gnru lookup+popup in PR #768; z46p parser + m64z map-emit in PR #777 (both MERGED). Closing stale in_progress markers; map-face sprites tracked separately in tuxlink-90xb.

title: APRS map-face symbol rendering: per-pin sprite icons on the Tac Chat map
description:
Fast-follow to tuxlink-gnru (symbol lookup + popup identity, PR #768). Render the APRS symbol on each map PIN (at-a-glance differentiation), not just in the click popup. The offline basemap glyph stack is Latin-only SDF, so emoji can't render via a MapLibre text-field layer (tofu in WebKitGTK). Correct vehicle: a symbol SPRITE SHEET (PNG/SDF) registered as MapLibre 'images' + a symbol layer using icon-image keyed off symbolTable/symbolCode (reuse lookupAprsSymbol to pick a sprite id). 

NEEDS: (a) operator/design pass on the visual treatment (sprite source — generate from aprs.org symbol chart or hessu/aprs-symbols; how overlays draw; how it composes with the existing ambiguity-amber / stale-grey honesty encoding on the current circle layer); (b) a WebKitGTK visual smoke (grim) since canvas render can't be vitest-verified (chromium_not_webkitgtk_proxy). Part of the full-APRS epic (tuxlink-18q2).
notes:
GATE LIFTED 2026-06-18: operator explicitly requested authentic APRS icons on map pins (chose 'authentic sprite sheet' over emoji/category-pins). This is the #1 item from the tuxlink-hzwc UX sweep; deferred here because it needs a vendored sprite asset + render pipeline + smoke, not a quick fix. PLAN: vendor hessu/aprs-symbol-index (CC0; the de-facto open set aprs.fi uses) — note its sprites are build-generated, so either run its generator or build a sprite from the SVGs; load via map.addImage + a symbol layer with icon-image keyed off symbolTable/symbolCode through lookupAprsSymbol; compose with the existing ambiguity-amber/stale-grey circle honesty encoding; add attribution to LICENSE/NOTICE (public-repo decision). Verify via grim on the converged build (no WebKitGTK map render in headless env).

title: APRS symbol-table support: render heard-station symbols on the Tac Chat map (RX)
description:
Part of the full-APRS epic (tuxlink-18q2, label roadmap-2026-06-16). The position parser already decodes symbol_table + symbol_code chars and carries them to the frontend (InboundPosDto.symbolTable/symbolCode), but AprsPositionsMap renders every station as an identical generic blue dot — a weather station, a Jeep, and a digipeater look the same. 

SCOPE (atomic, pure-data core + minimal render wiring): a TS symbol-lookup module mapping the 2-char APRS symbol (table char + code char) to a canonical {name, glyph}. Full PRIMARY table ('/'), full ALTERNATE table (backslash), and OVERLAY support (table char is an alphanumeric other than '/' or backslash → alternate-table symbol at code char, with the table char as an overlay). Pin to the APRS symbol spec (aprs.org/symbols, aprslib symbol data). Pure, exhaustively unit-tested. Then wire: emit/derive glyph+name and render the glyph on the map marker (MapLibre text symbol layer, CSP-safe, no sprite assets) + show the symbol name in the station popup.

OUT OF SCOPE (fast-follow): bespoke SVG sprite sheet matching aprs.fi exactly; symbol selection UI for own-beacon (ties to hj4k beacon TX). 

RF-honesty: render only the symbol actually decoded off the wire.
notes: Lookup module + popup identity shipped in PR #768 (branch bd-tuxlink-gnru/aprs-symbols, worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-gnru-aprs-symbols). Map-face sprite render split to tuxlink-90xb. Close on PR merge.
