title: Build the 'Update propagation data' feature (internet + RF-catalog fallback)
description:
Find-a-Station needs an 'Update propagation data' button (designed into the layout next to 'Update station list', NOT yet placed — a non-functional button is a placeholder stub). Operator wants: internet-first is FINE, but the fallback to catalog-based requests OVER RF must be present and ROBUST (the defining scenario: a station cold-booted from storage, fully offline, sets up over the radio).

VERIFIED END-TO-END STATUS (today, traced not asserted): tuxlink CANNOT update propagation at runtime AT ALL. The SSN forecast is include_str! at compile time (propagation/ssn.rs); NO code writes/persists an updated forecast from any source. The over-radio catalog INQUIRY sender EXISTS (catalog/composer.rs + catalog_send_inquiry -> To: INQUIRY@winlink.org, Subject: REQUEST, body=catalog filenames; used today for station lists PUB_*) but is NOT wired for solar data, and there is NO solar-reply parser.

HOW WLE DOES IT (verified from decompiled RMS Express, library-of-hamexandria/winlink-re/decompiled): (1) SFI fetched over internet by SFIhelper.GetSFI (background thread Main.UpdateSFIthread -> Globals.UpdateSFI, Globals.cs:6261) ONLY if HaveInternetConnection; the install logs show the source is NOAA SWPC: 'SFI = 197 from https://services.swpc.noaa.gov/text/wwv.txt'. (2) SSN is DERIVED from SFI via WL2KInterop.GetSSN(SFI, SFIpercent/100); 'SFI Percent' default 80 = 80% live SFI + 20% table. (3) Gateway/path indices downloaded from the Winlink server (UpdatePropagation.DoUpdate -> GetChannelsList), gated NeedPropagationUpdate() = |SFI-lastSFI|>delta AND >=2h. Offline: recompute locally from cached data.

GROUNDED DESIGN (primary/fallback; primary can be better than WLE):
 PREREQUISITE: make the forecast runtime-mutable — write to a writable path (like prefs); engine reads writable-then-bundled fallback. Nothing works without this.
 PRIMARY (online): NOAA SWPC. Better than WLE: SWPC publishes predicted SMOOTHED SSN directly (the exact VOACAP input) rather than deriving SSN from daily SFI; also grab SFI+K for the conditions bar. (wwv.txt is the verified WLE endpoint; confirm exact SWPC product/format before coding.)
 RF FALLBACK (robust, mandatory): reuse the EXISTING catalog_send_inquiry to request catalog item PROP_SGAS ('Daily Solar and Geophysical Activity Summary (used for SSN)', ~1.5KB) or PROP_WWV ('Daily WWV Solar Flux, A & K Index', 621B) from src-tauri/resources/catalog/winlink-queries.txt (PROPAGATION category). CMS replies over radio -> ingest + parse the SGAS/WWV text -> persist. The inquiry SEND half is shipped; needs: request wiring for PROP_SGAS + a reply parser + persist.
 LAST RESORT (offline): bundled ssn-forecast.json (current behavior), last-known-good, never blocks.
 HYGIENE (from WLE): cache + freshness stamp; manual button = force-refresh; auto only when stale; gate cadence.
 OPEN DESIGN Q: model-correct (SWPC smoothed SSN, what VOACAP wants) vs WLE-style responsive (live SFI->SSN). Default model-correct, show live SFI as context.
NOTE: do NOT use any internet SOURCE the operator hasn't approved — the SOURCE choice was explicitly NOT approved; only the internet-primary + robust-RF-fallback SHAPE is agreed. Deferred to a focused follow-up session (operator).
notes: FOUNDATION MERGED 2026-06-16: PR #765 (Layers 1+2 — solar parsers, runtime-mutable forecast, orchestration core) merged to main (commit 50e48b27) per operator for today's combined release; ot71 verify CI was green. Branch merged-dead, worktree disposed. REMAINS OPEN for L3-5 (reqwest fetch + Tauri cmds + RF reply ingestion; UI button + conditions bar; static-only Codex adrev + wire-walk) — start on a FRESH worktree off updated main per docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-16-ot71-update-propagation-data.md. Foundation is inert until L3-5 wire it.

title: Find-a-Station reachability recompute is slow (~5s) on prefs change
description:
Operator-observed (2026-06-16): changing power/antenna/height/ground/noise/SNR re-colors the map but takes upwards of 5s; feels like a bug, not just compute.

MECHANISM: useReachabilityMap (src/catalog/useReachabilityMap.ts) runs ONE voacapl prediction PER visible on-band station, bounded pool PREDICT_CONCURRENCY=6. With ~50+ visible stations that's ~50 voacapl subprocess runs. The reachability-reload fix (shipped #745, commit a79dcc68) made the FULL sweep re-run on EVERY prefs change (predictReload dep) — previously it only ran on station-set change (which was the stale-tier bug). So the cost is now felt on every tweak.

FIRST STEP (do NOT skip — operator intuition 'more like a bug'): MEASURE per-call voacapl latency (voacapl + itshfbc are stageable; time a single run) and confirm whether it's N-runs-at-6-concurrency vs a per-call inefficiency (e.g., make_scratch_itshfbc symlink setup per call) vs double-firing effects (check useReachabilityMap deps don't fire twice per change).
THEN: debounce the recompute (coalesce rapid changes) + gate it (WLE itself gates propagation updates on SFI-delta + >=2h cadence — precedent). Consider caching per (grid,band,hour,prefs) and only recomputing changed inputs. Show a loading affordance so it doesn't read as frozen.
Deferred from the #745 session to a focused follow-up (operator).
notes: FIX SHIPPED (PR #763, opossum-marsh-oriole 2026-06-16): debounce + loading affordance. All 4 CI checks GREEN. Leaving in_progress pending operator smoke (drag height/power -> map re-colors ONCE after settle) + merge. Do NOT close before merge.

title: Packet B2F greeting sends empty locator (GPS arbiter not wired into native_packet_connect)
description: On-air 2026-06-16 (K7YCA-8): our greeting went out as '; K7YCA DE N7CPZ ()' — EMPTY grid — while WoAD sent '(DM33wp)'. Root cause: native_packet_connect resolves the locator with the static cms_locator(config) (reads only config.identity.grid), whereas native_connect (telnet) was wired to effective_broadcast_locator(config, position) which sources the live GPS/manual grid from the shared PositionArbiter (tuxlink-686). The packet path was never threaded the arbiter, so a GPS-derived grid (not written to config.identity.grid) never reaches the packet B2F greeting. FIX: pass self.position into native_packet_connect (Arc, Send+'static, safe across spawn_blocking) and resolve via crate::position::effective_broadcast_locator(config, position.as_deref()) instead of cms_locator. Mirrors the telnet path. Did NOT cause the CMS unknown-client rejection, but the locator is a core Tuxlink feature and must be broadcast.
notes: Fix pushed (PR pending). Did not add a packet-greeting integration test (FakeAx25Stream) — follow-up. resolve_locator/effective_broadcast_locator already tested.
close_reason: Closed

title: ARDOP: Show Raw is a no-op — emit raw ARDOP frames into the session log
description: SessionLogSection showRaw filter is wired (line 80) but no ARDOP modem code emits raw-level lines into SessionLogState (grep of src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/ardop for log emission: empty). The wire-tap is only installed for the telnet/B2F NativeBackend, not the ARDOP transport. Add a raw/wire sink for the ARDOP cmd+data ports so Show Raw reveals ardopcf protocol traffic.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION SPEC (swallow-vetch-lupine, 2026-06-16) — deferred from the
ARDOP-debug batch (PR with vyby/nnjz/5g5d) as the riskiest blind change; needs
on-air ARDOP to verify, so it wants its own focused pass.

DIAGNOSIS (confirmed on origin/main):
- SessionLogSection showRaw filter is wired (line ~80): showRaw ? all : entries
  without level==='raw'. useSessionLog projects source==='wire' (and
  level trace/debug) → 'raw'.
- A WireSink mechanism EXISTS: winlink_backend.rs ~1127
  `type WireSink = Arc<dyn Fn(&str) + Send + Sync>` (tuxlink-nki); NativeBackend
  taps B2F wire lines via .with_wire_log(sink) → appends LogSource::Wire +
  emits live. So the plumbing pattern is established.
- GAP: nothing in src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/ardop/ feeds any wire sink. Two
  distinct 'raw' streams an operator would want, neither wired for ARDOP:
  (a) B2F protocol lines (;PQ/;PR/proposals) — shared B2F engine; check whether
      run_ardop_b2f_exchange threads a WireSink the way NativeBackend.connect does.
  (b) ardopcf cmd-port traffic (NEWSTATE/REJ/FAULT/PTT) — THIS is the REJ-frame
      stream the operator sees; read in the cmd reader thread at
      winlink/modem/ardop/session.rs ~76-145 (reads lines, parses to Command,
      sends via mpsc). NOT tapped.

PLAN:
1. Add `wire: Option<WireSink>` to CmdSocket (session.rs); the reader thread
   calls wire(raw_line) for each line BEFORE parsing to Command (so REJ/NEWSTATE
   show even when unparsed). Keep it Option so tests/listen paths can pass None.
2. Thread the sink from the command layer (modem_ardop_connect / _b2f_exchange /
   ardop_listen — all have AppHandle) down through ArdopTransport construction +
   init into CmdSocket::connect. The sink closure = move |s| emit_session_line_with_source(
   &app, &log_buffer, LogLevel::Trace, LogSource::Wire, format!("ardopcf< {s}")).
   (Outbound cmd writes can tee similarly: "ardopcf> CMD".)
3. Optionally tap the data pump (data.rs pump_decoder) for ARQ frame kinds, but
   the cmd-port stream is the high-value one — do that first.
4. Verify: arm/connect ARDOP, toggle Show Raw, confirm cmd-port lines appear and
   are hidden when Show Raw is off. Needs on-air or a Dire Wolf/loopback ardopcf.

TESTS: a CmdSocket reader unit test asserting the wire sink is invoked per line;
a SessionLogSection test already covers the showRaw filter.
close_reason: Closed

title: Map remembers + restores prior operator viewport on start (APRS tac-chat map + Find-a-Station)
description:
Operator request (2026-06-15): the map should remember the prior viewport (center+zoom) and load directly at it on start, instead of mounting at the default world view and then laboriously fetching unneeded tiles + animating a flyTo to the operator position.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (origin/main): the shared src/map/MapLibreMap.tsx constructs at initialCenter (default 0,0) + initialZoom (default DEFAULT_ZOOM=2), then an async-arrival effect (MapLibreMap.tsx:239-251) drives map.flyTo() when the operator center resolves AFTER construction. AprsPositionsMap mounts <MapLibreMap> with NO initial center (always world z2 then fly). StationFinderMap passes initialCenter={me} from operatorGrid, but operatorGrid is async so cold-start is null -> world view -> flyTo. No viewport persistence exists anywhere (no localStorage/config). Not previously filed.

SCOPE: APRS tac-chat map (AprsPositionsMap) + Find-a-Station map (StationFinderMap). Both share MapLibreMap.

SPEC:
- Persist last viewport (center lon/lat + zoom) per surface in localStorage, keyed per map (e.g. tuxlink:map-viewport:aprs, tuxlink:map-viewport:station-finder). Transient view state, not config.json.
- On mount: if a saved viewport exists, initialize the map directly there AND suppress the async operator-position flyTo (the whole point: do not pan away from where the operator left it).
- First run (no saved viewport): keep today's behavior (fall back to operator grid/GPS, else default world view).
- Persist on viewport settle (moveend), debounced, capturing both center and zoom.
- Reusable hook (usePersistedViewport(key)) + MapLibreMap emits center+zoom on moveend (it emits onZoomChange today; add center).

DONE = wire-walk flows: (1) pan/zoom the APRS map, restart -> opens at that exact view, no flyTo. (2) same for Find-a-Station. (3) first run (cleared storage) -> falls back to operator grid, no regression. (4) operator can still recenter on self via existing controls.
notes: BUILT (PR pending). Operator clarified scope 2026-06-15: central ask = auto-restore last viewport (done). First-run = operator-centered at Z6 for BOTH maps (not world view); recenter 'center on me' control added (operator endorsed). Storage hardened vs throwing localStorage getter (Codex P2). DoD flows: (1) pan/zoom map -> restart -> opens at that view, no flyTo; (2) same for Find-a-Station + no pan to operator; (3) FIRST run -> centered on operator at Z6 (world view only if no grid); (4) recenter control snaps back to operator. usePersistedViewport hook (localStorage per-surface, validated, debounced) + MapLibreMap onViewportChange + RecenterControl. 862 tests green, tsc clean.
close_reason: Closed

Verified type-clean live in the converge build; grim pending operator reopen.

title: send_message tags Outbox from DEFAULT identity, not ACTIVE (multi-FULL mail mis-routes)
description: Pre-existing (latent until multi-FULL identities are real). winlink_backend.rs send_message (~1595) stores to Outbox via bare mailbox.store, which tags the sidecar from the mailbox DEFAULT namespace (native_mailbox.rs:122) rather than the ACTIVE identity (session_id.mycall()). With >1 FULL, a message composed under active FULL B gets tagged default-A, so B's CMS drain (winlink_backend.rs:316 / 2674 passes Some(active mycall)) skips it and A may pick it up. Fix: store via mailbox.for_identity(active).store(...). Surfaced by Codex R2 during the tuxlink-nx3g self-heal review; scoped OUT of that P0 (sole-FULL unaffected; multi-callsign is 'coming soon'). Needs an end-to-end test: second FULL authenticates -> queues -> only its own CMS drain ships it.

title: Local auto-triage for new GitHub user issues (Pi headless Claude Code)
description: A local, always-on (Pi) auto-triage workflow for incoming alpha-user GitHub issues. A user-level timer runs headless 'claude -p' with a versioned triage prompt that: finds issues opened since the last run; reads them (incl. screenshots); root-causes read-only (investigate discipline); files/labels a bd issue; drafts a fix on a worktree branch + opens a DRAFT PR; writes a triage summary + a ready-to-send user reply WITHOUT posting it. Boundary (operator-chosen): triage + draft only — NEVER auto-comments on the user's issue and NEVER merges (a guard hook hard-blocks gh issue comment / gh pr merge in triage mode). Runs locally on the Pi so it inherits full context (auto-memory, bd Dolt, hooks) — the high-fidelity 'version of Claude', not a context-free cloud runner. Motivated by #691 (Orv/W6BI: Post to Outbox → 'no active identity' error). Operator decision 2026-06-15.

title: [BUG] Find-a-Station map not displaying operator-position pin (operator report 2026-06-14)
description: Operator: 'the find a station map doesn't display operator position anymore, last I checked.' Investigation (sandbar-crag-cardinal): StationFinderMap.tsx renders the operator pin (OperatorPin → OPERATOR_LAYERS circle layer, white fill #eaf3fb + blue stroke) via the SAME useMapOverlay mechanism as the station pins (which DO render). The pin's source gets its feature iff me=gridToLatLon(operatorGrid) is non-null; the unit test 'places the operator pin only when a grid is set' passes. operatorGrid is sourced in StationFinderPanel via position_current_fix (GPS) → config_read().grid fallback (the q1tm regression fix is present). So by inspection the code is correct and the pin renders WHEN LOCATION IS SET. Could not reproduce from the agent shell (no WebKitGTK/app). LEADING HYPOTHESIS: location not resolved when checked (no GPS fix + no manual grid) → no pin (correct 'set your location' behaviour), OR a WebKitGTK-specific render issue jsdom tests can't catch. DIAGNOSTIC NEEDED from operator during a build: (1) does the path-forecast render or say 'set your location'? (2) is the status-bar grid populated? If location IS set and pin still absent → real render bug, needs grim repro. ENHANCEMENT regardless: the real pin is an unlabeled dot; the approved mock has a 'YOU · grid' label — adding a clear label/treatment would make it recognisable. File: src/catalog/StationFinderMap.tsx (OperatorPin/OPERATOR_LAYERS lines ~74-88,164-181).
notes: ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED → PR #715 (verified live). Cause: useMapSource/useMapLayer/useMapOverlay gated adds on isStyleLoaded(), which stays false forever with a large offline region-pack basemap → ALL overlays (station pins + operator pin) silently never added (diagnosed via injected on-screen diag on the running app: getSource/getLayer false, 0 errors, 141 basemap layers). Fix: attempt-and-retry on load+styledata instead of gating. Verified live (close/reopen Find-a-Station surfaced pins, operator-confirmed). 257 src/map+src/catalog tests green. This subsumes the broader "no pins" report. Converge worktree has the fix applied locally so the running app works until reconverge from merged main.

Recolour --m-vara/ardop/packet off the green/orange ramp. Bands stay text tabs. Map pins signal-only; MAP legend = Signal key ONLY (modes self-legend via chips). Selected pin = white rim + glow, NOT an outline-offset ring (the offset ring read as two stacked circles — operator caught). Shapes via clip-path on the existing __sw swatch span. title: No centralized Tauri-command-error logging: 233 commands, ~18 files emit tracing::error
description: Observability follow-up (spruce-finch-gorge audit, deferred behind tuxlink-4b96/ebyt). Most of the 233 Tauri commands return Err(String) to the frontend without also tracing::error! — so a backend command failure is inconsistently logged to tuxlink.<hour>.jsonl. With #710 an uncaught reject surfaces via unhandledrejection, and the operator usually sees handled errors as UI states, so this is medium (not blocking the manual cut). Ideal fix: a centralized command-result logging seam (log Err at the IPC boundary) so every command failure is in the structured log. Broad change — scope carefully.

title: APRS positions map: RF-honesty follow-up (ambiguity, dedupe, staleness)
description: Forward-fix of the 3 Codex findings on PR #696 (tuxlink-6vgt), now MERGED to main. (1) P1 RF-honesty: ambiguous (space-padded) APRS positions are plotted as exact pins; thread an ambiguity level from the now-merged parser (#692) through InboundPos->DTO->HeardPosition->map and render a geographic uncertainty circle instead of a false-exact dot. (2) P2 dedupe: position dedupe key is coords+symbol, so a same-location comment/status change is suppressed; fold a comment hash into the key. (3) P2 staleness: heard positions never expire; prune/age by 'at' and show last-heard age in the popup. Findings captured in tuxlink-6vgt notes; transcript dev/adversarial/2026-06-14-aprs-6vgt-codex.md.
notes: PR #705 (base main). Code-complete: P1 ambiguity uncertainty-region + 2x P2 (dedupe comment+ambiguity, staleness prune/age) + 6 Codex findings all fixed. 16 FE tests + tsc green; Rust via CI. Awaiting CI + operator merge/smoke.

title: APRS beacon TX: transmit own position on the open channel (GPS/Maidenhead, precision-reduced default)
description: Transmit OUR OWN position as an APRS position report on the open tac-chat channel. Separate from tuxlink-6vgt (which is RX-only: hearing + mapping others). Ties to existing GPS + Maidenhead precision-reduction (feedback_gps_precision_reduction: broadcast position precision-reduced by default = 4-char Maidenhead; operator opts into finer precision) and BeaconSettings (near tuxlink-oc93 native UV-Pro settings). Needs: position-report ENCODE (uncompressed/compressed) building on src-tauri/src/winlink/aprs/framebuild + message encode; a beacon control surface (auto on/off + interval + precision + manual 'beacon now'); RADIO-1 (agent writes/tests, operator keys TX; working abort, no runaway-TX; NO tuxlink-added airtime caps beyond standard APRS beacon interval per feedback_no_tuxlink_added_safeguards). Model: project_aprs_tac_chat_open_channel.
notes: Branch bd-tuxlink-hj4k/beacon-tx (pushed, no PR yet — feature incomplete). DONE+TDD: encode_position (uncompressed encoder) + ambiguity masking; precision_to_ambiguity (4char->4, 6char->2); beacon_info payload builder (grid->broadcast_grid->grid_to_lat_lon->encode w/ ambiguity). OPERATOR DECISION 2026-06-14: beacon is AMBIGUITY-HONEST (advertise only the precision shared; TX mirror of #705 RX honesty). REMAINING: beacon config (enable+interval), scheduler (auto interval + manual 'beacon now', clean stop on disable/disconnect, no runaway TX — use TxQueue::enqueue_once, worst-case 1 frame), Tauri command (aprs_beacon_now + config set), beacon UI controls, Codex round, wire-walk. Driver hook: native_driver run loop / engine tick; arbiter position via config grid + privacy.position_precision.

title: APRS position-report parser: uncompressed + compressed (RX)
description:
Pure parser for APRS POSITION reports on the promiscuous RX path — the data dependency for the Tac Chat positions map (tuxlink-6vgt). Today parse_info (src-tauri/src/winlink/aprs/message.rs) handles only message/ack/rej (DTI ':'); position DTIs (!,=,@,/) fall through to None.

SCOPE (atomic): parse uncompressed (DDMM.mmN / DDDMM.mmW, with position ambiguity) + compressed (base-91) position reports → lat/lon (f64) + symbol table/code + comment. Handle the 4 DTIs: '!' '=' (no timestamp), '@' '/' (7-char timestamp skipped). Pin to aprslib/direwolf (e.g. '!4903.50N/07201.75W-' => 49.0583,-72.0292; compressed '/5L!!<*e7>' => 49.5,-72.75). Pure fn, fully unit-testable (no radio/UI/map). Add AprsPayload::Position variant + dispatch in parse_info; engine handles it as a no-op for now (emit + map = tuxlink-6vgt).

OUT OF SCOPE (fast-follow): Mic-E (lat in dest callsign + offset-encoded info field) — the complexity cliff, common on mobile trackers. Separate issue.

RF-honesty: parse only what's in the frame; no estimated positions.
notes:
--- 2026-06-14 (delta-opossum-tamarack): Mic-E ADDED to the same PR #692 ---
parse_mice(dest, info) ported from aprslib mice.py (commit db49523f). Lat+msgbits+ambiguity from AX.25 dest addr; lon/offset/EW from dest+info; reversed symbol order; DTIs `'` 0x1c 0x1d. 13 position tests pass + clippy clean (TDD'd in throwaway crate). Scope unchanged: pure parser, wiring to engine/map is tuxlink-6vgt (which passes the dest address to parse_mice). l2pu now covers ALL position formats (uncompressed + compressed + Mic-E).

title: Make APRS Tac Chat (+ map) severable as a popped-out second window
description:
Let APRS Tac Chat (and its positions map) detach into a fully popped-out SECOND WINDOW, tethered to Tuxlink's managed backend (same live listener/transport/delivery state) but visually separate — a second VIEW onto the one live session, not a second client.

OPERATOR RATIONALE: the default target is a single-screen laptop, where there isn't room for both the mailbox reading pane and tac chat at once. On one screen: split-the-diff / switch between tac chat and the reading pane (the shared-dock surface per the IA spec). On a SECOND screen: pop tac chat + map out to keep both experiences live simultaneously, tethered through the managed backend but on separate displays.

DELIBERATE INLINE-UI EXCEPTION: tuxlink's standing rule is inline-only, NO pop-up windows (the Compose window is the lone sanctioned exception; ref feedback_inline_ui_no_window_clutter). This pop-out is the operator's explicit, authorized SECOND exception to that rule — requested on purpose. Do NOT 'fix' it back to inline-only as a violation. The popped window must share the managed-backend session/state with the main app (one listener, one transport, one delivery-state source), so detaching never forks state or opens a second radio client.

Depends on the tac chat dock surface + open-channel model existing (tuxlink-ckmb; project_aprs_tac_chat_open_channel).

title: Expandable APRS station-position map in Tac Chat (reuse Find-a-Station map)
description:
Add an expandable/collapsible map to the APRS Tac Chat surface that plots station positions parsed from incoming APRS traffic, reusing the map already built for Find a Station: src/catalog/StationFinderMap.tsx on the shared MapLibre stack (src/map/MapLibreMap.tsx + basemapStyle/MapContext/mapHooks; vector basemap from tuxlink-ndi4). The map is a secondary, expandable region of the tac-chat surface, not always-on (single-screen real-estate is tight — see the pop-out issue).

DEPENDENCY (net-new, the real work): APRS POSITION PARSING on the promiscuous RX listener. Today the APRS RX path decodes messages only — grep finds no lat/lon / Mic-E / compressed-position parsing in src-tauri/src/winlink/aprs. This is the Phase 3 'position/beacon RX' work the design doc parked (docs/design/2026-06-12-aprs-tactical-chat-design.md, Out-of-scope: 'Position/beaconing (RX+TX) — natural Phase 3, ties to existing GPS + Maidenhead precision-reduction work'). Parse APRS position reports (uncompressed + compressed + Mic-E) and symbol from heard frames; plot the reused map.

RF-HONESTY: plot only positions actually decoded from heard frames — no fabricated/estimated locations; precision shown as received.

Relates to the APRS open-channel tac chat (tuxlink-bcsy; model: project_aprs_tac_chat_open_channel).
notes:
--- 2026-06-14 (fjord-vale-kite): Codex adrev — 3 deferred findings (PR #696) ---
P1 ambiguity: ambiguous APRS positions plotted as exact pins (RF-honesty); fix needs #692 parser to expose ambiguity (coordinated #692+#696). P2 dedupe: key=coords+symbol drops same-location comment/status changes; add comment/info-hash to key. P2 staleness: heard positions never expire; prune/age by 'at' + show last-heard. Address as a follow-up commit on bd-tuxlink-6vgt/positions-map BEFORE merge. Transcript local at dev/adversarial/2026-06-14-aprs-6vgt-codex.md.

title: Docs: cover Winlink password recovery-address precondition + tactical-address exclusion (the #1 Winlink pain)
description:
Our docs disclaim password-recovery ownership and point at winlink.org (02:137-140, 02:155) but never state the canonical fact that winlink.org self-recovery ONLY works if a password recovery email address was set on the account IN ADVANCE — otherwise the operator is stuck contacting Winlink admins. The Hamexandria winlink-annex corpus (~/Code/library-of-hamexandria/winlink-annex) shows password issues are 618/4105 threads = 15.1% of all Winlink Programs Group traffic — the single largest pain theme — driven almost entirely by no-recovery-address-set. Verified canonical method via url-to-markdown of winlink.org/content/password_reset + /password_problems ('Be sure to have set a password recovery address in your account profile.').

Two gaps to close:
1. Recovery-address precondition — preventive nudge in 02 'Your Winlink account' section (keyring hook: tuxlink keeps your password in the keyring so you rarely need this, but set a winlink.org recovery address anyway because it is the only self-service path if you ever lose it). Optional 29 troubleshooting entry.
2. Tactical-address exclusion — winlink.org self-service tool does NOT work for tactical addresses (forum-only). Newly relevant because tuxlink now ships tactical identities (27/32). Add the caveat where tactical identities are described.

FOLLOWS tuxlink-ivc1 (#680) — both touch 02/29; keep new content in non-overlapping hunks (02:137-140 paragraph + a new 29 entry; ivc1 touched 02:144-156 + the 29 attachment bullet). Net-new guidance, deliberately kept OUT of the #680 staleness PR per operator call.
notes: Shipped to PR #682 (tanager-ridge-larch): 02 'Your Winlink account' gets the recovery-address precondition (preventive + reactive) + tactical-address forum-only caveat. lint:docs green. Separate from #680 per operator; edits above #680's hunks so merge order is free. Awaiting merge.

title: Compose attach: per-image resize picker + live size + CMS-limit (120KB) awareness
description:
Follow-on to tuxlink-mg4s (image attach + resize, merged PR #674). Operator wants a resize CHOICE with live resized-file-size feedback for flexibility, and flagged that 1024px images may exceed the CMS limit.

GROUNDING: the CMS message-size limit is ~120 KB total (body + attachments; JPEG/HEIC don't compress further under B2F LZHUF) — documented in ui_commands.rs:239 (MAX_RFC5322_BYTES comment). mg4s's presets (640/1024/1600) were generic photo sizes, NOT CMS-calibrated; a 1024px JPEG is ~130-250 KB and would likely be rejected. The live size readout is essential because file size varies by image content — a fixed preset can't guarantee the fit.

SCOPE:
- Recalibrate ResizePreset px (transcode.rs): Small=480 / Medium=640 / Large=800 / Original. Default Medium.
- useAttachments: retain source path + per-item opts; add re-transcode (setOptions(index, {preset,format})) that re-invokes prepare_attachment and replaces the item (updates newLen live).
- Compose attachment list: per-image size dropdown + JPEG/WebP toggle; live size display ("480px JPEG · 42 KB").
- attachmentFormat: CMS_LIMIT_BYTES (~120KB) + an exceedsCms helper; total-message-size line warns when total (attachments + rough body) approaches/exceeds the limit.
- backend prepare_attachment already parameterized on preset/format — no command change.

Tests: useAttachments re-transcode; Compose picker renders + re-transcodes; CMS warning helper. Method: no cold cargo (tsc + vitest local; draft PR -> CI both arches). Wire-walk before ready.

POSSIBLE FUTURE: a "fit under 120KB" auto-size (iterative re-encode to a byte target) — noted, not in this scope.
notes:
CMS 120KB limit CONFIRMED via Hamexandria winlink-annex corpus (real Winlink-group threads, 2026-06-04 scrape): multiple operators cite '120kb message size limit' (hard CMS limit; only circumventable via Post Office mode for LOCAL exchanges). Winlink Catalog item limit is tighter (<100KB). An operator describes the exact manual workflow this feature automates: 'creating a low-res version ... so it will fly under the attachment limit.' Validates: 1024px is too big; resize-to-fit + live size readout is operator-needed. Use 120KB as CMS_LIMIT_BYTES.

title: APRS settings sync to/from radio (GET/SET_APRS_PATH, BeaconSettings) — native UV-Pro
description: Like the BTECH/Benshi native app, offer bidirectional APRS-settings sync with the radio: pull the radio's APRS config (path, SSID, beacon settings) into tuxlink, or push tuxlink's settings to the radio. Maps to GAIA commands GET_APRS_PATH(72)/SET_APRS_PATH(71) + GET/SET BeaconSettings + GET/SET_POSITION (benlink command.py: BeaconSettings{packet_format,aprs_ssid,...}, TncDataFragment). Native-only (UV-Pro GAIA); generic KISS TNCs have no such control channel. The native app's sync UX is reportedly 'a bit cursed' (operator) — study com.benshikj.ht decompile + benlink before designing the tuxlink flow; do NOT copy the cursed UX. Adjacent to native APRS messaging (tuxlink-mjlh / 7my9 / ve3j). Operator-raised during tuxlink-iehg APRS-chat redesign 2026-06-13. NOTE: per-message digipeater path is already a tuxlink-side frame field (identity.path → build_ui_frame, no radio conflict) — this issue is about syncing the RADIO's own APRS/beacon config, a separate concern.

title: README pass: APRS tactical chat, UV-Pro native, HF+U/V strategic/tactical fusion positioning
description: Significant new features shipped (APRS tactical chat, native UV-Pro support) need README coverage, plus positioning verbiage for the unified strategic(HF)/tactical(U/V) comms fusion. Goal: keep README current for discoverability/indexing ('people find it and know what it is'). No screenshots yet. Features are in-product but rough (alpha). Voice: tuxlink formal/declarative/present-indicative, no temporal hedging, no defensive self-assertion, alpha = plain status + looking-for-testers. title: Forms/attach: image resizer + broad camera-format ingest (airtime; HEIC iPhone ingest)
description:
Designed 2026-06-13 (lupine-ridge-marten) with operator. WLE-parity item (WLE has a built-in attach-time image resizer). Airtime-aligned: resizing a photo is the dominant lever for sending images over RF. NOT YET BUILT — locked design captured here for a future build session.

CONTEXT / WHY:
- At marginal HF (~110 B/s) a 2MB phone photo is ~5 HOURS of airtime; resized ~50KB is ~8 min. Resize is a ~40x win.
- B2F LZHUF compression (already shipped, winlink/lzhuf.rs) does NOTHING for already-compressed JPEG/HEIC — only pixel-dimension/quality reduction shrinks a photo. So "attachment compression" is ALREADY at parity (it's the protocol); the resizer is the actual gap.
- This is WLE PARITY (a WLE feature), compose-side (NOT RADIO-1 gated), sender-side (zero interop risk) — not a tuxlink-added safeguard.

LOCKED DESIGN (two opposite optimization targets — ingest=breadth, wire=narrowness):
1. INGEST (decode) — accept whatever device shows up in a contingency: JPEG, PNG, and crucially HEIC (iPhone default; Linux desktop often can't open it natively → operator can't attach a photo they can't decode). HEIC decode needs libheif+libde265 (C deps; Rust via libheif-rs). The `image` crate alone won't do HEIC. Platform asymmetry: macOS/Windows decode HEIC at OS level, so Linux is the only mandatory bundling target. Pure-Rust HEVC decode is not production-ready. Tradeoff to confirm at build time: HEVC patent surface — decode-only (libde265) is widely distributed/lower-risk and the commonly-accepted case.
2. RESIZE — the ~40x win; format-agnostic pixel-dimension/quality reduction. Offer presets before send (WLE does this).
3. WIRE (encode) — narrow + safe: JPEG default (decodes everywhere incl. Winlink Express — safe when recipient is unknown). WebP opt-in for tuxlink->tuxlink (~30% smaller than JPEG, fast encode on Pi, natively displays in WebKitGTK 2.52, royalty-free). AVIF (~50% smaller, royalty-free) noted as a possible later 'max compression' opt-in — BLOCKED on (a) slow rav1e encode time on the Pi and (b) verifying AVIF decode in our WebKitGTK + mac/Win bundles. HEIC is INGEST-ONLY, never a wire format (HEVC patents + WebKitGTK can't decode it for display).

DEPENDENCY NOTE: tuxlink has NO image crate yet (Cargo.toml) — building this adds the first image-processing dep. WebKitGTK on the Pi is 2.52.3.

OPEN SUB-DECISION for build session: confirm bundling libheif on Linux now vs OS-decode-where-available vs defer HEIC ingest. Operator leaning: bundle (contingency 'people show up with whatever devices' is the driving scenario).

Refs epic tuxlink-zkuk (forms push). Related airtime reasoning lives in tuxlink-z0gx --notes.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-13 (lupine-ridge-marten) — PR #674 (DRAFT), CI-GREEN both arches, wire-walk PASSED. Awaiting operator smoke + ready/merge.

Single ship (no phasing) per operator. Backend `media` module: transcode_image_bytes (decode broad incl. HEIC via libheif → resize Lanczos3 preset → JPEG default/WebP opt-in encode) + prepare_attachment command. Frontend: attachmentFormat helpers, useAttachments hook, Compose picker+drop+list wired into the existing message_send (attach.toDto()); backend send path (compose_message_with_files→set_attachments) already existed. dialog:allow-open granted to compose window. CI installs + .deb/.rpm depend on libheif/libde265/libwebp.

KEY CI FIX: libheif-rs pinned EXACTLY =1.0.2 (libheif-sys ^2.1 → 2.2.1+1.17.6) because 2.x needs libheif>=1.21, NOT in Ubuntu 24.04 (ships 1.17.6). Decode API identical in 1.0.2 (verified). So HEIC ingest uses the distro's own libheif — no bundling/source-build needed.

WIRE-WALK (operator flows, both ✅ wired, full file:line chains):
1. HEIC in ~/pictures → upload: Attach button→handlePickFiles→prepare_attachment→is_heic→decode_heic→resize→JPEG→list→message_send→Outbox B2F.
2. 4000x3000 3.78MB JPG → upload: same chain, image-crate decode→resize to 1024px→JPEG re-encode (shows "resized 3.78MB→NKB").
Honest note: preset/format fixed at medium/jpeg (no UI size selector yet) — neither flow requires choosing, so gate passes; a picker is a possible enhancement.

DEFERRED (documented, not phasing-for-convenience): AVIF encode (needs Pi-encode-time + WebKitGTK-decode viability spike). HEIC is ingest-only, never a wire format.

OPERATOR TODO: smoke in a real build (attach a real HEIC + the 3.78MB JPG, send) — needs libheif/libwebp on the dev machine; then mark #674 ready + merge.

title: SBC codec (pure-Rust encode+decode) for UV-Pro audio transport
description:
Implements the SbcCodec trait (uvpro/audio/codec.rs) the audio transport (tuxlink-bcsy) depends on. SBC = Bluetooth sub-band codec; the audio wire payload is SBC, not raw PCM. Params (from benlink audiotransmit.py): 32kHz mono s16le <-> SBC, bitpool=16, subbands=8, blocks=16, msbc=false, global_quality=bitpool*118=1888.

RISK/DECISION (operator): no mature pure-Rust SBC ENCODER on crates.io (mini_sbc=decode-only ~9.9k dl; libsbc=C-FFI to BlueZ libsbc; 'sbc' crate is an unrelated broadcast channel). Two paths: (a) PORT a pure-Rust encoder from the public A2DP/SBC spec + FFmpeg sbcenc.c reference (FFmpeg LGPL -> compatible with tuxlink GPL-3; matches repo's no-C-dep ethos), decoder via mini_sbc or own; (b) libsbc C-FFI (battle-tested, but a system-lib build dep on CI for both arches). Test: decoder golden-vector against benlink FFmpeg output; encoder via encode->decode round-trip + cross-decode by a reference decoder (compliant encoders differ byte-wise, so NOT a golden-byte test for encode). Refs: dev/scratch/benshi-re/benlink (audio.py, examples/audiotransmit.py).
notes: RESOLVED 2026-06-13 (delta-opossum-tamarack, PR #684 tuxlink-st5n): encoder amplitude refinement is NOT needed. Full-image SSTV round-trip through the actual UvproSbcCodec (vendored into dev/tools/sstv-proto tests) reconstructs pure-color quadrants at MAE 0.0, gray ~2.4, for both Robot36 and PD120. SSTV is FM — SBC amplitude error doesn't move recovered frequencies. The MAE-156 concern stands only for voice/non-FM payloads.
close_reason: Closed

title: SSTV inline images in UV-Pro chat (full interop with the native app)
description:
Add inline image send/receive to the UV-Pro native chat over SSTV — RCS-equivalent tactical image chat. SHIP REGARDLESS: buildable now from open-source prior art, and preserves FULL interop with the stock Benshi/BTECH app (a field op on a bare UV-Pro running the vendor app exchanges images with a tuxlink EOC station — no shared software needed). Strong README/demo feature.

GROUNDED MECHANISM (decompile + HTCommander, see design doc): NOT a BT audio profile. App does software SSTV encode (om.sstvencoder / HTCommander src/SSTV/Encoder.cs) -> 32kHz mono 16-bit PCM -> streams over the existing GAIA RFCOMM link via the c1-enum audio opcodes TX_AUDIO/TX_AUDIO_STOP (v4/g2.java:420) + RX_AUDIO/RX_AUDIO_STOP (v4/f0.java:20), chunked by com.benshikj.ii.AudioSendThread/IILink (IILink.cancel = clean abort). Radio modulates onto RF. RX = mirror -> STFT decode (HTCommander ShortTimeFourierTransform.cs).

DECOMPOSITION: (1) net-new GAIA AUDIO TRANSPORT backend (TX_AUDIO/RX_AUDIO PCM streaming over UvproSession) — the real work, ~APRS-backend scale, and a reusable asset (voice later); (2) SSTV codec port from HTCommander C# (encode: Robot36 + a PD mode; decode: STFT) — the easy DSP part; (3) inline image UI in AprsChatPanel (composer attach + inbound auto-decode thumbnail).

NO 60s TX cap — operator owns ~5 radios, confirms NO such TOT; HTCommander's 60s doc claim is unreliable, do NOT propagate. RADIO-1: single-shot, finite (operator-selected mode length), working abort (TX_AUDIO_STOP); no tuxlink-added cap (no-added-safeguards rule).

GROUNDING TO DO before/at build: extract exact TX_AUDIO/RX_AUDIO opcode bytes + PCM framing/pacing from the decompile (c1 enum + AudioSendThread) and confirm via an Android Bluetooth HCI snoop log of a real image-send (host-side, plaintext — NOT RF BT sniffing; Classic BT freq-hops + is encrypted). Refs: dev/scratch/benshi-re/ (decompile + HTCommander). Design doc: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-xygm-recover-handoffs-design-20260613-014520.md
notes:
--- UPDATE 2026-06-13 (delta-opossum-tamarack): COMPONENT 2 (SSTV CODEC) DONE ---
SSTV codec (tuxlink-st5n) CLOSED, PR #684. Pure-Rust Robot36+PD encode + free-running FM decode at dev/tools/sstv-proto; full image round-trip passes, AND the decisive gate (round-trip through the real UvproSbcCodec) passes at MAE ~0 — SBC needs NO refinement for SSTV (see tuxlink-vgvn note). Codec is std-only and ready to promote into src-tauri/.../uvpro/audio/ when the UI (tuxlink-yfyn) consumes it.
NEXT for the feature: (1) Component 1 transport wiring — bcsy Tasks 5-6 (UvproSession::open_audio/abort_audio + c1 keying) in the bd-tuxlink-bcsy/sstv-transport-wiring worktree, gated on transport Codex adrev (quota-deferred); (2) Component 3 inline UI tuxlink-yfyn (composer attach + inbound thumbnail in AprsChatPanel, inject UvproSbcCodec at open_audio) — the whole-feature WIRE-WALK gate fires there.

title: Two-channel releases: nightly prereleases + operator-promoted stable milestones
description: Root cause (investigate): the daily-cron fix (tuxlink-w7yl) added a daily merge path but didn't PREVENT direct gh pr merge of the chore:release PR, so agents kept cutting releases (#653/#657 merged outside release-merge). Deeper issue: even daily batching surfaces ~60 releases/alpha, confusing end users. Operator chose two-channel model. Implement: (1) draft-pull-request:true in .github/release-please-config.json so the release PR can't be casually merged; (2) release-merge.yml does gh pr ready then merge (daily cron + dispatch) — nightly cadence; (3) release.yml softprops/action-gh-release step gets prerelease:true so every auto-release is a GitHub pre-release (hidden from Latest); (4) new promote-release.yml (workflow_dispatch, tag input) marks a chosen release --prerelease=false --latest = the stable milestone end users see; (5) CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md: agents never merge the release PR nor promote. Supersedes the soft doc-rule approach of tuxlink-w7yl.
notes: PR #667 open. Two-channel: draft release PR (can't ad-hoc merge) + release-merge.yml ready+merge nightly (prerelease) + promote-release.yml operator-only stable. release.yml marks prerelease (softprops + explicit gh release edit guarantee). CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md updated. Awaiting CI/merge. NOT retroactive — historical v0.41-0.58 stay normal; optional bulk-prerelease cleanup pending operator say-so.
close_reason: Closed

title: Self-hosted vector OSM basemap (dark/light, meshmap.net look) — no cross-service tile dependency
description: DESIGN-FIRST (brainstorm before code). Ship tuxlink's own self-hosted dark/light OSM vector basemap, emulating meshmap.net's look, with NO cross-service dependency. NOT satellite imagery. Motivated by the 2026-06-13 LAN-tiles saga (k61j): consuming an external raster tile server is fragile + coverage-gapped. CONFIRMED FACTS this session (do not re-litigate): (1) the Pi/Geographica :8093 serves VECTOR OSM tiles today (.pbf, application/x-protobuf, gzip) for basemap southwest5 + publiclands overlay — proven by probe; raster on the Pi is ONLY satellite imagery. (2) Operator rejects Geographica's darkmatter style (clones Google-Maps-dark); wants meshmap.net's look. (3) meshmap.net = MapLibre + open dark vector style (OpenFreeMap dark / Protomaps dark / dark-matter class) over OSM vector tiles; emulating is legally fine (open tooling + OSM/ODbL, keep '(c) OpenStreetMap contributors' attribution). (4) tuxlink map is Leaflet+RASTER today; the meshmap look needs a VECTOR renderer: MapLibre GL (full swap) OR protomaps-leaflet (stay in Leaflet). (5) Reuse Geographica's OSM->MBTiles build pipeline (scripts/tileserver_config.py, mbtiles tooling) + dark/light styles; do NOT reuse TileServer-GL (a Node service = the dependency to avoid). Serve from a bundled PMTiles/MBTiles via tuxlink's own tiny reader over the now-working tile:// scheme. (6) Disk: vector region ~hundreds of MB, planet ~120GB; styles free; no tile-server process. OPEN DECISIONS: renderer (MapLibre vs protomaps-leaflet); format/serving (PMTiles single-file vs MBTiles+reader); coverage (bundle region vs world-low-zoom+regional vs pull-on-demand+cache); exact dark style to match meshmap; light style; relationship to the just-shipped raster LAN-tiles feature (replace vs coexist). Full context: dev/handoffs/2026-06-13-dahlia-spruce-osprey-*.md
notes:
D1 (region-pack distribution) RESOLVED by operator 2026-06-13. MODEL: extract-on-demand from the PUBLIC Protomaps planet via go-pmtiles HTTP-Range (NOT tuxlink-hosted packs) — one-time online download per area, then permanent/offline. tuxlink hosts only a tiny (~KB) manifest naming the current planet build URL (builds rotate ~monthly; 20240801 already 404s). TIERS (all measured z0-14 from build.protomaps.com/20260608): bundled world z0-6 = 43MB ships IN .deb (offline-first); metro 17MB/8s; STATE 203MB/20s; MULTI-STATE ~1GB/40s = DEFAULT 'Wide' pack; CONTINENT full z0-14 = 15-35GB (operator: fine, no zoom cap — most users download only their own continent, one-time). DISCOVERY: proactive offer at location-set ('Download offline detail for your area? DM43/Phoenix ~1GB') anchored on operator grid + a discoverable Tools->Offline-maps MANAGER (list/add/delete; presets Local/Regional/Wide + named CONTINENT picks). Reactive zoom-into-undownloaded = gentle hint only, not the trigger. MECHANISM (reuses phase-1/2 plumbing): go-pmtiles SIDECAR (tauri-plugin-shell, already a dep) Range-extracts bbox -> Rust basemap::validate (PMTiles-v3 + 13-id schema + size budget, ALREADY BUILT) -> atomic install (NamedTempFile/fsync/rename + manifest, R5 pattern) -> PmtilesRegistry register_path -> served via the SAME tile://pmtiles/<id> 206 seam -> R7 dual-source compositing (overview maxzoom6 + region minzoom6). Pack manager UI + sidecar + manifest are the only NEW phase-4 pieces. SUB-DECISIONS still open for the D1 doc: exact default box degrees; fixed-box-vs-true-admin-region; manifest hosting location. NEXT: merge #681 (phase 3) on green; write D1 design doc + update plan; then phase 4 build.

title: LAN tiles: offline-cache path non-functional (lan-cached dead, breaker-before-cache) + test_tile_source pollutes cache + 404-is-success probe
description: Deferred from the tuxlink-k61j bug hunt (2026-06-12). Separable subsystem work, not needed for the online LAN-tile path to work. (1) B2/multipass: serve_tile checks the circuit breaker BEFORE the cache-first lookup, so a degraded source returns 503 without serving on-disk cached tiles; status_core never produces StatusKind::LanCached (dead code, cached_at always None); so a source that goes offline collapses to bundled raster instead of serving cache — breaks the 'pre-cache at home, go off-grid' EmComm story. Fix: cache-first before breaker; actually produce LanCached. (2) B4: test_tile_source dry-run writes the probed 0/0/0 tile to disk via fetch_tile_single_flight (contradicts the 'no persist' contract). (3) DESIGN DECISION (probe philosophy): commands.rs validate() maps a single-coord 404 -> LanLive, so a wrong-path/empty server validates as 'source active'. Options: (a) require a positive image fetch (may reject valid sparse servers lacking 0/0/0), (b) serve-time all-404 downgrade partial->incompatible, (c) probe a sweep. Needs operator input. Hunt reports: dev/bug-hunts/2026-06-12-lan-tiles-{exploratory,holistic,multipass}.md.

title: APRS messaging over native Benshi GAIA (HT_SEND_DATA TX + RX_DATA RX) — UV-Pro unified path
description:
Backend slice surfaced by the 2026-06-13 vendor-app decompile (dev/scratch/benshi-re/DECOMPILE-FINDINGS.md). GROUND TRUTH (com.benshikj.ht.btech.ham command enum v4/v.java; cross-verified against benlink BasicCommand 0-76, exact match): the native Benshi GAIA protocol is ONE command connection that multiplexes control + messaging + position. Messaging opcodes: HT_SEND_DATA(31) TX, RX_DATA(57) + EVENT_NOTIFICATION/DATA_RXD(2) RX, SET_MSG/GET_MSG; APRS path SET/GET_APRS_PATH(71/72). The official app does control+chat over one link and NEVER mode-switches; 'KISS' in the vendor app is a radio settings toggle (kiss_en) that exposes the radio as a dumb external TNC — NOT the app's comms path.

SCOPE: extend the nx95 native backend (currently control-ONLY) to carry APRS messaging over native GAIA: encode/send AX.25/APRS UI frames via HT_SEND_DATA, receive inbound via the RX_DATA / DATA_RXD notification path, surface to the existing aprs chat core. Result: on a UV-Pro, a single native connection gives control + chat + position together — NO KISS, NO mode-switch, control strip always-live. KISS floor (Layer 1) stays for generic Classic-SPP TNCs (Mobilinkd-class). This resolves spec open-question #1 (docs/design/2026-06-12-aprs-tactical-chat-design.md): 'if native, Layer 2 implements the Benshi message path — changes the Phase 2 build'. RADIO-1/ADR-0018: write+test with mocks/loopback (golden vectors like nx95); on-air operator-gated; bar = working abort + no command-storm. Depends on nx95 (control backend + GAIA framing).
notes:
Task 8 (capability-gated transport select) SHIPPED + merged to main via PR #642 (merge commit 7056dd24, 2026-06-13). Operator shape-decision: declared KissLinkConfig::UvproNative{mac} variant (operator declares native vs KISS; not runtime-inferred). aprs_listen_start now selects transport from the declared link (aprs_transport_from_link); AprsState::start_native reuses the connected UvproSession. All six Task-8 allow(dead_code) markers removed — native driver (Task 7) now has a live caller. CI green amd64+arm64 (clippy -D + tests).

Backend slice COMPLETE. Closing.

Task 9 (Codex cross-provider adrev on the GAIA fragment codec, tncdata.rs/message.rs) was OPERATOR-DEPRIORITIZED 2026-06-13 ('self-adrev as a crutch; merge on CI green') — the fragment codec (Tasks 1-4) ships without the Codex round per that call. If desired later, the staged prompt scope: fragment boundary math (53-byte chunking, fragment_id wraparound >63), reassembler RF-loss handling, with_channel_id split, panic paths. Codex quota resets ~13:49 daily.

Frontend (operator-facing native declaration UI + always-live control strip) is tuxlink-ve3j, now UNBLOCKED. Native APRS is operator-reachable once ve3j lands the link-picker option + control-strip connect.

title: APRS Phase 2 frontend — always-live UV-Pro control + chat (unified native model)
description:
Frontend for APRS Phase 2 on the UNIFIED NATIVE model (architecture corrected 2026-06-13 by vendor-app decompile — see dev/scratch/benshi-re/DECOMPILE-FINDINGS.md). SUPERSEDED PREMISE: the original 'KISS xor native control, single-host mode-switch' framing was an artifact of two separate RFCOMM backends, NOT a radio limitation. Ground truth: the native Benshi GAIA protocol carries control + messaging + position over ONE connection (the official app never mode-switches). 

NEW DESIGN: on a UV-Pro, control + chat ride one native connection (via tuxlink-7my9's native-messaging backend) — control strip is ALWAYS-LIVE alongside the conversation, no handoff, no confirm, no self-inflicted LinkBusy. Capability-gated: control surface present only on the Benshi/UV-Pro profile; a generic Classic-SPP KISS TNC (Mobilinkd-class, Layer 1) shows plain chat, no control strip. Real LinkBusy now only means an EXTERNAL holder (phone). Fill the AprsChatPanel.controlStrip seam with the always-live device surface (channel/freq/mode + battery/RSSI), co-presented with the chat per HTCommander's AprsTabUserControl pattern. Now DEPENDS on tuxlink-7my9 (native messaging backend) in addition to nx95 (control). On-air validation blocked by tuxlink-9ky. Visual study redone on the unified model (dev/scratch/aprs-mode-switch-mocks.html → superseded; new unified mock).
notes: Frontend SHIPPED + merged to main via PR #662 (merge a08fff71, 2026-06-13). Built: src/uvpro/ (UvproControlStrip always-live device surface + useUvproControl hook + uvproTypes); UV-Pro segment in ModemLinkSection (gated allowUvproNative, VHF/packet only) -> linkKind UvproNative; AppShell capability gate mounts the strip into AprsChatPanel.controlStrip iff linkKind===UvproNative. Plus backend fix: connect_link/connect_link_with_abort now open a UvproNative link as a KISS RFCOMM pipe (was erroring) so the operator's packet/Winlink KISS-mode workflow on the same UV-Pro works — native-vs-KISS is an APRS-layer choice, not a link-open refusal. All 4 wire-trace flows (declare/switch, connect, send, change settings) now have complete UI->backend paths. Local: typecheck clean, 35/35 vitest, vite build clean. CI green amd64+arm64. On-air + WebKitGTK render still unverified (operator-only smoke; tuxlink-9ky).

title: Batch releases to daily cadence (cron-merge release PR + manual override)
description: release-please currently cuts 5-11 releases/day because the chore:release PR is merged per-session; each fires a full multi-arch Tauri artifact build and inflates the version (0.41->0.56 in 3 days). For a packaged desktop app that's too granular. Fix: add a scheduled workflow (release-merge.yml) that merges the open release-please PR once/day (batching the day's merges into one release/one minor bump/one changelog section), with workflow_dispatch for on-demand manual override, respecting the existing .github/RELEASE_FREEZE marker. Merge step MUST use RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN (PAT) not GITHUB_TOKEN so the resulting push triggers release-please.yml to cut the release (anti-recursion). Document in CLAUDE.md: agents never merge the chore:release PR; releases cut by the daily cron or manual dispatch. AGENTS.md parity check.
notes: PR #650: release-merge.yml (daily 14:00 UTC cron + workflow_dispatch override, respects RELEASE_FREEZE, merges via PAT) + CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md 'never merge the release PR' rule. title: tuxlink-2f2n Phase 2 — native UV-Pro Benshi control backend
description: Layer 2 of the APRS tactical chat epic (tuxlink-2f2n): the native Benshi/Vero radio-control capability profile for the BTECH UV-Pro. On-screen device control over the radio's native Bluetooth link — read status (channel, frequency, mode, battery, RSSI) and send control (set channel/frequency/mode, connect/disconnect). Implemented as a capability profile in tuxlink's transport-agnostic messaging core (mirrors the modem/KISS abstraction; one more profile that ALSO exposes control). Protocol ground truth = HT Commander open-source (sanctioned RE per winlink-RE-authoritative-sources; this is the OPPOSITE of the clean-sheet VARA rule). Deliverable: backend + a documented Tauri command/event API (uvpro_connect/disconnect/set_channel/set_frequency/set_mode + status event) that the parallel frontend session wires to. Single-BT-host arbitration (KISS OR native, not both) designed cleanly. RADIO-1/ADR-0018: write+test with mocks/loopback; on-air execution operator-gated; bar = working disconnect/abort + no command-storm. Do NOT touch the KISS aprs/ module on PR #642; coordinate via dep edge. Spec parent: docs/design/2026-06-12-aprs-tactical-chat-design.md (Layer 2 section).

Design brainstormed + operator-approved 2026-06-12 (mock: dev/scratch/contacts-shapes.html; design doc: docs/design/2026-06-12-contacts-outline-redesign.md). Key moves: (1) callsign-first identity (Winlink carries callsigns not names) — callsign headline, name as optional subtitle / '+ add name'; (2) ONE outline list in the message-list pane: global search › collapsible groups holding members inline (labels not folders — a contact in 2 groups shows under both) › Ungrouped at bottom (incl. just-heard New/Save, dissolving the old Suggested widget); (3) polymorphic reading-pane detail: member→contact detail, group-header→inline group management (rename/membership/delete); (4) carry the favorites CONNECTION RECORD into contact detail (✓/✗ strip + observed line + ToD hint), read from the SHARED favorite attempt store keyed by callsign, honest empty-state when no direct sessions; (5) multi-select→Add to group/Remove (NO 'Message all'); (6) lives in the message-list+reading-pane footprint so the 400px radio panel coexists exactly like Mail.

title: APRS tactical chat in tuxlink (KISS floor + UV-Pro native profile)
description: Polished tactical APRS messaging inside tuxlink's Winlink workspace. Power-consolidation thesis: run VHF tactical chat on the plugged-in Pi instead of a battery-powered phone+HT, so the field op sheds a charge demand (solar/heat/voltage-conversion reality). Two-layer protocol: (1) transport-agnostic APRS/UI-frame messaging core over KISS (works over the existing UV-Pro BT KISS + managed Dire Wolf + any TNC) = the broad floor, ship first; (2) native Benshi/Vero profile for the UV-Pro adding on-screen device control over the same BT link = the premium tier, later. Chat UX bar = BTECH Pro app (iMessage-over-radio); protocol ground truth = HT Commander source (open). RF-honest delivery states (sent/heard/ACKed/timed-out, no fake 'delivered'). tuxlink does Winlink LEGIT (registered SID), unlike HT Commander's WLE-SID spoof. Single-BT-host handoff is acceptable (tooltip). NO chat-history sync (power-consolidation, not continuity, is the why). Winlink-over-APRS gateway is a SEPARATE parked idea. Spec: docs/design/2026-06-12-aprs-tactical-chat-design.md. Brainstormed in office-hours 2026-06-12 (glade-clover-bison).
notes: Frontend redesign (slate-arroyo-marsh, 2026-06-13): Plan 1 (surface: timestamps/Acked-HH:MM/67-char counter/open-channel cue) + Plan 2 (re-home to shared switchable right dock; status-strip control entry 1 + dock tabs entry 2; lifted useAprsChat; removed 'aprs' pseudo-folder; controlStrip seam for native control). All on PR #642, CI-green (4/4) at HEAD a79f6219. Remaining for #642: operator on-air smoke (blocked by tuxlink-9ky BT). Deferred: entry 3 (View-menu, Rust menu-id parity); UV-Pro control-strip content = new issue tuxlink-ve3j. Native backend tuxlink-nx95 shipped (PR #647).

title: Plumb cms_web_api_key config field for tactical CMS-registration verification
description:
Phase 5 (tuxlink-tseu) shipped the tactical CMS-registration gate, but NativeBackend's TacticalRegistrationVerifier is constructed with an EMPTY access key because no config field carries the Winlink web-service access key yet. Consequence: every tactical CMS connect fail-closes (refused) because the verifier returns KeyMissing -> Unknown -> Refuse. This is the safe default and does NOT block Phase 5, but tactical+CMS is unusable until a key is configured.

Needed: (1) add a 'cms_web_api_key' field (String, optional) to src-tauri/src/config.rs (NOT a keyring secret, NOT a transmit credential -- a service-access token; do not store in keyring); (2) thread it into bootstrap::install_native via NativeBackend (a with_tactical_verifier-style builder or set the verifier's key from config); (3) Phase 7 UI surface to enter it. Prior art: Pat embeds a WDT-issued key (dev/scratch/tuxlink-pat/internal/cmsapi/api.go:35) -- tuxlink must obtain its OWN WDT-issued key, NOT reuse Pat's. Endpoint/auth confirmed in the Phase 5 plan's Task 1 prior-art addendum.

Until this lands, tactical CMS verification fail-closes (correct + safe).

title: Forms G8b: direct print (form → system print dialog → printer, no save-to-disk)
description:
Completes the 'print' half of cumx's stated scope ('on-demand portable export PDF/print'). G8 (tuxlink-cumx, PR #621) shipped Export-PDF (Save dialog → file). Operator: a hardcopy currently means Export-PDF → open the file → print — an annoying save-to-disk detour. Add a direct Print affordance.

Mechanism: reuse the SAME WebKitPrintOperation on the form's live child webview, but call run_dialog(parent) instead of set_print_settings(file)+print(). run_dialog shows the GTK system print dialog (printer picker + page setup, including 'Print to File' as one option) and prints on confirm — no intermediate file. Returns PrintOperationResponse::{Print,Cancel}.

Scope: forms::pdf_export::print_webview (run_dialog) + forms_print Tauri command (returns bool printed/cancelled) + printForm() helper + a 'Print…' button beside 'Export PDF' in BOTH WebviewFormHost and WebviewFormViewer chromes. Same operator-smoke posture as G8 (display-gated). Discipline: contained, interop-neutral → straight TDD. Part of Forms epic tuxlink-zkuk; follow-up to cumx.

Gates green local: clippy --all-targets clean, backend 5/0, frontend 30/0. Awaiting CI; operator smoke = open form → Print… → confirm printer reach (display-gated). No new deps (reuses G8's gtk/webkit2gtk/unstable). Worktree: worktrees/bd-tuxlink-954o-forms-print.

title: Forms G12-C: SeqInc message sequence-numbering directive (27 forms)
description:
G12 parity-audit delta (tuxlink-kaz3). 27/152 bundled .txt templates use the SeqInc directive (and 4 reference a <seqnum> tag) for per-form message serial numbering — radiogram / RRI message numbers, net log sequence, etc. tuxlink parses neither (import.rs handles only Form:).

This is STATEFUL (unlike To:/Subject:/Msg:): SeqInc increments a persisted per-form counter each send, so it needs durable storage of the last-used sequence per form id, survive restarts, and a reset affordance. Distinct feature from the G12-A template engine; depends on G12-A's .txt parser to read the directive but adds the counter store + <seqnum> resolution.

NOTE: SeqSet appears 0 times in the current bundle (only SeqInc) — scope to SeqInc; SeqSet can be added if a future bundle uses it. Part of Forms epic tuxlink-zkuk.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-2tom-seqinc. Branch: bd-tuxlink-2tom/seqinc. Created 2026-06-12 11:24:01 UTC by peregrine-lupine-magnolia.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #638 (merge 5280f5e6, 2026-06-12). SeqInc per-form serial numbering: parser + persisted SeqCounterStore + send-time allocate/stamp + Settings reset section. CI green both arches; operator smoke pending.

title: Forms G12-B: host-tag substitution engine (<MsgSender>/<ProgramVersion>/<Callsign>/<DateTime>/<MsgTo>/<MsgCc>)
description:
G12 parity-audit delta (tuxlink-kaz3). The .txt templates use a SECOND tag namespace beyond <var fieldname>: host tags the WLE host substitutes from station identity / message context, NOT from user input. tuxlink substitutes NONE.

EVIDENCE (152 non-changelog .txt in src-tauri/resources/wle-forms/Standard_Forms):
  <MsgSender>     97 forms   (sender callsign in the body/footer)
  <ProgramVersion> 95 forms  (originating-client version footer)
  <Callsign>      10 forms
  <DateTime>       4 ; <Date> 1 ; <Time> 1
  <MsgTo>          3 ; <MsgCc> 3   (echo recipients into the body)
  <seqnum>         4  (see G12-C SeqInc — sequence numbering)

These live in the same To:/Subject:/Msg: blocks G12-A (tuxlink-o4p9) parses, so this is the substitution half of that engine: once the .txt is parsed, host tags must resolve from FormParameters (senders_callsign, submission_datetime) + message envelope (to/cc) + the version string. Without it, <MsgSender>/<ProgramVersion> render literally or vanish.

Depends on G12-A's .txt parser. Also relevant to the 6 native FormDef forms IF their hand-authored body_templates ever reference host tags (currently they don't — they pre-bake the footer). Part of Forms epic tuxlink-zkuk.
notes: MOSTLY SUBSUMED by o4p9 (PR #633): render_template substitutes <HostTag> from a host_tags map covering MsgSender/ProgramVersion/Callsign/GridSquare/DateTime/Date/Time/MsgTo/MsgCc — the main host tags from the audit (MsgSender x97, ProgramVersion x95). After o4p9 merges, re-scope pj7p to any remaining/uncommon host tags (or close as done). No longer blocks on the .txt parser — that shipped in o4p9.
close_reason: Subsumed by o4p9 (PR #633, shipped). forms::txt_template::render_template substitutes the host-tag namespace from send_webview_form's host_tags map: MsgSender, ProgramVersion, Callsign, GridSquare, DateTime, Date, Time, MsgTo, MsgCc — i.e. every host tag the G12 audit found in the bundle (<MsgSender> x97, <ProgramVersion> x95, <Callsign> x10, <DateTime>/<MsgTo>/<MsgCc>), reachable via the .txt To/Subject/Msg rendering. The only audit host tag NOT covered here is <seqnum>, which is tuxlink-2tom (SeqInc) territory. No separate engine needed.

title: Forms G12-A: runtime .txt message-template engine for catalog/org forms (To:/Subject:/Msg: + tag substitution)
description:
HIGHEST-SEVERITY delta from the G12 parity audit (tuxlink-kaz3). Interop-sensitive → build-robust-features + cross-provider Codex round.

PROBLEM
tuxlink has two form-send paths:
  1. send_form (FormDef path) — only 6 forms (ICS213, Form-309, Position, Bulletin, Damage Assessment, Check-In) with hand-authored subject_template/body_template. Correct WLE projection.
  2. send_webview_form (generic path) — the ~131 OTHER bundled authoring forms + ALL imported org/custom forms. This path IGNORES the form's .txt template entirely:
       - Subject = field_values['subject'] (exact match) else 'Form: <id>'.
       - Body = sorted 'key: value' dump with a 'form_id:' header.
       - Recipient = whatever the UI 'to' arg carried.

WLE's actual contract: the .txt template defines the message via To:/Subject:/Msg: lines with <var fieldname> substitution + host-tag substitution. tuxlink never parses these at runtime (import.rs parses ONLY the Form: directive, for detection).

EVIDENCE (bundle: src-tauri/resources/wle-forms/Standard_Forms, 152 non-changelog .txt):
  - 145/152 define 'Subject: <var X>'  → tuxlink emits generic 'Form: <id>' for the ~131 generic-path forms (field is e.g. 'Subjectline'/'newsubject', not 'subject'). Changelog warns subject edits break the RRI server + ICS-213 sorting/filtering.
  - 134/152 define a 'To:' line; 104 of those are a FIXED tactical/agency address (dyfi_reports_automated@usgs.gov x6, shares@mail.cisa.dhs.gov, welfare.winlinkph@gmail.com, per-form addresses). tuxlink ignores them → operator must KNOW + hand-type the address; a wrong address SILENTLY breaks the receive-side data pipeline (the DYFI→USGS aggregation case is the synthesis's flagship example).
  - 149/152 define a 'Msg:' projection → tuxlink ships a raw key:value dump instead (lower severity: WLE-aware receivers render the XML attachment via the Viewer; matters for non-WLE/plaintext receivers + body fidelity).

WHY IT MATTERS / SCOPE
Directly hits the epic's originating user pain: imported AAMRON/ARES org forms ARE generic-path forms (their .txt has To:/Subject:/Msg:), so import 'works' but the sent message has the wrong recipient/subject. Recipient + subject deltas are interop-correctness; body is fidelity.

DEFINITION OF DONE (full engine, not a slice — alpha = vettedness):
  - Parse the governing .txt's To:/Subject:/Msg: at send time for generic-path forms.

  - Produce the WLE Msg: body projection instead of the key:value dump.
  - Host-tag substitution is its own issue (G12-B-tags) but shares this parser.
GROUNDING-FIRST: re-verify on a sample of real generic-path forms that field NAMES in the HTML match the .txt <var> names (the engine is only correct if they align). Part of Forms epic tuxlink-zkuk.

title: Forms push — G7: self-contained form payloads (fix version-skew receipt failure)
description:
Eliminate WLE's worst real-world failure: the receiver must have the exact matching display/viewer form installed or receipt degrades to 'required display html not available'. Make outbound form payloads self-contained (embed or pre-render the viewer) so a received form ALWAYS renders regardless of the recipient's installed form versions. Tuxlink's clearest differentiator vs WLE. Verify interop implications with WLE recipients.

Part of the Forms push (epic tuxlink-zkuk). Research: dev/scratch/2026-06-11-forms-push-synthesis.md.
notes:
SESSION 2026-06-13 (lupine-ridge-marten) — design analysis, NOT built. Operator wants a long think before any build.

Key findings that should drive the decision:
1. INTEROP CEILING: A stock Winlink Express recipient lacking the form looks up display_form in its own library and hard-fails ("required display html not available"). It has NO mechanism to use a viewer embedded in the payload. So self-contained payloads CANNOT fix WLE recipients from the sender side — the achievable win is tuxlink->tuxlink (and future self-contained-aware clients) only.
2. AIRTIME KILLS EMBEDDING: A compressed viewer is ~5-15KB on top of a ~1-3KB field-value payload. Real per-form added TX time: AX.25 1200-baud packet ~110s (≈2 min!); ARDOP HF / marginal VARA HF ~65s; decent VARA HF ~20s; VARA FM wide ~3s. On a net passing 250 forms over packet/HF this is hours. Operator HARD-REJECTED adding 5-15KB ever. Embedding (any-form OR custom-only) is off the table.
3. tuxlink ALREADY degrades better than WLE: a received unknown form already shows (a) the human-readable plaintext body projection (To/Subject/Msg) AND (b) KeyValueView field list. WLE hard-fails; tuxlink does not. So the graceful-degradation differentiator is arguably ALREADY delivered.
4. MACRO REFRAME (operator steer): forms are a machine-readable data pipeline. Value concentrates in receive->machine (step 4): auto-ingest field VALUES into a dataset -> aggregate/map/CSV/KML/rollup/ICS-309. That step needs field VALUES (already travel), NOT the viewer HTML. "Self-contained payloads" is a refinement of the human-render step (step 3, already working) that does nothing for step 4 and harms step 2 (airtime). It is misaligned with where the value is.

AGENT RECOMMENDATION: do NOT build self-contained payloads as a fidelity/embedding feature. If forms value is to advance, the real prize is the receive-side data/aggregation layer (G1-G4, currently parked pending evidence: yv6i/09l9/ike9/td3k). Open question for operator: what is driving the forms push right now — getting to the step-4 data payoff, or something about steps 1-3 in real use?

title: Forms push — EPIC: WLE forms parity + the data-aggregation value layer
description:
Concerted forms enhancement/parity push (research 2026-06-11, delta-magpie-bog). Synthesis: dev/scratch/2026-06-11-forms-push-synthesis.md + 3 backing research reports (A: WLE capability inventory from decompiled source; B: operational usage from hamexandria+annex; C: operational usage from web/AARs).

THESIS: A Winlink form is a machine-readable typed DATA RECORD; the text body is a human-readable projection. The payload is the XML field/value record. Value is downstream of receipt: receiver auto-extracts XML into a dataset WITHOUT opening the message, then aggregates/filters/maps/rolls up hundreds of submissions by machine (250 freetext emails = 250 reading tasks; 250 form check-ins = 1 CSV + a map of 250 pins). Forms = the data contract with served agencies; feed external systems (ShakeOut 2023: DYFI->USGS MMI/PAGER + ArcGIS), auto-gen ICS-309, validation, locked legal records.

STRATEGIC CONCLUSION: the highest-value WLE forms capability is RECEIVE-SIDE AGGREGATION — exactly the piece tuxlink has NOT built. The forms engine (compose/send/render via in-app webview — already better than WLE's external-browser+localhost:8001) is shipped (ytya/xipa/hnkn); the VALUE LAYER (forms->dataset->map/CSV/rollup) is missing.

CHILDREN (all 'Forms push —' tagged): G1 aggregation engine (P1, core) · G2 CSV export · G3 map+filters+retention · G4 summary+KML · G5 in-app import · G6 org bundle import · G7 self-contained payloads (fix version-skew receipt failure) · G8 on-demand PDF/print export · G9 native validation · G10 reply-form round-trips · G11 forms docs/discoverability · G12 template/tag parity audit.

This epic is blocked-by its children (bd dep edges). Polished forms UX is a follow-on brainstorm, not in this epic.
notes: FORMS-DEMAND EVIDENCE (2026-06-14, jay-fox-willow via office-hours): operator publicly issued the burden-inversion challenge (name a real radiogram-load-bearing incident, not a hypothetical). Reply (K2EFG/Bergen Co EC) was a weak non-answer: substituted voice-interop-need for form wire-efficiency, gave a grid-down SCENARIO not a real incident, and the supporting RF claims were wrong (QSY-is-coordination-not-mode-limit; JS8Call decodes shared-band-below-noise so 'need a clear band' is false; regional H&W is an NVIS job that wants LOW simple antennas). Source has an admitted 'seat at the table' incentive → low signal quality. DECISION (operator): one noisy thread is not strong enough to change scope either way — FLOOR UNCHANGED (G5-G12 stay active; interop = table stakes for the Winlink-on-Linux wedge), COP (G1-G4) stays deferred; keep actively gathering real operational ground truth. Full analysis appended to dev/scratch/2026-06-13-winlink-forms-efficiency-rebuttal.md (gitignored, local on pandora). The 2026-06-11 'evidenced floor + ground-truth gate' strategy (~/.gstack design doc) stands unchanged.

title: Find-a-Station: center map on operator location + parallelize reachability prediction (speed)
description: Two UX-polish behaviors found after 0.52.1. (1) Map opens centered at [0,0] (mid-Atlantic) instead of the operator's grid: react-leaflet MapContainer center/zoom are mount-only props, but StationFinderPanel loads the operator grid asynchronously from config_read AFTER the map mounts, so it never recenters. Fix: a useMap()+effect child that setView's to the operator latlon when the grid resolves (no change to the frozen BaseMap C11 contract). (2) Station loading is sluggish: useReachabilityMap runs one voacapl process per on-band station fully serially (for...await predictPath). Backend already uses spawn_blocking so calls can run concurrently. Fix: bounded-concurrency parallel map, preserving cancellation + Unavailable-break semantics. Child of axq0.
notes: Both fixes implemented + merged-ready on PR #604. (1) RecenterOnOperator child setViews to operator latlon once config_read resolves (real-Leaflet smoke: ends on FM18lw, not [0,0]). (2) useReachabilityMap parallelized via PREDICT_CONCURRENCY=6 worker pool. Gates: tsc + src/catalog+src/map vitest 29 files/179 tests green; no Rust changed. Awaiting CI + operator review/merge.
close_reason: Both fixes merged to main via PR #604 (mergeCommit c246e7bd): map recenters on operator grid (real-Leaflet smoke FM18lw not [0,0]); reachability parallelized (concurrency 6). 4/4 CI green incl. both verify. Rides release 0.52.2 (PR #605). axq0 umbrella stays open for operator smoke.

title: Find-a-Station band selector: full amateur HF bands, not the ALE-derived 80/40/30/20m subset
description:
The Find-a-Station band selector offers only 80/40/30/20m (src/catalog/bandPlan.ts HF_BANDS). That subset is ALE-channel-shaped, not gateway-finder-shaped: it includes one WARC band (30m) but omits the other WARC bands (17m, 12m) and the major workhorse bands (15m, 10m), plus 160m and 60m. Operator diagnosis: the band set leaked from a prior ALE discussion into the Find-a-Station spec intent.

ROOT CAUSE: the design spec itself (docs/design/2026-06-10-find-a-station-propagation-map-design.md §7) specifies 'Reachability on: 80/40/30/20 m'. The implementation faithfully built the under-specified spec. NOT an engine limit: propagation/deck.rs active_hf_frequencies_khz accepts the full 1.8-30 MHz HF range (voacap is per-frequency).

FIX: expand bandPlan.ts (Band type + HF_BANDS + RANGES + LABELS) to the full amateur HF allocation (ITU R2): 160/80/60/40/30/20/17/15/12/10m. Correct the spec §7 line. Reachability map, prediction, and band bar flow from HF_BANDS/bandForKhz automatically. Update bandPlan/reachability/StationFinderControls tests for the new set. Watch band-bar layout with 10 tabs (CSS). 60m is channelized (range 5330.5-5406.5 covers the US 5 channels).

title: Multi-select drag-to-folder moves only the dragged row, not the whole selection
description: In the Inbox, selecting N messages (ctrl/shift-click) then dragging them onto another folder only moves the single row under the cursor, not all selected. Root cause: MessageRow.onDragStart (src/mailbox/MessageList.tsx) always sets a single-message DataTransfer payload {id, folder} and never consults selectedIds. FolderSidebar drop parses one id and routes to single-message moveByIdToFolder. tuxlink-l80q (PR #515) made the context menu + bulk bar selection-aware but left the drag path single-only. Backend message_move_bulk + AppShell bulkMoveToFolder already exist; fix is frontend wiring: make the drag payload carry all selected ids when dragging a selected row, and route a multi-id drop to the bulk handler. Mirror the l80q OS-convention: dragging an UNSELECTED row acts on just that row.
notes: Fix shipped in PR #603 (branch bd-tuxlink-hh1j/multiselect-drag). Root cause: drag payload was single-id, never consulted selectedIds; l80q made context-menu+bulk-bar selection-aware but not drag. Frontend wiring only; closes on merge.
