title: Map: ingest standard Web Mercator (3857) LAN tiles; correct the wrong 4326-only spec
description: Operator correction 2026-06-11: the 'never public OSM' posture means no public-tile-server ABUSE (the lazy WLE pattern), NOT 'refuse Web Mercator / no network ingestion'. Self-hosted LAN tile servers serving standard 3857 XYZ (e.g. Geographica TileServer GL) are the INTENDED case. The dyop/offline-map spec's deliberate 'BaseMap stays EPSG:4326; reject Mercator on CRS mismatch' (docs/design/2026-06-08-offline-map-foundation-approach.md §1; docs/plans/2026-06-09-dyop-lan-tiles-plan.md) is WRONG and must be superseded. The LAN-only/SSRF host gatekeeper is the real control and STAYS. Scope: (1) BaseMap support EPSG:3857; (2) gatekeeper accept Mercator, keep LAN/SSRF policy, invert/drop tiles/crs.rs reject-Mercator; (3) fix validation false-accept (crs.rs probe derives metadata URLs from the raw {z}/{x}/{y} template so always Unknown; MapTileSourceSettings hardcodes crs:Geodetic so Unknown always accepts); (4) wire tileSource into all 4 BaseMap consumers so zoom unlocks (absorbs tuxlink-n6xu + the station-map side of tuxlink-24px); (5) DESIGN FORK: the bundled offline base is plate-carree world-equirect-2048.png which can't overlay a Mercator map - decide the offline base/fallback; (6) projection math (projection.ts, gridGeometry.ts: Maidenhead lattice, GRIB bbox, position picker) currently assumes 4326 equirectangular linear px<->latlon - needs Mercator-aware geometry across every map pane. Warrants brainstorm -> spec correction -> plan -> build on its own branch off main. See memory project_lan_tiles_mercator_ok.
notes: BUILT + PR #606 OPEN + MERGEABLE (conflicts with main resolved 2026-06-11: StationFinderMap union of main's RecenterOnOperator/OPERATOR_ZOOM + this branch's tileSource). All 10 plan tasks done + final-review hygiene cleanup. Gates: cargo test, clippy --all-targets, tsc, vitest 2380, build, lint:docs all green locally; CI running on the merge commit. Map now EPSG:3857, ingests standard Web Mercator LAN tiles; CRS gate deleted, LAN/SSRF gate intact (final review verified host.rs 0-diff). Absorbs tuxlink-n6xu + tuxlink-24px (consumer wiring done). OPERATOR SMOKE PENDING (GUI): grim render gate @1920x1080 + Geographica live-config (localhost:8090/styles/darkmatter/{z}/{x}/{y}.png -> lan-live, tiles render, zoom unlocks; public URL still rejected). Close on merge.

title: Align Network Post Office send-flow with CMS (drop per-message Outbox checklist)
description:
Network Post Office is the only send/receive mode with a per-message Outbox selection checklist. Per the 6c9y design's own routing table (docs/design/2026-06-08-telnet-post-office-design.md §1.1), Network PO carries NORMAL mail into normal Winlink routing — same destination as CMS, differing only on the transport axis. The send-time-selection leakage guard is justified ONLY for Telnet RMS Post Office (local -L pool, 'never forwarded globally'); it was applied to Network PO purely for UI uniformity between the two PO panes (post-office-mocks.html), breaking consistency with the rest of the app's send/receive flow (CMS/VARA/ARDOP/Packet all just Connect + drain queued). Also the source of the confusing 'Outbox is empty — Connect to receive only' copy (operator-flagged 2026-06-11).

CHANGE (operator-decided option 1, 2026-06-11):
- Backend ui_commands.rs post_office_exchange: build_outbound_proposals(mailbox, intent, drain) where drain = if local { Some(selected) } else { None }. Network (Mesh) drains whole Outbox like CMS; local (-L PostOffice) keeps explicit-selection leakage guard. build_outbound_proposals already supports None=drain-all (status-quo CMS).
- Frontend TelnetPostOfficeRadioPanel.tsx: render 'Send from Outbox' checklist ONLY when mode==='local'. Network: no checklist/selection state, Connect label just 'Connect', one-line help note. Tidy local empty-state copy.

TESTS: backend — network drains all (ignores selection), local respects selected set. Frontend — network has no po-outbox-section, local unchanged.

SCOPE: pure TCP (no RF), reversible. Relates to tuxlink-u5hl (compose-time routing flag stays unnecessary for Network PO — its mail is undifferentiated from CMS).

title: Macro-action B: ingest by-message station-list reply into the station catalog/cache (radio-refresh parity)
description:
WLE-parity gap (operational). WLE 'Update Via Radio' posts a CMSWEBSVC inquiry, and the reply is auto-correlated by MID (ExpressDatabase.cs RMSChannelsUpdateHF) and merged into the channel table. tuxlink can SEND the PUB_* inquiry (catalog_send_inquiry rides the active transport, radio-capable) but the reply is display-only (CatalogReplyView in MessageView) — never ingested into the station list/cache. So a no-internet operator cannot refresh their gateway list over radio end-to-end.

Build: parse a received station-list reply (catalog_parse_reply already exists) and merge it into the station model + stations_cache so it populates Find-a-Station / the catalog offline. Surface both refresh paths (internet 'Get stations' + radio request) as proper 'update' actions. Likely couples with the configurable-service-codes work. Net-new operator behavior — may want a brief design pass before build.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-xrbw-radio-list-ingest. Branch: bd-tuxlink-xrbw/radio-list-ingest. Created 2026-06-11 09:37:06 UTC by fir-oak-isthmus.
close_reason: Closed

title: Configurable Winlink service codes (default PUBLIC), keyring-stored — facilitate MARS/SHARES/EMCOMM
description:
WLE-parity. The station/gateway catalog is hardcoded to serviceCodes=PUBLIC (src-tauri/src/catalog/commands.rs:146). Operators in groups (MARS, SHARES, EMCOMM, or any sysop-tagged set) can't see their gateways. Make service codes configurable.

GROUNDING (decompiled WLE + live probe + Hamexandria annex):
- A service code is a sysop-assigned TAG on a gateway registration. The listings endpoint (…/listings/<Mode>Listing.aspx?serviceCodes=X) returns only gateways tagged X. Verified keyless: probe of serviceCodes=EMCOMM returned a filtered subset with no key sent.
- It is a CLIENT-SIDE DIRECTORY FILTER ONLY. It is never sent at connect/session time (absent from all *Session.cs in the decompile), never gates a connection. tuxlink's existing text endpoint already honors arbitrary codes — no JSON /gateway/channel/report or web-service key needed.
- PUBLIC and EMCOMM are the only publicly-blessed codes. MARS/SHARES codes are FOUO member secrets (winlink-annex: 'should not be shared outside the SHARES organization'). WLE ships them as plaintext constants — a flaw we do NOT replicate.

SPEC:
- Make serviceCodes configurable; default 'PUBLIC'. Space-separated multiple codes (winlink.org convention).
- Thread through ListingMode::listing_url + catalog_fetch_stations + the stations_cache CacheKey (codes must be part of the key so PUBLIC vs a custom set don't collide).
- STORAGE: OS keyring (service 'tuxlink', via src-tauri/src/winlink/credentials.rs pattern), NOT plaintext config.rs/config.toml. NEVER hardcode any code; NEVER log code values.
- UI: a service-codes settings field + quick-picks for the PUBLIC ones only (PUBLIC, EMCOMM). Operator pastes their own member-issued FOUO code. Do NOT ship MARS/SHARES tokens.
- TDD plumbing task (bd-issue-is-the-spec per discipline triage). No adrev ceremony.

OUT OF SCOPE: macro-action B (radio-refresh reply ingestion) = separate issue.

title: U2 — persistent station-list cache (offline last-known-good)
description: Spec §6 of docs/design/2026-06-10-find-a-station-propagation-map-design.md (authoritative). Today's StationsCache (src-tauri/src/catalog/) is in-memory only → a cold offline launch has NO stations. Add disk persistence of last-known-good listing PER MODE under app_data_dir(), loaded on launch. Offline-first fetch flow: on open show last saved list immediately with freshness caption ('updated N ago'), no blocking call, NO modal; 'Check for newer list' is the ONLY network action, operator-initiated (success→refresh+restamp; failure→keep saved list + say so); cache ONLY good parses, never overwrite good data with an error body. Small TDD-against-spec backend unit (per §9 — NO cross-provider adrev ceremony, this is plumbing). Open items §12: on-disk cache format + location + eviction; freshness-stamp wire shape. DoD: a cold offline launch shows the last-known-good station list with a freshness stamp.
notes: BUILT + PR #581 open (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/581), HEAD 05a4215, CI-gated. 3 tasks done (stations_disk load/save; StationsCache persist wiring; lib.rs .setup() registration w/ graceful fallback). 77 catalog tests + clippy + doctests green; reviewed (9/9 pass). Backend-only — frontend StaleCaption already consumes fetchedAtMs. DoD (cold offline launch shows last-known-good) met in code; operator cold-offline smoke post-merge. Close on merge.

title: Inbox routing rules: sort delivered messages into folders/subfolders on delivery
description:
Operator request (2026-06-10), companion to tactical/multiple-callsign work (tuxlink-zjne). Add delivery-time routing rules (email-filter style) that sort INCOMING messages into folders + subfolders for organization, evaluated when a message is stored to a per-callsign Inbox.

DRIVING USE CASE (from zjne): tactical identities ride under a parent FCC callsign and have NO own mailbox. To keep the parent's Inbox sane, a tactical call (e.g. AIDSTATION-1) can get its OWN custom folder + subfolders, and mail addressed to that tactical address auto-routes there on delivery. Generalizes beyond tactical: route by recipient identity/tactical address, sender, subject, form type, etc.

SCOPE:
- Rule model: ordered match conditions (to-address / tactical address, from, subject pattern, form type, transport) -> action (file into folder/subfolder, mark, etc.). First-match or all-match semantics TBD in design.
- Custom folder + subfolder creation (user folders already exist: list_user_folders / move_user_folder / move_between in native_mailbox.rs). Routing places a stored message into a target user folder at delivery time.
- Evaluated at the store() boundary on inbound delivery (note: the search-index unread predicate was just aligned in tuxlink-mzm4 — routing must keep index/list/unread consistent for the destination folder).
- UI to create/edit/reorder rules (inline, per inline-UI rule; no popups).

OPEN QUESTIONS (design): first-match vs multi-match; default Inbox fallback when no rule matches; whether rules are per-full-callsign or global; interaction with the .read sidecar + search index on routed delivery; ordering/priority UI.

PREREQ: design pass. Built alongside zjne so tactical-folder routing lands with the identity model.

title: Support multiple / tactical callsigns (operate under more than one identity)
description:
Operator request (2026-06-10). Tuxlink today models a SINGLE station identity: config.rs IdentityConfig has one `callsign: Option<String>` (CMS path) plus one free-form `identifier` (offline path). Winlink supports TACTICAL ADDRESSES — role/station aliases (e.g. EOC-1, ARES-SEATTLE, SHELTER-3) that an operator sends/receives under IN ADDITION to their FCC callsign — and many EmComm operators run more than one identity. The config comment already acknowledges this gap: 'The CMS is authoritative for actual callsign / tactical-address.'

WANT: let an operator register and switch between multiple identities — their FCC callsign plus one or more tactical callsigns/addresses — and have outbound/inbound, the compose From, the mailbox, keyring entries, and the dashboard ribbon reflect the active one.

OPEN QUESTIONS (resolve in design before building — this touches the identity model + keyring layout, a hard-to-undo decision, so it belongs in the build-robust-features pipeline, NOT a quick patch):
- Data model: a list of identities with one 'active', vs FCC-callsign + tactical-alias overlay. How does this interact with the existing CMS-vs-offline callsign/identifier split + Config::validate rules?
- Per-identity credentials: tactical addresses authenticate under the owning FCC callsign's secure-login on CMS — keyring keying + which password is used per transport (CMS vs P2P vs packet).
- UX: how the operator switches active identity (ribbon? settings?), and whether mailbox/drafts are per-identity or shared.
- Which transports/intents honor a tactical From (CMS, Telnet-P2P, packet, VARA/ARDOP) and Part 97 station-ID implications (the FCC callsign must still ID the station — RADIO-1 / control-operator concern; surface to operator).
- WLE parity: how Winlink Express models tactical addresses (reference prior-art per winlink_re_authoritative_sources before adopting specifics).

PREREQ: office-hours/brainstorm + design pass (per skill routing: product ideas → office-hours). File captures the request; do not implement before the identity-model design is settled.
notes: Design + full 7-phase TDD plan complete (branch bd-tuxlink-zjne/zjne-identity-spec). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-10-multiple-tactical-callsigns-design.md. Master plan (canonical contract + resolved open questions + session breaks): docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-tactical-callsigns-master-plan.md. Per-phase plans: phase-1..7 in same dir. Execution phases: d4wp(P1 ready)->7iy2(P2)->0063(P3)->{2ns7(P4),tseu(P5),5ekg(P6)}->noa0(P7). Companion: 73nl (routing rules). Architecture is Codex-vetted capability/handle model; IdentityHandle is Clone(Arc)+non-Serialize per master-plan cross-phase reconciliation #1. CMS lookup confirmed = Winlink AccountTacticalExists (api.winlink.org/account/tactical/exists). Start execution at Phase 1 (tuxlink-d4wp) with subagent-driven-development.

title: [WLE parity] Favorite station list has no edit/delete/rename UI (RF modes); Network PO has no edit-in-place
description:

RF modes (ARDOP/Packet/Telnet) — favorites are READ + star-to-promote ONLY:
- Star a recent to promote / unstar a favorite to demote. NO delete, rename, reorder, or field-edit UI.
- Backend commands EXIST but are unwired to UI: favorites/commands.rs favorite_delete (line ~119), favorite_upsert (line ~84). useFavorites.ts exposes upsert()/remove() but radio panels never call them.
- Only explicit CREATE path is the Catalog Builder behind Tools -> Find a gateway by catalog -> star (src/catalog/CatalogBuilderPanel.tsx ~175). Not discoverable from the radio panes that DISPLAY favorites (FavoritesTabs.tsx / FavoriteRow.tsx show only star + Connect).
Network Post Office — PARTIAL: add/remove inline in TelnetPostOfficeRadioPanel.tsx (~425) but NO edit-in-place (backend network_po_favorites_set exists at ui_commands.rs ~5540, frontend never calls it; must remove + re-add).

SCOPE: design pass first (favorites-edit UX — likely an inline edit/delete affordance on FavoriteRow + a manage surface). Wire favorite_delete/favorite_upsert/network_po_favorites_set to UI. WLE-parity per [[feedback_wle_features_yes_ux_no_use_os_conventions]]. UI work => brainstorm before build.

title: [bug] Docs Mermaid flowcharts render oversized + node text clipped (0.44.0)
description:
REGRESSION on 0.44.0 (operator-reported). In-app Help docs render Mermaid flowcharts enormously oversized and with node text partially clipped.

ROOT CAUSE (marsh-lupine-isthmus investigation 2026-06-10, verified against origin/main):
- NOT missing useMaxWidth: src/help/ReadingPane.css:206 already has `.mermaid-diagram svg { max-width: 100%; height: auto }`; CSS max-width:100% overrides any fixed width attr, so that alone can't overflow.
- NOT a version bump: pnpm-lock.yaml pins mermaid@11.15.0, unchanged since first added (4b48493).
- NOT the recent mermaid commits: ebbef4c/57aee1c/a11a7ae are all fill/stroke/contrast theming, none touch sizing.
- HYPOTHESIS (one cause, both symptoms): Mermaid v11 renders flowchart node labels as htmlLabels (foreignObject) using the ambient page font. ReadingPane.css:54 applies font-size: var(--help-font-size, 18px) which cascades into the labels, but Mermaid MEASURED node boxes at its base-theme font (~16px). Rendered font > measured box => text clips AND inflated labels balloon the whole diagram. src/help/useMermaidRender.ts injects raw SVG and never normalizes width/font.

FIX DIRECTION: decouple the mermaid label font from --help-font-size (pin .mermaid-diagram foreignObject/label font to Mermaid's measured size) and/or set themeVariables.fontSize + flowchart.useMaxWidth:true in src/help/mermaidLoader.ts to match.

VERIFICATION: MUST be confirmed via real WebKitGTK render + SVG inspection (grim on pandora), NOT Chromium/Playwright. Automated gates (typecheck + vitest src/help/) guard the config; operator grim-smoke confirms the visual.
notes: PR #558 opened (branch bd-tuxlink-3xnf/mermaid-sizing, worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-3xnf-mermaid-sizing). Fix: normalizeMermaidSvgSize() pins intrinsic width/height from viewBox + drops inline max-width. Gates green: typecheck clean, vitest src/help 134/134. AWAITING: CI (full vitest both arches) as merge gate + operator WebKitGTK grim-smoke for the visual confirmation (jsdom/Chromium can't reproduce). Leave in_progress until PR #558 merges.
close_reason: Fix shipped in PR #558 (merged 2026-06-10): normalizeMermaidSvgSize pins intrinsic size for WebKitGTK. Operator WebKitGTK grim-smoke still owed post-merge per project norm.

title: Position 6-char precision: wire PositionMapWidget to LAN tile substrate + zoom so the gate unlocks
description: Follow-up to tuxlink-sdbd (PR #551). The Position precision selector ships with 6-char GATED off because PositionMapWidget passes no tileSource to BaseMap (frozen C11) and has no zoom controls, so the live view can never reach SIX_CHAR_MIN_ZOOM=12 — sixCharAllowed() is structurally false. To actually unlock 6-char: (1) the a1cc §5 shared control surface gives the picker real zoom controls + live view.zoom; (2) coordinate a BaseMapProps C11 widening so PositionMapWidget can pass a validated tileSource (per design §8.6, gated on dyop). Then thread the live view.zoom into PositionPickerOverlay's sixCharAllowed call (currently hardcoded to RASTER_VIEW_ZOOM=2 with a forward-looking comment). Design: docs/design/2026-06-08-map-picker-v2-design.md §6/§8.6. Depends on a1cc.
notes: Absorbed into tuxlink-7h2m / PR #606 (PositionMapWidget + all map panes wired to useTileSource; 6-char gate reads live zoom). Close when #606 merges.

title: README banner: strip defensive self-assertion (follow-up to #524)
description: Follow-up to the merged README rewrite (PR #524, tuxlink-b542). Operator copy critique: 'Tuxlink is in alpha, and it works' — something that works does not announce it; the assertion plants the doubt it tries to kill and reads as insecure self-promotion. Same with 'not an aspirational sketch', 'full-featured client, not a minimal one', 'direct about its edges' — defining the project by what it is NOT concedes the reader's default suspicion. Strip all defensive/protest-too-much constructions; let the Features list + install instructions carry the confidence. Edits: (1) banner → 'in alpha and looking for testers' + plain install/testing ask; (2) remove 'full-featured client, not a minimal one' from What-Tuxlink-is; (3) 'direct about its edges' → neutral lead-in. Docs-only.

title: Find a Gateway station rows do not prefill modem and star controls are misleading
description:
Operator report 2026-06-09: after the closed station-lookup favorites fix, Find a Gateway results still cannot actually be selected and plugged into an active modem, and row stars render as filled/selected by default instead of reflecting persisted favorite state or being user-toggleable.

Current-code findings: src/catalog/StationResults.tsx renders only a filled ★ button for every favoritable row; it has no row/select action, no persisted favorite-state input, and no unstar/toggle path. src/catalog/CatalogBuilderPanel.tsx wires ★ to favorite_upsert + favorite_star, which can create a favorite but does not prefill the active Packet/ARDOP modem. VARA station result usage is broader because the current VARA panel only manages the local/remote VARA host/ports, not a gateway target field.

Scope: make Find a Gateway rows perform the expected prefill-only selection for active modem panes that already support favorite prefill (Packet and ARDOP), without initiating RF/network transmission. Make star controls display actual favorite state and toggle star/unstar where the favorites store supports the mode. Preserve RADIO-1: selecting a station may populate fields only; the operator still clicks the modem's own connect/send action.
acceptance_criteria:
- Find a Gateway station results show unstarred vs starred state based on persisted favorites, not a filled ★ on every row.
- Clicking the star toggles favorite state where supported, including unstar for an already-starred station.
- Selecting/clicking a station result from an active Packet or ARDOP modem pre-fills that modem's target/gateway field and closes or otherwise acknowledges the picker; it does not transmit or invoke connect.
- Unsupported modes (including current VARA gap unless its gateway target exists) do not pretend to prefill the VARA host field.
- Regression tests cover star state/toggle and prefill-only selection behavior.
notes: jay-bluff-fir opened PR #525 (bd-tuxlink-0ml3/station-picker-prefill). Fix: StationResults now reflects persisted favorite state with ☆/★ and toggles favorite_star for existing rows; CatalogBuilderPanel can emit prefill-only station selections; PacketRadioPanel and ArdopRadioPanel consume same-window prefill events to populate target fields without invoking connect; VARA intentionally excluded until it has a gateway target field. Local gates passed: focused vitest (100 tests), pnpm run typecheck, git diff --check, pnpm run build (existing Vite warnings only).
close_reason: Reconciled by grounding-sweep grouse-thistle-wren 2026-06-10: shipped — dedicated branch merged via PR #525. Closed to ground tracker as source of truth; reopen if scope remains.

title: GitHub issue action opens blank issue instead of prefilled template with logs
description:
Operator report 2026-06-09: opening a test issue through Tuxlink/GitHub currently lands on a blank new GitHub issue. The previously suggested support flow — automatically using an issue template and preparing/uploading logs ready to attach — is either not shipped or not working.

Expected: the in-app report-issue/support action should open GitHub with a useful prefilled issue template, including app/build/environment context and clear log attachment handling. If GitHub/browser constraints prevent automatic attachment upload, the app should prepare a log bundle locally and make the issue body/instructions point the operator to the generated artifact or provide a copy/upload affordance. It should not drop the operator into a blank issue page.

Scope to investigate: Help/About/support menu action, GitHub issue URL construction, issue-template query parameters, log bundle generation/export, and tests around URL/body generation. Treat the operator's blank-issue result as the regression symptom.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reproduce the current report-issue flow opening a blank GitHub issue.
- The flow opens the intended GitHub issue template or equivalent prefilled issue body.
- Build/app/environment context is prefilled.
- Log collection is prepared in a way the operator can actually attach or paste; if auto-upload is impossible, the limitation is handled in-product rather than silently skipped.
- Regression tests cover the generated GitHub URL/body and log-preparation handoff.
notes: Fix in PR #531. Root cause: ?body= blank-issue URL dropped under blank_issues_enabled:false (and ?template= falls back to blank too, operator-verified). Fix: route to static /issues/new/choose + move diagnostics to a Copy-diagnostics clipboard button. All local gates green (Rust cargo test + clippy --all-targets; vitest + tsc). HOLD for operator confirm that /choose renders the chooser (GitHub behavior unreachable from Pi). Close on merge.
close_reason: Closed

title: Connection log tail censors raw output operators should be able to inspect
description:
Operator report 2026-06-09: the operator-facing connection log tail pane censors/redacts raw connection output. That is wrong for Tuxlink's local operator workflow: operators should be able to inspect their own unredacted session output when debugging connectivity, protocol, modem, or gateway behavior.

Expected: the visible log tail exposes the raw/unredacted connection output to the local operator, or provides an explicit raw view/export that is unredacted. Any privacy/safety redaction policy should apply only to share/export flows where logs leave the operator's machine, not to the local diagnostic pane by default.

Scope to investigate: connection/session log pipeline, log-tail UI, any redaction/sanitization helpers shared with external bug-report/export flows. Preserve separate redacted-share behavior if it exists, but do not silently censor local operator diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Reproduce and identify where connection log output is redacted before reaching the operator-facing tail pane.
- Local log tail/raw view shows unredacted connection output.
- Any external share/export/bug-report path still has an intentional redaction story, with tests covering the local-vs-share boundary.
- Add/update regression coverage for a representative value that was previously censored in the local pane.
notes: PR #536 CI is green on all required checks as of this session: amd64/arm64 verify and amd64/arm64 build all passed. Ready for operator merge. PR: https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/536
close_reason: Fixed in PR #536 (merged 2026-06-10, merge commit 6e52bcb): local operator session-log tail now preserves raw/unredacted lines, while diagnostic export/share redacts at the export boundary with regression coverage.

title: Verify catalog-reply formatting parser (CatalogReplyView) state in converged build — operator never sees it render
description: Operator report 2026-06-09: 'we built a catalog nice-formatting parser for better display of popular catalog requests but I've never seen it in the converged build.' INVESTIGATION DONE (crag-harrier-towhee, 2026-06-09, against origin/main): IT EXISTS and IS wired. src/catalog/CatalogReplyView.tsx (landed commit 7b6489e 'feat(catalog): location-aware builder UI + reply view', tuxlink-a2gd lineage) calls a Rust parseReply (useCatalog.ts) and renders structured-or-raw with a toggle; it is rendered at src/mailbox/MessageView.tsx:515. WHY IT'S NOT SEEN — it is GATED by isCatalogReply(message) (MessageView.tsx:48): requires From containing 'SERVICE' (case-insensitive) AND Subject starting with 'INQUIRY - '. Further, the structured view only handles AREA-WEATHER products; everything else falls back to raw <pre> by design. LIKELY ROOT CAUSES the operator never sees it: (a) no message in the store matches the From/Subject predicate (ties to the WLE-catalog-migration test-data issue — real catalog INQUIRY replies would trigger it); (b) the predicate is narrower than real-world catalog reply From/Subject formats; (c) only area-weather replies get the structured treatment. ACTION: feed a real catalog INQUIRY reply through it (after the test-data import), confirm it renders structured; if the predicate/parser is too narrow vs real popular-catalog replies (GRIB, position, etc.), widen coverage. No code change yet — verification + scope decision first.
notes: Implementation PR opened: https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/538 (commit e6c381b). Investigation found the local mailbox does contain real SERVICE/INQUIRY catalog replies, including NWS area-weather replies. Fix preserves bare native B2F From/To/Cc header values in ParsedMessageDto when mail-parser cannot materialize RFC address objects, keeping From: SERVICE replies eligible for CatalogReplyView. Regression coverage added at Rust DTO and React render boundaries.
close_reason: Reconciled by grounding-sweep grouse-thistle-wren 2026-06-10: shipped — dedicated branch merged via PR #538. Closed to ground tracker as source of truth; reopen if scope remains.

title: fix(test): PositionFormV2 C9 test flakes on async grid-set race (broke 0.39.1 release CI on amd64)
description: The 0.39.1 release-please CI run (27186852003) failed on the amd64 verify job; arm64 passed the same commit. Failing test: src/compose/PositionFormV2.test.tsx:79 'keeps the manual grid input editable while the offline map is mounted (C9)' — AssertionError: expected 'position-form-v2__map' to contain '--active'. ROOT CAUSE (not arch-specific, not a product bug): PositionFormV2.tsx:216 derives the map-mount's '--active' modifier from `grid`, which is set asynchronously by the position_current_fix invoke. The test awaits only findByLabelText (the input's presence, rendered on the first pass before setGrid flushes) then asserts --active synchronously → races the GPS-fix re-render. amd64 lost the race this run. FIX: await the async grid landing before asserting its derived class (e.g. await screen.findByDisplayValue('CN87US'), matching the pattern the file's other tests use). Test-only change.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-pt6a-positionformv2-c9-flake. Branch: bd-tuxlink-pt6a/positionformv2-c9-flake. Created 2026-06-09 08:45:14 UTC by gulch-ivy-kite.

description: Design umbrella for the post-#481 map-picker UX rework. Operator walkthrough (2026-06-08, agent shoal-magnolia-fjord) enumerated: (GRIB) can't pan — drag is overloaded by box-draw, no physical controls, only pinch-zoom navigates; (Position) map is tiny inside the compose window + no zoom + no LAN offline-tile ingest (a specific spec request for higher zoom). Visual-companion brainstorm produced an APPROVED design: (1) a shared, complete picker control surface — Pan/Draw mode toggle (Pan default), +/- zoom + fit-world/fit-selection, draggable box handles, Maidenhead-overlay toggle, jump-to grid/coords, live cursor coords + scale bar, region/locator summary, tile-source status pill; (2) Position picker becomes an expand-to-overlay in-app picker (GRIB-panel pattern, not an OS window) launched from a 'Pick on map…' button, with a 4-char-default/6-char-opt-in precision selector + Reset-to-GPS; (3) LAN HTTP tile server as the precision foundation — Rust gatekeeper fetches only from an operator-configured permitted LAN host, serves to webview (CSP stays 'self'), on-disk cache, fallback-to-bundled, raises BaseMap maxZoom when a source is live. Decomposes into tuxlink-dyop (tiles, foundation), tuxlink-a1cc (shared nav controls), tuxlink-sdbd (Position overlay). Design doc: docs/design/2026-06-08-map-picker-v2-design.md.

title: Full-screen Catalog / Request Center — request-first redesign (shape approved 2026-06-09)
description:
Replace the cramped vertical Catalog Request modal (CatalogRequestPanel) with a full-screen 'Request Center', and give it a proper home for the info-category requests removed from Find a Gateway in 6jpf (PR #490).

APPROVED SHAPE (operator 2026-06-09, mock v4 'request-first'):
- Request-FIRST landing: lead with the frequently-used requests as location-aware cards grouped by section — Weather (area forecast, nearby METARs, marine, hazards), Propagation & space (propagation forecast, aurora, solar), Nearby stations (gateways near you, winlink info). NOT GRIB-first.
- Location chip 'Near <grid>' (e.g. CN87) in the header; relevant cards prefill/filter by the operator's grid.
- Search + 'Browse full catalog by category' reveal the full 1,477-item / 41-category browse (3-pane: category nav | items | basket) for the long tail / power users.
- Parameterized/custom requests (GRIB — niche, Saildocs) DEMOTED to a 'More:' link → opens a form in the center pane (lat/lon box + 'Center on my area' + grid/hours/params). Room for future 'Position forecast', 'Nearby METARs' forms.
- Request basket (right rail) + Send; when items span rails, tag each (CMS inquiry vs Saildocs) and Send-all dispatches per rail.

WHY: 1,477 catalog entries across 41 categories is a browse+search problem the single vertical modal serves poorly; and the common requests (weather/propagation/stations) were buried. GRIB sees little use and should not be front-and-center.

OPEN DECISIONS (iterate later):
1. 'Nearby stations' appears here AND Find a Gateway is in the radio panels + Tools (#490) — decide canonical home vs intentional dual-access (request list here / dial from panel).
2. Dual-rail basket: unified 'Send all' across CMS+Saildocs, or per-form Send.
3. Exact common-request set + their catalog filename mappings (US.ALL, AUR_TONIGHT, INQUIRIES, PROPAGATION, SAT_KEPS, PUB_* etc.).
4. Full-screen route/view vs large modal; relationship to the existing Message -> Catalog Request menu entry and the separate GRIB File Request panel (likely absorb both).

MOCKUPS (local-only, gitignored): .superpowers/brainstorm/1136216-1780984992/content/ — catalog-fullscreen-v1 (browse), catalog-fullscreen-v3-custom (GRIB form), catalog-request-first-v4 (APPROVED shape). Re-render via the brainstorming visual-companion server.

STATUS: shape approved; full spec + implementation plan + build deferred per operator ('go with this shape for now, iterate later'). Next session: writing-plans from this shape, then build behind the discipline (it touches the persisted request flow + menu IA).
notes: BUILD COMPLETE 2026-06-09 (dahlia-tamarack-canyon). Executed the full plan (docs/plans/2026-06-09-request-center-plan.md) Groups C-F via subagent-driven-development: per-task spec+quality reviews, 3-lens review loop per group, final whole-feature audit (all 8 decisions + 9 adrev revisions verified, file:line matrix). Old CatalogRequestPanel + GribRequestPanel deleted; Request Center is the sole path. Full vitest 1995/1995 green, typecheck 0. PR #513 open (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/513) — CI verify (clippy --all-targets + full vitest, both arches) is the merge gate. Worktree worktrees/bd-tuxlink-eymu-request-center stays until merge. Post-merge: opportunistic grim WebKitGTK smoke (deferred: :1420 contended, not a pre-merge gate). Close this issue on merge.
close_reason: Reconciled by grounding-sweep grouse-thistle-wren 2026-06-10: shipped — dedicated branch merged via PR #513. Closed to ground tracker as source of truth; reopen if scope remains.

title: Investigate: does production CMS volunteer ;PM: sender/date/subject lines in secure-login mode?
description: Research gate (2026-06-09, gulch-ivy-kite) for tuxlink-fzek confirmed the B2F proposal phase exposes only MID + uncompressed/compressed size; sender/date/subject/attachments are body headers unavailable pre-download. WLE surfaces From/Subject/Date via a separate ;PM:/LM channel it requests ONLY in non-secure mode (B2Protocol.cs:939 gates the secure path); in secure-login (MD5 ;PR:) sessions WLE falls back to CheckMessageListSecure = MID+size only. tuxlink uses secure login, so it sits in that regime. OPEN QUESTION: WLE's client never ASKS for ;PM: in secure mode, but that does not prove the server would not SEND it. EXPERIMENT: live telnet capture (authorized, no RF — see memory cms-telnet-testing-authorized) against the dev CMS (cms-z.winlink.org) with pending mail seeded in the account, secure-login, and observe whether the server volunteers ;PM:/LM lines unprompted. If it does, sort-by-sender/date/subject becomes feasible and the inbound selector can grow From/Subject/Date columns (3-col → 6-col). Decided 'ship size+est-time now, experiment later' (operator, 2026-06-09). Full research: dev/scratch/2026-06-09-winlink-proposal-metadata-research-gulch-ivy-kite.md
notes: RESOLVED NEGATIVE (2026-06-09, gulch-ivy-kite). Live secure-B2F capture vs cms-z (account N7CPZ, pending mail seeded via GRIB+catalog): CMS offered 11 messages as bare FC lines only — ZERO ;PM: lines, no From/Subject/Date/attachment. Secure login confirmed (;PQ:->;PR: in dump). So RF-only/in-band metadata = MID + sizes only; human identity is online-REST-API-only. BONUS finding: proposals arrive in 6 batches each requiring immediate FS — no see-all-before-deciding offline. Evidence: dev/scratch/2026-06-09-pm-capture-plaintext-gulch-ivy-kite.txt + -VERDICT-*.md
close_reason: Closed

title: InboundSelectionPanel modal stacks below app chrome (z-index 50) — same defect as tuxlink-29zx
description:
src/connections/InboundSelectionPanel.css uses the identical fullscreen-modal pattern that caused tuxlink-29zx: background rgba(0,0,0,0.5); display:grid; place-items:center; z-index:50. The app chrome stacks higher — menubar z-index 90, its dropdown 100, resize handles 200 (src/shell/chrome/chrome.css). So when the inbound-selection panel is tall enough that its header/controls land in the titlebar/menubar band, the chrome paints over them and they become unreachable (the same 'controls outside the top of the window' symptom the operator hit on Find a Gateway).

FIX: raise the overlay z-index above all chrome (1000, matching the CatalogRequestPanel / GribRequestPanel / userFolders / now-fixed CatalogBuilderPanel convention). One-line CSS change + a stacking-order regression assertion mirroring the one added in CatalogBuilderPanel.test.tsx (overlay z-index > max chrome z-index).

Not fixed inside the tuxlink-29zx PR (#484) to keep that branch scoped to the Find a Gateway dialog. Discovered by delta-bog-cypress while fixing 29zx. Audit note: the app's modal z-index convention is inconsistent (50 / 100 / 1000 across overlays); a sweep to standardize on a single 'above-chrome' value would prevent recurrence.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-76zy-inbound-zindex. Branch: bd-tuxlink-76zy/inbound-zindex. Created 2026-06-09 11:42:32 UTC by jay-bluff-fir.

title: [flaky] PositionFormV2 C9 offline-map test flakes on amd64 CI (--active class race)
description:
src/compose/PositionFormV2.test.tsx > '<PositionFormV2> > keeps the manual grid input editable while the offline map is mounted (C9)' intermittently FAILS on the amd64 verify runner while passing on arm64.

Failure: at PositionFormV2.test.tsx:79 — expect(mount.className).toContain('--active') — Received 'position-form-v2__map' (the '--active' modifier not yet applied).

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis): the offline map applies the 'position-form-v2__map--active' class asynchronously on mount (effect/leaflet load), and the test asserts synchronously after render without awaiting the class. Under the slower/heavier amd64 CI runner the class isn't applied yet → race. arm64 happens to win the race. This is the same timing-flake class as the AppShell/App/useInboundSelection full-suite flakes.

Observed: blocked PR #487 (tuxlink-fr0d) verify (amd64) 2026-06-09; the PR diff is favorites/telnet-only and cannot affect a src/compose position-form test. Re-run cleared it.

FIX: make the assertion deterministic — await the '--active' class (await waitFor(() => expect(mount.className).toContain('--active'))) instead of asserting synchronously, OR await the map-mount effect. Owner: offline-map foundation (tuxlink-z9u4 lineage). Non-blocking for the smoke-walk PRs; re-run is the interim unblock.

Discovered by delta-bog-cypress during the converged-build smoke-walk fix sprint.
notes: Duplicate of tuxlink-pt6a (filed same day without spotting wynv first). Fixed by PR #496 — await findByDisplayValue('CN87US') before the --active assertion. Closing as duplicate.
close_reason: Closed

title: UX: default 'review pending messages' ON + move control out of the GPS/Privacy settings modal to an inline home by Send/Receive
description:
Operator feedback 2026-06-09 (post tuxlink-bsiy ship + tuxlink-u9z8 staleness fix): the inbound-review feature functions once enabled, but its design is wrong. Two issues:

1. OFF by default. For a core emcomm bandwidth-budget feature, prompting should be the default behavior (Shape B: prompt by default, keep an opt-out). 'Default OFF for WLE parity / zero behavior change' was a non-reason — no users.

2. The toggle is buried in the SettingsPanel modal, reached via the 'GPS & Privacy settings' menu item, as the third item under GPS state + Broadcast precision. Operator: 'the buried setting is really the offensive issue — it doesn't belong where it currently is at all.' Also violates the inline-UI-no-pop-up-windows pet peeve (SettingsPanel is a modal overlay).

Fix: (a) default review_inbound_before_download = true (config.rs default + wizard/bootstrap fixtures); (b) relocate the control out of SettingsPanel to a discoverable inline surface adjacent to the Send/Receive (cms_connect) action — keep it as an opt-out to auto-download-all, inline, no modal. Mock the placement for operator eyeball before building (high-fidelity, per UI conventions). Ship as a small PR; CI-green is the merge gate; operator smokes on warm main.
notes: PR #491 open off main (commit e53cd79). Default review ON + segmented 'On connect: Review|Download all' control in DashboardRibbon (operator-picked Variant A); removed from SettingsPanel. Local verify green: tsc, full vitest 1881, clippy --all-targets. Awaiting CI both arches, then merge.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #491 (merge e90a1cf, CI green both arches), merged to main 2026-06-09. Default review_inbound ON; inline 'On connect: Review|Download all' segmented control in the dashboard ribbon (operator-picked Variant A); removed from the GPS/Privacy SettingsPanel modal.

title: Flaky test: connect_rejects_concurrent_call_when_already_in_progress (sleep(50ms) sync) intermittently fails CI / blocks releases
description: src-tauri/src/modem_commands.rs — the busy-guard test 'connect_rejects_concurrent_call_when_already_in_progress' (added by 57a9850, busy-guard refactor) synchronizes with a hardcoded std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)) to wait for a spawned worker thread to flip connect_in_progress before firing the second connect. Under CI load the worker is sometimes not scheduled + busy-bit-set within 50ms, so the second call is NOT rejected and the assertion at modem_commands.rs:1349 panics. Observed: release-please PR #483 (release 0.38.0) verify(amd64) FAILED on this test, then PASSED on a re-run of identical code (run 27178890784 fail -> 27179181303 amd64 success). NOT caused by #481 (offline-map) or #482 (winlink inbound-review): neither touches modem_commands.rs. Fix: replace the sleep with a deterministic sync primitive — have the worker send on a std::sync::mpsc channel AFTER it sets the busy bit, and have the test block on that receive before the second call (per docs/pitfalls/testing-pitfalls.md §5: use a barrier/sync primitive, not a timing assumption). RF-path-adjacent (modem) -> operator green-light before changing; test-only, no transmit path.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-753p-deflake-concurrent-connect-test. Branch: bd-tuxlink-753p/deflake-concurrent-connect-test. Created 2026-06-09 02:15:52 UTC.
close_reason: PR #486 merged — flaky concurrent-connect busy-guard test replaced sleep(50ms) with deterministic mpsc handshake; verify green on both arches

title: Rewrite 'What Tuxlink exposes today' section in direct, formal, authoritative voice
description:
The 'What Tuxlink exposes today' documentation section uses temporary-sounding, 'living-document' phrasing that reads as unprofessional. Rewrite it (and apply the standard to shipped docs generally) to read as direct, formal, authoritative reference material:
- State capabilities as definite present-indicative facts ('Tuxlink connects to X'), not provisional ('Tuxlink can currently X', 'for now').
- Remove temporal/living qualifiers: 'today', 'currently', 'for now', and the section-title 'today' itself.
- Remove self-deprecating/provisional words such as 'honest'.
- Eliminate passive voice; use active voice throughout.
No hedging, no temporal framing. Operator standard set 2026-06-08 (session shoal-raven-gorge).

title: Compact collapsed-drawer abort reachability — decide zero-expand abort
description: FZ-M1 compact mode (tuxlink-h7q7) collapses the radio panel to a 44px grip drawer. When collapsed during a live RF session, the panel's Abort/Disconnect is hidden behind one expand-tap. Mitigation shipped: the grip shows an HONEST amber session-state tick (deriveDrawerSessionState switches on status.kind) so a connecting/active session is glanceable. OPEN DECISION (operator-owned, radio-UX/safety — not built speculatively per feedback_no_tuxlink_added_safeguards): do you want a zero-expand abort (ribbon Abort wired to every transport's disconnect, not just CMS connecting)? Surfaced in the FZ-M1 PR operator smoke-note. Found by Claude adrev F3 (2026-06-07, taiga-raven-wren).

title: Drafts: clicking a draft opens it for editing instead of viewing in the reading pane
description:
SYMPTOM: In the Drafts folder, clicking any item in the draft message list immediately opens it for EDITING (drops into Compose/edit mode). There is no way to just look at a draft's contents without entering edit mode.

EXPECTED: Clicking a draft should display it in the READING PANE (view-only), consistent with how received/sent messages behave. Opening for editing should be a SEPARATE, explicit user choice (e.g. an 'Edit' action / double-click / Edit button in the reading-pane header), not the default single-click behavior.

WHY: View vs edit are different intents. Forcing edit-on-click means an operator can't quickly scan a draft's content without risking accidental edits, and it breaks the uniform 'click row -> reading pane' mental model the rest of the mailbox uses. Aligns with the inline reading-pane idiom (no pop-out window clutter).

SCOPE: Drafts list row click handler -> route to reading-pane viewer; add explicit Edit affordance to open Compose with the draft loaded. Reported by operator 2026-06-07 (session shoal-raven-gorge, /btw).
notes: PR #506 opened by jay-bluff-fir: https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/506. Fix routes Drafts row click into the reading pane, reads local drafts through useMessage without backend message_read, and adds an explicit Edit Draft action while suppressing Archive/Move for local drafts. Verification: useMessage vitest, MessageView vitest, AppShell vitest, typecheck, git diff --check, pre-push lint:docs.
close_reason: Fixed by merged PR #506: https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/506

title: Preview image attachments in the message reader
description: Smoke-walk item 6: inbound messages can now list attachments and Save As, but operators still have to save/open image files externally just to identify their contents. Provide a safe in-reader preview path for common image attachments so multi-attachment messages can be triaged without a many-click save workflow.

Preview fetches attachment bytes on demand from stored message content, renders a bounded in-reader image, and does not transmit or contact CMS/RF. title: Session log should summarize B2F message movement
description: Source: dev/scratch/Tuxlink bd Issues-to-File.txt item 4. Telnet and other modem plain/operator logging should be more verbose during B2F exchanges, including listing incoming messages and delivery outcomes so operators can tell what moved without opening raw wire output.

title: Make Codex commit attribution visible in GitHub contributors

Research against OpenAI Codex docs and public GitHub examples shows the canonical trailer is Co-authored-by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>, which maps to @codex. Add project-scoped Codex config and AGENTS.md guidance so future Codex commits transparently show Codex involvement while preserving the existing Agent trailer workflow.

title: [bug] logging retention updates do not reach future rotation sweeps
description: Holistic bug hunt 2026-06-06 found that logging::init captures RetentionConfig once and passes it by value to disk_consumer; logging_set_retention saves new settings and runs only an immediate sweep. Future hour-rotation sweeps keep using the startup config until restart. Evidence: docs/bug-hunts/2026-06-06-logging-reactor-panic-holistic.md.
acceptance_criteria: After logging_set_retention, the next disk-consumer rotation/scheduled sweep uses the new days/MB cap without app restart; regression coverage mutates retention then triggers the later worker action.

title: alpha-logging: on-error probe trigger (Codex P2 #9)
description: Codex impl-adrev P2 #9: env_probes::spawn_runner only fires on first_paint trigger. The on-error subsystem trigger requires the Fanout Layer to expose an error-broadcast tap that spawn_runner subscribes to (spec §9.2). Current location: env_probes/mod.rs:164-166. The comment there already notes the gap ('On-error subsystem trigger: deferred — the Fanout Layer would need to expose an error-broadcast tap'). Implementing this requires: (1) a new error-event tap on FanoutLayer/broadcast_tx (subscribe to only error/warn events); (2) spawn_runner subscribing to that tap; (3) per-probe on-error handler invoking the appropriate probe with trigger='on_error'. Deferred from Codex P2 batch B due to architectural scope — the Fanout Layer change touches the Layer impl and FanoutLayerHandle.

title: Inline image rendering in MessageView (follow-up to tuxlink-9ylw placeholder)
description: Follow-up to tuxlink-9ylw (PR #401, 2026-06-05). The placeholder ships in #401: [Binary content (N bytes) — see attachments] This issue tracks the actual inline image rendering work — turning that placeholder into a visible image where appropriate. ## Design surface (decide during scoping) ### 1. Byte → renderable URL bridge Two viable patterns; pick one: - **Base64 data URLs** — encode attachment bytes inline into ``. No Tauri protocol surface; bloats DOM for large images (a 200 KB JPEG → ~270 KB string in the React tree). - **Custom Tauri protocol** — e.g. `tauri://attachment//`. Server-side handler in Rust reads from the message store. Cheaper rendering, but adds an asset-protocol entry to `tauri.conf.json` CSP allowlist (related to tuxlink-bt2q for CSP discipline). ### 2. MessageView rendering paths (three distinct cases) - **Single-part image message** — no text body at all (the canonical CMS-Z Catalog reply). Render the image fullbleed as the body. - **Text message with image attachments** — render text body normally; show image previews in or alongside AttachmentStrip. - **HTML message with `cid:` inline references** — the `` references map to multipart-related parts. Separate slice; depends on text/html body rendering existing first (currently MessageView only handles text/plain). ### 3. Sizing + UX - Max-width to viewport; preserve aspect ratio - Click-to-zoom modal (modern email convention) — or open in OS image viewer via tauri-plugin-shell? - Lazy load matters for emcomm: HF/ARDOP operators may not want auto-load on bandwidth-constrained sessions. Consider a per-message-or-per-folder "show inline / show as attachment only" toggle. Default-on for most cases; opt-out for HF transport? - CSP allowlist constraints (currently strict — see tuxlink-bt2q for OSM tile precedent) ### 4. Scope decisions to make - Image-only first (image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp), or include video/audio too? (Suggest image-only first.) - Show inline by default, or behind a "Show images" button (like Gmail spam protection)? Modern Winlink operators expect inline; spam isn't a concern on amateur radio. - Thumbnails in folder list, or just in the open message view? (Suggest just message view to start.) ## Out of scope here - HTML body rendering generally (separate issue if/when needed) - Forms-with-images rendering (HTML Forms support already has its own surface) - Video/audio attachment preview - Generic attachment surfacing for messages with no text and no attachments-stripe-named parts (covered by tuxlink-4or5) ## When to schedule Operator note 2026-06-05: deferred to next-week's token-budget reset (Sunday). Not blocking ship.
notes: Smoke walk item 6: inline image/attachment rendering is not just cosmetic. Operators need to discern attachment contents without a many-click save/open/organize flow, especially when a message has many attachments and not all need permanent local organization.

title: PR #398 follow-up: gate Reply-with-form button on formPayload presence (Codex P2)
description:
Codex post-merge adrev on PR #398 (tuxlink-ltkv) flagged a UX-honesty gap: hasReplyWithFormSupport returns true for Bulletin_Initial based on formId alone, so MessageView shows the 'Reply with form' button even when XML payload parsing failed (formPayload=null). buildReplyDraft then falls back to plain reply at line 144 (!message.formPayload guard) — operator clicks 'Reply with form' but gets a plain reply window. Same gap exists for ICS213_Initial, but the production parse path is robust enough that it's hard to hit in practice; the Bulletin extension widens the surface.

**Repro:** MessageView.test.tsx 'shows the Reply-with-form button for Bulletin_Initial' fixture uses parsed({ isForm: true, formId: 'Bulletin_Initial' }) WITHOUT formPayload — test asserts the button shows. If a user clicked it, buildReplyDraft would short-circuit to plain reply.

**Recommended fix:** option (a) gate the button on formPayload presence — either (a1) MessageView checks message.formPayload alongside hasReplyWithFormSupport(message.formId), or (a2) extend hasReplyWithFormSupport(message) to take the full ParsedMessage and require formPayload truthy. Update the 'shows for Bulletin' test fixture to provide a payload.

**Codex transcript:** dev/adversarial/2026-06-05-pr398-bulletin-reply-codex.md (gitignored)
**Audit angle:** UI-affordance honesty (button promises a capability the underlying code can't deliver in some inputs)
**Severity:** P2 (UX nit, not data loss; falls through to plain reply gracefully)
notes: Implemented in PR #542 (commit d10dc28): MessageView now requires message.formPayload before showing Reply with form; positive tests include real parsed payloads; regression covers supported form ID with null payload; unknown/unsupported forms remain gated even with payloads. Verification: pnpm vitest run src/mailbox/MessageView.test.tsx; pnpm run typecheck; pnpm run lint:docs; git diff --check; pnpm run build (passes with existing Vite warnings).
close_reason: Reconciled by grounding-sweep grouse-thistle-wren 2026-06-10: shipped — dedicated branch merged via PR #542. Closed to ground tracker as source of truth; reopen if scope remains.

title: vara-ardop dial degrade: narrow unwrap_or_else to MessageRejected only (Codex re-review #2 P2)
description:
Codex re-review #2 of PR #399 (granite-oak-basalt's Phase 3-4 dial-degrade closure) flagged that the implementation degrades ALL BackendError variants to empty-outbound, not just the intended safety-gate's BackendError::MessageRejected.

Sites (both in winlink_backend.rs):
1. ARDOP dial @ winlink_backend.rs:2044-2049 — run_ardop_b2f_exchange's unwrap_or_else masks BackendError::Io from mailbox::list etc.
2. VARA dial @ winlink_backend.rs:2351-2356 — run_vara_b2f_exchange's unwrap_or_else, same shape.

Impact: corrupted or unreadable mailbox now looks like a successful empty send/receive instead of fail-closing as before. Real data-integrity P2 — distinct from the safety-gate degrade case (which was intended).

Reference implementation: ui_commands.rs::telnet_p2p_connect got the narrow handling right (matches MessageRejected specifically; propagates other BackendError variants). ARDOP + VARA dial paths should adopt the same pattern.

Discovered: 2026-06-05 Codex re-review #2 on PR #399 (already merged at 06:36:20 UTC). Transcript: worktrees/bd-tuxlink-0ye6-vara-ardop-panel-alpha-polish-v2/dev/adversarial/2026-06-05-phase3-4-re-review-2-codex.md (gitignored).

Follow-up against main, not amendment to PR #399.
notes: Fixed in PR #553. Extracted dial_outbound_or_propagate(): only BackendError::MessageRejected degrades to empty outbound; all other errors (corrupt mailbox Io etc.) propagate via ? — fail-closed, mirroring ui_commands::telnet_p2p_connect. Both dial sites (run_ardop/run_vara_b2f_exchange) call it. Answer paths intentionally degrade-all (out of scope). Unit test + clippy --all-targets green.

title: GPS live detection monitoring + dashboard event emission (slice 4)
description:
Wires the existing dashboard ribbon to react to GPS state changes mid-session. Operator confirmed the dashboard ribbon already exists and works — this bd ships the event SOURCE, not the consumer.

Depends on: tuxlink-9xy1 (bd-1). Strongly recommended: tuxlink-{bd-3-id} so the native NMEA detection is live too.

## Scope

### Background detection task

Expand `src-tauri/src/position/gpsd.rs:run_gpsd_client` (or split into a parallel monitoring task) to also poll:

- `/dev/ttyACM*` + `/dev/ttyUSB*` device enumeration — every 5 s, cheap readdir.
- ModemManager presence — every 30 s, D-Bus query.
- dialout group membership — only at startup + after a successful pkexec `add-dialout` (group membership doesn't change without explicit action).
- Fix-age tracker — emit `position://fix-stale` when last good fix exceeds the threshold (default 60 s, configurable).

### Tauri events emitted

- `position://source-changed` — payload: { old_source_id, new_source_id, reason: 'new-device' | 'source-lost' | 'config-changed' }.
- `position://fix-stale` — payload: { age_seconds, last_good_fix_grid }.
- `position://triage-required` — payload: { open_diagnostics: ['dialout-missing' | 'modemmanager-running' | 'no-device' | ...] }.

The dashboard ribbon already exists and consumes these events. This bd verifies the event contract matches what the ribbon expects and adjusts naming/payload to fit if needed.

### In-app toast for major changes

Small bottom-right notification ("GPS changed: new device detected · Review") that auto-dismisses after 8 s. Click bounces to Settings → Source picker. Toast notifications are debounced — rapid state changes (USB device flapping on a loose cable) collapse into a single toast within a 3 s window.

### Modal interrupt only when GPS becomes unavailable during an active Winlink connection

"GPS is now dark. Using stored manual grid <grid>. Continue session?" Yes / No / Open Settings. This is the only case where silence is harmful — broadcasting wrong position mid-Winlink-session is a real consequence.

## Acceptance criteria

- Sue's EOC arrival: laptop boots, gpsd unreachable, USB puck plugged in → `position://source-changed` fires → amber pill on dashboard → click jumps to Settings → Source picker with new device pre-selected as recommended.
- Dave's overnight distro upgrade: morning launch, dialout lost, ModemManager running → `position://triage-required` fires at startup → red pill → click jumps to Settings → Troubleshoot with both cards expanded.
- Mid-Winlink-session GPS loss: modal interrupt renders, not for idle-app GPS loss.
- Toast notifications debounced: rapid state changes collapse into a single toast.
- Tests: unit tests for the state machine that decides which event to emit; integration test for the toast / modal trigger logic; manual smoke test for the existing-dashboard-ribbon consumption path.

## Effort

3-4 days eng time.

title: GPS native NMEA serial reader — no gpsd required (slice 3)
description:
Sue's full zero-config path. With this shipped, an operator with a USB GPS puck and no gpsd installed gets a working position fix without installing or configuring anything beyond the dialout group fix (from bd-2 or operator-side manual).

Depends on: tuxlink-9xy1 (bd-1). Recommended (not required): tuxlink-{bd-2-id} for the dialout pkexec path.

## Scope

- **Open `/dev/ttyACM*` or `/dev/ttyUSB*` directly** via the `serialport` crate (already in src-tauri/Cargo.toml from tuxlink-7fr). Default 4800 baud; auto-detect 9600 / 38400 if 4800 sees no sentences in 3 s.
- **NMEA parser** — small dependency (e.g. `nmea` crate) or hand-rolled. Handle:
  - `$GPGGA` (fix data including lat/lon/satellites/HDOP) — primary.
  - `$GPRMC` (recommended minimum: lat/lon/speed/track/date) — fallback when GGA absent.
  - `$GPGSA` (satellites used) — for fix-quality display.
- **Lat/lon → Maidenhead conversion** reuses src-tauri/src/position/maidenhead.rs.
- **Source picker UI:** when gpsd unreachable AND a serial device detected AND user is in dialout, the native source becomes the recommended green card with "Use this" pre-selected.
- **PositionArbiter extension:** add `Source::NativeNmea { device_path, vendor, model }` variant alongside existing gpsd source. Privacy gating (gps_state) applies identically.
- **Bluetooth NMEA via /dev/rfcomm*** as a stretch goal within this bd, OR a sub-bd if BlueZ pairing UX expands scope.

## Out of scope

- Bluetooth pairing wizard (BlueZ pairing is its own UX rabbit hole; defer until user feedback requests it).
- u-blox proprietary binary protocol (some pucks default to UBX; if a device sends only UBX, surface a triage card "your GPS is configured for UBX, not NMEA — see u-center to switch" rather than parsing UBX).

## Acceptance criteria

- Sue's EOC arrival path: no gpsd reachable + USB GPS on /dev/ttyACM0 → tuxlink reads it directly → grid displayed in Settings + dashboard pill within 5 s of plug-in.
- gpsd present + native source both available → user can switch in Source picker; PositionArbiter respects the selection.
- USB device disconnect → source dies cleanly, dashboard pill goes red, manual fallback activates.
- Unit tests cover NMEA parsing of representative real-world sentences (u-blox 7/8/9, GlobalSat, Garmin USB pucks).

## Effort

4-5 days eng time.

description: Codex Phase 3-4 boundary review 2026-06-04 P2 cluster. See dev/handoffs/2026-06-04-dune-bison-salamander-phase3-4-complete-codex-p1s-found.md section 3 P2 details. Sweep landing on bd-tuxlink-0ye6/vara-ardop-panel-alpha-polish-v2 alongside the P1 sweep.

title: VARA Phase 3-4 polish sweep: stop-state cleanup + b2f payload widening + HF/FM arming + DTO wire-in (Codex P2#1-#4)
description:
Codex Phase 3-4 boundary review found 4 P2 findings on the VARA side. Related polish; ship together.

P2#1 — Clear VARA abort + owner state on stop (vara/commands.rs:1202-1210):
vara_stop_session_inner drops only transport + active mode; leaves abort_writer/abort_stream + transport_owner untouched. Stale ABORT writer can survive into next session if cloning fails; closed status overlay can incorrectly show listenerArmed.
Fix: clear abort_writer, abort_stream, transport_owner alongside transport in stop_session_inner.

P2#2 — Accept full VARA B2F intent/kind payload (vara/commands.rs:1229-1233):
parse_vara_b2f_intent only accepts cms/p2p — no radio-only. Command signature has no transport_kind. Shared panel sends SessionIntent+transportKind for every B2F command; spec capability matrix includes radio-only outbound. VARA radio-only invokes will fail; VARA-FM dials cannot be distinguished.
Fix: take full SessionIntent enum + TransportKind like ARDOP's modem_ardop_b2f_exchange. Validate VaraHf/VaraFm.

P2#3 — Pass VARA HF/FM kind into listener arming (vara/commands.rs:919-924):
vara_open_session receives transport_kind, but auto-arm call drops it. arm_vara_listener_inner records/rejects as TransportKind::VaraHf unconditionally. Opening VARA-FM with P2P/radio-only writes HF arm/reject forensics.
Fix: thread transport_kind through arm_vara_listener_inner; use it in arms/reject records.

P2#4 — Populate lifecycle DTO fields from real state (vara/commands.rs:316-324):
VaraStatus.listener_armed + exchange accessors still return false/None stubs. Task 3.0 stub accessors were supposed to be wired by Phase 3.2-3.6 but got missed for these two. Shared panel can't render or gate lifecycle correctly.
Fix: wire listener_armed to VaraListenState; wire exchange to arbiter transport_owner (or dedicated exchange_state field).

Recommended ordering: do AFTER P1 fixes (tuxlink-pdnw, tuxlink-0iqi, tuxlink-u5hl) since P2#1 + P2#4 may interact with the close-vs-consumer race fix.

Codex transcript: dev/adversarial/2026-06-04-phase3-4-boundary-codex.md (gitignored)

title: Wire arbiter into modem_vara_b2f_exchange + listener consumer
description:
Task 3.4 (modem_vara_b2f_exchange) shipped with the existing transport-take pattern (`VaraSession::take_transport`), not the Task 4.3 arbiter (`take_transport_for_outbound` + `transport_yield_request` notify). Wiring the dial command to the arbiter today would deadlock against a listener consumer task that does not yet honor the yield-request notify.

Required changes:
1. `vara_listener_consumer_task` in src-tauri/src/ui_commands.rs: add a select! arm on `transport_yield_request.notified()` that breaks the accept loop, sends the held transport through `transport_yield_tx`, and awaits the return via `transport_return_rx`.
2. `modem_vara_b2f_exchange` in src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/commands.rs: swap the current `session.take_transport()` call for `session.take_transport_for_outbound().await`, and after exchange wrap `return_transport_from_outbound` so the listener can re-arm.
3. Tests: end-to-end "listener armed, outbound dial yields + returns, listener re-arms" using the test-only `transport_yield_notify_clone` + `transport_yield_sender_clone` plumbing from VaraSession.

See:
- TODO comment inside `modem_vara_b2f_exchange` (search for 'arbiter wire-in')
- Task 4.3's TransportOwner state machine docs in src-tauri/src/modem_status.rs
- ARDOP's parallel deferral if/when it adds the same arbiter behavior

title: Deep B2fEventSink threading through backend.connect (ARDOP/VARA/packet) — Mode 5 discriminator + transport-kind precision
description: Follow-up from tuxlink-7do4 Task 12 scope reduction (2026-06-04). The smart auth-failure diagnostics banner uses result-level classification for cms_connect today (BackendError → FailureMode), which gives Mode 2/3/4/6 + Uncategorized correctly but collapses Mode 1 + Mode 5 to Mode 1 (NetworkUnreachable). To get accurate Mode 1 vs Mode 5 discrimination + TransportFailureKind subkinds (DNS/TCP/TLS), thread Option<Arc<dyn B2fEventSink>> through backend.connect to the run_exchange call site. Touches: winlink_backend.rs Backend trait, all backend impls (ARDOP, VARA, packet, P2P telnet), ui_commands.rs cms_connect call site. Privacy fix unaffected (already at telnet.rs WireTap layer).

title: feat(connect): smart auth-failure diagnostics — distinguish CMS response classes in connect panel
description:
Currently the connect-panel error state collapses several distinct CMS auth-failure modes into a generic 'auth failed'. The 4,105-thread corpus (PR #390 archive) shows this opacity drives much of the user-pain: 618 password threads + 309 account-lock threads + 536 callsign threads.

## Scope

Distinguish at least 5 failure modes in the connect path and surface each as a distinct connect-panel state with a specific recovery action:

1. Network unreachable — DNS / TCP / TLS failure before CMS replies. Recovery: 'check your internet connection'.
2. CMS rejected the connection (client-SID/TLS failure) — per project_cms_rejects_unknown_clients memory, prod CMS rejects unregistered SIDs. Recovery: 'tuxlink-side bug or environment issue, file with us'.
3. CMS rejected the password — B2F got to the password challenge and the credential was wrong. Recovery: deep-link button to winlink.org reset flow + 're-enter password' affordance.
4. CMS rejected the callsign — server says callsign isn't registered or isn't approved. Recovery: deep-link to winlink.org account page + 'verify your account is registered'.
5. Auth succeeded but session dropped mid-exchange — RF or network blip. Recovery: 'try again, no credential issue'.

## Design constraint: blocked from prod CMS

The big catch-22: we can't hit prod CMS to characterize its responses. Engineering recipe (per condor-hemlock-fir 2026-06-04 strategy discussion, captured in PR #390 archive README):

- Pat (la5nta/pat) is the primary reference — open-source Go client whose B2F handling code reflects actual prod-CMS response behavior.
- cms-z.winlink.org is the dev integration target (we use it today per project_cms_rejects_unknown_clients).
- The corpus (4,105 threads) surfaces user-visible WLE error strings — sideways window into which prod responses fire in practice.
- RF-via-RMS is the ground-truth escape hatch — RMS gateways proxy to prod CMS, so connecting on-air to a real RMS surfaces real prod responses. Operator-consented per RADIO-1, one-shot characterization viable.
- Ship defensively: unknown response patterns fall through to generic 'auth failed (uncategorized)' with raw response in session log.

## Out of scope for this issue

- View-password and credential-export rejected by operator 2026-06-04 ('modern apps don't do this; password manager is the answer').
- Layer-2 pre-condition detection ('warn before destructive ops') — deferred until specific user-pain trigger surfaces.
- Layer-3 Winlink-side changes (their reset flow, app-passwords, structured error codes) — advocacy not engineering.

## Test strategy

- Unit tests for the auth-response-taxonomy parser against fixtures derived from Pat's response strings.
- Integration tests against cms-z for happy-path + at-least password-rejection.
- Operator-consented RF spot-check session against an RMS for the responses cms-z can't reproduce.

## Evidence package backing this work

- dev/research/winlink-group-corpus-2026-06-04/ (PR #390) — 4,105 threads, 618 password-related, 309 account-lock-related, 536 callsign-related.
- dev/research/2026-06-04-winlink-group-pain-points.md — synthesis explicitly calls out 'auth-failure opacity drives user-pain' theme.

Filed 2026-06-04 by condor-hemlock-fir per operator direction. Type: task (feature work, multi-day, has UX + protocol + test scope).
notes: PR #391 open as of 2026-06-04. 30 commits, ~9010 LOC, full adrev disposition in spec §14 + post-impl Codex fixes (BLOCKER + 3 MAJORs). Awaiting operator review + merge. Issue stays in_progress until merge per ADR 0017. Follow-ups filed as tuxlink-y9go (deep backend.connect threading), tuxlink-pqbt (Tauri integration test), tuxlink-ryh0 (credential scope on Re-enter-password).
close_reason: Closed

title: docs: deepened Winlink Programs Group corpus (4,105 threads) — update research synthesis
description: PR #370's research note was synthesized from a 25-thread sample. A subsequent 4,105-thread scrape (2026-06-04, condor-hemlock-fir, hit Google's anti-automation gate at thread ~4112 with checkpointed corpus preserving everything before) provides much stronger evidence. Themes the original synthesis under-weighted: Forms (22% of corpus, 902 threads — promotes 'soft observation' to actionable), Install/setup (26.4%), Help/lost/new-user (20.8%). Themes confirmed structural rather than anecdotal: Password (618 threads, 15.1%), Callsign (536, 13.1%), Linux/Pi/Wine demand (100 threads with 23 explicit subject-line mentions — unmet-demand signal for tuxlink's positioning). Themes deprioritized vs Win-self-inflicted: version-archive complaints (mostly Win10 .NET breakage). title: tux-rig-watchdog: PR_SET_PDEATHSIG belt-and-suspenders parent-death detection
description:
Add Linux's PR_SET_PDEATHSIG so the watchdog receives SIGTERM automatically when its parent process dies, regardless of whether the parent dropped the stdin pipe cleanly.

Today's watchdog (PR #378, tuxlink-23ps) relies on stdin EOF detection: when the parent dies, the OS closes the pipe → watchdog sees EOF → releases PTT. That works for ALL parent-death cases including SIGKILL. PR_SET_PDEATHSIG is a second independent path that fires SIGTERM at the kernel layer — useful if a future child somehow inherits a different stdin source (e.g. parent dup2's something onto fd 0 before spawning).

Scope:
- In tux-rig-watchdog's main, immediately after parsing args (before opening tty):
  - On Linux: prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG, SIGTERM)
  - On non-Linux: no-op
- Already-installed SIGTERM handler turns this into the existing 'release PTT and exit' path
- No CLI change; this is purely an additional internal safety primitive

Test coverage:
- Build still succeeds on Linux (the prctl call should compile)
- Existing watchdog tests still pass
- A manual smoke would require spawning the watchdog from a parent that gets killed; documented in PR body but not in unit tests

Out of scope:
- Heartbeat protocol on stdin (independent feature)
- Watchdog clustering (one-per-rig)

Depends on: PR #378 / tuxlink-23ps (watchdog primitive) MERGED. Independent of PR #382 (tuxmodem-tx --watchdog integration).

title: scripts/tuxmodem-loopback-smoke.sh: agent-runnable loopback validation for the tuxmodem program
description:
Add an operator/dev shell script under scripts/ that exercises the agent-runnable CLI loopback for the tuxmodem program. Validates the encode/decode pipeline for all three frame modes (raw, sync, multi-sync) end-to-end without RADIO-1 risk.

Behavior:
- Cargo-builds tuxmodem-tx + tuxmodem-rx
- For each (frame_mode, payload) test case:
  - Generates a payload (raw: 5 bytes 'TEST!', sync: 5 bytes 'SYNC!', multi-sync: 200 bytes pseudorandom)
  - Calls tuxmodem-tx --write-wav with the appropriate --frame-mode
  - Calls tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav with --expected and matching --frame-mode
  - Asserts CLEAN MATCH in the output
- Reports PASS/FAIL per case and overall

Operator usage:
  bash scripts/tuxmodem-loopback-smoke.sh

Exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any-fail.

Out of scope:
- Real on-air testing (RADIO-1)
- BER vs SNR sweeps (Phase 11)
- AWGN injection (would require channel-sim integration)

Independent of all in-flight PRs (after PR #377 merges, multi-sync is on main; this script depends on that being landed).

title: Relicense Tuxlink MIT → GPL-3.0-or-later
description:
Operator decision 2026-06-04: MIT was too permissive. Relicensing forward as sole author (verified via git log --all --format='%an' | sort -u — only Cameron + automated bots). GPL-3.0-or-later chosen over AGPL-3.0 because Tuxlink is a desktop app the operator runs locally, not a SaaS endpoint (Tuxmodem's AGPL3 fits because the modem is a network endpoint other apps connect to; Tuxlink is the opposite shape).

Scope:
- LICENSE: replace MIT with GPL-3.0 verbatim (from /usr/share/common-licenses/GPL-3)
- src-tauri/Cargo.toml: add license = 'GPL-3.0-or-later'
- package.json: add license = 'GPL-3.0-or-later'
- src-tauri/tauri.conf.json: add bundle.licenseFile so .deb/.rpm/AppImage ship the license
- README.md:12: flip badge from MIT to GPL3
- Sanity: cargo license check for GPL2-only or AGPL outliers

Does NOT affect past releases (v0.0.1 through v0.27.0 remain under MIT in perpetuity). Forward versions (v0.28.0+) are GPL-3.0-or-later.

Vendored wl2k-go fixture (src-tauri/tests/fixtures/wl2k-go/) keeps its MIT attribution via the bundled LICENSE-wl2k-go.txt — MIT incorporates cleanly into GPL3 with preserved attribution.

title: WebviewFormHost pre-PR polish: tauri://error listener + ResizeObserver coalescing + comment fix
description:
3 'Important' findings from code-quality review on Task 9 (WebviewFormHost, commits 40a5a2a + a2b34a8 on bd-tuxlink-tzr5/forms-alpha-p1-frontend). None block ship but all should land before opening the P1 frontend PR (Task 13).

## Findings to address

### I1 — silent webview-creation failure
File: src/compose/WebviewFormHost.tsx:170-211
setStatus('open') runs unconditionally after the synchronous Webview constructor returns, with no listener for tauri://error. If Rust-side webview creation fails (OOM, capability mismatch, host crash), operator sees 'open' state but no form. Fix: attach webview.once('tauri://error', ...) immediately after construction. Optionally gate setStatus('open') behind webview.once('tauri://created', ...).

### I2 — ResizeObserver comment misrepresentation
File: src/compose/WebviewFormHost.tsx:201-206
Comment claims observing document.body catches 'global layout reflows that change the placeholder's POSITION (but not its size)' — but ResizeObserver on body only fires when body's own bbox changes. Sibling-element collapses that shift the placeholder down WITHOUT resizing the body leave the webview at stale coords. Fix options: (a) tighten the comment to accept the limitation, (b) add a MutationObserver or RAF poll fallback. Comment fix is the minimal correct move.

### I3 — setPosition/setSize IPC storm on resize
File: src/compose/WebviewFormHost.tsx:197-198
Each ResizeObserver tick fires both setPosition + setSize as separate Tauri IPC round-trips. Window-drag-resize generates many ticks/sec — Pi host risk: visible flicker, queue backup. Fix: wrap in requestAnimationFrame coalescer (capture latest rect, fire once per animation frame). Pattern mirrors useModemIsActive dedup at src/modem/useModemStatus.ts:62-64.

## Minor items (deferred — not in scope)
M4 activeToken set order, M5 useCallback caller-discipline doc, M6 aria-describedby for fallback button, M7 getCurrentWindow synchronous-API doc, M8 no cleanup-path test. Address inline if natural during P1 polish; otherwise leave.

## When to land
Before opening the P1 frontend PR (Task 13). Single polish commit on bd-tuxlink-tzr5/forms-alpha-p1-frontend.

title: Wizard: distinguish callsign-validation failure modes (FCC ULS, Winlink registration, reciprocal-ops)
description: Surfaced by Winlink Programs Group user-pain research (tuxlink-yzn6, 2026-06-04). High-engagement complaint on the group: operators with FCC-valid callsigns get opaque 'not valid' rejections from WLE; they can't tell whether the failure is format / FCC ULS / Winlink registration / reciprocal-ops. Tuxlink's wizard should distinguish: (1) format-invalid (not a callsign-shape string), (2) FCC ULS not found (US callsign not in public DB; could be very-new license that hasn't propagated), (3) Winlink not registered (callsign is real but operator hasn't completed the Winlink-side web registration), (4) reciprocal mismatch (foreign callsign without US reciprocal privileges). Each gets a distinct error string + a distinct recovery action. See dev/research/2026-06-04-winlink-group-pain-points.md theme #2.

title: tuxmodem-tx: --write-wav PATH (encode to file instead of soundcard)
description:
Small follow-up to tuxlink-i3bz (PR #366) that closes the fully-agent-runnable loopback test plan with tuxlink-xvrb (PR #367 / tuxmodem-rx).

Add a --write-wav <PATH> mode to tuxmodem-tx that emits the encoded waveform to a 48 kHz f32 mono WAV file via AudioBuffer::write_wav, INSTEAD of opening an audio device + asserting PTT. Pure file I/O — no audio device, no PTT, no RF risk.

Why: today the operator's only path to validate the TX/RX pair end-to-end is to physically loopback the radio's audio via a cable AND verify with --record-wav. With --write-wav, the agent (and operator) can run:

  tuxmodem-tx --write-wav /tmp/out.wav --payload 'TEST' --mode wide-floor
  tuxmodem-rx --decode-wav /tmp/out.wav --expected 'TEST'

End-to-end smoke without touching radio or PTT. Proves the in-memory roundtrip the lib tests already cover, but at the CLI level.

Args added:
- --write-wav <PATH>  (mutually exclusive with the soundcard-output mode + --dry-run)

Existing budget/airtime gate still applies (the buffer duration is reported even though no PTT asserts). Symbol-only assumption (Phase 12+ caveat) applies the same way to the WAV output as to soundcard output.

Out of scope:
- WAV INPUT to tuxmodem-tx (--read-wav). The encode path is what tuxmodem-tx does; reading wav doesn't make sense there.
- Multi-symbol framing (PHY Phase 10).

Depends on: tuxlink-i3bz / PR #366 (tuxmodem-tx must exist as a crate first). Stacked PR.
