title: d3zwe/Elmer runner: make agent Limits configurable (per-turn timeout, max turns)
description: d3zwe hardcodes Limits::default() (per_turn_timeout 120s). Too tight for slow local hardware (gpt-oss:20b on N305 blew the 120s first-turn budget). Local edit on r2-poe added env overrides D3ZWE_TURN_TIMEOUT_SECS / D3ZWE_MAX_TURNS in src-tauri/d3zwe/src/main.rs. Fold a clean version into the Elmer in-app runner's Limits config (operator-tunable). Not yet committed to repo.

title: [flaky-CI] RigControlSection.test.tsx 'renders detected serial ports in the CAT-port picker' flakes on amd64 verify
description: Confirmed flaky 2026-06-28 on PR #943 (a pure npm-dep-bump diff): the test failed once on verify(ubuntu-latest,amd64) with a single 1/294 failure, then PASSED on an unchanged re-run; arm64 verify + local (arm64) both green throughout. No relation to the diff (dep bumps only). Same class as tuxlink-9xqz (AprsSettings arm64 flake). Likely an async/waitFor race in the serial-port-detection render path. Stabilize the waitFor / mock timing.

title: chore(deps): upstream-pinned dependabot deferrals (rmcp 0.8->1.4 migration, glib 0.20, rand 0.7 parent)
description:
Deferred from the 2026-06-28 dependabot triage (PR #943 shipped the npm bumps + dismissed rmcp as not-exposed). These need upstream/parent moves, not a lock bump:
- rmcp 0.8.5 -> 1.4.0: clears the (already-dismissed-as-not-exposed) DNS-rebinding alert + future-proofs if an HTTP transport is ever added. MAJOR API migration touching tuxlink-mcp-core (server) + d3zwe (client over UDS); re-verify arm/taint + the UDS handshake after. Not urgent (we don't run the vulnerable transport).
- glib 0.18 -> 0.20 (MEDIUM, VariantStrIter unsoundness): pinned ^0.18 by the Tauri/gtk-rs/webkit2gtk stack; cargo update refuses 0.20. Pick up when Tauri adopts gtk-rs glib 0.20. VariantStrIter not used directly by Tuxlink.
- rand 0.7.3 (LOW): pinned ^0.7 by a transitive parent (0.8.6 already coexists for newer consumers). Identify + bump the parent crate, or dismiss not_used (the rng()+custom-logger path is unused).

title: FEATURE (brainstorm): Elmer — in-app model assistant pane (MCP-driven, local-first)
description: Office-hours brainstorm 2026-06-27. In-app right-hand assistant pane ('Elmer', the ham term for a mentor) that drives a local/cloud model against Tuxlink's existing MCP tools, so a stuck operator gets plain-language diagnose/lookup/compose/operate without a separate terminal/Claude Code. Architecture: VS Code's agent-pane shape (native Rust agent-loop + pluggable model-provider seam + MCP tools, arm/taint enforced at tool execution) but local-first by default (offline/contingency). One layered surface for field operator (plain default) / power op (progressive disclosure) / tinkerer (BYO endpoint). 'Copilot' rejected (MS/GitHub trademark). Substrate already exists (TCP-to-local-service pattern + shipped MCP + arm/taint). Design doc: dev/scratch/elmer-design.md (+ gstack copy); mock: dev/scratch/copilot-pane-mock.html. NOT greenlit to build; gated on tuxlink-lzqz8 (real gpt-oss-20b over live MCP) validating the local-model field-operator experience first.
notes: MERGED to main via PR #950 (2026-06-29, redwood-falcon-bluff). All 13 tasks (executor, M2 approval, ElmerSession, provider, Tauri commands, React pane) + CI green both arches (2661 tests). R2 build confirmed operational pre-merge. Operator merging to compile+test live on R2 off main. REMAINING: live R2 validation + wire-walk gate (5 flows: advisory/taint-rearm/stage-approval-send/stop/offline) before closing. Default Elmer model=llama3 (set config.elmer.agent_model to a pulled model e.g. gpt-oss:20b). Known follow-ups: token-before-spawn race in cancel_and_abort (bounded/secure); flush-by-MID hardening (v1=whole-set+freeze+re-digest); staged_by provenance omitted v1.

title: MCP rig_status: safe live VFO readout (no ungated rigctld spawn)
description: rig_status (tuxlink-wxwlr) reports config-only; the live VFO/mode/PTT readout was removed because a transient rigctld spawn opens an unauthenticated command-capable CAT server from a read-only un-gated path (Codex P1, 2026-06-27). Add a SAFE live readout: either (a) read the last-known VFO from an ALREADY-running session's rig / the live-VFO poll (no new server), or (b) bind rigctld to authenticated loopback / behind the egress gate. Until then rig_status's vfo_hz/mode/ptt are null.

title: Emit a user-visible line on bootstrap AutoAuth::Unavailable (genuine auto-unlock failure)
description: Proper-defensive-UX replacement for the abandoned locked-chip (tuxlink-xzidc / closed PR #928). When launch auto-auth genuinely cannot establish the active identity (AutoAuth::Unavailable — no/mismatched stored credential), bootstrap.rs currently ONLY does tracing::warn! — unlike the Healed/HealFailed arms which call emit_backend_line (user-visible). So a genuinely-locked identity is silent to the operator. Fix: add emit_backend_line on the Unavailable arm with an actionable message ('<callsign> could not be unlocked automatically — re-enter your password in Settings -> Winlink Account, or unlock from the callsign menu'). This is a RELIABLE backend signal (no frontend race), unlike inferring 'locked' from identity_active (which is fetched once with no auto-auth-settled refetch -> startup-race false positives — the reason the chip approach was dropped). Small, no false-positive surface. NOTE: do NOT auto-heal a MISMATCH (only missing is safe to heal — a stale CMS password must not overwrite a good activation secret).

title: Re-add gateway last-heard as parsed last_update_ms (structured) to find_stations
description: From the MCP station-intel handoff (maple-sumac-larch) + confirmed during cold acceptance (cedar-magnolia-crag): station-intel dropped the free-text 'last_update' field (injection surface). Re-add it as a PARSED last_update_ms: Option<u64> so the agent gets last-heard freshness as structured data for better gateway selection (currently find_stations gateways carry no recency signal). Small additive follow-up to the station-intel surface (tuxlink-cvx84.10).

title: Land stage_prediction_engine on main's converge-build.sh (dev predict_path on pristine-main builds)
description: Cold-acceptance finding (cedar-magnolia-crag, 2026-06-27, tuxlink-l9sq4): a pristine-main 'pnpm dev:converged' does NOT stage the offline-HF-prediction engine (voacapl binary + itshfbc coefficient tree), so MCP predict_path / Find-a-Station returns 'voacapl binary not found'. NOT an epic defect and NOT a packaged-build problem: release.yml stages both at bundle time (docs/reference/voacapl-ci-bundling.md), and the operator's LOCAL converge-build.sh already has a 'stage_prediction_engine' phase that works. The gap is only that that phase was never merged to main's scripts/converge-build.sh. Fix: port the operator's stage_prediction_engine step onto main's converge-build.sh so a clean-main dev converged build is self-sufficient (helps cold-acceptance agents + new contributors). Low priority — does not affect shipped artifacts or the operator's normal workflow.

title: Tuxlink MCP: extract core API + ship MCP server (first adapter)
description:
Office-hours design APPROVED 2026-06-25 (agent bluff-maple-birch). Design doc: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-ant8s-ardop-connect-fixes-design-20260625-211105.md

Reframe: deliverable is a transport-agnostic core API (ports-and-adapters); MCP is its first non-GUI adapter. Approach A (embedded in running app), local-only transport (stdio/UDS, no TCP). Security led by appsec (cred non-exposure, input validation, least-privilege, redaction-at-MCP-sink); armed-grant transmit gate is secondary Part-97 layer. Consent = operator-present delegated send authority w/ auto-expiry (NOT per-invocation).

GATING FIRST ACTION: 30-min client-compat spike (one-tool rmcp server -> Claude Desktop + Claude Code over stdio + UDS) to de-risk the Rust-MCP/Claude-Desktop disconnect bug and settle transport + shim language.

Key repo fact for build: transmit gating is ARDOP-only today; cms/packet/message_send/send_form are ungated; existing consent token is single-use (must be REPLACED w/ armed many-send grant, not reused). Unified gate = net-new across modem_commands/ui_commands/winlink_backend = biggest chunk.

Open Qs: least-privilege tool cut; transport mechanics; session_log redaction verify; relay-case shape; cross-platform; concurrent-caller (default single-caller). Next: scoping pass or /plan-eng-review before build.
notes:
EPIC CODE-COMPLETE 2026-06-27 (maple-sumac-larch): all 6 phases (3.1-3.6) MERGED to main. 3.1 transport spine (rmcp-over-UDS + tuxlink-mcp shim + server_info); 3.2 Tier-1 reads (ports-and-adapters, redaction-at-sink, taint); 3.3 egress gate (guarded_egress, poison-fail-closed, Operator/Agent, abort); 3.4 Tier-2 writes (armed+taint, validators) + Tier-3 compose; 3.5 knowledge (16 resources + 3 prompts, static-embed); 3.6 operator arm UI. Crates: tuxlink-security, tuxlink-mcp-core, tuxlink-mcp (shim), tuxlink-mcp-testserver + monolith mcp_ports.rs + React arm surface. Tiers 1+2 GREEN across all phases (CI + agent-runnable claude-mcp round-trips: server_info, mailbox read+4char grid+taint, egress unarmed-denied/armed-allowed/tainted-denied, drive-write+traversal-rejected+compose-staged, resources list+read+answer). 4 Codex adrev rounds (3.1 transport, 3.3 gate, 3.4 validators, + 3.3 surface) — all findings remediated; packet_listen/telnet_p2p DEFERRED to cvx84.7 (can't honor gate/abort semantics without deeper backend work). Reachability trace: all 5 wire-walk flows trace to file:line on main. REMAINING GATES (operator/external): (a) tier-3 full-app operator smoke; (b) AGPLv3 relicense tuxlink-tm0cp before ANY network-exposable packaging (local UDS/stdio build is fine as-is); (c) operator greenfield wire-walk flow confirmation (the 5 flows were agent-designed/operator-delegated, not greenfield).

title: Tuxlink MCP — expose Tuxlink capabilities as an MCP server
description: Operator epiphany 2026-06-25 (captured, NOT yet specced — operator to flesh out the vision). Idea: expose Tuxlink's Winlink/radio capabilities (e.g. compose/send/receive B2F mail, connect to RMS gateways over ARDOP/VARA/telnet, query the RMS list, station/modem status) as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, so AI agents can drive Tuxlink as a tool — AI-assisted EmComm / off-grid radio email. Scope, surface, auth/RADIO-1 safety boundaries, and which capabilities to expose are ALL pending operator definition. Placeholder to not lose the idea; needs office-hours/brainstorm before any build.

title: ARDOP on-air: FT-710 USB audio (C-Media) resets on TX = RFI; ardopcf can't recover mid-connect (NOT a tuxlink defect)
description: 2026-06-23 FT-710 on-air bring-up. Symptom: ARDOP connect keys the radio once then dies; no-PTT configs do a clean 3x. Root cause from captured ardopcf stdout + dmesg: on the first TX the FT-710's internal USB audio device (C-Media 0d8c:0013 at usb 3-2.2) RESETS ('usb 3-2.2: reset full-speed USB device'; ardopcf logs 'ALSA Error pcm_hw.c:148 sync_ptr1(): No such device' -> 'Unable to recover' -> RXEnabled/TXEnabled false -> RemodulateLastFrame ignored -> CONNECT FAILED). i.e. RF from the radio's own transmission knocks its USB audio off the bus. tuxlink + ardopcf behave correctly; init/keying/TX-frame all work. This is a station/RFI hardware issue (chokes/ferrites on USB, SWR/grounding, lower power, powered hub) and would affect any digital app identically. NO tuxlink code change required for the root cause. POSSIBLE resilience enhancement (low priority, likely upstream ardopcf): retry-with-backoff to reopen the audio device after a transient USB reset so a brief glitch doesn't kill an ARQ session. Diagnostic wrapper used: dev/scratch/ardopcf-logged (captures the stdout tuxlink discards -> see also the P1 observability issue to capture ardopcf output in-app). Vindicates the whole ARDOP-as-shipped chain: 0.75.2 bundles a working ardopcf (CODEC+webgui+ptt), init verified, modem drives the radio; only the RF/USB hardware interaction blocks the QSO.

title: pbf 5.x bump blocked: re-vendor protomaps-leaflet for pbf's ESM named export (or pin pbf<5)
description: Dependabot PR #875 (pbf 4.0.2 -> 5.1.0) fails vite build: pbf 5.0 dropped the default export (ESM migration). The vendored minified bundle src/vendor/protomaps-leaflet/index.js does 'import Pbf from pbf' (default), now unresolved -> 'default is not exported by pbf@5.1.0'. pbf is consumed ONLY by that vendored bundle. Fix: re-vendor protomaps-leaflet built against pbf 5 (named export 'import { Pbf } from pbf') and verify the basemap still renders (WebKitGTK/grim), OR add a dependabot ignore for pbf major (5.x) and stay on 4.x. Do NOT hand-edit the minified vendored bundle. The other 11 dependabot bumps (868-879 minus 875) merged clean 2026-06-22.

title: Winlink map: source gateway grid at connect-time (ribbon raw-dial) + Packet live-arc peer
description: Wire-walk follow-up from tuxlink-s1o1 (merged PR #885, agent jay-pine-butte 2026-06-22). The Winlink map plots a called gateway's diamond from favorite.grid (store.rs recent_gateways), which the Station Finder populates from the catalog on save/use. GAP 1: connectDispatch.ts:53 builds the status-bar ribbon dial as {mode, gateway} with NO grid, so calling a gateway that was never saved/used-from-Finder records grid:None -> no diamond. Fix: look up the catalog grid (callsign->grid, the StationFinder catalog already has it) when recording a ribbon dial, OR backfill favorite.grid from the catalog in recent_gateways. The design decision-1 said 'callsign->catalog grid'; the impl used favorite.grid — this closes that intent gap. GAP 2: the live arc reads modem:status.peer, set by modem_ardop_connect (ARDOP). Confirm/extend the AX.25/uvpro (Packet) status path sets peer so the Packet live arc renders (diamond already works for Packet). VARA arc is separately tracked in tuxlink-5q31.

title: Winlink map: VARA live-link animation (needs VARA ModemTransport impl)
description: Deferred from tuxlink-s1o1. The live link animation is ARDOP-only for v1 because VaraTransport (src-tauri/src/winlink/modem/vara/transport.rs) is a bare TCP struct with no ModemTransport trait impl — so no modem:status telemetry drives a VARA connection. VARA gateways still appear as history diamonds, but the arc does not animate. Blocked on a VARA ModemTransport implementation surfacing state/bytes/throughput on the modem:status event. See docs/design/2026-06-22-winlink-map-layer-design.md.

title: Winlink map Tier 2: real ack/retry animation via backend ARQ frame-event channel
description: Deferred from tuxlink-s1o1 (truthful-now grammar shipped without ack/retry). To animate real ACK/NAK/retry on the Winlink link arc, the ARDOP transport must expose per-frame events to the frontend (new Tauri event channel + transport-level frame-type/retry emission). SPIKE FIRST: can ardopcf's cmd-socket cleanly yield ack/nak/retry/frame-type? The TNC frame stream currently stays inside Rust (src-tauri/.../ardop/transport.rs); only aggregate arq_flags + ping-derived quality reach the frontend. If the spike fails, do not promise ack/retry. See docs/design/2026-06-22-winlink-map-layer-design.md.

Live theater map: where normal FT8 activity (=> infra/power/internet alive) persists, disaster extent inferred from FT8 silence vs per-grid baseline priors (CONFOUNDED by MUF/time-of-day/rural sparsity — validate the silence inference before building UI on it), reachable+likely-uplinked gateways as a map layer. The MAP READOUT/UX IS ITS OWN DEDICATED DESIGN BRAINSTORM (gold-outline encoding is a seed, not the spec) — run it before building. WSPR as a complementary lower-density beacon.

Per alpha bar, build fully (setting UI + persisted store + applied to AprsPositionsMap STALE_MS and useAprsPositions POSITION_TTL_MS), not a half-slice.

title: Lengthen APRS map stale grey-out (~1-2h) and position TTL

Currently STALE_MS=15min (grey) in AprsPositionsMap.tsx and POSITION_TTL_MS=60min (drop) in useAprsPositions.ts. Bump STALE_MS to ~90min and POSITION_TTL_MS to a longer value (>stale, e.g. ~3h). Trivial constant change; confirm exact values with operator. Separate follow-up off merged tuxlink-qnu6 (PR #849).

title: [bug] P3: window set_title rejected by ACL — repeated unhandledrejection spam in frontend logs
description: Today's logs (tuxlink.2026-06-19-*.jsonl) show repeated: 'frontend error: Command plugin:window|set_title not allowed by ACL' (source: unhandledrejection), and the older variant 'window.set_title not allowed. Permissions: core:window:allow-set-title'. The frontend calls window setTitle but the Tauri capabilities/ACL doesn't allow core:window:allow-set-title, so each call rejects unhandled and spams the structured log as error-level. Fix: either add core:window:allow-set-title to the window capability set, or stop calling setTitle / guard it. Low severity (no functional break) but it pollutes the error log and could mask real frontend errors.

Future pass: evaluate advanced frontend skill packs / frameworks / motion+interaction techniques to make the site astounding (e.g. richer hero motion, scroll-driven storytelling, View Transitions, frontend-design skill). Deferred by operator 2026-06-18 ('needs to exist' first). Elevation, not a rewrite.

title: [SECURITY P3] forms_export_pdf / logging_export / report_issue_flow write to renderer-supplied output paths (backend should own the dialog)
description: Sibling pattern to tuxlink-hyfo, surfaced by Codex during the hyfo adrev (2026-06-18). Three Tauri commands still accept a renderer-supplied destination path and fs::write/build_archive to it: forms_export_pdf (outPath), logging_export (output_path), report_issue_flow (output_path). Same untrusted-renderer class as hyfo, but LOWER severity: the written bytes are the user's OWN logs/forms/PDF (not attacker-influenced received-message bytes), so the blast radius is redirect/overwrite of the user's own export rather than writing attacker bytes to an arbitrary path. Defense-in-depth fix: mirror hyfo — move each save dialog into the backend command (Rust tauri-plugin-dialog blocking_save_file off the async runtime) so no renderer path reaches the write. Not exploitable without a separate webview compromise.

title: Flaky test: managed_direwolf spawn_device_busy_short_circuits hardcodes TEST_KISS_PORT_BUSY (port-bind race)
description: winlink::ax25::managed_direwolf::tests::spawn_device_busy_short_circuits_without_spawning asserts at managed_direwolf.rs:875 that a HARDCODED TCP port (TEST_KISS_PORT_BUSY) is still bindable to prove nothing was spawned. Under cargo's parallel test execution the port can be transiently held by another process/test, so bind() fails and the test flakes — unrelated to the code under test. Observed failing on PR #723 (reqwest bump, otherwise unrelated). Fix: bind an ephemeral port (:0) and read back the assigned port, or otherwise remove the hardcoded-port dependency, so the assertion can't lose a port race.

Once configured the operator rarely revisits them. Wrap the config group in a collapsible <details> (mirror VaraRadioPanel's .expander pattern); default-open when audio devices aren't both set, collapsed once configured. Pre-redesign quick win, separate from the larger ARDOP UI redesign.

title: Dependabot: ignore libheif-rs major bumps (needs system libheif >=1.21, absent on Ubuntu 24.04)
description: Dependabot proposes un-buildable libheif-rs major bumps (PR 727: 1.0.2 to 2.7.0). libheif-rs 2.x pulls libheif-sys 5.x which requires SYSTEM libheif >=1.21; Ubuntu 24.04 (CI/build/target) ships 1.17.6 so the libheif-sys build script fails on every job. Cargo.toml already pins libheif-rs to =1.0.2 and documents this. Add a cargo-ecosystem dependabot ignore for semver-major on libheif-rs + libheif-sys, mirroring the npm vitest ignore.
notes: PR #729 (base main). Adds cargo dependabot ignore for libheif-rs/libheif-sys semver-major (mirrors npm vitest ignore). Root cause of #727's all-CI-fail: libheif-sys 5.x needs system libheif>=1.21, Ubuntu 24.04 ships 1.17.6. Once #729 merges, dependabot auto-closes #727.

title: Dependabot: ignore libheif-rs/libheif-sys major bumps (system libheif < 1.21 on Ubuntu 24.04)
description: Dependabot repeatedly proposes un-buildable libheif-rs major bumps (e.g. PR #727: 1.0.2->2.7.0). libheif-rs 2.x pulls libheif-sys 5.x, which pkg-configs against the SYSTEM libheif and requires >=1.21; Ubuntu 24.04 (CI/build/target) ships 1.17.6, so the libheif-sys build script fails on EVERY job. src-tauri/Cargo.toml already pins libheif-rs="=1.0.2" + documents this. Add a cargo-ecosystem ignore rule for semver-major on libheif-rs + libheif-sys, mirroring the existing npm vitest ignore. Remove if the build/runtime baseline ever moves to libheif>=1.21.

title: 45 frontend .catch(()=>{}) swallows hide failures from logs (unhandledrejection can't see caught rejections)
description: Observability follow-up (deferred behind tuxlink-4b96). 45 .catch(()=>{}) sites swallow promise rejections entirely — the global unhandledrejection forwarder (tuxlink-4b96) cannot see them because they're caught. Most are intentional best-effort (window positioning, cleanup) and fine; a few may hide real failures. Audit each: where a swallow could mask a meaningful failure, replace catch(()=>{}) with catch((e)=>reportFrontendError('<site>', ...)). Low priority; selective.

title: Map P3 polish (post-ndi4): overview maxzoom bleed, resolve_sidecar PATH-fallback, missing-sidecar UX, first-paint flash, manifest-load-error UX
description: Grab-bag of P3 vector-basemap polish surfaced during the ndi4 map-crisis session (gully-kingfisher-bayou handoff 2026-06-14). NOTE: this was described as 'filed' in that handoff but no bd issue actually existed — filing it now (spruce-finch-gorge) so it isn't lost. Items: (1) overview maxzoom translucent-bleed — the unclamped world overview can bleed translucently above z6 at pack edges; (2) resolve_sidecar PATH-fallback hardening — commands.rs resolve_sidecar() falls back to bare 'pmtiles' on PATH when the bundled externalBin sibling is absent, which in a packaged build yields an opaque spawn failure (cf. the Exec-not-on-PATH .desktop lesson); (3) missing-sidecar UX — surface a clear actionable message instead of a raw string error when the go-pmtiles sidecar can't be resolved/spawned; (4) first-paint flash — brief flash/empty frame before the basemap style loads; (5) manifest-load-error UX — friendlier surface when basemap_refresh_manifest fails (currently keeps the cached manifest silently). BUILD-GATED: items 1/4 are visual (verify via grim on WebKitGTK, NOT chromium); items 2/3 need the packaged-build/sidecar context to observe the real failure; item 5 needs a UX decision. Best done in a session with the operator's converge build available to observe.

title: ATAK-over-APRS/UV-Pro open-source transport plugin feasibility (+ multi-network EmComm thesis)
description:
THESIS (deferred, NOT alpha scope — see dev/scratch/2026-06-13-tuxlink-multi-network-emcomm-thesis.md on pandora): Tuxlink as a multi-network EmComm platform bridging APRS + Meshtastic/MeshCore + Reticulum. Three strategies: (1) separate native channels in tac chat [trivial first slice, builds transport I/O]; (2) semantic cross-ecosystem bridging [the trap — identity/consent/LCD hell, skip]; (3) networks as DUMB TRANSPORT for a Tuxlink overlay ('speak Tuxlink', send bytes) = the destination, instantiates the clean-sheet bearer-agnostic substrate, ATAK-precedented. Roadmap: 1 -> open ATAK plugin -> 3; skip 2.

NEAR-TERM CONCRETE ACTION (research started 2026-06-14): feasibility of building + open-sourcing an ATAK-over-APRS/UV-Pro transport plugin (kills a predatory ~$200/license market; scope-SAFE = separate Android/ATAK-CIV SDK codebase, does NOT touch the tuxlink alpha; on-ramp to strategy 3 because CoT-over-RF == Tuxlink-over-RF). Research questions: (a) ATAK-CIV plugin SDK status/license; (b) enumerate existing open vs closed APRS/Meshtastic ATAK transport plugins; (c) pin down the specific $200 product(s) + what they actually do; (d) confirm UV-Pro data path (tuxlink already does native UV-Pro control). Deliverable: go/no-go + parts list. Codex flagged scope creep (fair); resolution = thesis != feature, finish the Winlink-on-Linux wedge first.
notes: SNIFF-TEST FLAG (operator, 2026-06-14): the prior research leaned on vendor self-description — re-verify before trusting. GoTAK 'MYCELIUM proprietary self-healing mesh' is UNVERIFIED vendor marketing and likely rebranded AX.25 digipeating, not novel routing; verify what it actually transmits on-air next session. The skip-the-plugin conclusion does NOT depend on that claim (it stands on Android-vs-Linux platform mismatch + BTECH-Relay already existing). If GoTAK's mesh is just digipeating, the predatory-thin-wrapper framing revives (academic — BTECH-Relay already undercuts free). Also confirm BTECH UV-Pro 'native KISS in fw 0.7.11+' on real hardware before relying on it for tuxlink's own APRS-over-UV-Pro path. See thesis doc RESOLVED + SNIFF-TEST blocks.

title: ndi4 phase-4 self-adrev P2 follow-ups (compositing polish + sidecar UX)
description: Deferred P2 findings from the 2026-06-13 phase-4 self-adrev (P0 + P1 fixed in PR #688): (1) overview maxzoom not clamped → coarse overzoomed overview can bleed under translucent pack layers above z6 (cosmetic; opaque earth/landcover cover most); evaluate clamping overview detail layers to maxzoom 6 while keeping its background global. (2) resolve_sidecar falls back to bare 'pmtiles' on PATH even in a packaged build → binary-plant risk + confusing UX; gate the PATH fallback to dev only, return a purpose-built error in packaged builds. (3) missing-sidecar UX: a local tauri build (no CI jq) ships without the sidecar → download fails with a raw spawn errno string; detect up front + surface 'map downloader not included in this build'. (4) MapLibreMap first-paint flash: overview-only construct then immediate setStyle when packs already installed (cosmetic, WebKitGTK). (5) OfflineMapsSettings: a manifest-load failure is invisible (only list failures show an error); add a manifest-load error/loading state. (6) sweep_orphans deletes any *.part with no guard against an active writer — currently startup-only (safe); document the contract or guard if more callers are added. Source: dev/adversarial (3-lens self-adrev), PR #688 body.

title: Flaky test: GpsSourcePicker 'rescans on demand' (waitFor timeout, amd64)
description: src/location/GpsSourcePicker.test.tsx > 'rescans on demand' intermittently fails on contended runners: 'AssertionError: expected 1 to be greater than 1' via a waitFor timeout (Timeout.checkRealTimersCallback, ~1012ms). Observed on CI run 27466554606 (verify ubuntu-latest/amd64) while arm64 + a prior amd64 run on the same code passed (2681/2682). Timing race — the on-demand rescan's 2nd invocation isn't observed within the waitFor window on a slow runner. Likely fix: assert on the rescan trigger (e.g. mock call count via waitFor on the mock, or fake timers) rather than a real-timer count>1. Blocks unrelated PRs' CI (hit during tuxlink-vgvn SBC codec). Not the codec's code — GPS work stream (cf tuxlink-yy1m).

title: 'Tuxnet' digital image/data chat mode (Tuxlink-to-Tuxlink premium) — DEFERRED on clean-room modem
description:
The premium counterpart to SSTV: rich messaging (images + data) carried as a FILE over tuxlink's clean-room FM data modem (v0.5+), with FEC + ARQ — perfect-image-or-retransmit instead of SSTV's analog smear, with a tunable speed/quality tradeoff. Same ~2.5kHz FM voice channel ceiling as SSTV, so the win is RESILIENCE + control of the tradeoff, NOT raw speed (channel-bounded). Rides the same TX_AUDIO/RX_AUDIO transport (mode-agnostic PCM) as SSTV — different waveform, not different pipe.

SCOPE INSIGHT (operator, 2026-06-13): this is a TUXNET thing — interop between capable Tuxlink stations ONLY (proprietary waveform, point-to-point ARQ), vs SSTV's broadcast interop with any ham/app. And it is NOT a per-message toggle: you cannot/should-not switch between APRS-1200-packet and the modem mode mid-stream — you OPERATE in the modem mode. So it becomes a whole GLOBAL OPERATING MODE for chat. UI challenge: handle that global-mode selection elegantly (how chat + control + the rest of the app present when the station is in Tuxnet modem mode). Layers above SSTV like APRS-native premium layered above the KISS floor.

BLOCKED: the clean-room modem does not exist yet (v0.5+ design posture). DEFER. Operator sleeping on the design. Do not start until the modem is real. Clean-sheet rule applies (concepts only; no VARA/digital-SSTV internals).

Related: SSTV interop floor ships first (separate issue). Design context: ~/.gstack/projects/cameronzucker-tuxlink/administrator-bd-tuxlink-xygm-recover-handoffs-design-20260613-014520.md

title: Native APRS stop->restart without reconnect errors 'already listening' (take_aprs_receiver single-take)
description: In the shipped native path (tuxlink-7my9/ve3j), AprsState::start_native takes the session's inbound receiver once per connection (UvproSession::take_aprs_receiver). After aprs_listen_stop aborts the native driver, the receiver is dropped; a subsequent aprs_listen_start WITHOUT reconnecting the UV-Pro gets None and surfaces the misleading 'native APRS is already listening on the UV-Pro' (operator is NOT listening — they stopped). Recovery exists (disconnect+reconnect the control strip resets the channel), but it's clunky. FIX: make the receiver re-subscribable — a UvproSession::subscribe_aprs that installs a fresh mpsc channel into the driver (via with_driver->set_aprs_sender) each call, replacing the single-take take_aprs_receiver; remove the now-vestigial connect-time aprs_rx field/setup. Minor edge (always-live model rarely stops); backend change, Mode A (CI compiles).

title: Flaky test: GpsSourcePicker 'rescans on demand' (waitFor toBeGreaterThan times out under CI load)
description: src/location/GpsSourcePicker.test.tsx:103 — 'rescans on demand' intermittently fails on CI with 'expected 1 to be greater than 1' (the rescan call count hasn't incremented within the waitFor real-timer window). Async/timing flake unrelated to tile work; surfaced on a Rust-only push to #659 where the FE was unchanged and the prior CI run was green. Likely needs a more robust waitFor (or fake timers / explicit await on the rescan invocation). Low priority.

title: Flaky test: GpsSourcePicker 'rescans on demand' intermittently reds CI (expected 1 to be > 1)
description: src/location/GpsSourcePicker.test.tsx > 'rescans on demand' fails intermittently with AssertionError: expected 1 to be greater than 1 — a timing race where the async rescan call-count hasn't incremented when asserted. Cross-arch flake: passed arm64, failed amd64 on the same commit (PR #642 CI 2026-06-13); cleared on re-run. Unrelated to the changes it failed under. Fix: await the rescan / use findBy or waitFor on the call count instead of a bare synchronous assertion.

title: Evaluate agent GitHub-issue triage/response capability (operator-gated)

KEEP OPERATOR-GATED for now — agent drafts, operator approves/posts. Scope: a triage skill or a checklist; decide which actions are auto (relabel? draft comment?) vs always operator-confirmed (public replies, close). Capture the policy in CLAUDE.md once decided. Raised by operator 2026-06-12 alongside #648.

title: APRS messaging over the native UV-Pro link (HT_SEND_DATA), collapsing control+data onto one BT link
description: Parent-epic (tuxlink-2f2n) premium-tier thesis: route APRS message TX/RX over the native Benshi HT_SEND_DATA path (TncDataFragment) instead of KISS, so the UV-Pro carries control + chat over ONE Bluetooth link. Builds on the tuxlink-nx95 native control backend (RFCOMM+GAIA codec, UvproSession) + Phase 1a's APRS message format. Depends on Phase 1a (PR #642) landing. Larger than control: needs DATA_RXD event handling, TncDataFragment encode/decode + fragmentation (>50 bytes), and integration with the chat core. RADIO-1: this path TRANSMITS (operator-gated on-air).

title: UV-Pro link arbitration: KISS/packet path should consult the native owner-lock
description: Phase-2 limitation from tuxlink-nx95: the native UV-Pro control profile holds a process-global owner-lock (UvproLinkLock) so uvpro_connect fails fast with LinkBusy if the radio is held. But the Winlink packet / APRS-over-KISS path (KissLinkConfig::Bluetooth) does NOT yet consult that lock, so a conflict FROM the KISS direction (starting a packet session while native control holds the radio) surfaces as a raw RFCOMM socket error, not a clean LinkBusy. Teach the KISS connect path (winlink_backend / aprs listener) to acquire/check the shared UvproLinkLock so the arbitration is symmetric and the operator gets a clear 'radio in use by UV-Pro control' message either direction.

title: Packet panel: listener identity badge parity
description: Phase 7 (tuxlink-noa0) added per-listener identity badges to the shared ListenArmButton (ARDOP/VARA/Telnet-P2P panels). PacketRadioPanel uses a bespoke blocking-answer arm control with its own local armed state, not the shared ListenArmButton, so it carries no identity badge. Add parity if wanted: surface the captured listener identity on the packet answer control. Discovered during Phase 7 build (moraine-swallow-bayou, 2026-06-12). Low priority — packet answer is a different surface; not a defect.

title: APRS 1b: REJ delivery-state UX + reply-ack emit
description: Phase 1a parses+tolerates reply-ack and terminates retransmit on inbound REJ (emits Rejected). 1b: richer REJ UX, and consider emitting the modern reply-ack {NN}AA form (currently parse-tolerated, not emitted).

title: APRS 1b: listening-state indicator polish + BT host-handoff UX
description: Phase 1a has a Start/Stop toggle + a basic listening indicator. 1b: polish the listening/not-listening states across the BT host-handoff (UV-Pro single-RFCOMM-host disconnect/reconnect), with the BTECH-Pro-style disconnect affordance + tooltip per the design.

title: Managed packet: gate Connect on a configured managed link (UI divergence)
description: Self-adrev I1 (2026-06-12). PacketRadioPanel connectionMode is session-local + defaults to 'managed'; packet_connect acts on the PERSISTED linkKind, not the visible toggle. If the operator sees Managed UI but never picks a sound card, Connect uses the old persisted link (Tcp/Serial/None), not Managed — confusing divergence. Not a RADIO-1 hole (Dire Wolf only spawns when persisted link actually is ManagedDireWolf). Fix: gate Connect on config.linkKind==='Managed' when connectionMode==='managed', or surface 'pick a sound card to connect'.

title: P8: managed Dire Wolf pre-connect readiness chip (reframe from live-health)
description: Slice B P8, deferred from the managed-Dire-Wolf build (tuxlink-yq3l). The plan specced 'process up / KISS reachable / decode indicator', but that assumed a long-lived modem. Managed Dire Wolf is spawned per-connect and SIGINT-stopped at disconnect (P4/P6), so there is no idle process to poll. Reframe P8 as a PRE-CONNECT READINESS indicator shown when Managed mode is selected in the packet panel: a small status chip from a new packet_managed_readiness() command returning { direwolfInstalled (P3 direwolf_presence), version, deviceResolved (resolve_managed_device vs read_sys_snapshot), deviceBusy (probe_device_busy) }. Tells the operator they are ready to connect before they try. During-connect liveness is already surfaced by the existing progress lines ('Starting managed Dire Wolf…') + named errors (P6). Optional polish; the feature is complete + operator-reachable without it. Consider a quick operator confirm on whether the readiness chip is wanted vs leaving the progress-line + named-error surface as-is.

title: Weather geometry: dissolve same-product zones to shrink bundle below ~1MB
description: tuxlink-z1b7 bundles 3733 mapped zone polygons (2.1MB, gzip ~530KB). Floor is feature-count-dominated. Dissolve (polygon-union) zones sharing a product into one MultiPolygon per product (~500 features) to remove shared internal edges + cut size below ~1MB. Needs a polygon-union dependency (turf/polygon-clipping). Deferred from z1b7 (adrev flagged dissolve complexity).

title: Weather resolver coverage tail: cross-state-office zones auto-pick (currently browse-all)
description: tuxlink-z1b7 maps 93% of zones to a primary product; the 291 unmapped are mostly zones forecast by an out-of-state office (IL zones under St-Louis LSX / Paducah PAH; etc.) whose product files under a neighbor state's WX_US_<ST> bucket. They safely fall to the always-on browse-all card. Improve to auto-pick via geometry-union across state buckets so these get a primary card too. Office-name gaps (e.g. ILX 'Central Illinois' != 'Lincoln' product) also drop coverage.

title: Forms G12-D: verify need for Readonly (49) / Def: (2) directive handling — likely low/no-op
description:
G12 parity-audit delta (tuxlink-kaz3), filed at P3 because the audit suggests LOW or NO impact — verify before building.

EVIDENCE (152 .txt): Readonly 49 forms, Def: only 2 forms.
  - Readonly: in WLE locks fields after submit (legal-record forms — ICS/HICS 'cannot be changed after submitted'). In tuxlink's webview model the form's OWN HTML/JS enforces field disabling; the rendered page is single-shot (no post-submit re-edit in the loopback session). Likely already covered by the template's markup → no tuxlink-side work. VERIFY: open a Readonly form (e.g. a HICS form) in the webview and confirm locked fields are non-editable as authored.
  - Def: (default values) only 2 forms; defaults are typically baked into the HTML 'value=' attributes anyway. Likely no-op.

Action: confirm the above empirically; close as won't-fix/no-op if confirmed, or re-scope if a real gap surfaces. Part of Forms epic tuxlink-zkuk.
close_reason: Verified no-op (2026-06-12). Readonly: 112 bundled HTML forms already carry their own readonly/disabled attributes, which WebKitGTK honors natively when rendering the form — tuxlink needs no action. The WLE .txt Readonly directive is a POST-SUBMIT field lock (legal-record forms 'cannot be changed after submitted'); moot in tuxlink's single-shot webview model where a submitted form's loopback session closes and there is no re-edit path. Def: (2 forms) defaults are baked into HTML value= attributes. No code needed; the audit hypothesis (likely low/no-op) confirmed.

title: Packet path: dedicated live-config-refresh (stale-snapshot) regression test
description: Phase 3 Task 3.8 moved the packet station call from config.identity.active_full to NativeBackend::active_identity(). The old set_config_refreshes_the_live_config_used_by_connect test proved packet_connect_inner reads live_config() fresh (regression guard for tuxlink-ka7/tuxlink-p5u) VIA the callsign observable. With the callsign gone from config, that test was renamed to connect_requires_active_identity_before_packet_link_open and the stale-snapshot property is no longer exercised on the packet path (config.packet.params is only read after KISS link-open, so a refused-connection test can't observe it). Add a dedicated test that drives a successful KISS link (the two-peer TCP-KISS harness already exists) and asserts that packet.params from a post-construction set_config() — not the construction-time snapshot — reach native_packet_connect. Discovered during tuxlink-0063 Phase 3 session-2 code review.

title: Phase 4 prereq: reconcile per-FULL inbox dir case with normalized stored Callsign
description: Final integration review of Phase 2 (tuxlink-7iy2) flagged: the migration moves the flat inbox to per_full_root(mbox_root, call_str) using the RAW legacy callsign string, while the stored FullIdentity.callsign is Callsign::parse(call_str) (may normalize/uppercase). If a legacy v1 config held a lowercase callsign, the per-FULL inbox dir name and the stored identity's callsign differ in case. Harmless in Phase 2 (the active_full mirror still drives current readers; Phase 2 never reads the per-FULL inbox). Phase 4 (tuxlink-2ns7), which wires per_full_root into live folder resolution, MUST reconcile the dir-name case against the normalized stored callsign. Found 2026-06-11.
close_reason: Reconciled in Phase 4: namespace segment, migration target, and read path all use normalized Callsign::as_str() (PR #627).

title: Phase 2 follow-up: make identity-migration steps self-idempotent (not gated on store-FULL sentinel)
description: Task 5 review (tuxlink-7iy2) flagged: MigrationPlan::execute gates ALL post-save steps (inbox move, Sent/Outbox tag) behind a single 'store has >=1 FULL' sentinel. If the process dies after store.save() but before the inbox move, a re-run no-ops and the flat inbox is silently never migrated under the per-FULL root (no data LOSS; messages still readable at <root>/inbox, but Phase 4's per-FULL reader won't see them). Fix: make each post-save step self-idempotent (move guarded by its own per_full_inbox.exists() test, tagging is already write-idempotent) and run them unconditionally on re-entry rather than gating on the store-FULL sentinel. Found 2026-06-11.

title: Network Post Office: AREDN mesh node/service discovery
description:
Operator request (2026-06-10). 'Network Post Office' is tuxlink's operator-facing label for WLE's AREDN MESH telnet path (Telnet to a locally-run RMS Relay over an AREDN mesh; MESH proper = Winlink over AREDN). Today tuxlink ships NO AREDN support — feature-inventory rows 2.12 (AREDN MESH → CMS) and 9.5 (AREDN mesh services discovery, WLE's ViewMeshServicesJson) are both confirmed unshipped (docs/design/2026-06-02-winlink-express-feature-inventory-verification.md).

WANT (this issue = the DISCOVERY half): let the operator discover reachable Network Post Office / RMS Relay nodes + services on the local AREDN mesh instead of hand-typing a host. WLE does this via DialogUpdateMeshNodes hitting the local node's sysinfo JSON — http://localnode.local.mesh:8080/cgi-bin/sysinfo.json?services=1 — and listing advertised services. Surface discovered nodes in the Network Post Office connect UI (the session-type selector / radio panel) as selectable targets.

KNOWN DIVERGENCE TO SHIP (from the closure plan §2.12): WLE stores a 'Mesh Master Node' setting but IGNORES it, hardcoding the localnode URL — tuxlink should honor the configured master-node host as the sysinfo base URL (fix the latent bug rather than inherit it). Also: WLE stores AREDN node passwords as plaintext .dat — tuxlink defaults to OS keyring (no-disk-creds-default); do NOT inherit plaintext.

OPEN QUESTIONS (design before build):
- Mesh reachability: discovery only works when the host is actually on an AREDN mesh (localnode.local.mesh resolves); behavior + messaging when off-mesh.
- Parse target: which sysinfo.json service fields identify a Post Office / RMS Relay (default plaintext-B2F port 8772 per esy7 notes) vs other mesh services to filter out.
- Refresh/caching model + how discovered nodes feed the connect target list.
- AUDIENCE GATE: the feature inventory flags AREDN MESH as 'Specialized — confirm mesh is target audience' before investing. Operator to confirm demand.

DEPENDS ON the esy7 deep-dive (docs/scratch/winlink-re/findings/aredn-mesh-telnet.md) for the WLE-grounded spec. PREREQ: office-hours/audience-confirm + design pass before implementation.
notes:
FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED from AREDN source (grouse-thistle-wren office-hours 2026-06-10): sysinfo.json?services=1 survives OLSR->Babel — services now sourced from the arednlink daemon (/var/run/arednlink/services), verified in aredn/aredn @ d3f7282. Design doc: docs/design/2026-06-10-aredn-post-office-discovery-design.md (Approach B: list + reachability, Mesh tab in FavoritesTabs; local-mesh only per operator network-citizenship call). Classifier grounded: name contains WINLINK or POST OFFICE + link!='' ; dial by numeric ip; follow the 307 redirect to /a/sysinfo. Supersedes the 2026-06-08 'discovery OUT (OLSR-coupled)' decision — that doc needs a correction note. esy7 now narrowed to capturing real sample payloads.
Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-1w7t-aredn-po-disc. Branch: bd-tuxlink-1w7t/aredn-po-disc. Created 2026-06-11 02:48:07 UTC.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #582 (grouse-thistle-wren): AREDN Network Post Office discovery — local-mesh, arednlink-sourced classifier, bounded liveness probe, dial-by-IP, configured master-node honored. The esy7 dep was satisfied by direct AREDN-firmware source-grounding (aredn/aredn @ d3f7282) rather than a separate deep-dive; esy7's broader §2.12 telnet-to-CMS scope (if any) stays open independently. Codex adversarial round deferred to tuxlink-4kgp (quota).

title: Product gap: Winlink ACCEPTLIST helper flow
description:
The WLE help audit surfaced spam-control/ACCEPTLIST behavior as an operator-admin topic. Tuxlink docs can explain the concept, but the app does not yet expose a helper flow to inspect, update, or guide operators through Winlink ACCEPTLIST management. Determine whether Tuxlink should provide a native helper, a message-template assistant, or docs-only guidance.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Research the current Winlink ACCEPTLIST command/user workflow from primary or locally available reference material before designing.
2. Decide whether Tuxlink should implement a helper flow or defer to docs/manual Winlink behavior.
3. If implemented, make the flow explicit about what message/command will be sent and require the normal operator send action.
4. Add docs and tests for any shipped helper behavior; otherwise document the intentional absence.

title: Product gap: new-message notification and forwarding preferences
description:
The WLE help audit found preference/help coverage for new-message notification and forwarding behavior. Tuxlink alpha has mailbox and inbound-review controls, but no explicit notification or forwarding/rule preference family. Decide the Tuxlink-native scope for desktop notifications, inbound-message alerts, automatic forwarding, and inbox rules; implement or document the intentional absence.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Define which notification and forwarding/rule behaviors belong in Tuxlink alpha versus later.
2. If implemented, expose controls in a coherent settings surface and make defaults conservative for field operation.
3. If deferred, update migration/settings docs to say what is not shipped.
4. Add tests for any shipped preference persistence and notification/forwarding behavior.

title: Product gap: operator usage statistics view
description:
The WLE help audit found that Winlink Express documents usage statistics, while Tuxlink has logging and diagnostics but no operator-facing usage-statistics view. Decide and implement a Tuxlink-native statistics surface if useful: session counts, sent/received message totals, bytes transferred, transport breakdown, recent failures, and retention/privacy behavior. Until implemented, docs should avoid implying this exists.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Design the minimum alpha-worthy usage-statistics surface or explicitly defer it with rationale.
2. If implemented, expose statistics from existing durable session/log/message data without counting private message content.
3. Add user-facing docs for the surface or for the intentional absence.
4. Include tests for aggregation semantics and empty-state behavior.

title: Docs: fill WLE-derived glossary gaps
description:
The WLE help audit marks the glossary as covered/partial and calls for a cross-check after the docs pass. Current glossary is strong, but missing several WLE/operator terms that now appear elsewhere or matter for migration: ACCEPTLIST, channel data, tactical address, Robust Packet, Iridium GO, RMS Trimode/RMS Packet, and radio-only adjacent terms. Add concise, Tuxlink-native definitions without copying WLE prose or promising unsupported features.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Update docs/user-guide/30-glossary.md with missing WLE-derived terms that improve operator comprehension.
2. Keep unsupported modes clearly marked as unsupported/not shipped where applicable.
3. Preserve glossary style and user-guide link rules.
4. Validate docs links and help-topic bundle tests.
notes: PR #572 opened from bd-tuxlink-7znu/glossary-wle-terms. Added WLE-derived glossary terms: ACCEPTLIST, channel data, Iridium GO, RMS Packet, RMS Trimode, Robust Packet/RPR, and tactical address, with unsupported modes clearly marked. Validation: git diff --check; pnpm lint:docs; pnpm exec vitest run src/help/topics.test.ts.

title: Message Delete / Trash workflow (single + bulk) — net-new
description: Split out of tuxlink-l80q (multi-select completion) on 2026-06-09. Message Delete does NOT exist in tuxlink today: no Trash/Deleted folder, no mailbox_delete command, and MessageContextMenu.tsx:37 deliberately excludes 'Deleted' as a move destination ('unimplemented'). The l80q multi-select work bulk-ifies the actions that EXIST (read/unread, Archive, Move) and explicitly defers Delete to here so it can be designed properly rather than half-bolted on (alpha=vettedness). SCOPE: design + build a real message delete/trash workflow — a Deleted/Trash folder (or soft-delete flag), a delete command (single-message), destructive-action confirm, retention + restore (undo / move-back) + 'empty trash' semantics, THEN bulk Delete layered on (mirroring message_set_read_state_bulk / message_move_bulk). GATE: net-new UX feature -> brainstorm first (retention/restore/empty model is the design crux), then plan+build. Depends on l80q's bulk-handler plumbing landing first (reuse the bulk command pattern).

title: Winlink over APRS (APRSLink) on 144.390 MHz
description: Operator request 2026-06-09. Add a Winlink transport that sends/receives Winlink messages over APRS via the APRSLink gateway service, on the 2m APRS national channel 144.390 MHz (US). APRSLink lets a station relay short Winlink messages through the APRS network (APRS-IS-connected igates -> Winlink). NET-NEW RF TRANSPORT: gated on office-hours/brainstorm (design the UX + transport integration) before build, and on RADIO-1 (per-invocation licensee consent for any on-air TX; agent work = make the on-air path runnable+observable, operator transmits). Scope to confirm during design: message-size limits over APRS, addressing/SSID, igate dependency, position vs message frames, AX.25/KISS reuse from the 1200-baud packet work (tuxlink-7fr).

title: converge_build_fixtures: modes 2-6 rotted post-v2 (PR #207); suite not CI-wired
description: Discovered during the tuxlink-edvb.1 cross-vendor adrev (2026-06-09, crag-harrier-towhee). tests/converge_build_fixtures_test.py modes 2,3,4,5,6 FAIL on origin/main (verified against the committed scripts/converge-build.sh, not just local edits). Root cause: the v1->v2 disposable-worktree refactor (PR #207) changed converge-build's internals but the v1-era fixture stubs were not updated — e.g. maybe_wipe_build_artifacts now references ${DISPOSABLE_WT_DIR}, which fixture 06's stub never defines, so it dies 'unbound variable' under set -u before any assertion; modes 4 & 5 exercise functions (stash_bd_state, resolve_untracked_collisions) that the README itself documents as 'eliminated by v2'. SECONDARY (evidence-sufficiency) finding: this suite is NOT run by any CI job — origin/main is green despite 5/8 fixtures failing, so the convergence-machinery regression tests provide false assurance. ACTION: (a) rewrite modes 2/4/5 as v2 structural-immunity assertions (or retire them per the README's own note); (b) fix mode 3/6 stubs to define DISPOSABLE_WT_DIR; (c) wire 'python3 -m unittest tests.converge_build_fixtures_test' into the verify CI job so rot is caught. Modes 1, 7, 8 currently pass. Out of scope for edvb.1 (which fixed the mode-8 root-target gap).

title: MessageList multi-select a11y: aria-selected should reflect bulk selection + aria-multiselectable/grid nav
description: From tuxlink-etxt Task 8 review. The message list now has two selection notions: open (isOpen, drives reading pane) and bulk (inSelection, the Set). aria-selected on each role=row currently reflects only isOpen, so a bulk-selected-but-not-open row announces aria-selected=false (wrong for SR). Full fix needs: aria-selected={isOpen || inSelection}; container role=grid + aria-multiselectable=true; and the grid keyboard-navigation pattern (arrow roving, Home/End) to make the multiselectable announcement honest. Deferred as a dedicated a11y pass (half-wiring multiselectable without the nav pattern is worse than none). Tuxlink-etxt ships Ctrl/Shift+click + Space/Enter; this is the SR-completeness follow-up.

title: store() seeds search-index unread with Inbox-only predicate (inconsistent with list() after tuxlink-etxt)
description: native_mailbox.rs store() (~line 66) seeds the search-index 'unread' column as 'folder == MailboxFolder::Inbox'. After tuxlink-etxt Task 2, list()/list_user() surface unread for Inbox + Archive + user folders from the .read sidecar. So a message stored DIRECTLY to Archive (or a user folder) gets index unread=false while list() reports unread=true. Orthogonal to the read/unread FEATURE (which reads the sidecar, not the index); affects only search-filtered-by-unread. Fix: align store()'s index seed with list()'s predicate (matches!(folder, Inbox | Archive)) and consider user-folder store paths. Found in tuxlink-etxt Task 2 code review (2026-06-08).
notes: Fixed in PR #560. store() seeded index unread as (folder==Inbox); list()/list_user surface unread for Inbox|Archive(+user folders) post-etxt, so a message stored directly to Archive showed unread in folder view but read in the index -> dropped from search-filtered-by-unread. Fix: seed matches!(folder, Inbox|Archive) (list()'s predicate). MailboxFolder is system-only so no direct user-folder store path; moves already sync via update_unread/index_set_folder. TDD red->green + 43 mailbox tests + clippy --all-targets green.
close_reason: Closed

title: Opt-in permitted tile server + Rust tile-gatekeeper + Settings URL field (finer-than-4-char map precision)
description: Split from tuxlink-z9u4 per the 2026-06-08 offline-map adversarial review (C10). The bundled-only foundation (z9u4) ships first with ZERO network tile fetching. THIS issue adds the opt-in capability the locked spec (docs/design/2026-06-07-map-pin-grid-design.md §3) describes: a Settings 'Map tile server URL (permitted source only)' field, a Rust backend tile-gatekeeper (the webview NEVER fetches tiles directly; the backend fetches only from the operator-configured permitted host and serves to the picker so CSP stays 'self'), an on-disk tile cache, and fallback-to-bundled on failure. This is what enables finer-than-4-char precision (the bundled static world image physically resolves only to ~4-char). Public OSM is NOT a permitted option. Open design surface (which hosts are 'permitted', Geographica integration, auth) — needs its own brainstorm/plan before build. DEPENDS-ON tuxlink-z9u4 (the BaseMap + offline source chain).
notes: Implemented Phases 0-10 (full backend gatekeeper + serving + commands + frontend layer/settings/pill + circuit breaker). Per-phase Claude adversarial reviews + cross-provider Codex round (9 real findings fixed incl 3 P1 SSRF/concurrency). PR #517. Follow-up: a1cc must place MapTileSourceSettings + TileStatusPill.
close_reason: Closed

title: Packet relay-chain support for Favorites (additive)
description: Forward gap deferred from the Contacts+Favorites delivery (tuxlink-egmp). The Favorite schema (src-tauri/src/favorites/store.rs) has NO relay/digipeater-path field, so a packet favorite pre-fills only the target callsign — not a 0-2 hop relay chain (the PacketRadioPanel Connect supports relays, but favorites cannot store them). Deliberately out of v1 per the adrev-hardened plan. Additive later: add an Option<Vec<String>> relay field to Favorite (serde default-tolerant, no schema bump needed), have the packet onPrefill set the relays state, and carry it into the FavoriteDial. Not data-loss; purely a convenience gap.

title: Scrub dead Pat references (comments, deprecated field) — keep the 2 live Pat-named paths
description:
Pat sidecar is fully stripped (PR #175, merged 2026-05-31; NativeBackend sole backend). Residual 'Pat' strings cause grep/onboarding confusion (a 2026-06-08 session was misled). Scrub the DEAD ones in a dedicated PR.

SCRUB (dead, safe):
- Stale/misleading comment src/session/logProjection.ts:151-160 (describes a 'pat' LogSource value no longer in the union at L30 — 'backend'|'transport'|'wire').
- Deprecated config field src-tauri/src/config.rs:28 pat_mbo_address (#[deprecated], never read). VERIFY first that the Config serde ignores unknown fields (no deny_unknown_fields) so old config.json still deserializes after removal; remove the #[allow(deprecated)] pat_mbo_address: None literals in tests too.
- History/parity breadcrumb comments mentioning Pat: winlink_backend.rs:31,861; winlink/mod.rs:1,4; bootstrap.rs:69,137; app_backend.rs:3,11,37,40; native_mailbox.rs:1-11; wizard.rs:23-26,384,444; Cargo.toml:23,34-37; Compose.tsx:10-14,410-411; useStatus.ts:499; mailbox/types.ts:14,24; wizard/types.ts:12; Step3TestSend.tsx:5. Reword to drop Pat naming (keep any genuinely-useful 'native replacement' context).
- Test-fixture strings ('Pat HTTP server ready', 'Pat process started', etc.) where they don't assert real behavior.

KEEP — DO NOT scrub (live, functional, NOT the sidecar; deleting breaks things):
- X-Pat-Transport incoming MIME header (ui_commands.rs:1039) — wire-compat for messages from peers running Pat (common across Winlink). tuxlink never emits it.
- 'tuxlink-pat' keyring service key (credentials.rs:29 + winlink_backend.rs:2377,2703) — one-time credential-MIGRATION fallback. Confirm with operator that creds are already migrated before any future removal.

NOT Pat (leave): JPATS Winlink forms, getpat.io Forms CDN, GitHub PAT token, dev/scratch/tuxlink-pat (gitignored reference copy).

SEQUENCING: run AFTER PR #465 (catalog-builder) and tuxlink-raez (contacts-favorites) merge — the breadcrumb comments live in winlink_backend.rs/bootstrap.rs/app_backend.rs/wizard.rs which those branches may touch; a concurrent scrub PR would conflict. Verify build + clippy --all-targets -D warnings after.
notes: UPDATE 2026-06-08: the 'tuxlink-pat' keyring service is being REMOVED in tuxlink-kc3q (operator decision), not kept — it was found to be a LIVE (mis-pointed) password source for ARDOP/VARA-HF CMS, not a dead failsafe. So the KEEP list for this scrub is now ONLY: X-Pat-Transport incoming MIME header (peer wire-compat, ui_commands.rs). Everything else (deprecated pat_mbo_address field, comments, test fixtures) remains scrub-able. Sequence after PR #465 + tuxlink-raez + tuxlink-kc3q land.

title: [bug] logging export manifest can report stale outer archive size
description: Holistic bug hunt 2026-06-06 found build_archive writes the pass-1 compressed size into manifest.compression.outer_archive_bytes, then rebuilds the final archive from modified manifest bytes. The embedded manifest can disagree with the final archive size and ExportResult.archive_size_bytes. Evidence: docs/bug-hunts/2026-06-06-logging-reactor-panic-holistic.md.
acceptance_criteria: Export manifest compression.outer_archive_bytes equals the actual final archive byte length and returned ExportResult.archive_size_bytes; regression coverage validates the manifest extracted from a generated archive.

title: Verify CheckInForm wire-format keys match WLE template (if re-registered with simplified schema)
description:
If the operator picks option (b) for tuxlink-4ai0 (keep simplified UI but map to WLE payload at submit), then the simplified field set in src-tauri/src/forms/templates/checkin.rs needs a submit-time mapping layer in src/compose/CheckInForm.tsx::buildPayload() that translates the 7 UI fields into the corresponding WLE field names. Wire-format alignment test must verify the EMITTED payload keys match the WLE template (msgto, organization, etc.) NOT the simplified UI keys.

If the operator picks (a) full WLE schema or (c) ship-as-generic-message, this issue is moot.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, tuxlink-4ai0, PR #392 follow-up.
close_reason: Closed

title: PositionFormV2 GPS IPC failure: add retry button + error banner
description:
If position_current_fix IPC fails (gpsd not running, no fix), PositionFormV2 currently logs to console and renders with empty grid + no operator-visible error. Add an inline retry button + error banner pattern matching Ics309FormV2's preview-fetch error state.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Replace window.prompt with proper modal for FormDraftLibrary slot label entry
description:
All 3 wired forms in PR #392 (PositionFormV2, Ics213Form, BulletinForm) — plus CheckInForm if/when re-registered (see tuxlink-4ai0) — use window.prompt for slot label entry. Limitations: blocking, inaccessible, no validation feedback, accepts whitespace-only labels (no trim).

Replace with a proper modal that: explicit label/input/button layout; trims whitespace and rejects empty; shows duplicate-label warning if proposed label already exists for this form_id; keyboard-accessible (Tab/Enter/Esc).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: FormDraftLibrary: 'Update slot' in-place affordance
description:
In PR #392, 'Save as slot…' on all 3 wired forms (PositionFormV2, Ics213Form, BulletinForm) always creates a NEW slot — even when a slot is already selected. This is intentional v0.0.1 UX (the always-create comment in each form's saveSlot documents the decision). Operators who want to update an existing slot must delete + recreate.

Future UX: either (a) a second 'Update slot' button visible when a slot is selected, or (b) a modal on Save that disambiguates create-vs-update intent. Either approach needs the saveSlot to switch from setSlots(prev => [...prev, newSlot]) (append) to a replace-in-place pattern (prev.map(s => s.slot_id === updated.slot_id ? updated : s)).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Bundle Leaflet offline tile pack for PositionFormV2 (offline-first UX)
description:
PR #392 PositionMapWidget uses Leaflet with OSM online tiles (blocked by current CSP — see tuxlink-bt2q) + blank-canvas + grid-square overlay fallback. A bundled tile pack (e.g. low-res world map at zoom 0-5) would give a richer offline UX independent of the CSP allowlist decision. Operator decision 2026-06-04 deferred this to P3.

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: React.lazy form-registry entries to split Leaflet + printpdf from main bundle
description:
HTML Forms P2 (PR #392) added Leaflet (~143 kB gzip) and printpdf-related JS to the main index chunk. Both load on app launch even when the operator never opens Position Report or ICS-309. React.lazy on the form-registry entries would defer these until the operator picks the relevant form.

The vitest testTimeout was raised from 5s to 15s (Task 2 commit 6487ec2) to accommodate the cumulative form-registry growth — a band-aid for this issue. Once React.lazy lands, the testTimeout can drop back to 5s (or 10s for safety). Also resolves the 5 AppShell vitest-flake class that surfaced in PR #392's full sweep (tests pass in isolation, time out under cumulative module-load pressure).

Refs: tuxlink-hnkn, PR #392 follow-up.

title: Smart auth diagnostics: thread credential_scope into Re-enter-password affordance (Codex post-impl MAJOR #6)
description: The Mode 3 Re-enter password inline form currently renders unconditionally and writes to the configured primary callsign from config_read. Per spec §4.3 (i), an aux/unknown-scope failure should route to 'Try a different callsign' (the wizard) instead, to avoid overwriting the wrong password. Requires threading CredentialScope into AuthClassified events (Rust → Tauri → React) and consuming it in the banner. Filed as Codex post-impl MAJOR #6 follow-up.

title: Tauri-runtime integration test for cms_connect_test (tuxlink-7do4 Task 14)
description: Drives a real Tauri AppHandle through cms_connect_test against a synthetic in-memory CMS to verify the end-to-end auth-only contract + single-flight + AttemptId emission. The unit-level behavior is already covered by session::run_exchange_with_events tests; this is the integration smoke.

title: Task 11 follow-ups: viewer substitution-in-script + dedupe token mint + OpenViewerResult shared type
description:
Minor + deferred Important items from Task 11 code-quality review (commit 223c93c). None block ship; address if natural during Task 13 polish or as standalone follow-ups.

## I1 (Important) — substitute_var_placeholders runs inside script/style blocks

src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs:357-389. Function walks entire HTML doc and replaces every {var X} with html_escape(value). If a WLE viewer template uses {var X} inside a <script> block (intended as a client-side reference), substitution corrupts JS source. NOT a security issue (html_escape neutralizes <), IS a correctness issue if any WLE catalog viewer relies on unsubstituted {var X} for client-side use. Risk unknown — depends on WLE catalog conventions.

Mitigation: (a) note as known limitation in docstring; (b) skip substitution inside <script>/<style> blocks (more correct, more code). Suggest tracking in docs/pitfalls/implementation-pitfalls.md.

## M1 — dead branch in open_webview_viewer's viewer-filename resolution
src-tauri/src/ui_commands.rs:1360-1362. find_form's BUNDLED_FORMS branch is dead code in practice (all 5 native forms have React Views, so MessageView never mounts WebviewFormViewer for them). Intent is defensive. Comment should acknowledge.

## M2 — OpenViewerResult interface duplicates OpenFormResult shape
WebviewFormViewer.tsx:41-45 vs WebviewFormHost.tsx:76-80. Same 3 fields. Extract to shared module (src/forms/types.ts or new file).

## M3 — fieldValues HashMap collapses multi-value fields
MessageView.tsx:202-205. FormPayload.fields is Array<[k, v]> tuples; same key can appear multiple times. HashMap keeps last. Acceptable for Viewer read-only display; add a comment documenting the intentional collapse.

## M4 — OpenedViewerSession doesn't derive Debug
Symmetric with OpenedSession (also no Debug). Add #[derive(Debug)] to both for easier debugging.

## M5 — mint_session_token retry loop duplicated in open + open_viewer
http_server.rs:780-784 and :812-815. Extract mint_unique_token(&mut guard) helper.

Also dedupe with mint_consent_token from modem_status.rs per tuxlink-7cn1 (separately filed).

## Parent
bd: tuxlink-tzr5 (alpha-forms umbrella)

Add a paragraph to the Architecture section + a bullet to Current features. Match the existing pre-alpha tone: 'working code paths exist' not 'use this in production'.

Out of scope:

title: Extract shared 16-hex token-mint utility (dedupe modem_status + forms::http_server)
description:
Both src-tauri/src/modem_status.rs::mint_consent_token (~line 182) and src-tauri/src/forms/http_server.rs::mint_session_token (~line 523) implement byte-for-byte identical 16-hex random token generators (16-iter loop over rand::random::<u8>() & 0xF -> char::from_digit). The forms version's doc comment explicitly cross-references the modem version.

Extract to a shared util module so a future hardening (CSPRNG, length change) only edits one place. Suggested location: src-tauri/src/util/token.rs or src-tauri/src/security/token.rs. Also introduce a named const for the length (currently '16' is repeated across the mint function, tests, and the FormSessionRegistry doc comment) — e.g., SESSION_TOKEN_HEX_LEN = 16.

Caught by code-quality reviewer on PR for tuxlink-tzr5 (P1 forms Task 8). Non-blocking — flagged as I1 (Important) + M3 (Minor) but both deferred to follow-up.

Effort: small. Touches ~3 files. No behavioral change.

title: useMermaidRender hook doesn't re-run on topic switch
description: Latent bug found during Task 14 code review of tuxlink-ymiv PR #1 (PR #347). The src/help/useMermaidRender.ts useEffect is keyed on [containerRef] which is a stable ref object, so it runs ONCE on ReadingPane mount. Since HelpView renders ReadingPane with no 'key' prop, switching to a topic that contains Mermaid blocks AFTER first mount won't re-trigger the hook. Currently latent because no shipping topic uses Mermaid. Fix: change dep array to [containerRef, html] in src/help/useMermaidRender.ts. Must land BEFORE any docs PR ships a Mermaid-containing topic. Discovered by Task 14 reviewer during PR #1 implementation.

title: Docs Phase 3: Hamexandria-informed conceptual content with 6 attribution disciplines
description: Phase 3 of the tuxlink-s8qu epic (parent). Hamexandria-informed how-it-works content explaining ARDOP / VARA / Packet / Telnet from a conceptual frame — protocol overviews, when-to-use-which-mode, common pitfalls. Operator's six attribution disciplines (VERBATIM): (1) NEVER copy YouTube transcripts verbatim into docs/user-guide/*.md; (2) USE Hamexandria as a research aid to inform tuxlink's own (originally-written) explanations; (3) where a creator's framing is genuinely the right framing to cite, attribute prominently (creator name, video title, link to source video) and quote only what fair use plainly permits; (4) prefer to link OUT to the creator's video rather than embed their words; (5) get explicit permission from creators for any substantial extract; (6) maintain a CREDITS / acknowledgments section listing creators whose explanations shaped the docs. Operator framing: 'creators we are referencing are my people and they work hard for little compensation. We do not want to steal their work.' Hamexandria access: 'uv run ham-search' in dev/scratch/ham-knowledge-store/ (gitignored). Cross-checks against the spec doc docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-html-forms-full-parity-design.md (likely outdated for s8qu specifically; consult original tuxlink-s8qu description as source-of-truth).
notes: Absorbed into tuxlink-ymiv spec. Phase 3 (Hamexandria conceptual content) is now distributed across content PRs #2-#6 per the per-section research note workflow in spec §5.2. The six attribution disciplines are codified in spec §5.1.
close_reason: Closed

title: Docs Phase 2: new topic files for shipped-but-undocumented features
description: Phase 2 of the tuxlink-s8qu epic (parent). Phase 1 (polish + accuracy on existing 10 topics) shipped via PR #336. Phase 2 scope: add NEW topic files under docs/user-guide/ for features that exist in the code but lack a dedicated topic. Candidates: (a) the help window itself (meta-topic explaining the in-app Documentation viewer + sidebar + reading-width + font-size + search affordances); (b) Saildocs GRIB request workflow (src/grib/GribRequestPanel.tsx) — the operator-facing 'how do I request a GRIB' walkthrough including the message-format the back-end expects; (c) Theme Designer detail — go deeper than 08-color-schemes' current treatment, covering every token group + the per-token color picker + Save / Cancel semantics; (d) any other shipped feature surfaced during Phase 2 scoping. EACH new topic file needs (i) a number prefix > 10 (or insertion into the existing range with renames), (ii) registration in src/help/topics.ts SECTIONS array, (iii) accuracy probe against the current code, (iv) cross-links to neighbors using bare .md refs (no anchors, no out-of-bundle paths — per Phase 1's hygiene work). Likely also need screenshots — operator's framing in tuxlink-s8qu: 'images either cropped or full screenshots where possible. They're often worth 1,000 words.'
notes: Absorbed into tuxlink-ymiv spec. Phase 2 (new topic files) is now PR #2 onward in the spec's phasing, with per-PR bd issues filed at each PR's start. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-docs-knowledge-base-design.md.

title: @media print stylesheet — hide dashboard/sidebar/statusbar on print
description:
Follow-up to tuxlink-j0m3 (Print menu wiring). With no @media print rules, window.print() captures the full app chrome (dashboard ribbon, sidebar, status bar, radio panel if mounted) alongside the MessageView. That's serviceable for "screenshot the message to PDF" but not what an email print should look like.

Wanted behavior:
- @media print hides .dashboard-ribbon, .sidebar, .statusbar, the radio-panel column.
- MessageView prints full-width with the header (from/to/subject/date) + body + form attachments.
- Page-break-inside: avoid on the message header block so it doesn't split across pages.
- Tauri webview honors @media print rules natively — no Tauri-side work needed.

Out of scope:
- Multi-page form rendering (HTML Forms work; deferred until form rendering itself ships).
- Print-only views (a separate route) — start with stylesheet rules.

Files touched (expected): src/shell/AppShell.css OR a new src/print.css imported from main.tsx.
notes:
Smoke walk item 28: current Print behavior produces a vertically formatted screenshot of the visible Tuxlink app, not an intelligent printout of the message contents. Acceptance should verify actual message-focused print output, not merely that Ctrl+P opens a print dialog.

Implemented print stylesheet in bd-tuxlink-zdfj/print-stylesheet: hides shell chrome/list/radio panel for print, lets MessageView print full-width, and avoids splitting the message header. Quality gates passed locally. Closure remains blocked by open dependency tuxlink-j0m3.

title: Docs expansion + polish pass + Hamexandria-sourced content (ethical attribution)
description: The current docs/user-guide/ tree (10 topics from PR #214 by gulch-osprey-bog) is a solid first slice but is incomplete: (a) features shipped since that PR (themes / theme designer, status bar, VARA TCP, ARDOP UI, HTML Forms, search, user folders, MessageView attachments, Saildocs GRIB framework, WLE catalog, message sort) are not documented; (b) the existing topics need a polish pass for voice + accuracy + cross-links; (c) per operator: include explanatory content drawn from Hamexandria (the local-vector-DB built from amateur-radio YouTube transcripts, memory: reference_hamexandria) about how Tuxlink and Winlink work conceptually — protocol overviews, when-to-use-which-mode framing, common pitfalls. **CRITICAL: Hamexandria content is third-party creator material with real copyright; treat it ethically.** Operator's words: 'creators we're referencing are my people and they work hard for little compensation. We don't want to steal their work.' Implications: (1) NEVER copy transcripts verbatim into docs/user-guide/*.md; (2) USE Hamexandria as a research aid to inform tuxlink's own (originally-written) explanations; (3) where a creator's framing is genuinely the right framing to cite, attribute prominently (creator name, video title, link to the source video) and quote only what fair use plainly permits; (4) prefer to link OUT to the creator's video rather than embed their words; (5) get explicit permission from creators for any substantial extract; (6) maintain a CREDITS / acknowledgments section listing creators whose explanations shaped the docs. Pairs with tuxlink-0gsy (help-window redesign) — the redesigned window surfaces this expanded content. Suggest splitting into phases: (P1) polish + accuracy pass on existing 10 topics; (P2) topics for shipped-but-undocumented features; (P3) Hamexandria-informed conceptual / how-it-works topics with attribution discipline.
notes: Phase 1 shipped via PR #336 (willow-yew-esker). Phase 2 + Phase 3 carved out as tuxlink-m38d + tuxlink-v8lw with dep edges.

title: Investigate gstack upstream divergence from April 14 snapshot
description:
On 2026-05-05 03:29:30 the gstack install at ~/.claude/skills/gstack/ was updated; the update emptied the bundled workflow sub-skills (office-hours, investigate, ship, plan-eng-review, design-consultation, design-review, document-release, retro, checkpoint, health, qa, review, plan-ceo-review, plan-design-review, design-html, design-shotgun, devex-review, plan-devex-review, qa-only) while leaving the empty subdirs in place. The tuxlink CLAUDE.md skill routing went silently broken for ~28 days until the 2026-06-02 thistle-swallow-cedar session discovered it.

Recovered by restoring the full April 14 self-export from /home/administrator/claude-code-export/skills/gstack/ to ~/.claude/skills/gstack/, then symlinking each CLAUDE.md-routed sub-skill to the top level of ~/.claude/skills/ (the Claude Code harness does not recurse into sub-skill directories).

This issue tracks the followup decision: WHY did gstack restructure? Is there a current upstream (the SKILL.md says 'Regenerate: bun run gen:skill-docs' — there's a bun-based source) that we should pull from? Were the workflow sub-skills intentionally removed (perhaps refactored into a separate gstack-workflows package) or accidentally dropped during a regen? Until this is decided, we are on a frozen April 14 gstack codebase — fine for now but blocks any future gstack updates.

Not blocking. P3.

title: [bug] ARDOP Capture/Playback dropdowns show all ALSA plugin chains; restrict to hardware-only entries
description: Follow-up to tuxlink-y7x7 (PR #242). The Capture and Playback dropdowns surface every entry arecord -L / aplay -L return — including plugin chains (lavrate, samplerate, speexrate, speex, upmix, vdownmix, jack, oss, null, sysdefault, usbstream HDMI passthroughs) that aren't useful for ARDOP. The backend already classifies plughw:CARD= / hw:CARD= entries as isHardware; the frontend uses that for SORTING but should use it for FILTERING. Restrict the dropdown to isHardware entries only; leave the manual-fallback input for the rare 'I really want pulse' case. PTT dropdown already filters to kind=='usb' so no change needed there. Update 'No devices found' hint to say 'No USB audio interfaces found — plug one in and Refresh, or use Manual.'

title: VARA: extend DashboardRibbon + useStatusData to recognize VARA session state
description:
Operator (2026-06-02): top dashboard ribbon shows 'Telnet' + 'disconnected' even when VARA session is Open. Cause: useStatusData (src/shell/useStatus.ts) + DashboardRibbon (src/shell/DashboardRibbon.tsx) read from a unified pipeline that knows ARDOP, Packet, Telnet — not VARA.

Fix (separate from MYCALL/session_log work):
1. Add VARA polling to useStatusData (or have it subscribe to a vara:status event the backend emits)
2. Extend the connection-tone calculation to map VaraState.{open,connecting,error} → ribbon state
3. Update the mode label so when VARA is active, ribbon reads 'VARA HF' / 'VARA FM' instead of the prior mode
4. Tests on the new tone calculation + label mapping

This is the larger surface — touches DashboardRibbon + useStatus + status pipeline. Defer to land after the immediate MYCALL/session-log polish.

title: [bug] Outbox sidebar entry missing queue-depth badge; status bar shows '1 to send' but sidebar shows no count
description: When a draft is queued in Outbox, the status bar's outbox segment correctly shows '1 to send' (src/shell/AppShell.tsx:589 reads outbox.messages.length directly). But the FolderSidebar Outbox entry does NOT show a count badge. Root cause: the counts object passed to FolderSidebar (src/shell/AppShell.tsx:224-227) only populates inbox + sent — outbox is omitted. FolderSidebar already renders a badge when counts[item.id] is set (FolderSidebar.tsx:94-98), so the fix is to add outbox: outbox.messages.length to the counts literal. The outbox useMailbox handle is already in scope (line 191) for the status bar. Surfaced 2026-06-02 by operator after PR #219 (tuxlink-su2h) enabled the Outbox folder entry but didn't wire its count.
